AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

This chapter gives basic information about this guide, training and support.

1.1

About this Guide

This guide describes how to perform specific operations on AVEVA NET Dashboard  while

accessing the application from a mobile device or a tablet running on iOS 7+,  Android or

Windows.

Following are some of the key operations you can perform using the mobile site:

· Browse for information using the asset breakdown structure
· Search for tag and document content and filter the results by object type
· View tag summary information, including attribute data and associated

documents

· View and navigate drawings, documents and 3D models
· Access the Basket for quick access to your saved tag and document links

1.2

Audience

The AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site is meant for project or operations

staff needing to access AVEVA NET Workhub information from mobile and tablet devices.

1.3

Related Documents

· AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
· AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

1.4

Product and Training Information

For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of

AVEVA's website (http://www.aveva.com/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA

Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

Release 5.1.8

Page 5

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.5

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact

AVEVA Helpdesk (https://avevahelpdesk.aveva.com), and register a support incident.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)

and the details of the incident. We will endeavour to fix the software issue or consider

the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the

status of the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA

Helpdesk for which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact

the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

Release 5.1.8

Page 6

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This section gives an overview of AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard with specific

reference to its mobile site.

Release 5.1.8

Page 7

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.1

About AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard is a powerful and secure web-based solution for

collating, contextualising, cross-referencing and visualising data and documents of all

types, whatever their location or origin.

The AVEVA NET Gateways are the key to the data interoperability power of AVEVA NET.

This extensive suite of interfaces provide access to all types of information, whether

originating from AVEVA's or third-party software, from business systems, or from tailored

applications implemented by AVEVA's Enterprise Solutions specialists. The Gateways scan

the information sources and automatically extract tag to tag, tag to document and

document to document relationships. Documents published through the Gateways are

accurately rendered and enhanced through the automatic creation of hotspots and

hyperlinks which provide additional intelligence for easy identification and navigation of

tags.

The information and relationships extracted by the Gateways are securely stored and

maintained within AVEVA NET Workhub. AVEVA NET Workhub exploits this

interconnectivity to provide powerful search capabilities which, combined with its

knowledge of tag and document associations, delivers fast and effective location of

project and asset information. Access to this information can be tailored by category,

security level or detail level to suit the needs of different types of user. Through its

aggregation of information from disparate sources AVEVA NET Workhub is also uniquely

placed to report on the quality, consistency and completeness of that information,

improving project compliance and enabling efficient handover to operations.

Release 5.1.8

Page 8

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

AVEVA NET Dashboard provides end users with a single point of access to the aggregated,

validated information. All types of project and asset information can be browsed and

visualised quickly and intuitively through a simple web interface; from tag and document

metadata, electronic and scanned documents, reports and project plans, to 2D drawings,

3D models and laser scans. Through the automatic creation of hotspots and hyperlinks,

which provide additional intelligence to aid in easy identification and navigation of tags,

AVEVA NET Dashboard significantly reduces the amount of time spent looking for

information, and ensures that the information found is always of known quality.

AVEVA offers a range of information management solutions, based on the unique AVEVA

NET™ technology, to meet the needs of different users at different points in the asset life

cycle. These solutions deliver an open environment that provides access to information

regardless of the application source, and with the appropriate level of information access,

validation and control to support critical project and operational activities. The result is a

single, trusted source of information that reduces risk through rapid and better-informed

decision making.

Release 5.1.8

Page 9

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.1.1

AVEVA NET Dashboard - Mobile Site

You can access AVEVA NET Dashboard using your mobile devices or tablets.

The supported mobile Operating Systems are iOS 7+, Android and Windows.

The AVEVA NET Dashboard mobile site has limited functionality and does not support the

following:

· Advanced Search
· Saved Searches
· Query Forms
· Reports
· Sets
· Export
· Settings

You must access the AVEVA NET Dashboard main website to perform the above

operations.

Release 5.1.8

Page 10

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 3 - Logging on to AVEVA NET Dashboard - Mobile Site

3

Logging on to AVEVA NET Dashboard - Mobile Site

When you access the AVEVA NET Dashboard using a mobile or tablet device, you are

automatically redirected to the mobile version of the website. If your device has the

Request Desktop Site feature enabled in its browser, you can use that option to switch to

the desktop version of the website.

Note: After you have logged on to the mobile site, you can tap the user icon and then tap

the Change Role link to switch to any of the roles you have been given access to.

Release 5.1.8

Page 11

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 4 - Using Search

4

Using Search

You can search for tag and document content in the AVEVA NET Dashboard mobile site.

You can also filter the search results by object type.

To initiate a search using the AVEVA NET Dashboard mobile site:

1.

Log on to the AVEVA NET Dashboard mobile site.

The Home page is displayed.

2.

Tap the search icon.

The search page is displayed.

3.

Type a search term in the search box displayed and tap the search icon again.

The search results are displayed. The items are listed by ID and Type.

Note: To add items to your basket, tap the 'basket' icon on the search results

page. Then, the system shows a notification confirming that the items are

added to the basket. For more information, see Using Basket.

4.

Tap the search icon visible in the top-right of the page to initiate a new search.

Release 5.1.8

Page 12

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 4 - Using Search

4.1

Filtering the Search Results

You can filter the search results by using the configured search categories at the bottom

of the search results page.

After a filter has been applied, subsequent searches from the search results page comply

to the search filter criteria. If the number of categories is more than the maximum that

the device screen can display, you should scroll across or swipe horizontally in the footer

section to see them all.

Release 5.1.8

Page 13

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 5 - Navigating in the Mobile Site

5

Navigating in the Mobile Site

When you search for an item and then tap one of the search results in the AVEVA NET

Dashboard Mobile site, the Summary View of the item is displayed.

When you tap the 2D or 3D document sections, the default 2D or 3D document is

displayed and the current item is highlighted on the model. You can manipulate the

model in 3D space to select any component.

On tapping any item on the model, a Content Card is shown on the screen at the bottom

of the model. The Content Card displays the ID, Name and the Type of the selected item.

To close the Content Card, tap the close icon (X).

To show the list of documents, tap and hold the 2D or 3D menu item for a while.

Release 5.1.8

Page 14

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 5 - Navigating in the Mobile Site

To refresh the view to show the selected document, tap any of the document names in

the list.

Release 5.1.8

Page 15

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 5 - Navigating in the Mobile Site

To expand the current 2D or 3D document to full screen, tap the Full Screen button

(shown on the top-right corner). Expanding the document hides the header and the

footer. Tapping the button again restores the document to normal view.

Release 5.1.8

Page 16

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 6 - Using Item Summary Views

6

Using Item Summary Views

When you tap an item either in the search results or in the basket, a configurable Item

Summary View is displayed based on the item's type. You can see the Mobile View,

which is similar to the Summary View and the Content Card View in the AVEVA NET

Dashboard desktop website.

The Mobile View loads different HTML pages based on the item type. You can create your

own versions of the BootstrapSampleViews.xml and

BootstrapSampleDataGroups.xml files that can be found in the path: [Installation

Path]\Import\Server\Bootstrap.

For more information, see the Customising Sample HTML and CSS section (under Part 3 -

Loading Data and Other Configuration) in the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard

Installation Guide.

Following are the sample HTML pages, which are used for loading the Mobile View:

· mobileDefaultSummary.html

· mobileDocumentSummary.html

· mobileTagSummary.html

Release 5.1.8

Page 17

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 6 - Using Item Summary Views

If you open a document of type SVG, PDF, Microsoft Office document or any sub-class of

Document Content, then the Document View (which loads the

mobileDocumentSummary.html) is displayed.

Release 5.1.8

Page 18

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 6 - Using Item Summary Views

If you open an item which is a sub-class of Functional Artifact, then the Item Summary

View (which loads the mobileTagSummary.html) is displayed.

Note: The display of both Document View and Item Summary View are configurable

using the desired data groups.

In the Item Summary View page, you can:

· Tap the basket on the top-right corner to add the item being viewed to your

basket.

· Tap the < icon to go back to the previous search results page.
· Tap the home icon on the top-right corner to go to the Home page.

The icons in the footer of the Item Summary View correspond to specific mobile

summary sections. You can access information about an item by navigating to the

different sections. These sections are configurable and can be used in a View to display

the data in a variety of ways using data groups.

Release 5.1.8

Page 19

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 6 - Using Item Summary Views

For more information about configuration of the Mobile Item Summary View, refer to

the Configuring Views section (under Part 3 - Loading Data and Other Configuration) in

the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Release 5.1.8

Page 20

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 7 - Accessing Documents

7

Accessing Documents

You can access 2D documents (for example, PDF, SVG drawings or Microsoft Office

documents) and 3D models in the AVEVA NET Dashboard mobile site.

You can also view documents using the Accusoft™ Viewer and access the document

contents from an external DMS.

7.1

Viewing 2D Documents

You can view 2D documents on the mobile site from either the search results or the

basket. When you tap the ID of any item in the search results or the basket, you can see

the corresponding item view. When you tap a 2D Document type (for example, PDF, SVG

drawings or Microsoft Office documents), you are directed to the Document Viewer.

The Document Viewer displays the ID and name of the document in the header. You can

scroll down a multi-page PDF or MS Word document and zoom in, zoom out and pan

across an SVG document.

To start a new search,  tap Search in the header. Tapping the Back icon takes you back to

the previous page.

To add the document being viewed to the basket, tap Add to Basket in the header. The

system shows a notification confirming that the item is added to the basket.

Release 5.1.8

Page 21

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 7 - Accessing Documents

7.2

Viewing Documents in Accusoft™ Viewer

If you have deployed the Accusoft™ web deployment package and configured the

Accusoft™ Server, you can view 2D documents using the Accusoft™ Viewer. All of the

Accusoft™ Viewer functions that are available on the desktop site are also available on

the mobile site, except the ones for the annotation tools.

Release 5.1.8

Page 22

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 7 - Accessing Documents

The Accusoft™ viewer is fully adaptive, so it supports a wide range of screen sizes and

orientations by changing how it renders certain screen elements. For example, on devices

with screen sizes below 7 inches, or resolutions below 800x600 pixels, a different icon set

with bigger touch regions is displayed. Also, the layout of the toolbar adapts to fit the

screen and resolution size — with specific icons being either displayed differently or

hidden altogether. The example below shows what the viewer toolbar looks like on a 5

inch screen, with the touch area highlighted. The ‘zoom’ toolbar icon changes from a ‘+’

and ‘–‘ with a scale factor in between, to a magnifying glass, to make the most use of the

screen space.

Release 5.1.8

Page 23

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 7 - Accessing Documents

7.3

Accessing Document Contents from an External DMS

You can view the files stored in an external DMS (such as SharePoint™ or Documentum™)

within the Document Viewer on the mobile site when:

· The requisite configuration is present in the web configuration file, and
· You have the correct rights to view the document.

If you as a user have not been authenticated to view the file in the current session on

either the desktop or the mobile site, a dialog box similar to the following is displayed

where you must enter your user credentials for the DMS. This prompt occurs only once

per session.

After you authenticate, you can view the files in the Document Viewer.

Release 5.1.8

Page 24

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 7 - Accessing Documents

7.4

Viewing 3D Models

You can view 3D Models on the mobile site from either the search results or the basket.

When you tap on an item of 3D Models type from either the search results or the basket,

you are redirected to the Document View showing the 3D model. You can manipulate the

model in the 3D space to select any of its component.

When you tap on a specific component of the 3D model, the Content Card is shown on

the screen. The Content Card displays the basic details of the selected component.

Tapping on the Content Card header refreshes the page to display the Summary View of

the selected item.

Release 5.1.8

Page 25

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 8 - Using Basket

8

Using Basket

After you log on to the mobile site, you are taken to the Home page, where you can see

the number of items you have in your basket. The basket icon has a badge only when

there are items in the basket. In the example screenshot given below, the number of

items in the basket are four.

Release 5.1.8

Page 26

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 8 - Using Basket

The contents of the basket are the same on the desktop and the mobile site:

· Tap the basket to see the contents inside it.

· To remove an item from the basket, tap the corresponding remove icon (X).
· To empty the basket, tap Clear Basket at bottom-right of the page.
· To start a new search, tap the ‘add’ icon (+) at top-right of the page .
· To navigate to the previous page, tap the ‘<’ button. Using this button, you can

navigate all the way back to the Home page.

Release 5.1.8

Page 27

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

CHAPTER 8 - Using Basket

Note: To add items to your basket, tap the basket icon on the search results page. A

notification is shown confirming that the items are added to the basket.

Release 5.1.8

Page 28

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

Welcome to AVEVA Change Manager User Guide.

This chapter explains how this guide is organised and introduces other sources of

information that can help you.

1.1

About this User Guide

This user guide contains the information you need to work with AVEVA Change

Manager™ effectively. It contains detailed information about the following:

· How to navigate through AVEVA Change Manager.
· How to manage Working Set and Modification Projects.
· How to manage Working Set Tags and Documents.
· How to use Fast Track modification of existing objects.
· How to apply changes to multiple Tags and Documents using Bulk Upload

functionality.

· How to validate data against defined information standards and generate

reports.

1.2

Summary of Chapters

Chapter Name

Description

Product Overview

Gives an overview of AVEVA Change Manager.

Getting Started

Introduces you to AVEVA Change Manager and explains
a few functions you need to start working. Specifically,
this chapter describes how to log in, navigate, use the
menus, edit your User Profile and exit.

Manage Modification Projects Contains an explanation of Modification Projects,

Manage Working Sets

detailing how to create, view and update the projects. It
also explains how to manage, activate and void
Modification Projects, how to send a Modification
Project for approval and how to cancel the approval.

Introduces you to Working Sets, describes how to create,
view details, update, download and transfer them. The
chapter explains how to reserve a new Tag, and remove,
view and update a Tag, within the context of a Working
Set. It also explains how to reserve a new Document, and
view, remove and update a Document, upload physical
files to a Document within the context of a Working Set.

Release 5.0.11

Page 6

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

Chapter Name

Description

Process Working Sets

Fast Track Modification

Documentation Centre

Report Centre

Provides details on how to activate and void a Working
Set, fix Working Set validation errors, update Tag and
Document statuses, send for verification, cancel
verification and complete a Working Set.

Provides details on how to quickly modify Tags and
Documents.

Gives you an overview of the Documentation Centre and
the associated links.

Explains how to generate the Working Set reports
provided in the Report Centre.

1.3

Audience

This guide is intended for anyone who uses AVEVA Change Manager. It is assumed that

you are familiar with using the Internet and more specifically, the organisation of

engineering information and processes used in your company.

1.4

Related Documents

· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

1.5

Product and Training Information

AVEVA's website (https://www.aveva.com/en/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA

Regional Support Centre (https://www.aveva.com/en/support).

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.6

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact

AVEVA Knowledge & Support Center (https://softwaresupport.aveva.com), and register a

support incident.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)

and the details of the incident. We will endeavour to fix the software issue or consider

the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the

status of the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA

Knowledge & Support Center for which you should have valid login credentials. For any

other queries, contact the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre

(https://www.aveva.com/en/support).

Release 5.0.11

Page 8

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This chapter gives an overview of AVEVA Change Manager.

2.1

AVEVA Change Manager

AVEVA Change Manager is part of AVEVA’s Information Engineering category of products

within the AVEVA Enterprise portfolio. It provides the essential means for robust and

visible management of the continual changes to information in the digital asset which

accompany the many changes – large and small – made to the physical asset during its

lifecycle.

Throughout an asset’s working life, both in normal operations and during projects such as

brownfield modifications or data clean-up exercises, many different users need to access

and change technical information that originates from a variety of sources. Not only must

this information remain both accurate and available, it must be maintained so by the

rigorous control of any changes made to it. Achieving this requires standardised tools,

business processes and procedures for controlling changes to information and

synchronising them across multiple business systems.

AVEVA Change Manager enables Owner Operators to optimise, control and monitor their

change management process for all technical information. It can be customised and

tailored to your needs. Combined with AVEVA NET, the solution highlights information

changes to its users, enabling better-informed decision making. Implemented as a

combined solution with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, users are able to validate new and legacy

data against configurable, predefined information standards before making controlled

updates using AVEVA Change Manager. By assuring information integrity, AVEVA Change

Manager delivers increased operational efficiency and regulatory compliance on all types

of plants.

The key features of AVEVA Change Manager:

· Full management of changes to technical information created by Modification

Projects and work packages.

· Reservation of new Tags and Documents resulting from changes entered directly

in AVEVA Change Manager or by an upload process.

· Validation of all data against information handover specifications, external

application registers and code tables.

· Support for ISO 15926.
· Class library driven, multi-lingual.

Release 5.0.11

Page 9

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

· Efficient information search functions.
· Fast-track capability for expediting urgent changes without the need for a formal

approval process.

· Best-practice workflows to support complex business processes.
· Integration with AVEVA NET for browsing, navigating, contextualising and

visualising the entire information asset.

· Integration with Enterprise Asset Management (EAM), Document Management

(DM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, whether AVEVA’s or third-

party.

· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

· Role-based user access control, enabling internal and external organisations to

manage large quantities of information in an orderly manner.

· Full audit trail of all changes.
· Better-informed decision making, leading to greater efficiency in both routine

asset operations and brownfield projects.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3

Getting Started

This chapter introduces you to AVEVA Change Manager. Specifically, this chapter provides

information on the following topics:

·

·

Log on to AVEVA Change Manager

Access the Home Page

· Menus and basic page components
·

Edit User Profile

·

User Roles

3.1

Assumptions and Prerequisites

Assumptions

This document assumes that AVEVA Change Manager has been installed and deployed by

following the instructions provided in the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager Installation Guide. This document assumes that AVEVA Change Manager has

been configured for you to use in accordance with the requirements, procedures and

policies of your company by the authorised personnel.

Prerequisites

In preparation for using AVEVA Change Manager the following prerequisites should be

met:

· The same version of the Class Library is uploaded into AVEVA Change Manager
and AVEVA NET Workhub™ and it contains the definitions for the required

Assets.

· Reference Data are available, for example, Assets, Systems, Disciplines,

Contractors, Areas.

· The user account has been created for you by the system Administrator and you

have been granted the required permissions.

· If you are managing documents and physical files in AVEVA Change Manager,

Document Management System (DMS) should be enabled. If you are managing

only the metadata, this is not required.

· If you do not have any DMS to manage physical files, the local DMS should be

enabled.

Release 5.0.11

Page 11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

3.2

Start and Log on

The following table details the software requirements for running AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

or AVEVA Change Manager.

Client Software Requirements

Component

Operating systems

Requirement
· Microsoft® Windows® 8.1
· Microsoft® Windows® 10

Web browsers

· Microsoft® Internet Explorer® 9, 10, 11
· Microsoft® Edge®
· Google Chrome™ browser

To start and log on to AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Use a web browser to enter the appropriate URL for AVEVA Change Manager.

2. On the log on page enter your User Name and Password. If your user

credentials are already saved on the log on page, click Login.

Note: If you do not have permission to access AVEVA Change Manager, you

can request permission from the system Administrator by clicking Request

Access.

3.

To change the language and user locale settings, expand Options and then

select a value from the following drop-down lists:

· Language
· Format date/time & currency

4.

To save your user credentials in the web browser so that you don't have to re-

enter them every time you log on, select the Remember me option.

5.

Click Login.

Note: This language and user locale settings change the language of the pages in AVEVA

Change Manager and user locale for a single session. If you want to keep the same

settings across sessions, you need to edit the Manage My Profile page after logging on.

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.3

Home Page

The AVEVA Change Manager Home Page is the initial or first page the user will see after

log on. If no Class Library upgrades are in progress the first page all users, including

Administrators, are redirected to is the Home page. This page is used to facilitate

navigation to other pages on AVEVA Change Manager and is divided into four area, each

containing different menu that list the functions, tasks and links available to users. The

AVEVA Change Manager Home Page and the functions available are configurable and

depend on a user's Role. If you are unable to access any of the features described in this

guide, contact your system Administrator for configuring it and granting access rights.

3.3.1

Basic Page Components

When using AVEVA Change Manager, you might come across and therefore should

become familiar with the following page components:

Components

Description

Title Bar

The title bar on the top of every pages indicates the name of the
page or the particular step the user has to perform. It also displays
the AVEVA logo, and the link to the Home Page, My Profile, and
Logout link.

Information Icon

This displays a summary of a particular menu or functionality when
you move the pointer over a specific information icon.

Reset button

To discard the changes and stay on the current page, click Reset. If
you click Reset after clicking Save or Close, you cannot discard the
changes on a page.

Save button

To save the changes and stay on the current page, click Save.

Close button

Cancel button

Back button

If you have applied all the changes on the particular page and want
to return to the Home Page or previous page, and also save all the
changes at the same time, click Close. To return to the previous
page if you do not want to make any changes on a specific page,
click Close.

If you do not want to perform a particular function, task, step,
process on a page and return to the Home Page or previous page,
click Cancel.

To return to the previous page or step, click Back. The Back button
is available on the pages where you need to make a selection or
the pages are part of multi-step wizard.

Confirm button

To confirm/accept/verify a particular action on the final page/step
of the wizard, click Confirm.

Next button

To continue to the next page/step of the wizard when your
confirmation is required, click Next.

If you want to search for a particular item or information in the

Steps bar

If you want to check which step you are at and how many steps are
left for you to complete in the wizard, check the Steps bar.

on Bar

The message or notification bar under the Title bar displays helpful
hints, messages or notifications. It displays error message when
you encounter an error while performing a step or process.

3.4

Edit User Profile

Your User Profile contains basic information about you and can be updated after you log

in to AVEVA Change Manager.

To update your profile information:

1.

2.

Click My Profile in the top right corner of the Home Page title bar.

In the Manage My Profile page, you can edit the following:

· Display Name: This is required field.
· E-mail: This is a required field.
· Phone Number
· User Interface Language
· Format Date/Time & Currency
· Time Zone

Note: You cannot edit the User Name field.

3.

Click Save, after you have made changes to your profile.

You can select a value from the User Interface Language, Format Date/Time & Currency

and Time Zone list to keep the language and user locale settings across sessions.

Localisation consists primarily of translating the pages in AVEVA Change Manager, error

messages and application prompts as well as formatting the date, time and currency.

3.5

User Roles

To manage individual users access to functions, features and tasks in the application, the

user privileges are organised by user Roles. A Role is a set of permissions that are granted

to a user thus controlling access to application modules and workflow processes. Each

Role also determines the authorisation granted to a user to perform particular functions

and operations. All users that have a specific Role assigned inherit the privileges of that

Role. Since any user may have more than one Role, the user inherits the set of the

privileges of those Roles.

For example, a Contractor can send Working Sets for Verification, but only the

Information Manager is authorised to verify Working Sets. Also, Administrators,

Contractors and Super Users cannot make changes to Working Sets that are sent for

verification, but the Information Manager can add, modify and remove the objects

contained in Working Sets that are yet to be verified.

Release 5.0.11

The following table provides a summary of Roles in AVEVA Change Manager:

Role Name

Administrator

Super User

Responsibility

Members of this Role are the only users who have access to
Administration page. Members of this Role can manage users,
Role, Reasons (for Void and Cancel Verification) as well as
update the Class Library version.

Members of this Role have access to Manage Job pages to
review the status and the progress for all the Bulk Upload Jobs,
and they are authorised to update Tags and Documents by
means of Fast Track functionality.

Information Manager Members of this Role are the only users that are allowed to do
the verification process. This Role have access to the following
process: Complete Verification and Cancel Verification.
Members of this Role can approve and reject approval of
Modification Projects in AVEVA Change Manager.

Contractor

Members of this Role can manage all Working Sets and
Modification Projects. Members of this Role can upload Tags
and Documents using Bulk Upload functionality.
Important: The Select Modification Project step is mandatory,
while creating a Working Set by Contractors.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

4

Manage Modification Projects

AVEVA Change Manager supports brownfield change management processes by

supporting Modification Projects (minor and major) and Steady State Operations. The

Modification Projects and the Working Sets are the main units to manage the changes to

the validated data. The tasks performed in AVEVA Change Manager focuses on the

maintenance of permanent plant facilities.

Note: A Brownfield Modification is defined as a Modification Project that involves

changing or adding of data that is owned by the Owner Operator. Throughout the

Brownfield Modification the Owner Operator can at any point change the data.

A Modification Project can be defined as a unit for tracking and capturing a collection of

changes that are being made to Tags and Documents. Each Modification Project may

refer to several Management of Change (MOC) projects or Change Requests. As a best

practice, after a Modification Project is created a Working Set should be assigned to it. By

creating an open Modification Project and assigning relevant Working Sets to the project,

a complete history of changes would be available and organised within one project.

4.1

Create a Modification Project

You, assigned the Role of Contractor, can create a Modification Project to describe and

capture the scope and the schedule for the modifications that need to be applied to the

objects of the appropriate Asset. After the scope is defined and described, the

Modification Project is sent for approval to the Information Manager representing the

Owner Operator.

To create a Modification Project:

1. Go to the Home Page, on the Manage Mod. Projects menu, click Create.

2. On the Available Assets list, in the Asset column, select an Asset code that the

Modification Project being created is related.

3. On the confirmation page, do the following:

· In the Origination date box, specify the project initiation date. You can
also use the calendar icon to make a quick date selection. This is a

required field.

Note: The date format applicable is dependent on the settings in the

User's profile.

Release 5.0.11

Page 19

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

· By default, the code of the Modification Project is auto-generated. If
you want to provide custom code for your Modification Project you

need to clear the Autogenerate option to enable the Project Code box.

The user-defined Project Code has to be unique within the Asset.

Note: As a best practice keep the Autogenerate option checked for

automatically generating the Project Code.

· In the Project Title box, type the title of the Modification Project. This is

a required field.

4.

5.

Click Confirm to confirm the creation of the Modification Project.

You will be redirected to the Modification Project Details page in case of

successful creation and new project creation message (similar to the following

message) will be displayed under the title bar:

Example: A new Modification Project was created. You may now continue to

work on this Modification Project here.

4.2

Specify Additional Details about the Modification Project

You can specify more details related to the Modification Project or update the previously

provided information on the Modification Project Details page.

To specify additional details about the Modification Project:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, you can update the following

details:

· Title. This is a required field.
· Origination date. This is a required field.
· Planned finish date. The Planned finish date should be later than the

origination date.

· Justification
· Description

2.

Click Save, to save the changes you have made to the Modification Project.

You cannot update a Modification Project after the Modification Project is approved.

Once it is approved no changes are allowed for Modification Project.

Modification Project History Log

To view the history log:

1.

2.

Click History tab.

In the Log page, inspect the change history for the Modification Project.

Release 5.0.11

Page 20

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

4.3

Send Modification Project for Approval

A Modification Project with a New status can be sent for approval to the Information

Manager. The status of the Modification Project changes from New to For Approval after

it is sent for approval.

To send the Modification Project for approval:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, on the Status bar, check the

Modification Project status which would be marked as New.

2.

Click For Approval.

3. On the List of available users, in the Display Name column, select a user as an

approver of your Modification Project.

4. On the confirmation page, you can enter a message in the Message to

approver box, for the approver which will be added to the auto-generated e-

mail delivered to the e-mail address associated with the approver user profile.

5.

To confirm sending the Modification Project for approval, click Confirm.

4.4

Approve the Modification Project

You can approve the Modification Project, sent to you for approval, so that it gets

activated and available for being referenced when creating the Working Set for the

necessary modifications. You should be assigned to Role of Information Manager to

approve the Modification Project. The status of the Modification Project changes from

For Approval to Active after it is approved.

To find and open the Modification Project waiting for approval:

· Using the Ongoing Tasks menu:

1. On the Home page, expand the Modification Projects For Approval list.

2. Find and click the link for the Modification Project that you are going to

approve.

Release 5.0.11

Page 21

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

· Using the Manage Modification Projects menu:

1. Go to the Home Page and then click Manage Mod. Projects link.

2. You will be redirected to the page listing Modification Projects based on

their current status under the following tabs representing the specific

status of the Modification Project:
· New Modification Projects
· Modification Projects for Approval
· Active Modification Projects

3. Click the Modification Projects for Approval tab to access the list of

Modification Projects with For Approval status. If you cannot find a specific

Modification Project, use the Search Box.

4.

In the Number column, click the Modification Project that you are going to

approve.

To approve the Modification Project:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, on the Status bar check the

Modification Project status which would be marked as For Approval.

2.

Click Approve.

3. On the confirmation page, read through the information displayed on the

Modification Project Activation message box.

4.

To confirm Modification Project approval, click Confirm.

4.5

Cancel Approval of Modification Project

The approval of a Modification Project can be rejected by the approver of that

Modification Project. You should be assigned to Role of Information Manager to cancel

the approval of the Modification Project. The status of the Modification Project changes

from For Approval to New after the approval is rejected.

To reject the approval of the Modification Project:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, on the Status bar check the

Modification Project status which would be marked as For Approval.

2.

Click Cancel Approval to reject the approval.

3. On the Status bar, check the Modification Project status which would change

to New.

Release 5.0.11

Page 22

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

4.6

Transfer Modification Project

You can transfer a Modification Project to assign the Modification Project to another user

or Information Manager, so that person would be responsible for providing more details

to it or for approving it.

To transfer the Modification Project:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, on the Other menu, click Transfer.

2. On the List of available users, in the Display Name column, select a user to

whom you want to transfer the responsibility.

3. On the confirmation page, you can enter a message in the Message to new

owner box. This is added to the auto-generated e-mail which is delivered to

the e-mail address associated with the user's profile.

4.

To assign the Modification Project to another user, click Confirm.

4.7

Void Modification Project

The New, For Approval and Active status of a Modification Project can be changed to

Void, if required. A Modification Project with a Void status cannot be referenced when

creating a new Working Set in the Working Set Details page.

To void the Modification Project:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, on the Status bar, click Void.

2. On the confirmation page, read through the information displayed on the Void

Modification Project message box.

3. On the Reason for voiding list, select the reason for voiding the Modification

Project. This is a required field.

4. On the confirmation page, you should enter a message in the Message to Log

box, for data tracking purposes. However, this field is not mandatory and will

let you progress without entering a message.

5.

To void the Modification Project, click Confirm.

After you void a Modification Project it is moved to historical Modification Project

automatically. To access the historical Modification Project list:

1. Go to the Home Page and on the Manage Mod. Projects menu, click View

Historical.

2.

In the Historical Modification Projects list, in the Code column, click a code to

view the Modification Project Details page.

Release 5.0.11

Page 23

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

4.8

Complete Modification Project

The Modification Project with Active status can be marked as complete. The status of the

Modification Project changes from Active to Complete.

To mark the Modification Project as complete:

1. On the Modification Project Details page, Status bar check the Modification

Project status which would be marked as Active.

2.

Click Complete.

3. On the confirmation page, read through the information displayed on the

Modification Project Completion message box.

4.

To mark the Modification Project as complete, click Confirm.

After you complete a Modification Project it is moved to historical Modification Project

automatically and can be accessed on historical Modification Project list.

Release 5.0.11

Page 24

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Manage Modification Projects

4.9

Managing Sub-Project

When you have set Modification Projects and Work Package settings in

OwnerDomain\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml to true, and have created a Modification

Project, you can see the Sub-Projects tab enabled.

Creating and Editing Sub-Project

To create or edit a Sub-Project using the Information Manager Role:

1.

Create or edit a Modification Project.

2. Optionally, use the Sub-Project Prefix to define a custom prefix. The manual

input is restricted to 10 characters.

3. Use the Planned finish date field to select the date you plan to complete the

Modification Project.

4. Optionally, select the Mandatory Sub-Project check box.

Note: You cannot approve a Modification Project that has this check box

selected and does not contain a Sub-Project.

5.

Choose between Sub-Project as Free Text and Choose Sub-Project from List

options to govern how the inputs appear in the Working Set.

6.

Switch to the Sub-Projects tab and click Create.

Note: The Sub-Project Code field is auto-generated. However, you may input a

value manually. The manual input is restricted to 16 characters.

7. Optionally, in the New Sub-Project Details window, enter the values for the

Sub-Project Prefix, Sub-Project Title (the input limit is set to 64 characters)

and Description fields.

8.

Click Confirm to save the changes and go back to the Manage Modification

Project Details window.

9.

Click Save.

After the modification is approved, the status for all the Sub-Projects linked to it changes

to Active. For only the Information Manager Role, the Create button is enabled to create

Sub-Projects when the Modification Project is in the Active status. A Contractor can

neither create nor delete any Sub-Project. When the Modification Project is in the New or

For Approval status, even a user with a Contractor role can create a Sub-Project.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5

Manage Working Sets

Working Set is one of the fundamental components in AVEVA Change Manager and is

managed throughout the change management lifecycle. In AVEVA Change Manager, a

Working Set is manually created and is used to represent the scope of work for data

change. Depending on your configuration, it will have to be associated with a discipline,

or this association can be optional. One or more Working Sets can be associated with a

Modification Project.

The following are the key tasks you can perform within a Working Set:

· Reserve new Tags and Documents.
· Modify new and existing Tags and Documents, including adding or removing Tag-

to-Document associations.

· Upload multiple Tags, Documents and Tag-to-Document associations.

5.1

Create a New Working Set

To create a Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Manage Working Sets menu, click Create.

2. On the Available Assets list, in the Asset column, select the Asset Code that

the Working Set being created is associated.

3.

Based on configuration settings, do the following:

· On the Available Disciplines list, in the Discipline column, click the
required Document Discipline code, see Select Document Discipline

Options.

· On the Available Modification Projects list, in the Code column, click

the Modification Project code.

· On the Available Sub-Projects (or Available Work Package if AVEVA

WorkMate is enabled) list, in the Code column, click the Sub-Project (or

Work Package code). For more information, see AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide.

4. On the confirmation page, in the Title box, type the Working Set title. This is a

required field.

Note: All other required fields are auto-populated based on the user selection

following the steps of the Working Set creation wizard. You can change the

previously selected values by navigating to the corresponding step of the

wizard by clicking the Back button.

Release 5.0.11

Page 26

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.

After you have entered/changed the value for all the required field, click

Confirm, to confirm creation of the Working Set.

6.

You will be redirected to the Working Set Details page in case of successful

creation. Working Set Number will be auto-generated. A new Working Set

creation message (similar to the following message) will be displayed under

the title bar: "A new Working Set was created. You may now continue to work

on this Working Set here."

Select Document Discipline Options

Selecting a particular Document Discipline code for the Working Set, will serve as a filter

to the type of Documents that can be managed within the Working Set, for example, only

Documents of the selected Document Discipline can be added or created as part of the

Working Set. This option is configurable to support different business cases.

Based on the configuration, the select Document Discipline step can be:

· Required – the users are forced to select the Document Discipline while creating

the Working Sets, there is no option to skip this step. No Next button is

available.

· Optional – the users have option either to select the Document Discipline or skip

this step by clicking Next button in the Working Set creation wizard.

· Disabled – the step of selecting the Document Discipline is not available in the

Working Set creation wizard.

Release 5.0.11

Page 27

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.1.1

Working Set Details Page

You can update the previously provided information or specify more details related to the

Working Set on the Working Set Details page. You can enter comment or any information

in the Comments box.

Document Discipline Options

The Document Discipline code removal and reselection of another value on the Working

Set Details page is configurable to support different business cases.

To remove Document Discipline Code or reselect another Code:

1. On the Working Set Details page, based on the availability of look-up and

remove button do the following:

· If you have selected a Document Discipline Code when creating the

Working Set, click the remove button for the Discipline box to remove

the Document Discipline Code.

· If you have not selected a Document Discipline Code when creating the
Working Set, click the look-up button for the Discipline box to select

either a new Document Discipline Code or reselect another Document

Discipline Code. On the Available Disciplines list, in the Discipline

column, click the required Document Discipline Code.

2.

Click Save to update the Working Set details.

Working Set History Log

To view the history log:

1.

2.

Click History tab.

In the Log page, inspect the change history for the Working Set.

Naming and Numbering Limitation

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard, AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager accept up to 450 characters in the Tag# and Document# name fields, whereas

AVEVA WorkMate accepts only 64 characters (this is by design). When you enable the

integration with AVEVA WorkMate, and have a greater number of characters in the

respective name fields, your name records appear truncated. To avoid data loss, it is

recommended that you limit the number of characters in the naming and numbering

rules.

Release 5.0.11

Page 28

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

Limitation with Unicode characters and AVEVA WorkMate integration

AVEVA WorkMate does not support Unicode Characters, such as the Cyrillic alphabet. So,

while a user can author items with these additional characters, this functionality should

not be used where AVEVA WorkMate integration is implemented.

5.2

Reserve a New Tag Number

You should reserve a new Tag Number when new items are added to the Plant, for

example, as part of the Brownfield Modification Projects and steady-state operations, if

the plant requires the installation of an additional pump that was not part of the original

plant design. If you do not need to reserve a new tag, you must update the information

for the existing tag, for example, when the class attributes have been updated, and you

need to provide the value for the newly introduced attribute for existing tag.

You can reserve a new Tag Number in the Working Set by selecting the Class that the Tag

belongs to, and specifying the values for some additional attributes required for the Tag.

The Tag Number can be provided as a free-text value or can be auto-generated, if there

are Naming Templates associated with the Class. When the Tag Number is being auto-

generated according to the Naming Template, you have an option to reserve several Tag

Numbers at once. Those Tag Numbers will have different auto-generated sequence

numbers.

Note: Even though you can manually reserve a sequence number for a new Tag, the

number must follow the last generated number.

To initiate reserving a new Tag Number:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Tags tab, to open the list of Tags

managed within the Working Set.

Note: The number in brackets displayed on the tab following Tags indicates

the number of the Tags already being managed in the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, click Reserve new Tag(s) button to launch the

Reserve Tag wizard. Alternatively, to initiate reserving a sequence number

available between existing records, see Reserving Numbers Between Existing

Records.

Follow the steps required for reserving a new Tag

The Reserve Tag wizard is dynamic to support reserving Tag Numbers in accordance with

some available name and numbering templates and reserving free-text Tag Numbers. The

steps that are common for both scenarios are:

Release 5.0.11

Page 29

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

· Choose Class
· Choose System or Parent Tag

Depending on whether a Functional Hierarchic Taxonomy linked to the Asset to which the

current Working Set is linked is defined in the Class Library, the following steps may or

may not be available:

· Choose Position
· Choose System or Parent Tag

Depending on the selection, the following steps may be available:

· Choose Area
· Choose Template
· Enter Attributes
· Confirm

The list of the following steps required to create a new Tag Number changes based on the

values selected in the previous step:

1. Under Functional Classes Taxonomy, browse through the Functional

Taxonomy to find the class for the new Tag by expanding the node in the tree

view.

2.

In the Class column, click the Class Name to select the Class for your Tag.

Note: Functional Taxonomies are defined in the Class Library Definitions and

they can be set up and configured for a specific Asset using AVEVA Information

Standards Manager™.

3. Under Breakdown Structure, browse through the Functional Hierarchic

Taxonomy to choose a position of the new Tag in the Asset breakdown

structure.

Note: Functional Hierarchic Taxonomies are defined in the Class Library

Definitions and they can be set up and configured for a specific Asset using

AVEVA Information Standards Manager™. If such a Taxonomy is not defined,

the page is not shown.

4. Under Available Areas, in the Area column, click the Area Code to select a

Area for the Tag.

Note: Click Next to skip this step.

5. Under Tag hierarchy, in the System column, click the System Code to select

the system to which the Tag belongs. You can also do the following:

Release 5.0.11

Page 30

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

· Expand the System node to access the list of existing Tags within that
System. You can also search for the particular Tag using the Search

option.

· Selecting the Tag Number in the tree view or in the search results will

assign it as a Parent Tag for the Tag you are creating.

Note: Tag hierarchy is not available if the System Code has been already set on

choosing a position of the new Tag in the Asset breakdown structure.

6. Do the following:

· Choose Template: Under Available Templates, in the Template column,
select the naming template assigned on the selected functional class.

· Enter Attributes:

·  The fields availability on Enter Attributes page depends on the
template selected during the Choose Template step – different

naming templates can have different elements based on their

configuration in the Class Library.

· Elements which represent sequence numbers will have

Automatically generate option available to enable you to provide

specific sequences rather than auto-generated values.

· Quantity of tags is the additional field available, which is not a

part of any naming template. You can specify the number of tag

numbers that you want to allocate. This field is available when

the sequences are supposed to be auto-generated (you can

allocate only one tag for a specific sequence number therefore

this field is disabled if the Automatically generate option is not

selected). There is a limit for the quantity of Tags that can be

generated. This is configurable option. By default, the quantity

limit is set to 10.

7. On the confirmation page, enter the Tag Number, which is a mandatory field,

and Tag Description, which is an optional field.

Note: This page will be available, only if the Tag Number is reserved as a free-

text value.

8.

Click Confirm, to confirm the creation of a New Tag. If a Summary message

box is displayed with the information about the Tags reserved, click Finish to

leave the wizard.

Release 5.0.11

Page 31

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

9.

You will be redirected to the Working Set Tags page in case of successful

creation. The newly reserved Tag will be displayed on the Tags list. A new Tag

creation message (similar to the following message) will be displayed under

the title bar: "A new Tag was reserved."

Notes:

· On the Enter Attributes page, do not use the Parent Tag Number attribute as a
free-text or look-up naming element field. Always use the Tag hierarchy page to

choose a System or Parent Tag. To change the Parent Tag Number selected

previously, you must navigate back to the Tag hierarchy page.

· The Tag Numbers should be unique within the Asset.
· If you want to search for existing main Tags of the selected Category and System,

use the available search box.

Tags list page Column description

Column and
Action

Description

Tag#

Displays the Number of the Tag.

Tag Description

Provides a brief description about the Tag.

Fill %

State

It gives an indication of the percentage of the populated attributes
for the Tag versus the total number of the attributes that can be filled
in for the Tags of that particular Class.

The State column reflects the state of the Tag within the Working Set.
The following states are displayed:
· New: Indicates that this Tag is newly reserved in the context of this
specific Working Set and further verification will have to happen to
publish it to the engineering information store.

· Failed: Not added to the engineering information store. A failed

state usually means that there are errors with the classification of a
Tag, or issues with the classification of one or many of the
attributes for the Tag.

· Checked In: Indicates that the Tag has passed validation without
any critical error or failure and has been published successfully to
the engineering information store.

· Checked Out: Indicates that the Tag has been successfully checked
out and you can start modifying its data. Further verification will
have to happen to publish the changes to the engineering
information store afterwards.

· Not Checked Out: Indicates that no updates can be applied to the

Tag. A Tag has to be checked out before you can modify it.

Release 5.0.11

Page 32

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

Column and
Action

Is Used

Installed

Description

In this column you can select the Is Used option for each Tag before
running the verification process of a Working Set. This option can be
selected when the state of a Tag is marked as New. You have to
select this option to indicate that the reserved Tag Number needs to
be published into the engineering information store. To mark all new
Tags as Is Used, select the header checkbox, which is available only if
at least one new editable Tag exists in the list.

Indicates that the Tag for which you have reserve a Tag Number has
already been installed on the Asset.
Note: This is not always available, as this option is dependant upon
the configuration. To perform this action, users must select the check
box.

Decommissioned Indicates that the existing Tag has already been decommissioned

Action

Error

Remove

from the Asset.
Note: This is not always available, as this option is dependant upon
the configuration. To perform this action, users must select the check
box.

The value in this column indicates the status of the Tag that it will
have when the updates are published into the engineering
information store.

The Error column identifies if a Tag has associated errors or not. If any
validation errors have been reported for the Tag it will be flagged
correspondingly. The errors could be the validation errors or the
errors related to check out/check in of the Tag. All errors are to be
fixed before the Tag can be published into the engineering
information store.

If you want to remove Tags from the Working Sets, click the Remove
link. In case the Tag being removed is a Tag Number newly reserved
in this Working Set, the Tag will be deleted from the engineering
information store as well. If any modifications have been applied to
the Tag and they haven’t been published to the engineering
information store the data will be discarded when the Tag is removed
from the Working Set.

5.3

Reserve Numbers Between Existing Records

You may use this functionality to search for and select the empty sequence numbers. To

manually select the sequencing:

1. Use the search panel on the top-right corner of the Tag name or Document

name template details page to find allocated sequence numbers.

2.

Select the Automatically generate check box.

3. Use the Start From and the quantity-related fields for Tags or Documents to

request the available sequencing.

If the application encounters an existing record, it skips the record and uses the next

available sequence number. For example, if the numbers 43, 44, 47 and 48 are available,

and you provide 43 as a start number and 4 as the quantity for the Tags or Documents,

the application skips 45 and 46, which are already taken, and uses the available ones.

Notes:

· When you manually select the sequencing, and then automate the generation
of Tags or Documents, the application may encounter a number you manually

selected and consequently report an error.

· To mitigate any performance concerns, the number of the returned records is

limited by the value of the property maxRows located in

NamingTemplate.xml. Refer to <Item name="NamingTemplate.Search"

.../>.

Release 5.0.11

Page 34

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.4

Add Existing Tags to the Working Set

You can add an existing "Active" Tag that needs to be modified, to a Working Set.

To add an existing Tags to the Working Set:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and then click the Tags tab.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, click Pick Tag(s) from the Tag Hierarchy.

3. On the Available tags organised by system list, click the System node to

access the list of existing Tags within that System.

4.

Select the required Tag Number. You can also select multiple Tags to be added

to the Working Set.

5.

6.

Click Confirm to confirm adding the Tags into the Working Set.

You will be redirected to the Working Set Tags page. The added Tags will be

displayed in the list with the state Not Checked Out.

The Tags listed on the Available tags organised by system list are grouped in a tree view.

The structure reflects the breakdown structure of the parent tag associations, starting

with the System and the main tags on the top.

5.5

Populate the Working Set with Tags

New and existing Tags can be added to the Working Set by uploading Excel (.xlsx) or tab-

delimited text (.txt) files that contain the information about the Tags that are being

added. Instead of adding and creating Tags manually one by one, you can use this option

of uploading whole list of Tags.

A Microsoft® Excel® spreadsheet template available for downloading can be used to

define the structure of the files that are uploaded for adding Tags. Once the template is

filled in, it must be saved as an Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file to proceed

with the upload. This option is available while you are in the progress with setting up the

Working Set, that is, when the Working Set is in the New status. After the Working set is

activated, the option for populating the Working Set in bulks is not available.

Release 5.0.11

Page 35

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To upload Tags into the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar, check the Working Set

status which should be marked as New.

2. On the Other menu click Upload Tags.

3. On the upload page, read the instruction for adding Tags by uploading Excel

(.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file.

4.

Click Excel Spreadsheet, to download the template available in the Excel

spreadsheet format.

After the template is downloaded using standard File Download dialog you can start

adding data into it. You can refer the Help worksheet of Tag.Template.xlt file

downloaded using the Excel spreadsheet link for more instructions on data preparation.

The columns in the Excel spreadsheet are predefined and you cannot configure additional

columns or delete any column.

The predefined columns are:

· Action (required) – to specify the type of update to the object:

·  0 (zero) = New – the Tag is reserved based on the values provided in the

data file.

·  1 = Modify – the Tag is added to the Working Set for further updates.
·  7 = On Hold – for existing Tags only. The Tag is added to the Working Set

and assigned On Hold status.

·  8 = Removed – for existing Tags only. The Tag is added to the Working Set

and assigned Removed status.

·  9 = Void – for existing Tags only. The Tag is added to the Working Set and

assigned Void status.

· TagName (required) – the Tag Number/Name of the Tag to upload.
· TagDescription (optional) – the Tag Description of the Tag that you are

uploading.

· ParentTagName (optional) – The Tag Number/Name of the parent tag.
· CategoryCode (optional/required – see note) – Category of the Tag in case of

NORSOK classification.

· FunctionCode (optional/required – see note) – Function Code of the Tag in case

of NORSOK classification.

· AreaCode (optional) – the Area Code that the uploaded Tag belongs to.

Release 5.0.11

Page 36

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

· SystemCode (required if Action is 0) – the System Code that the uploaded Tag

belongs to.

· ClassID (optional/required – see note) – the Class Id of the Functional Class that

the uploaded Tag belongs to.

Note: For new Tags, that is, those where the Action value is set to 0 (zero), you must

enter one of the following :
· the ClassID
· the CategoryCode AND FunctionCode

To upload the file:

1.

After the file is modified and saved (using the appropriate format as suggested

in the available instructions) go to the same page that you downloaded the

template from, click Browse, to navigate to the source Excel (.xlsx) or tab-

delimited text (.txt) file from your local machine or an accessible network

location.

Note: If uploading a tab-delimited file with Unicode characters, you must save

the file by selecting Save As and then selecting the file type as Unicode Text

(*.txt) before uploading to AVEVA Change Manager. This does not affect files

that do not use Unicode characters.

2.

Click Next, to proceed with the upload.

3. On successful preliminary validation you will be redirected to the confirmation

page, click Confirm to proceed with the upload.

4.

You will be redirected to the Home page. The upload of objects into the

Working Set will be done in the background. The results of the upload success

or failure, will be sent to the e-mail associated to your user profile. After you

get the e-mail you can continue working with the Working Set.

To check the Tags uploaded in the Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Ongoing Task menu, expand the New Working

Sets menu and click the Working Set link that you populated with the Tags.

Note: If the user who uploaded the Tags is not the owner of the Working Set,

then that user cannot find the Working Set in Ongoing Tasks. Also, if the

Working Set has been already processed further by some other user, it will not

have the New status. For more information about how to find the required

Working Set by its number from the Manage Working Set list, see Check

Working Set Status.

2. On the Working Sets Details page, click the Tags tab and then review the Tag

list, to check the correct number of Tags uploaded.

Release 5.0.11

Page 37

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.6

Update the Tag Details Manually

If the state of a Tag is shown as Not Checked Out or Checked In, the Tag details cannot be

edited. Based on your company or plant specific configuration, some of the fields that you

can update may be disabled or hidden on the Tag Details page.

Note: For more information about modifying the Tag details, see Activate the Working

Set.

To update the Tag details manually:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Tags tab, to open the list of Tags

managed within the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, click the Tag Number for the Tag to open its

Details page.

3. On the Tag Details page, as per your requirements, you can add new value,

remove, or reselect another value for the fields. Some of the fields may be

read only and cannot be edited.

4. On the Attributes table, as per your requirements, you can also change or

enter new value. Some of the fields may be read only and cannot be edited.

5.

Click Close, to confirm saving the changes and leave the Tag Details page.

5.7

Add Document Reference for Tag

To add Document Reference for the Tag:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Tags tab, to open the list of Tags

managed within the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, click the Tag Number and then click Document

References tab to open the list of Document referenced to the Tag.

3. On the Documents References list page, click Add Document Reference(s).

4. Under the Available Documents list, click the following tabs to refer to single

or multiple Documents for the Tag:

· Documents
· Documents in this Working Set

Release 5.0.11

Page 38

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

· Documents within Mod. Projects

Note: Only the new documents in scope of the same Modification

Project will be available for selecting.

· Selected Items

Note: The Selected Items tab lists all the Documents that have been

selected by you on the Documents, Documents in this Working Set and

Pick from other Working Sets tabs. This tab is blank unless you select a

Document on the other three tabs.

5.

Click Confirm, to add Documents Reference for the Tag.

Notes:

· If no Documents are listed on the Available Documents list Page, you can enter

the '%%%%' as the query text in the search box to display all the available

Documents.

· The Documents with an Active and Checked Out status are listed in the Available
Documents list and are linked to the particular Asset selected for the Working

Set.

Remove Document References

To remove Document References for the Tag:

1.

To open the list of Documents referenced to the Tag, click the Document

References tab.

2. On the Documents References list page, click the Remove link for the

Documents that you want to remove.

3.

To update the Document References page, click Save.

5.8

Upload Document Reference to Tag

References to Documents can be added to one or multiple Tags by uploading Excel (.xlsx)

or tab-delimited text (.txt) files that contain the information about the Documents that

are to be added as References. A Microsoft® Excel spreadsheet template available for

downloading can be used to define structure of the files that are uploaded for adding

Document References to Tags. Once the template is filled in it must be saved as an Excel

(.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file to proceed with the upload.

To upload References using Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file into a Tag:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Other menu click Upload Tag Refs.

2. On the upload page, read the instruction for adding Document References by

uploading Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file.

3.

Click Excel Spreadsheet, to download the template available in the Excel

spreadsheet format.

4.

After the file is modified and saved (using the appropriate format as suggested

in the available instructions), go to the same page that you downloaded the

template from, click Browse, to navigate to the source Excel (.xlsx) or tab-

location.

5.

Click Next, to proceed with the upload.

6. On successful preliminary validation you will be redirected to the confirmation

To open the Working Set and check the References uploaded into the Tag:

1. On the Home Page, on the Ongoing Task menu, expand the New Working

 Check Working Set Status.

Number.

3.

To check the details of the uploaded References, click the Document

References tab.

5.9

Reserve a New Document Number

To initiate reserving a new Document Number:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Documents tab, to open the list of

Documents managed within the Working Set.

Note: The number in brackets displayed on the tab following Documents

indicates the number of the Documents already being managed in the

Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Documents page, click Reserve new Document(s) button

to launch the Reserve Document wizard. Alternatively, to initiate reserving a

sequence number available between existing records, see Reserving Numbers

Between Existing Records.

Note: Even though you can manually reserve a sequence number for a new Document,

the number must follow the last generated number.

Follow the steps required for reserving a new Document

The Reserve Document wizard is dynamic to support the scenario of managing

Documents with different Document Disciplines within a Working Set. The steps that are

common for both scenarios are:

· Choose Class
· Confirm

Depending on the selection, the following steps may be available:

· Choose Document Discipline
· Choose Template
· Enter Attributes

Note: If the Working Set is connected to a Document Discipline, then the Documents

reserved within the Working Set will have this Discipline assigned by default.

Release 5.0.11
