You must perform the following steps:

1. Under Document Classes Taxonomy, browse through the Classes Taxonomy

to find the class that the Documents will belong to, by expanding the node in

the tree view. In the Class column, click the Class Name to select the Class for

your Document.

Note: Document Taxonomies are defined in the Class Library Definitions and

they can be set up and configured for a specific Asset using AVEVA Information

Standards Manager™.

2. Do the following:

· Choose Document Discipline: Under Document Disciplines, in the
Discipline column, click the required Document Discipline Code.

· Choose Template: Under Available Templates, in the Template column,
select the naming template assigned on the selected document class.

· Enter Attributes:

· The fields availability on Enter Attributes page depends on the
template selected during the Choose Template step – different

naming templates can have different elements based on their

configuration in the Class Library.

· Elements which represent sequence numbers will have

Automatically generate option available to enable you to provide

specific sequences rather than auto-generated values.

· Quantity of Documents is the additional field available, which is

not a part of any naming template. You can specify the number of

Document numbers that you want to allocate. This field is

available when the sequences are supposed to be auto-generated

(you can allocate only one tag for a specific sequence number

therefore this field is disabled if the Automatically generate

option is not selected). There is a limit for the quantity of

Documents that can be generated. This is configurable option. By

default, the quantity limit is set to 10.

3. On the confirmation page, enter a value for the following fields:

· Document#
· Description (optional)
· New Revision# (this field is required if the use of Document

Management System is enabled.)

Note: All other required fields are auto-populated based on the user

4.

5.

Documents List Page Column Description

Column and
Action

Description

Document#

Displays the number of the Document.

Description

Provides a brief description about the Document.

Doc. Type

Gives an indication about the type of the associated Document.

Revision#

Indicates the revision number of the Document.

State

The State column reflects the state of the Document within the Working
Set. The following states are displayed:
· New: Indicates that this Document is newly reserved in the context of
this specific Working Set and further verification will have to happen
to publish it to the engineering information store.

· Failed: Not added to the engineering information store. A failed state

usually means that there are errors with the classification of a
Document, or issues with the classification of one or many of the
attributes for the Document.

· Checked In: Indicates that the Document has passed validation

without any critical error or failure and has been published successfully
to the engineering information store.

· Checked Out: Indicates that the Document has been successfully

checked out and you can start modifying its data. Further verification
will have to happen to publish the changes to the engineering
information store afterwards.

· Not Checked Out: Indicates that no updates can be applied to the

Document. A Document has to be checked out before you can modify
it.

Release 5.0.11

Page 43

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

Column and
Action

Is Used

Description

In this column you can select the Is Used option for each Document
before running verification process of a Working Set. This option can be
selected when the state of a Document is marked as New. You have to
select this option to indicate that the reserved Document Number needs
to be published into the engineering information store. To mark all new
Documents as Is Used, select the header checkbox, which is available
only if at least one new editable Document exists in the list.
Is Used is automatically selected as soon as the physical files are
uploaded into the Document, if the application is configured to maintain
them.

Verified

Indicates if a Document has been verified by the Information Manager.

Error

Remove

Identifies if a Document has associated errors. Any flagged errors could
be validation errors or errors related to check out/check in of the
Document. All errors must be fixed before the Document can be
published into the engineering information store.

If you want to remove a Documents from the Working Sets, click the
Remove link. In case the Document being removed is a Document
Number newly reserved in this Working Set the Document will be
deleted from the engineering information store as well. If any
modifications have been applied to the Document and they haven’t been
published to the engineering information store, the data will be
discarded when the Document is removed from the Working Set.

5.10

Add Existing Document to the Working Set

You can add an existing Document with an Active status, which needs to be modified, into

the Working Set.

To add an existing Documents for the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Documents tab, to open the list of

Documents managed within the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Documents page, click Add Document(s) button.

3.

The following two options are provided to select the Document Number for

adding into the Working Set. You can also select multiple Documents to be

added to the Working Set:

· From the list of all available Documents, under the Documents tab.
· From the list of all available Documents that have reference to the Tag

added to the current Working Set, under the Doc. via Tag tab.

Release 5.0.11

Page 44

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

Note: Available Documents indicates the list of only the Documents that

have the same Document Discipline as the Working Set (if the Working

Set was created for a specific Document Discipline).

4.

Click the Selected Items tab to check all the Documents that you selected in

the Documents and Doc. via Tag tab.

5.

6.

Click Confirm, to confirm adding the Documents into the Working Set.

You will be redirected to the Working Set Documents page. The added

Documents will be displayed in the list with the state Not Checked Out.

5.11

Populate the Working Set with Documents

Documents can be added to the Working Set by uploading Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited

text (.txt) files that contain the information about the Documents that are being added.

Instead of adding and creating Documents manually one by one, you can use this option

of uploading whole list of Documents.

This option is available while you are in the progress with setting up the Working Set, that

is, when the Working Set is in the New status. A Microsoft® Excel spreadsheet template

available for downloading can be used to define the structure of the files that are

uploaded for adding Documents. Once the template is filled in, the file must be saved as

an Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file to proceed with the upload.

To upload Documents into the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar, check the Working Set

status which should be marked as New.

2. On the Other menu click Upload Documents.

3. On the upload page, read the instruction for adding Document(s) by uploading

Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file.

4.

Click Excel Spreadsheet, to download the template available in the Excel

spreadsheet format.

After the template is downloaded using standard File Download dialog you can start

adding data into it. Refer to the Help worksheet of Document.Template.xlt file

downloaded using the Excel spreadsheet link for more instructions on data preparation.

The columns in the Excel spreadsheet are predefined and you cannot configure additional

columns or delete any column.

The predefined columns are:

· Action (required) – to specify the type of update to the object:

Release 5.0.11

Page 45

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

·  0 (zero) = New – the Document is reserved based on the values provided in

the data file.

·  1 = Modify – the Document is added to the Working Set for further updates.
· DocumentNumber (required) – the Document Number/Name of the Document

to upload.

· DocumentDescription (optional) – the Document Description of the Document

that you are uploading.

· DisciplineCode (optional) – the Document Discipline that the Document belongs

to.

· DocumentType (optional/required – see note) – The document Type of the

uploaded Document.

· NewRevision (optional) – the Revision Number for the newly uploaded

Document.

· AreaCode (optional) – the Area Code that the uploaded Document is related to.
· SystemCode (optional) – the System Code that the uploaded Document is

related to.

· VendorDocumentReference (optional) – the vendor document reference of

the Document.

· ContractorCode (optional) – the contractor specified for the Document.
· TechnicalOrderNumber (optional) – the relative Purchase Order number.
· ClassID (optional/required – see note) – the Class ID of the Document Class that

the uploaded Document belongs to.

Note: For new Documents, that is, those where the Action value is set to 0 (zero), you

must enter one of the following:

· the ClassID
· the DocumentType

Release 5.0.11

Page 46

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To upload the file:

1.

After the file is modified and saved (using the appropriate format as suggested

in the available instructions) go to the same page that you downloaded the

template from, click Browse, to navigate to the source Excel (.xlsx) or tab-

delimited text (.txt) file from your local machine or an accessible network

location.

Note: If uploading a tab-delimited file with Unicode characters, you must save

the file by selecting Save As and then selecting the file type as Unicode Text

(*.txt) before uploading to AVEVA Change Manager. This does not affect files

that do not use Unicode characters.

2.

Click Next, to proceed with the upload.

3. On successful preliminary validation you will be redirected to the confirmation

page, click Confirm to proceed with the upload.

4.

You will be redirected to the Home page. The upload of objects into the

Working Set will be done in the background. The results of the upload success

or failure, will be sent to the e-mail associated to your user profile. After you

get the e-mail you can continue working with the Working Set.

To open the Working Set and check the Tags uploaded in the Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Ongoing Task menu, expand the New Working

Sets menu and click the Working Set link that you populated with the

Documents.

Note: If the user who uploaded the Documents is not the owner of the

Working Set, then that user cannot find the Working Set in Ongoing Tasks.

Also, if the Working Set has been already processed further by some other

user, it will not have the New status. For more information about how to find

the required Working Set by its number from the Manage Working Set list, see

 Check Working Set Status.

2. On the Working Sets Details page, click the Documents tab and then review

the Document list, to check the correct number of Documents uploaded.

5.12

Update the Document Details Manually

If the State of a Document shows as Not Checked Out or Checked In, the Document

details cannot be edited. Based on your company or plant specific configuration settings

and AVEVA Change Manager installation, some of the available fields that you can update

may be disabled/hidden/unavailable on the Document Details page. If the application is

configured to work with physical files, then the document must have a new revision

number supplied. If the Document Management System (DMS) is configured, all

attributes provided here are sent to the DMS. This also applies to Fast Tack Modification.

Note: For more information about modifying the Document details, see Activate the

Working Set.

To update the Document details manually:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Documents tab, to open the list of

Documents managed within the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Documents page, click the Document Number for the

Document to open its Details page.

3. On the Document Details page, as per your requirements, you can add new

value, remove, or reselect another value for the fields. Some of the fields may

be read only and cannot be edited.

4. On the Attributes table, as per your requirements, you can also change or

enter new value. Some of the fields may be read only and cannot be edited.

5.

To confirm saving the changes and leave the Document Details page, click

Close.

5.13

Add Tag Reference to Document

To add Tag References for the Document:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Documents tab, to open the list of

Documents managed within the Working Set.

2. On the Working Set Documents page, click the Document and then click the

Tag References tab to open the list of Tags referenced to the Document.

3. On the Tag References list page, click Add Tag Reference(s) to start adding a

reference.

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

4.

You can click the following tabs to refer to single or multiple Tags for the

Document, under Available tags organised by system list:

· Tags
· Tags in this Working Set
· Selected Items

Note: The Selected Items tab lists all the Tags that have been selected

by you on the Tags and Tags in this Working Set tabs. This tab is blank

unless you select Tags on the other two tabs.

5.

To add Tag References for the Document, click Confirm.

Notes:

· If no Tags are listed under Available tags organised by system list, you can enter
the '%%%%' as the query text in the search box to display all the available Tags.

· The Tags with Active and Checked Out status are listed in the Available tags

organised by system list and are linked to the particular Asset selected for the

Working Set.

Remove Tag References

To remove Tag References for the Document:

1.

Click the Tag References tab, to open the list of Tags referenced to the

Document.

2. On the Tags References list page, click the Remove link for the Tags that you

want to remove.

3.

To update the Tag Reference(s) page, click Save.

5.14

Upload Tag Reference to Document

References to Tags can be added to one or multiple Documents by uploading Excel (.xlsx)

or tab-delimited text (.txt) files that contain the information about the Tags that are to

be added as References. A Microsoft® Excel spreadsheet template available for

downloading can be used to define structure of the files that are uploaded for adding Tag

References to Documents. Once the template is completed, it must be saved as an Excel

(.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file to proceed with the upload.

To upload References using Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Other menu, click Upload Document

Refs.

2. On the upload page, read the instruction for adding Tag References by

uploading Excel (.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file.

3.

Click Excel Spreadsheet, to download the template available in the Excel

spreadsheet format.

4.

After the file is modified and saved (using the appropriate format as suggested

in the available instructions) go to the same page that you downloaded the

template from, click Browse, to navigate to the source Excel (.xlsx) or tab-

location.

5.

Click Next, to proceed with the upload.

6. On successful preliminary validation you will be redirected to the confirmation

Working Set will be done in the background. The results of the upload success

or failure, will be sent to the e-mail associated to your user profile. After you

get the e-mail you can continue working with the Working Set.

To check the References uploaded into the Document:

1. On the Home Page, on the Ongoing Task menu, expand the New Working

Sets menu and click the Working Set link that you populated with the Tag

Note: If the user who uploaded the References is not the owner of the

Working Set, then that user cannot find the Working Set in Ongoing Tasks.

5.15

Upload Physical Files into the Document

Physical files can be added to a Working Set Document by uploading a .zip archive file

that contains drawings you want to associate to the Document that are part of the

Working Set. The .zip archive file may contain one or more drawing files that you have

compressed. You have to indicate the master drawing files among the compressed files.

Only the .zip archive file format is allowed for uploading physical files to Working Set

Document.

When you upload multiple physical files of the same file type, AVEVA Change Manager

compares the names of the physical files against the names of the Documents contained

in the Working Set. The application then assigns the physical files to their corresponding

Documents. For any conflicting names of the physical files, you can manually select the

file that corresponds to a Document in the Conflict page of the Working Set.

Based on configuration settings during AVEVA Change Manager installation, this option

may be disabled or unavailable and you may not be able to upload physical files to a

Working Set Document. This option of uploading drawings is enabled when the Working

Set is in Active or For Verification status (when modifications to Tags and Documents are

expected to be done).

To upload physical files to a Document:

1. On the Working Set details page, on the Action menu, click Upload.

2.

3.

4.

5.

 On the Upload page, click Browse button.

In the File Explorer, navigate to the source file (.zip archive file containing the

files that you need to upload), select the file and click Open.

Note: The files in the archive should follow specific naming format.The file

name should be the Document Number of the document that you are

uploading files. The physical files to different Documents can be uploaded in

the same archive but should follow the naming format. The archive file size

can be configured, by default the maximum file size limit is 100MB.

Click Next to proceed with the upload.

To select a file as the Master, select the Master option for that file. Master file

is the file that will be retrieved when viewing the document in AVEVA NET

Dashboard.

6. Under Status for all Documents in the Working Set, check all the Documents.

7.

Click Confirm.

To view the physical files associated to the Working Set Document:

1. On the Working Set Documents page, click the Document number you have

uploaded the physical files.

Remove Physical Files

Use the PhysicalFilesDeletionEnabled value to enable/disable the physical file deletion

of Working Sets for AVEVA Change Manager. This parameter is available in

OwnerDomain\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml.

Value

true

Prerequisites Expected Behaviour

None.

The delete feature is enabled for Working Sets on the
Working Set Manage Document Details Files page. The
State column is available on the page, which shows the
following statuses of each attachment for documents
within the Working Set:
· For the non-existing or reserved documents, or for a

newly-added physical file:
· The value in the State column is set to New.
· The physical file record is completely removed from

the database after pressing Delete.
· For the existing or checked-out documents:

· The value in the State column for all the physical files
of the checked-out documents is Unmodified. When
the same document is uploaded, the value is changed
to Modified.

· The value in the State column when an existing file is
deleted is Deleted. If the same file is uploaded the
value in the State column is Modified.

· The value in the State column when a file is added is
New. If the same file is deleted, its corresponding
record is completely removed from the database.

false

None.

The delete feature is only available when the value in the
State column is set to New.

Download Physical Files before Check-Out

Use the NonCheckedOutPhysicalFileFetching parameter to enable the non-checked

out Document physical file of Working Sets. This parameter is available in

OwnerDomain\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml and affects only the Working Sets of the

New status. If it is set to true, you can click Get Files to draw physical files associated with

non-checked out Documents added to the Working Set.

5.16

Activate the Working Set

You have to activate a Working Set to start working with it and this process automatically

performs Check Out of all the Tags and Documents attached to the Working Set. The Tags

and Documents are available for editing after the Checked Out process is complete.

The Lock unless released feature is configurable. If configured, then while checking out a

Working Set or Tags and Documents, and if the Tags and Documents is associated with

non-historical Working Set, Check Out would be rejected. Error message will be displayed

detailing the reason for rejecting the Check Out.

A Working Set with a New status is activated by the owner of that Working Set. The

status of the Working Set changes from New to Active after it is activated.

Release 5.0.11

Page 53

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To activate a Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar check the Working Set

2.

3.

status which would be marked as New.

Click Active.

You will be redirected to the Home page. The activation of the Working Set

happens in the background and as the result of this process initiation, the

Working Set gets locked.

You will have to open the Working Set Details page or Working Set List pages to check the

status of the activation. Once the activation is finished the Working Set is unlocked.

5.17

Check for Non-permissible Attributes

If the Tags/Documents contain attributes that exist in AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard but not in the Permissible Class Libraries of AVEVA Change Manager, these

cannot be added to a Working Set.

To enable/disable checking for the non-permissible attributes, use the

checknonpermissibleattributes parameter. You can configure this parameter in

TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml, which is in the OwnerDomain folder of the installation

directory.

By default, the value for this parameter is set to true. In which case, AVEVA Change

Manager checks for non-permissible attributes for the Tags/Documents and, if found,

displays an error. If the value is set to false, the application does not check for the

attributes.

Note: This feature is not applicable for attributes that are required for creating

Tags/Documents, such as Area or Purchase Order#.

Release 5.0.11

Page 54

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.18

Microsoft Excel Support for Bulk Upload and Download

AVEVA Change Manager supports Excel for the Bulk Download and Upload functionalities.

You can use the application-generated Excel file to download and upload changes to

multiple Tags, Documents, Tag References and Document References that correspond to

the same Working Set.

The following are the details:

· Each Class, as defined by the Class Library, has its own worksheet.
· A worksheet contains grouped Tags and Documents. The Document

References are listed within the Tag-To-Document References worksheet, and

Tag References, within the Document-To-Tag References worksheet.

· By design, the application stops the upload process when it encounters an
empty row. Therefore, if your worksheets have an empty row after the

headings, the application reports an error.

· Worksheets that do not match any Class names are ignored by the application

during the upload process.

· Each worksheet has a structure for classifying the information. Each attribute

has a dedicated column where:

· Attributes can contain multiple values.
· A value for each attribute is listed along with its unit of measure (UOM).
· Multiple values and their respective UOMs are separated by a space.

Note: Obsolete attributes are grouped at the end of the worksheet, marked

in yellow and show a tooltip. If the attribute has the extension

<tim:ExcelBulkDownload ../> with the property hidden set to true, the

respective column is not to be included in the output file.
· The Tags, Documents and Reference worksheets are colour coded:

· Purple – Tags worksheets
· Blue – Documents worksheets
· Red – Reference worksheets

Release 5.0.11

Page 55

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.19

Partial Delivery

The Partial Delivery feature enables contractors to deliver the changes in technical

information by objects, tags or documents, not only the changes in scope of a particular

Working Set. This feature is enabled only for the Information Managers, who are

authorised to verify, approve and publish changes in the back-end systems (such as

AVEVA NET Workhub and AVEVA WorkMate).

The following are the characteristic features of Partial Delivery:

· Partial Delivery is always available.
· Partial Delivery related to the Release option is configurable. For more

information, see Lock Unless Released.

· Partial Delivery is available, when the Working Set is in Active or For Verification

statuses.

Important: You must log in as an Information Manager to perform Partial Delivery of

Tags or Documents.

To open the Working Set and check the Tags uploaded in the Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Ongoing Task menu, expand the Active Working

Sets menu or For Verification.

2.

Click the Working Set link that you have the Tags ready for Partial Delivery.

Note: If the user who performs Partial Delivery is not the owner of the

Working Set, then that user cannot find the Working Set in Ongoing Tasks. For

more information about how to find the required Working Set by its number

from the Manage Working Set list, see Check Working Set Status.

3.

Click the Tags tab, review the Tags list, and then click Partial Check In.

Note: Only the New Tags marked as Is Used will be available on the screen.

4.

In the Check In column, select the check box for each Tag, and then click

Confirm.

Important: Tags with errors cannot be checked in, until the errors are fixed.

If the Check In is successful, you will be redirected to the home page. Once the

Tags are checked in, they will disappear from the Partial Check In page, and

cannot be modified again in the same Working Set. However, if the Release

(Lock Unless Released) functionality is enabled, the checked in Tags appear in

the list to enable manual release of it.

Release 5.0.11

Page 56

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To perform Partial Delivery of Documents:

1.

2.

Expand the Working Sets node, and then click the Object.

Click the Documents tab, review the Documents list, and then click Partial

Check In.

Note: Only the New Documents marked as Is Used will be available on the

screen.

3.

In the Partial Check In page, go to the Check In column, and then select the

check box for each Document.

Important: Documents with errors cannot be checked in, until the errors are

fixed.

4.

Click Confirm.

If the Check In is successful, you will be redirected to the home page. Once the

Documents are checked in, they will not be available in the Partial Check In

page, and cannot be modified again in the same Working Set. However, if the

Release (Lock Unless Released) functionality is enabled, the checked in

Documents appear in the list to enable manual release of it.

Release 5.0.11

Page 57

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.19.1

Lock Unless Released

The Lock Unless Released (Release) feature enables you to choose to lock or allow

modification of Tags or Documents in other Working Sets. This feature is configurable. If

you select to Release, the Tag or Document can be checked out for modification on

another Working Set:

· If the Release mode is enabled, with the value set as true in the configuration
file, the Release option will be available in the Partial Check In page. It enables

you to choose to Release in addition to Check In the Tags/Documents:

· If you select the Release check box, the Check In check box is also selected

automatically.

· You can lock the Release check box, if you have released Tags/Documents

by mistake.

Important: Tags/Documents with errors cannot be checked in and therefore

released, until the errors are fixed.

· Once the Tags/Documents are checked in, the Check In option will be

disabled. You cannot revert a Check In action.

· If the Release mode is disabled, with the value set as false in the configuration
file, the Release option will not be available in the Partial Check In page. In this

case, as soon as a Tag/Document is checked in, it gets released and is available

for modification.

Release 5.0.11

Page 58

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.20

Check Working Set Status

To check the activation process and other status of a Working Set:

· Using the Ongoing Tasks menu:

1. On the Home page, expand the following list:

· New Working Sets
· Active Working Sets
· Working Sets for Verification

2. Find and click the link for the Working Set that you have activated.

· Using the Manage Working Sets menu:

1. Go to the Home Page and then click Manage Working Sets link.

2. You will be redirected to the page listing Working Sets based on their

current status under the following tabs representing the specific status of

the Working Set:
· New Working Sets
· Active Working Sets
· Working Sets for Verification
· Verified Working Sets
· Voided Working Sets

3. Click the Active Working Sets tab to access the list of Working Sets with

Active status. If you cannot find a specific Working Set use the Search Box.

4.

In the Number column, click the Working Set link that you have activated.

5.21

Transfer a Working Set

Transfer enables changing the owner of the Working set, for example when several users

need to work on the same Working Set.

Note: If the Information Manager needs to review the contents of the Working Set then

there is a specific process for that, namely to send the Working Set for Verification.

To transfer a Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Other menu, click Transfer.

2. On the List of available users, in the Display Name column, click a new owner.

Release 5.0.11

Page 59

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

3. On the Transfer the Working Set to a new owner confirmation page, you can

enter a message for the new owner which will be added to the auto-generated

e-mail delivered to the e-mail address associated with the owner's user

profile.

4.

To transfer the Working Set, click Confirm.

5.22

Generate Reports on a Working Set

You can generate reports on the Working Set to identify the list of Tags and Documents,

validations errors, changes/modifications and completeness status.

To generate report for a Working Set:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and then click the Reports tab.

2. On the Reports list page, click one of predefined report.

List of Reports

Report Name

Description

Working Set Tag Report Generates a report providing a list of Tags associated to a

specific Working Set.

Working Set Document
Report

Generates a report providing a list of Documents associated to
a specific Working Set.

Working Set Validation
Error Report

Generates a report providing a list of validation errors in a
specific Working Set.

Working Set Validation
Error Details

Generates a report providing a list of validation errors in a
specific Working Set into a CSV format file.

Working Set Summary Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and

changes done for the objects (Tags and Documents) associated
to a specific Working Set.

Working Set Tag Details Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and

changes to a Working Set Tags.

Working Set Document
Details

Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and
changes to a Working Set Documents.

Working Set
Completeness

Generates a reports detaining Tag data completeness, of a
specific Working Set, for each system, discipline and class.

Working Set Attribute
Completeness

Generates a report detaining Tag data completeness of a
specific Working Set, per attribute, providing the number and
the percentage of Tags that do not have a value for the given
class/attribute combination.

Release 5.0.11

Page 60

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.23

Download the Content of the Working Set

You can download copies of the Working Set details in various file formats, for example

Working Sets that contain Tags with Document references, and save it on your local

machine or an accessible network location.

To download copies of the Working Set details:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Action menu, click Download

Working Set.

2. On the Download Working Set dialog box, in the file format drop down list,

select one of the available file formats.

Note: If the Class Library does not contain the mappings required to export

the data in the selected file format, the following error message is shown:

"You cannot download the data using the selected file format since the Class

Library does not have the required data mappings."

3.

Click one of the following:
· Tags and Documents
· Tags only
· Documents only

4.

Select one or all of the following download options:
· Include References – Use this to download Working Sets containing Tags

and Documents with their respective references, if available.

· Include empty Attributes – Use this to generate a list of all attributes

applicable for the class. This would be helpful for dealing with Engineering

Contractors.

· Exclude obsolete Attributes – Use this to exclude all obsolete attributes
from the output file for simplification of the further file editing process. If

you do not select this option, only empty obsolete attributes are excluded

while non-empty ones can be downloaded and updated.

5.

Click OK, to download a copy of the Working Set details in the selected file

format and save the file. If you want to cancel the download of the Working

Set details, click Abort.

5.23.1

The following tables list the possible values of the possible states of the Tags and the

Note: The attribute state is used only within the XML output file (available in the Staging

or Excel output files, it is not presented) and cannot be used to change the state of the

by a third-party system involved in the data exchange process to provide useful

information about the objects exported.

Release 5.0.11

Page 62

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.24

Modify and Upload Data File

Changes for Tags and Documents can be imported through Bulk Upload to a Working Set

which exists in the system and has an Active status. You need to ensure that all the Tags

and Documents, which you need to apply changes to, are added or reserved in the

Working Set, and that they are checked out. You cannot apply changes to the objects

which are not part of the Working Set. After the Bulk Upload process is initiated and

confirmed, the Bulk Upload Job will be created and queued. The Working Set gets locked

until the Job is finished. After the successful processing of the Bulk Upload Job, all Tags

and Documents from the data set are updated in the Working Set with the information

contained in that data set. The status of a Working Set does not change and it can be run

through further processes. Upon completion of the Working Set, all the existing data,

which are a part of the uploaded data set will be updated in the target repositories. For

any failed bulk upload jobs, an error is logged at the Data-Set level and the error message

is associated with the corresponding Working Set. The downloaded Working Set BU XML

files also contain the error message.

To modify the downloaded file:

1. Open the file in the appropriate editor, for example a XML editor for the XML

file, and apply the required changes.

2.

Save the changes and .zip archive the file.

Supported File Format

AVEVA Change Manager only supports the following four file formats for Bulk Upload:

File Format

Description

XML file

MDB file

Tab-delimited
files

In accordance with the predefined BU XML Schema.

Microsoft® Access® Databases files of the predefined structure.

It is not a file but several tab-delimited text files with a predefined
structure that must be archived (.zip) in order to be uploaded using
the Bulk Upload functionality. Extensions should be added in the Class
Library being used, to configure the import and export of the data
using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper Staging (tab delimited) file format.

XLS/XLSX file

Microsoft® Excel® files of the predefined structure. For more
information, see Microsoft Excel Support for Bulk Upload and
Download.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

Steps for Uploading Data File

To upload the modified data file using Bulk Upload:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Action menu, click Bulk Upload. This

link in available only for the Working Sets in Active status.

2.

In the file explorer, navigate to the source file folder, select the file and then

click Open.

Note: The size of the file to upload can be configured, by default the maximum

3.

4.

file size is 100MB.

Click Next to proceed with the upload.

Review the information under Details Information and File Content retrieved

from the contents of the data file that you have uploaded. If any additional

changes are required, and where required data cannot be retrieved from the

uploaded data file, the user can enter them manually. For example, when the

fields required in the Working Set Details page are not specified in the data

file.

5.

Click Confirm to proceed with the upload.

You will be redirected to the Home Page and an upload processing message

(similar to the following message) will be displayed under the title bar:

Example: Job was created and added to a queue for further processing.

5.24.1

Check the Progress of the Uploaded Job

A user assigned the Role of Super User, can view the status of any on-going Bulk Upload

jobs in order to ensure that upload of the data is going smoothly and that there is no

blocking issues with the ongoing jobs that would prevent the whole queue of jobs from

processing. The job status also displays the number of processed Tags, Documents,

Systems, and Disciplines. You can also view the details of completed and cancelled Bulk

Upload jobs.

To check the status and progress of an ongoing Bulk Upload Job:

1. Go to the Home Page, on the Other Tasks menu, click Manage Bulk Upload

Jobs.

2. On the Queue tab, in the Number column, click a Job Number. The Queue tab

displays the jobs that are currently in the queue for processing.

Release 5.0.11

Page 64

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

3. Under Selected Job, view the status and progress of the specific job.

· Check the current status information of an ongoing job on the Status
bar. After a job is processed, it is moved from the Queue tab to the

Historical tab. To review the details/statistics of a job after it is

processed, click a Job Number on the Historical tab.

· If there are any errors identified during the job processing, the Show

Error Message link is displayed in the Action menu:

· Click Show Error Message to view the information about the

error.

· You will be shown different suggestions depending on the error.
If some of the data does not validate, you will be required to

inspect the data by navigating to the Working Set Details page.

· To close the error message, click OK.

· Click the Working Set number link, to review the status and contents of

the uploaded data on the Working Set Details page.

By default the status and progress information will be automatically refreshed every one

minute. You can use the Refresh Now button to refresh the status of all jobs on demand.

To set the automatic refresh time, edit the value in the number box for the Refresh Now

button.

Ongoing jobs status list

Status
Name

New

Description

After a job is created and added to the Jobs list on the Queue tab, the
specific job is marked New.

In Progress Once the job is picked up for processing, the status of the specific job is

changed to In Progress.

Completed After the job is processed, the status of the specific job is changed to

Complete.

Failed

If any unrecoverable error occurs while processing the job, the status is
marked as Failed.

· Completed
· Cancelled

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.25

Re-Reserve Documents with Edited Revision Number
AVEVA Change Manager supports editing the revision number of a reserved Document.

To edit the revision number of a reserved Document, which is also known as re-reserving:

1. Edit the revision number on a reserved Document and save it.

2. To view the Document in AVEVA NET Dashboard, click on the link (

) icon.

The Document is re-reserved with the edited revision number. This feature does not

update any other attributes.

You may perform Partial Check-in of Documents with the edited revision number. If you

partially check-in a Document in a Working Set with either Active or For Verification

status, you can still check-out that Document in another Working Set.

To check out the Document in another Working Set:

1. Reserve the Document in a Working Set.

2. Activate the Working Set.

3. Upload Physical Files for the Document, if any.

4. Validate the Working Set.

5. Perform Partial Check-in.

6. Create another Working Set.

7. Add the checked-in Document to that Working Set.

8. Check out the Document by activating the Working Set.

Your Document is now checked out successfully in another Working Set even though the
previous Working Set is not complete. For the re-reserved Documents, AVEVA NET
displays only the updated revision number.

Release 5.0.11

Page 66

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.26

Remove Reserved Tags or Documents from Working Set Sent for
Verification

When a Working Set is sent for verification, the reserved Tags (or reserved Documents)

contained within it cannot be removed unless you remove their Document references (or

Tag references). This is by design.

To remove a reserved Tag from a Working Set sent for verification:

1. Cancel the verification of the Working Set.

This changes the status of the Working Set to Active.

2. Delete the Document references.

3. Re-send the Working Set for verification.

This removes the references from AVEVA NET Dashboard.

4. Remove the Tag from the Working Set.

This completes the process of removing the reserved Tag from the Working Set.

To remove a reserved Document from a Working Set sent for verification, repeat steps 1

through 4.

Release 5.0.11

Page 67

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

5.27

Clone Tag

You can clone a Tag only if it is in reserved or checked out state and it has association

with a Naming-and-Numbering (N&N) template.

To clone a Tag:

1.

Click the Clone button displayed at the bottom of the Tag details page.

Note: The Clone button is enabled only if the Tag is reserved or checked out

and has no attribute, object or reference level errors.

All the local changes made, if any, are applied automatically and the Please

Confirm Tag Cloning dialog box is displayed.

2.

Provide inputs for the following fields:
· Quantity of tags*– (required) Specify the desired number of the cloned

Tags.

· Start From – (optional) Specify a start index or prefix for the Tags to be

cloned.

· Include Document References  – (optional) Select this checkbox only if you

want to retain the Document references from the Tag being cloned.

3.

Click Ok to confirm cloning of Tag. (If you do not want to continue the Tag

cloning process, click Cancel.)

The Summary page is displayed showing the list of Tags created. All new tags

are created as reserved and are in the same Working Set as the original tag. If

some Tags could not be reserved due to some errors, they are added to the

Working Set in Failed state.

4.

Click Create More to clone more Tags or Finish to complete the cloning

process.

5.28

Splitting of Working Set

AVEVA Change Manager enables you to select and move Tags and Documents from one

Working Set to another.

Release 5.0.11

Page 68

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

5.28.1

Moving Tags

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To move tags from one Working Set to another:

1.

Click the Move Tags option in the Action menu on the Working Set details

page.

Note: The Move Tags option is enabled only if the Working Set is new, active,

or sent for verification.

If there are no Tags in the current Working Set, an error message is displayed

informing that no applicable Tags have been found for migration. Otherwise,

the Tag Migration wizard is initialized.

2.

Select the target Working Set on the Select Working Set page where the list of

other new, active, or for verification Working Sets owned by you, or other

users if you are an Administrator or Information Manager is displayed. You can

see three tabs – New Working Sets, Active Working Sets, and Working Sets

for Verification.

Note:

· The Working Sets for Verification tab is displayed only if the current

Working Set has been sent for verification.

· Selection of a target Working Set is optional, so skipping its selection will

generate a new Working Set automatically.

3.

Select the Tags on the Select Tags page where the multi-selection list of the

applicable Tags fetched from the current Working Set is displayed. The

applicable Tags are either the checked out Tags that have not been verified

yet or the reserved Tags including partially updated ones.

Note: The following extra filters can be applied depending on the Working Set

selected on the previous page:

· if the target Working Set is new, then, only new, not checked out or failed

Tags are displayed;

· if the target Working Set is active or for verification, all applicable Tags

except the checked in or released ones are displayed.

4.

Click Confirm on the Confirm page where the selected Working Set and Tags

are presented for the final check.

Release 5.0.11

Page 69

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

5.28.2

Moving Documents

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Manage Working Sets

To move documents from one Working Set to another:

1.

Click the Move Documents option in the Action menu on the Working Set

details page.

Note: The Move Documents option is enabled only if the Working Set is new,

active or sent for verification.

If there are no Documents in the current Working Set, an error message is

displayed informing that no applicable Documents have been found for

migration. Otherwise, the Document Migration wizard is initialized.

2.

Select the target Working Set on the Select Working Set page where the list of

other new, active or for verification Working Sets owned by you, or other

users if you are an Administrator or Information Manager is displayed. You can

see three tabs – New Working Sets, Active Working Sets and Working Sets for

Verification.

Note:

· The Working Sets for Verification tab is displayed only if the current

Working Set has been sent for verification.

· Selection of a target Working Set is optional, so skipping its selection will

generate a new Working Set automatically.

3.

Select the Documents on the Select Documents page where the multi-

selection list of the applicable Documents fetched from the current Working

Set is displayed. The applicable Documents are either the checked out

Documents that have not been verified yet or the reserved Documents

including partially updated ones.

Note: The following extra filters can be applied depending on the Working Set

selected on the previous page:

· if the target Working Set is new, then, only new, not checked out or failed

Documents are displayed;

· if the target Working Set is active or for verification, all applicable

Documents are displayed.

4.

Click Confirm on the Confirm page where the selected Working Set and

Documents are presented for the final check.

Release 5.0.11

Page 70

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

6

Process Working Sets

This chapter covers the tasks needed to process a Working Set from initiation to

completion.

6.1

Working Set Validation Errors

A Working Set with an Active status can only be send For Verification to an Information

Manager after the validation errors with either the Documents or Tags have been fixed.

On the Working Set list page, a specific Working Set that contains a validation error with

either the Documents or Tags is displayed with a cross folder icon. The notification

displays a brief error summary when you move the pointer over the cross folder icon.

To indicate that the specific Tags or Documents have errors, the Error column on the

Working Set Tags/Documents page will be marked as selected. You can move the pointer

over the Error box to view the related error message.

Open the Tags/Documents Details page and fix the validation error.

6.2

Void Working Set

You can change the status of a Working Set to Void based on your requirements. After

you select the voiding process, all the changes you have performed on the Working Set,

which have not been published, are reverted. All the Tags and Documents that you have

reserved for the Working Set are also released. All not checked in Tags and Documents

will be removed from the Working Set, leaving the checked in objects untouched for

historical and audit purposes.

To void the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar, check the Working Set

status and then click Void.

2. On the Void Working Set confirmation page, read the Void Information.

3.

In the Reason for voiding list, select the reason for voiding the Working Set.

This is a required field.

4.

In the Message to Log box, you should enter a message for data tracking

purposes. However, this field is not required and will let you progress without

entering a message.

5.

To void the Working Set, click Confirm.

Release 5.0.11

Page 71

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

The Working Set is now voided, and the Status bar will display Void as the current status

for the Working Set. All the voided Working Set are viewable in the Voided Working Set

tab on Working Set list page. The undo check-out process for Documents and Tags starts

after you have selected Void for a Working Set.

6.3

Update Working Set Tag Status to Is Used

You must select Is Used for each newly reserved Tag before you run the verification

process of a Working Set. This option can be selected when the State of a Tag is marked

as New. Is Used can be set for Tags and Documents on Tag/Document Details page as

well.

To update the status of Working Set Tag to Is Used:

1. Open the Working Set Details page and then click the Tags tab.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, select Is Used and Installed for the Tags.

3.

To update the Working Set Tags page, click Save.

In specific business processes not all of the initially reserved new Tags are potentially

going to be used – some Tags might have been reserved in advance in bulks but only

number of them is what is finally needed. In order to ensure that the final list of Tags

contains only required Tags the users should review the list and select the Is Used option

and remove the Tags that are not in use.

6.4

Update Working Set Tag Status to Installed

You can also select Installed to indicate that the new items, for which Tag Numbers have

been reserved in the Working Set, have already been installed or added to the Plant. The

Installed status is configurable. This is only available for the Working Set with Active or

For Verification status. Installed can be set for Tags only on Tag Details page as well.

To update the status of Working Set Tag to Installed:

1. Open the Working Set Details page and then click the Tags tab.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, select Installed for the Tags.

3.

Click Save to update the Working Set Tags page.

Release 5.0.11

Page 72

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

6.5

Update Working Set Tag Status to Decommisioned

You can also decommission a Tag based on the requirements in a Plant. The

Decommissioned status is configurable. The Working Set Tag can be Decommissioned

after the State of a Tag is marked as Checked Out. Decommissioned can be set for Tags

only on Tag/Document Details page as well.

Note: When the Tag is checked in, it will be automatically assigned a Removed status in

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard.

To decommission a Tag:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Tags tab.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, select Decommissioned option for a single or

multiple Tags.

3.

Click Save to update the Working Set Tags page.

6.6

Set Working Set Tag Action

You can set a particular action to be taken for a specific Tag based on the requirements in

a Plant. This can be set after State of a Tag is marked as Checked Out. The action for a

Working Set Tag is set by the user as part of the steady-state operations project. The

status of the Tag changes to a particular action item after the Working Set is sent for

verification and verified.

To update the action for a Tag:

1. On the Working Set Details page and then click the Tags tab.

2. On the Working Set Tags page, in the Action column, select one of the

following value from the drop down list for a single or multiple Tags:
· On Hold
· Removed
· Void

Note: The same process can be carried out from the Tag Details page and Tag

List page.

3.

Click Save to update the Tag status.

Release 5.0.11

Page 73

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

6.7

Set Working Set Document Action

You can set a particular action to be taken for a specific Document based on the

requirements in a Plant. This can be set after the State of a Document is marked as

Checked Out. The action for a Working Set Document is set by the user as part of the

steady-state operations project. The status of the Document changes to a particular

action item after the Working Set is sent for verification and verified.

To update the action for a Document:

1. On the Working Set Details page, click the Documents tab.

2. On the Working Set Documents page, in the Action column, select one of the

following values from the drop down list for single or multiple Documents:
· Void
· On Hold
· Superseded
· Retained by Contractor

Note: The same process can be carried out from the Document Details page

and Document List page.

3.

Click Save to update the Document status.

6.8

Check In and Check Out the Working Set

When a Tag/Document is not checked out or checked in automatically, you can use the

Check In and Check Out options on the Working Set Details page. These options can also

be used if some object was added at the later stage and you need to check out manually.

While checking in a Tag or Document, if you face any validation error, you can use the

Check In option after fixing the error.

To use Check In and Check Out, click any of the following options on the on the Action

menu on Working Set Details page:

· Check In
· Check Out

Based on your selection, Check In/Check Out is done automatically.

Release 5.0.11

Page 74

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

6.9

Verification of Working Set

A Working Set with an Active status can be sent for verification to an Information

Manager. The status of the Working Set changes from Active to For Verification after it is

sent for verification.

You have to select Is Used for each Tag and Document before you run the verification

process of a Working Set. This option can be selected when the State of a Tag and

Document is marked as New. You also need to upload the physical files into the

documents, select Master files for those documents and specify the new revisions if

AVEVA Change Manager is configured to process physical files. All validation errors

should also be fixed.

To send a Working Set for verification:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar check the Working Set

status which would be marked as Active.

2.

Click For Verification.

3. On the List of available users list, in the Display Name column, select the user

with Information Manager Role to verify the data of your Working Set.

4.

In the Message to verifier box, you can enter some message for the

Information Manager which will be added to the auto-generated e-mail

delivered to the e-mail address associated with the Information Manager's

user profile.

5.

Click Confirm, to confirm sending the Working Set for verification.

6.10

Verify the Working Set and Check in the Changes

You can verify the contents of the Working Set, sent to you for verification. You should be

assigned to Role of Information Manager to verify the Working Set. Due to your

verification no any more changes or updates can be done on the Working Set. You finalise

the modifications by pushing the data into integrated back-end systems, for example,

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard and Document Management System. The status of

the Working Set changes from For Verification to Verification Complete after the

verification process is complete.

Find and open the Working Set waiting for the verification:

· Using the Ongoing Tasks menu on the Home page, expand the Working Sets for
Verification list, and then click the link for the Working Set that you need to

verify.

Release 5.0.11

Page 75

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

· Using the Manage Working Sets menu:

1. Go to the Home Page and then click Manage Working Sets link.

2.

Click the Working Sets for Verification tab to access the list of Working

Sets sent for verification. If you cannot find a specific Working Set use

the Search Box.

3.

In the Number column, click the link for the Working Set that you have

verify.

Check the metadata of the objects in the Working Set, open Working Set Details page and

do the following:

1. Click the Tags tab and click the Tag Number for the Tag to open the Details

Page.

2. Click the Documents tab and click the Document Number for the Document to

open the Details Page.

Check the contents of the files attached to the Document in the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Documents page, click the Document number you have

uploaded the physical files.

2.

3.

Click Files tab, to get access to the files uploaded into the Document.

Click on the link to the file in Modified column to see the uploaded file

contents.

After the content of the Working Set is reviewed, the Information Manager needs to

confirm that the files uploaded into the documents have been also been verified. For this

reason, the Verified option must be selected for all the Documents.

Confirm the verification of the Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar, check the Working Set

status which would be marked as For Verification.

2. On the confirmation page, read through the information displayed on the

Please Confirm Complete Verification message box.

3.

4.

Click Confirm, to confirm completion of the Working Set Verification.

You will be redirected to the Home Page. The update data are pushed into

back-end systems in the background and as the result of this process initiation

the Working Set gets locked. A check in process initiation message (similar to

the following message) will be displayed under the title bar: "Check in of the

current Working Set is now being processes."

Release 5.0.11

Page 76

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

5.

You will be redirected to the Home page. The activation of the Working Set

happens in the background and as the result of this process initiation, the

Working Set gets locked.

You will have to open the Working Set Details page or Working Set List pages to check the

status of the check in. Once the check in is finished the Working Set is unlocked and the

status of the Working Set changes to Verification Complete.

6.11

Check the data in AVEVA NET Dashboard

Find and open the Verified Working Set and using the Manage Working Sets menu, and

do the following:

1. Go to the Home Page and then click Manage Working Sets link.

2.

Click the Verified Working Set tab to access the list of Working Sets with the

Verification Complete status. If you cannot find a specific Working Set use the

Search Box.

3.

In the Number column, click the Working Set Number to open the Working Set

Details page.

To check the data in AVEVA NET Dashboard:

1. Open the Working Set Details page.

2.

Click the Tags tab and then click the information icon on the Tag Number, to

open a specific Tag on the AVEVA NET Dashboard. The Tag Summary page is

displayed with the information about the Tag. You can review the updated

data.

3.

Click the Documents tab and then click the information icon on the Document

Number, to open a specific Document on the AVEVA NET Dashboard. The

Document details is displayed with the information about the Document. You

can review the updated data.

6.12

Cancel Verification of Working Set

The verification of a Working Set with a For Verification status can be cancelled by the

owner of that Working Set. The owner is an Information Manager. The status of the

Working Set changes from For Verification to Active after the verification is cancelled.

The Working Set is sent back to the user who requested the verification.

Release 5.0.11

Page 77

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

To cancel the verification of a Working Set:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar, check the Working Set

status which would be marked as For Verification.

2.

Click Cancel Verification to cancel the verification.

3. On the Status bar, check the Working Set status which would change to

Active.

6.13

Complete Working Set

To archive a Working Set with a Verification Complete status you can mark it as

complete. The status of the Working Set changes from Verification Complete to

Complete.

To mark a Working Set complete:

1. On the Working Set Details page, on the Status bar check the Working Set

status which would be marked as Verification Complete.

2.

Click Complete.

All the Working Sets with Complete status are viewable in the Historical Working Sets list

on Manage Working Sets page.

To view details of an historical Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Manage Working Sets menu, click View Historical.

2. On the Historical Working Sets list, in the Number column, click a Working Set

Number to views the Working Set Details page.

Release 5.0.11

Page 78

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Process Working Sets

6.14

Validating Working Sets

When validation for a Working Set is in process, all the underlying attributes of Tags and

Documents are also validated/revalidated against the values initially set in the brownfield

bulk upload job. The revalidation is redundant when no changes are made to the Tags and

Documents. You can use the ReactiveValidationEnabled parameter in the

OwnerDomain\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml to change this behaviour. When you set

the value of this parameter to true, the underlying attributes will be validated only if their

values or units of measure have changed or the attribute has any errors or warnings

associated.

For those Working Sets that are either Active or in the verification process, the Action

panel appears on the Working Set Details page. Click on the Validate Working Set link in

the the Action panel to use the Validated check box to check the value of its

corresponding record in the table.

The Validated check box functions similar to the ReactiveValidationEnabled parameter,

and has the following possibilities. To view the error or confirmation message, hover over

the check box:

· Cleared: The underlying attributes of Tags and Documents need validation.
· Green-coloured tick: The underlying attributes are validated and no errors have

been reported.

· Red-coloured tick: The underlying attributes are validated and some errors have

been reported.

Release 5.0.11

Page 79

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Fast Track Modification

7

Fast Track Modification

If you need to do some minor modification on the existing Tag (for example, to fix the

typo in the Description), you can use the Fast Track modification option. You can also use

this option to do some minor modification on the existing Document, for example, to add

the reference to the Tag that was missing (assuming that the Tag itself is already

mentioned on the drawings uploaded in the Document).This type of modification is so

minor that there is no need to initiate any Modification Project to make the changes.

The Fast Track for Tags and Documents functionality is available only to users with Super

Users Role. It enables quick editing of Tags and Documents. Each Fast Track change will

cause an associated Working Set to be created automatically. If there is a conflict with

another business process changing the Tag or Document (for example, an existing, active

Working Set), this must be resolved before a Fast Track change may occur. Unlike other

methods of change management within AVEVA Change Manager, Fast Track changes will

facilitate direct edits to data (for example, changing a single attribute within an object).

The standard management flow for a Working Set is bypassed and immediately approved.

7.1

Fast Track Modification of the Existing Tag

To modify existing Tag using Fast Track modification:

1. On the Home Page, click Fast Track for Tags.

2. On the Available Assets list, in the Asset column, select the Asset Code that

the Tag belongs.

3. On the Tag hierarchy list, click the System nodes to expand a category and get

access to the list of existing Tags within that System. To find a particular Tag

you can also use the Search option. Click the Tag Number to select the Tag for

modification.

Note: The Tags in the Tag hierarchy list are grouped in a tree view that reflects

the breakdown structure based on the parent tag associations starting from

the System and the main tags on the top. The Tag with an Active status and

having a Parent Tag belonging to one of the available Systems are listed in the

Tag hierarchy.

4.

If required, go to the Tag Details page, and update the Tag Description field

with a new value.

5.

Click Finish, to proceed with the modification.

Release 5.0.11

Page 80

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Fast Track Modification

6. On the confirmation page, in the Comment box, you should enter a comment

for data tracking purposes. However, this field is not required and will let you

7.

8.

progress without entering a comment.

Click Confirm, to confirm and finalise the update.

If successfully updated, you will be redirected to the Home Page. A

confirmation message appears under the title bar of the Working Set, which

was created through the fast track process. The Working Set is automatically

created to track the changes made to the Tag. It bypasses the manual

verification process and it is moved to Historical Working Sets list

automatically.

7.2

Fast Track modification of the Existing Document

To modify an existing Document using Fast Track modification:

1. On the Home Page, click Fast Track for Document.

2. On the Available Assets list, in the Asset column, select the Asset Code that

the Document belongs.

3. Under Document Classes Taxonomy, browse through the Classes Taxonomy

to find the class for the new Documents by expanding the node in the tree

view. In the Class column, click the Class Name to select the Class for your

Document.

Note: Document Taxonomies are defined in the Class Library Definitions and

they can be setup and configured for a specific Asset using AVEVA Information

Standards Manager™.

4.

To search for the required document, use the Search option in the Available

Documents page.

5.

If you want to add a reference (optional):

a. Go to the Document Details page, click the Document References tab

and then click Add Document References to start adding a reference.

b.

c.

d.

Select the Document Numbers you want to add as a reference.

Click Confirm, to confirm and finalise the update.

You will be redirected to the Document Reference page, the selected

Document is displayed in the list with the Added state.

6.

Click Finish.

7. On the confirmation page, in the Comment box, you should enter a comment

for data tracking purposes. However, this field is not required and will let you

progress without entering a comment.

Release 5.0.11

Page 81

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Fast Track Modification

8.

9.

To confirm and finalise the update, click Confirm.

If successfully updated, you will be redirected to the Home Page. A

confirmation message appears under the title bar of the Working Set, which

was created through the fast track process. The Working Set is automatically

created to track the changes made to the Document. It bypasses the manual

verification process and it is moved to the Historical Working Sets list

automatically.

7.2.1

Create XML During Fast Track Modification of Documents

To enable sending of an XML file to DMS while performing Fast Track modification of

Documents, use the EnableUpdateDataFastTrackXml parameter. You can configure

this parameter in TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml, which is in the OwnerDomain folder of

the installation directory.

By default, this parameter is set to false. In which case, AVEVA Change Manager does not

send an XML. If the value is set to true, the application sends an XML, which contains the

UPDATEDATA command, into the OutBound folder of your DMS.

Release 5.0.11

Page 82

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Documentation Centre

8

Documentation Centre

The Documentation Centre menu has to be configured to a company or plant

requirements. It will contain links to predefined locations. The System Administrator can

configure the required locations and available content.

When you move the pointer over a specific link on the Documentation Centre menu,

Information about the document message is displayed. This provides a brief statistics

about a particular link. The Documentation Centre may contain links to Documents that

include the Company Policy, describe processes or steps to be followed by the user, and

other types of Documents related to the application. It may also contain links to internal

and external systems.

The Administrator can edit the DocumentationCentre.xml file located under the

application installation path, such as "\Customer\Website\XmlData\", to update the

Documentation Centre links.

Release 5.0.11

Page 83

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Report Centre

9

Report Centre

The Report Centre contains the links for the following reports:

· Working Set Overview
· Working Set Documents and Tags
· Working Set Tag list

On the Report Centre menu, you can also view a summary of the report by moving the

pointer over a specific report link.

9.1

Generate Working Set Overview Report

The Working Set Overview report lists all existing Working Sets on a specific Asset with a

specified Status. This report helps in monitoring existing Working Sets. To run this report

you have to specify the criteria for filtering and limiting the report output.

To generate a Working Set Overview report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Overview.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the overview of the

Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Select the Status for the Working Set Status from the Status list, that

you would like to be included in the report.

3.

Click Finish to generate the report in a separate window against the provided

report criteria.

9.2

Generate Working Set Documents and Tags Report

The Working Set Documents and Tags report lists all the Documents and Tags for all

Working Sets within a specific Modification Project (shown as an AFA Number). This

report helps to monitor the information processed in the Working Set for any

Modification Project. To run this report you have to specify the criteria for filtering and

limiting the report output.

Release 5.0.11

Page 84

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Change Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Report Centre

To generate Working Set Document and Tags report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Documents

and Tags report.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the information about

the Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Specify the AFA Number from the AFA Number list, to get the

information about the Working Sets within the selected Modification

Project (AFA).

3.

Read the Note text and then click Finish to generate the report in a separate

window against the provided report criteria.

9.3

Generate Working Set Tag List Report

The Working Set Tag List report lists Tags processed in all Working Sets within a specific

Modification Project and System. This report helps in monitoring the list of available Tags

and their criticalities. To run this report you have to specify the criteria for filtering and

limiting the report output.

To generate Working Set Tag List report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Tag List

report.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the information about
the Tags processed in the Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Specify the AFA Number from the AFA Number list, to get the

information about the Tags processed in the Working Sets within the

selected Modification Project (AFA).

· Specify the System from the System list, to get the information about

the Tags processed in the Working Sets of the specific System.

3.

Click Finish to generate the report in a separate window against the provided

report criteria.

Release 5.0.11

1

Preface

(after this, referred to as the Gateway). It includes the following key information:

· Creating and opening projects.
· Extracting text from files.
· Using the Configuration file.
· Running the Gateway from the command file.
