CHAPTER 7 - Working with Attributes

7

Working with Attributes

An Attribute is a value the user of an authoring tool defines. For example, Name, Design

Pressure or Length (as opposed to a property, which is an intrinsic value of the data

object itself and may also be referred to as metadata). When you extend a Class all its

attributes are inherited by its target and its child classes. Such attribute values can be

overridden if required. It is recommended that the attribute identifier value does not

contain white space.

When associating an Attribute to a Class, the Attribute can have properties based on the

instantiation. For example, the Number may carry a different label of Name on two

different Classes. That is, on a Physical Class it will be Asset Number and on a Document

Class it will be Document Number.

7.1

Defining Permissible Attributes

Before creating a permissible attribute, ensure that you have the Author privilege and

have the Standard locked for editing:

1.

You can create an Attribute at any level by right-clicking and selecting Add

Attribute. You can also create an attribute within a folder or group.

2.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the attribute. This supports

alphanumeric characters. This field does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a user-readable name for the identification in this optional field. If

left blank, it shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric

characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of the attribute.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Attribute.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Concept field, select all the concepts (Class Types) that this attribute

may be used on. If you do not select a specific concept, you will not be able to

find the attribute when searching for it from a Class of the unselected concept.

Release 2.2.1

Page 48

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Working with Attributes

6.

7.

8.

9.

Select a Group. This helps create a folder structure for similar Attributes.

Select a Discipline. Depending on whether the appropriate Aspect is applied to

an Enumerated List Object, the selection for the fields appear as either a

dropdown or a text field.

Select from a list of Data Types that define the Attribute.

Enter the acceptable number of characters or value in the Size field. For

example, if you enter 300 in Size and select String as your Data Type, the value

can contain up to 300 characters.

10. Select the UoM Required check box if the unit of measure is required for the

Attribute. Typically, a unit of measure may be required for Data Types, such

as Integer or Decimal.

11. Select the required Unit of Measure Class. For information, see Creating and

Defining UoM Class.

12. Select from the Presence dropdown.

13. Select the Validation Type for the Attribute. Based on your selection for the

field, the Validation Rule field will vary as follows:
· When you select Enumeration as the Validation Type, the Validation Rule

will be set to Enumeration List. It is here that you can associate an

Enumerated List with an Attribute.

· When you select Value Range Inclusive, the Validation Rule will be set to

Maximum Value and Minimum Value.

· When you select Regular Expression as the Validation Type, the Validation

Rule will ask you to provide the Regular Expression.

· When you select Association as the Validation Type, the Validation Rule will
ask you to set the Association Rule and the Target associated with the rule.

If no Validation Type is required, you can set the type as <unselect>.

14. Use the Min Occurs and Max Occurs fields, respectively, to specify the

minimum and maximum number of values to be provided for the Attribute

when loading data from other applications, such as AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

15. Select a Maturity Level for the Attribute. For information, see Defining

Maturity Levels.

16. Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

all Attributes will be listed in a folder in the tree view.

17. Enter an Aspect if you have an enumerated list associated with the Attribute.

This enables you to select from a fixed set of possible values or associations

for the Attribute.

7.2

Creating Permissible Associations

An Attribute can be used to define an association between a Target Class or Classes and

the Class that the attribute is associated to. If you are associating to multiple Classes, you

are restricted to the same Class Concept, meaning that, you can have a single association

to both a Document and a General Class. This type of an attribute is referred to as

permissible association rather than as permissible attribute.

The association can only be defined in one direction. However, the reverse can be done

using appropriate extensions, where required, such as in AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard.

To create an attribute as a permissible association, first define a permissible attribute and

then, use the following procedure:

1.

2.

3.

In the Details tab, go to the Properties Specific to Attribute section.

In the Validation Type field, select Association.

The Validation Rule field is replaced with Association Type and Valid Target

Class fields. In the Association Type field, enter the association condition, such

as Fulfilled By.

4.

The filter criteria can be toggled to show either only selected data or all

values, which populates the corresponding descendants as well.

5.

In the Target Class Field, search and select the target Class or Classes.

Note: You can select multiple Classes as long as they are from the same

Concept.

6.

Click Add and Close.

CHAPTER 8 - Managing Units of Measure

8

Managing Units of Measure

Unit of measure is applicable to only Attributes. A Unit of Measure or Unit of

Measurement (UoM) is used for measuring the same kind of quantity. For example,

millimetre and kilometre are measuring units, while length is a collection of such units,

and hence is called a Unit of Measure Class.

What is a Measure Class?

A Measure Class is a collection of Units of Measure, which can be associated to an

Attribute.

What is a Measure Unit?

A Measure Unit is a measurement item that contains the quantification information.

8.1

Creating and Defining UoM Classes

To create a UoM Class, ensure that you have the Creator privilege and have the Standard

locked for editing:

1.

Right-click and select Add Measure Class. You can expand a UoM Class and

create a Class within it to create a group.

2.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the UoM Class. This supports

alphanumeric characters. This field does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe what this UoM Class is and what it does.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

UoM Class.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

Release 2.2.1

Page 52

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Managing Units of Measure

5.

Enter a Group. This will create groups of similar UoM Classes. The system

creates groups based on the values provided here. The groups are displayed

within the Measure Classes tree structure.

6.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

the UoM Classes will be listed in the group folder in the tree view.

7.

Enter an Aspect if it is implemented on the Unit of Measure Class. For

information on Aspect, see Understanding Aspects.

The UoM Class is now defined.

Note: To discontinue using a UoM Class, select the Obsolete check box in the General

Properties section on the Details tab.

8.2

Creating and Defining UoM Units

To create a UoM Unit, ensure that you have the Creator privilege and have the Standard

locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click a parent folder and select Add Measure Unit.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the UoM Unit. This supports

alphanumeric characters. This field does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of the Unit.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

UoM Unit.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Measure Unit section, enter the Symbol for the

UoM Unit.

CHAPTER 8 - Managing Units of Measure

6.

Enter a value for the UoM Unit in the Standard field. This is a standard in a

way that maps to the international standards for measurement and

quantification.

7.

8.

From the System dropdown, select the measuring system.

Select a Group. This groups similar Unit of Measure Units, such as length. The

groups are displayed within the Measure Units tree structure.

9.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

all Unit of Measure Classes will be listed in the group folder in the tree view.

10. Enter an Aspect if it is implemented on the UoM Unit. For information on

Aspect, see Understanding Aspects.

The UoM Unit is now defined.

Note: To discontinue using a UoM Unit, select the Obsolete check box in the General

Properties section on the Details tab.

To associate UoM Units and UoM Classes:

1.

2.

3.

In a UoM Class, go to the Permissible Units tab.

Click Add.

In the Add Permissible Measure Units window, select the required UoM

Unit from the dropdown.

4.

Click Add to create the class and continue to create classes. Alternatively, click

Add and Close to add the Class and close the window.

CHAPTER 9 - Handling Enumerations

9

Handling Enumerations

Enumerations are applicable to Attributes and properties of Classes. The properties of

Classes and Attributes are dealt using Aspects. AVEVA Information Standards Manager

provides you the capability to create enumerated lists that can be applied to Attributes

when required.

9.1

Creating Enumerated Lists

To create an Enumerated List, ensure that you have the Creator privilege and have the

Standard locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click a folder and select Add List.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Enumeration List. This supports

alphanumeric characters. This field does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of the enumerated list.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Enumeration List.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

all Lists will be listed in a folder in the tree structure.

6.

Enter an Aspect if you have an enumerated list associated with Attributes. This

enables you to select from a fixed set of possible values or associations for an

Attribute. For information, see Understanding Aspects.

The Enumerated List is now created.

Note: To discontinue using an Enumerated List, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section on the Details tab.

To create an enumerated list item:

2.

Enter the required information:

a.

supports alphanumeric characters. This field does not auto-

increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special

characters, it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left

blank, it shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric

characters.

c.

3.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Enumeration List Item.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Lookup List Item section, the List field contains

the name of the Enumerated List name under which you are creating the list

item. You may change that if required.

6. Go to the General Properties section. Enter a value in the Sort Order field.

This value represents the order in which all Lists will be listed in a folder in the

tree view.

The Enumerated List Item is now created and is now visible under in the Items tab of the

Enumeration List. Repeat the steps to create more Enumerated List Items.

Note: To discontinue using an Enumerated List Item, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section on the Details tab.

CHAPTER 10 - Understanding Naming and Numbering

10

Understanding Naming and Numbering

Naming and Numbering helps you with the organisation and analysis of data. You can

have multiple templates on Classes, and they are derived by their child Classes. The

relationship of Naming Element and Naming Template is such that when a Naming

Element is associated with a Naming Template, the behaviour of the Naming Element can

be adjusted without disturbing the actual Element.

What is a Naming Template?

It is a structured collection of elements that can be applied to a Class.

What is a Naming Element?

A component of a naming template. This component must relate to an attribute.

10.1

Adding Naming Elements

To create a Naming Element, ensure that you have the Creator privilege and have the

Standard locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click the Naming Element folder and select Add Naming Element.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Naming Element. This supports

alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of this Naming Element.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Naming Element.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Naming Element section, enter a number in the

Sequence ID field. Any new record will auto-increment this number.

Release 2.2.1

Page 59

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Understanding Naming and Numbering

6.

In the Attribute field, select the source Attribute for the Element. For

example, for Discipline Identifier as the Naming Element, Discipline will be the

source Attribute. You can have only one Attribute.

7.

Select the Mandatory check box if the Attribute is mandatory.

8. Optionally, select the Hide on Empty Value check box. It is recommended to

leave the check box cleared if the Mandatory check box is selected.

9.

In the Prefix, Suffix and Padding fields, enter values for the respective fields.

These values define the Naming Element:

Prefix: Specifies a character sequence to prefix the Element value when

composing an object name.

Suffix: Specifies a character sequence to suffix the Element value when

composing an object name. Typically, either prefix or suffix is used for

an Element, but not both.

Padding: If the value provided contains less than the required number

of characters, the value will be padded by the padding characters. The

padding will be either on left or right, depending on the Data Type of

the associated Attribute.

10. The Min Number of Characters and Max Number of Characters fields display

the lower and upper acceptable limits, respectively, of the number of

characters that the field requires. If you set a value in Min Number of

Characters, ensure that you set a value in Padding. The values must be

positive integers. The maximum value must be greater than or equal to the

value for the minimum characters.

11. Enter a value in the Fixed Value field. This value represents a hard-coded value

for the Element.

12. Enter a Validation Regular Expression in its text field.

13. Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

all Elements will be listed in the folder in the tree view.

14. Enter an Aspect if it is implemented on the Element. Aspect lists similar

properties in a dropdown.

The Naming Element is defined.

Note: To discontinue using a Naming Element, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section on the Details tab.

Release 2.2.1

Page 60

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

·
AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Understanding Naming and Numbering

10.2

Adding Naming Templates

To create a Naming Template, ensure that you have the Creator privilege and have the

Standard locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click the Naming Template folder and select Add Naming Template.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Naming Template. This field

supports alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If you do

not input any data, it auto-populates the ID.

c.

Describe what the Naming Template contains.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Naming Template.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5. Optionally, in the Properties Specific to Naming Template section, enter a

Validation Regular Expression in its text field.

6.

From the Applicable For dropdown, select what the regular expression will be

used for. The options are:

Validation: Whether the template will be used for validation of a Class.

Allocation: Whether the template will be used for allocation.

Validation and Allocation: Whether the template will be used for both

validation and allocation.

None

7. Optionally, enter a value in the Sort Order field. The application uses this value

for sorting and listing of the Naming Templates.

8.

Enter an Aspect if you have an enumerated list associated with the Naming

Template. This enables you to select from a fixed set of possible values when

creating records.

The Naming Template is now defined.

Note: To discontinue using a Naming Template, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section on the Details tab.

CHAPTER 11 - Implementing Corresponding Identifiers

11

Implementing Corresponding Identifiers

When exporting or importing data from or into a data source, information discrepancies

can occur even in a simple case of misplaced plurals. Such situations can be countered by

implementing naming conventions. The naming conventions help us map the existing

data with that of standardised information by making it upgrade-proof and version-proof.

Having the same name (and case sensitivity) when upgrading to the latest version of

AVEVA Information Standards Manager ensures version-proofing of the data. The

application picks and applies the settings to the new and modified data. This is different

from the concept of Languages. For information, see Adding Language Translations.

Note: You must define a nomenclature type. You can have multiple maps for a

nomenclature type.

Release 2.2.1

Page 64

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 11 - Implementing Corresponding Identifiers

To implement Corresponding Identifiers:

1.

Click the Details button (

) on Catalogue or select the currently Active

Standard from the global toolbar at the top of your screen.

2. Go to the Naming Conventions tab.

3.

4.

Click Add.

In the Add Naming Convention window, enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the naming convention. For example,

ISO15926. This supports alphanumeric characters. This field does not

auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special

characters, it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Provide the details of the naming convention.

5.

To create the record, click Add. This brings you back to the Naming

Conventions tab.

In addition to having identifiers, you can specify descriptive names. The

application uses the value in the Separator field to separate the identifier and

the description. If this field is left blank, the colon (:) character becomes the

default separator.

6.

To reorder the records displayed in the the table, click the Sort Order value on

the Naming Conventions tab and enter a new value. The records take

precedence based on their order in the table.

7.

After reviewing the details provided, click Close.

The record that you create here populates into the Corresponding Identifiers section

when creating other records, such as Classes, Attributes, Units of Measure and

Enumerations.

CHAPTER 12 - Using Search

12

Using Search

Searching through the application is easy. Select the required filter criteria and run a

search.

The search capability provides:
· Filter Criteria

o Concept filter: Select this to select an entity type. The available options are

Class Library root, Functional Class, Physical Class, Document Class, General

Class, Attribute Class, Taxonomies, Units of Measure, Naming and Numbering,

Life Cycle, Lookup Lists, Extension Schemas and Configurations. If the check box

is cleared, all the entity types are selected.

o Modification filter: Select this to select an entity type. The available options

are Added, Deleted, Modified, Modified Ancestor and Modified Descendant. If

the check box is cleared, all the entity types are selected.

o Validation filter: Select this to select an entity type. The available options are

Errors, Warnings, Suggestions, Hints, Descendant Errors, Descendant Warnings,

Descendant Suggestions and Descendant Hints. If the check box is  cleared, all

the entity types are selected.

o Extensions filter: Select this to select an entity type. The available options are

Naming Conventions, Policy Rules and other third-party or plug-in validators. If

the check box is cleared, all the entity types are selected.

o Other filters: Select this to select an entity type. The only available option is

Translations. If the check box is cleared, all the entity types are selected.

· Filter Conditions

o ID

o Name

o Extensions

o Other

· Search Options

o Case Sensitive

o Regular Expressions (Regex)

o Invert Selection

CHAPTER 13 - Validating Standards

13

Validating Standards

Validation sets the rules for data coherence. AVEVA Information Standards Manager

enables you to validate a Standard against the available validators. You can execute a

validation from the Catalogue view. Validation action is only available when the Standard

is locked for editing.

After validating a Standard, the items in Standard are annotated with wavy lines of

different colours representing the severity of the issue. Items having multiple issues are

annotated with a wavy line in the red colour. A validation summary with the three most

severe issues is also displayed in the tooltip for the item. You can find the complete list of

issues under Show Validation Issues in the context menu for the item.

Validation annotations are only visual and will NOT be stored as a part of the Standard.

However, they may be exported into Microsoft Excel using AVEVA Standard ISM Format

and selecting the Include Validation Issues check box.

The following items can be annotated:

· Standard details
· Standard collections
· Entity tree
· Entity tab
· Entity details
· Entity properties
· Entity collections

The AVEVA ISM Naming and Numbering Rules and AVEVA ISM Policy Rules are available

out of the box. The other validators need to have the appropriate plug-in installed. To

check for available validators, either select Validate or click the information and help

 (

) icon and switch to the About tab.

Release 2.2.1

Page 69

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

The following is the list of validation annotations, sorted by the severest first:

Annotation

Explanation

Entity or one of its properties has one or more issues of
severity Error.

Entity has descendants, for example, a collection, with one
or more issues of severity Error.

Entity or one of its properties has one or more issues of
severity Warning.

Entity has descendants, for example, a collection, with one
or more issues of severity Warning.

Entity or one of its properties has one or more issues of
severity Suggestion.

Entity has descendants, for example, a collection, with one
or more issues of severity Suggestion.

Entity or one of its properties has one or more issues of
severity Hint.

Entity has descendants, for example, a collection, with one
or more issues of severity Hint.

CHAPTER 13 - Validating Standards

13.2

Managing Validation Rules

To manage validation rules:

1.

Click the Details button (

) on Catalogue and switch to the Class Library

Policy Configuration tab. You can use this tab to view and set the validation

rules. This is the default setting and helps preserve data integrity of the

Standard.

2.

Click Validation Rules to display a categorised list of rules for validation:

Note: Only those rules that validate for warnings and suggestions can be

modified. The values in the dropdown are hard-coded. Except for the user

with read-only privileges, all users can modify the validation rules by selecting

the required values from the dropdown.

3. Optionally, select the Compact View check box to display validation rules in a

summarised format.

4.

Expand a rule to view its underlying components. Select the required severity

of validation from the dropdown.

5.

Click Close.

The validation rules are now defined.

Note: Click the Restore to factory settings to restore the validation to the default setting.

13.3

Validating a Standard

To validate a Standard:

1.

2.

Click the Validate button.

In the Validate Class Library window, select from the list of validators by

selecting the corresponding check box.

Note: It is recommended that you select only one validator at a time and

resolve the identified issues. Repeat the validation process for all the

validators.

3. Optionally, select the Sign the Class Library on successful validation check

box. When you select the check box and the validators do not report any

error, the Standard is regarded as signed by the respective validator. This

means that the Standard is consistent with the rules set by the validator.

4.

Click the Validate button.

The application begins the validation process based on the validators you may

have selected.

9.

For details, hover over the squiggly line.

10. When the validation process is complete, read the validation confirmation

message, and then click OK.

It is recommended that a tagged release is validated and signed. The

information is available in the Approved By tab.

This completes the validation process. You can tag a Standard as Released. For

information, see Tagging a Release.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 14 - Managing Life Cycle

14

Managing Life Cycle

This Asset Life Cycle Management capability helps you configure object-level changes in

data for all its maturity levels. You can use AVEVA Information Standards Manager to

create Maturity Levels and Life Cycle Types and their data relationships with Classes and

Attributes.

What is a Life Cycle?

Throughout a project's execution, an asset passes through various life cycle stages,

starting from design conceptualisation to decommissioning. In information management

context, you may use Life Cycle Types to map that information from its generation to its

consumption. A Life Cycle Type can be associated with more than one Class and can

contain more than one Maturity Level. But, a Class can have only one Life Cycle Type.

What is a Maturity Level?

In the context of Life Cycle Management, the Maturity Levels establish definitive stages

for progression of information. You can use a Maturity Level to create a list of such levels

at which the information may change its form or requirements. A Maturity Level is

associated with Attribute. An Attribute can have more than one Maturity Level, while a

Maturity Level can apply to more than one Attribute and to more than one Life Cycle

Type.

14.1

Adding Life Cycle Types

To create a Life Cycle Type:

1.

2.

Right-click the Life Cycle Types folder and select Add Life Cycle Type.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Maturity Level. This field

supports alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If you do

not input any data, it auto-populates the ID.

c.

Describe what the Life Cycle Type contains. For example, the

duration or stages.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Life Cycle Type.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Life Cycle Type section, select a Concept from the

dropdown.

6.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

the Life Cycle Types are listed in a folder in the tree view.

A Life Cycle Type is created.

Note: To discontinue using a Life Cycle Type, select the Obsolete check box in the General

Properties section on the Details tab.

alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If you do not

input any data, it auto-populates the ID.

c.

Describe what the Maturity Level contains.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Maturity Level.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

the Presence field, the available options are:

Required: Select this for a mandatory value.

Preferred: Select this if it is good to have a value.

Optional: Select this if the absence of a value does not make a

difference.

Not Applicable

Creating a list of favourites items helps you access those items quickly. Therefore, if there

is a object that you use frequently, you can mark it to include it in your favourite list. Your

list of favourites appears on the global toolbar. The number of favourite objects in your

list also appears along side in a set of parentheses. For example,

. This

number changes as you continue to add or remove items from your list of favourites.

When clicking the Star icon (

) in the Content Discovery sidebar, the

Favourites Lists appears in the browsing area, which you can use to manage those lists.

Favourites lists are personal to the logged in user, and they display for only the active

Standard. You can create more than one list of favourites.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager provides you the capability to define aspect on

data objects. This provides a contextually relevant form of data governance by creating a

relationship between different data objects.

Class Groups.

alidation

onformance

Disciplines.

Types. This is primarily used for nomenclature mapping.

Transaction (Data Intents).

CHAPTER 17 - Implementing Configuration Schemas

17

Implementing Configuration Schemas

Most information management modules within AVEVA Information Standards Manager

are intrinsic. While all data is connected internally and has common properties, such as

identifier, name and description, some properties are exclusive to concepts, such as

attribute definition, group UoM class and data type. Any requirement for assigning

custom properties can be done using Extensions. You can use the information in this

chapter to manage extensions.

17.1

Understanding the Configuration Schema Model

A configuration schema is a structure of elements that can be visualised hierarchically. A

typical configuration schema includes the following elements: schema, extension point,

section, group, property and sub group. It is recommended to have only one schema per

Standard fragment.

· Schema: A schema element is the root in a configuration schema definition. It
contains a unique identifier that makes a schema reusable across multiple

Standards.

· Extension Point: A schema can have more than one extension point, each of

which may be a container for extensions that apply to one or more concepts. For

example, if a schema has extensions that apply to both Functional and Document

Classes, you can define those extensions in the same extension point.

· Section: The extension point can contain more than one section, each of which
may be a container for extensions that appear at a specific place or situation in

the user interface. The user interface for all extensions in a section will appear

together.

· Group: A section can contain more than one group, each of which may be a

container for one or more properties or sub groups.

· Properties: A group may or may not contain properties. Each property is a

representation of the uniqueness of the user interface.

· Sub Group: A group may contain sub groups. Each sub group can contain one or
more properties. A sub group is like a group, but it cannot contain any further

sub groups.

Release 2.2.1

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 18 - Adding a Language Translation

18

Adding a Language Translation

Adding a language translation is not restricted to providing the alternative for the object

name and description in other languages. You can use this feature to map objects in

different languages. This is not based on creating and managing nomenclature maps,

which are a collection of corresponding identifiers in naming conventions like ISO 15926.

To add a language:

1.

In the Culture Code dropdown, select the required code. This field is

mandatory.

2. Optionally, enter a name and its description.

3.

Click Add to create the record and continue to create more records.

Alternatively, click Add and Close to add the record and close the window.

Release 2.2.1

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 19 - Maintaining Taxonomies

19

Maintaining Taxonomies

A Taxonomy is a way of structuring Concept-based information without disturbing the

hierarchies laid within Concepts.

You can use taxonomies to catalogue the information contained within objects by

categorising them in groups and subgroups. Taxonomy provides a way of breaking down

Classes into arbitrary structures that contain a collection of Nodes. A node can further be

a collection of nodes.

For detailed understanding of taxonomies, see AVEVA Information Standards Manager

Importing from MS Excel document.

What is a Taxonomy Node?

A taxonomy node is a single point, or node, within the structured order of information. It

can contain a collection of nodes. The taxonomical hierarchy can be deep. Therefore, it is

recommended that the taxonomy identifiers be unique within a taxonomy node and none

of the node contain a white space (space between characters and words).

Release 2.2.1

Page 102

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 19 - Maintaining Taxonomies

19.1

Adding a Taxonomy

To create a taxonomy using the following procedure, ensure that you have the Creator

privilege and have the Standard locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click a Concept and select Add Taxonomy.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Taxonomy. This supports

alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of this Taxonomy.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Taxonomy.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Taxonomy section, select a value for Concept

from the dropdown. This value is applicable only for root-level Taxonomies,

such as Functional or Physical.

6.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

the Taxonomies will be listed in the tree view.

The Taxonomy is now defined.

Note: To discontinue using a Taxonomy Node, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section.

Release 2.2.1

19.2

To create a taxonomy using the following procedure, ensure that you have the Creator

privilege and have the Standard locked for editing:

1.

2.

Right-click a Taxonomy and select Taxonomy Node.

Enter the required information:

a.

Enter a unique identification for the Taxonomy Node. This supports

alphanumeric characters, and does not auto-increment.

Note: Whilst the application accepts white spaces and special characters,

it is not recommended to use them.

b.

Enter a name for the identification in this optional field. If left blank, it

shows the ID as the name. This field accepts alphanumeric characters.

c.

Describe the purpose of this Taxonomy Node.

3.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close. This brings you to the Details tab of that

Taxonomy Node.

4.

In the Corresponding Identifiers section on the Details tab, enter a

nomenclature map. You can enter more than one nomenclature map. For

information, see Implementing Corresponding Identifiers.

5.

In the Properties Specific to Taxonomy Node section, select a category or

discipline taxonomy, if available, in the Taxonomy field and a parent taxonomy

in the Ancestor field. However, if the extensions are suffixed with an

exclamation mark (!), then the Ancestor field appears empty. For a Class

Concept, this section appears as Properties Specific to Permissible <Class

Concept> Class.

6.

Enter a value in the Sort Order field. This value represents the order in which

the Taxonomy Nodes are listed in the tree view.

The Taxonomy Node is now created.

Notes:

· To discontinue using a Taxonomy Node, select the Obsolete check box in the

General Properties section on the Details tab.

· Ensure that the taxonomy name does not contain the pipe character (|). Out of
multiple segments delimited by the pipe character, the first segment is the

taxonomy identifier, while the successive segments are node identifiers.

Release 2.2.1

Page 104

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Information Standards Manager User Guide

CHAPTER 19 - Maintaining Taxonomies

19.3

Adding Permissible Classes

To add a Permissible Class on a Taxonomy Node:

1. Open the required Taxonomy Node and switch to the Classes tab.

2.

Click the Add (

) button. You can also right-click a taxonomy node and

select Add Permissible Class.

3.

In the Add Permissible Class window, select a permissible Class from the

dropdown.

4.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close.

19.4

Assigning Default Attribute Values

Assigning default value on a node ensures that within some consuming application, you

can use a value that you have defined using extensions.

To assign an Attribute's default value:

1. Open the required Taxonomy Node and switch to the Default Attribute Values

2.

3.

tab.

Click the Add (

) button.

In the Add Attribute Default Value window, select the required values in the

Attribute, Measure Unit and Value drop-down menus:

Attribute: The list of Attributes is derived based on the Discipline.

Measure Unit: This field is mandatory if the Value field is left blank.

Value: This field is mandatory if the Measure Unit field is left blank.

4.

To create the record and continue to create more records, click Add.

Alternatively, click Add and Close.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

Welcome to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide.

This chapter explains how this guide is organised and introduces other sources of

information that can help you.

1.1

About this Guide

This document covers the system administration functions and features provided for

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper™ and AVEVA Change Manager™. It contains detailed information

about the following:

· Add New Users, remove User Profiles and manage existing User Profiles.
· Functionality provided to each User Roles and add Members to existing Roles.
· Upload or update the Class Library.
· Manage, edit and add Reasons.
· Create and edit the Systems, Areas, Contractors and Disciplines associated to an

Asset.

1.2

Summary of Chapters

Chapter Name

Product Overview

Getting Started

Role Management

User Management

Class Library Administration

Manage Reasons

Description

Gives an overview of AVEVA Change Manager and
AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

Explains how to log on, access the Home Page and
Administration page. It also provides an overview of the
functions available on the Administration page.

Explains how to view available Roles and add/remove
users from existing Roles.

Shows you how to create a new user,
activate/deactivate User Profiles and manage the
information in existing User Profiles.

Explains the steps you have to perform to upload or
upgrade Class Library definition.

Shows you how to manage and edit existing Reasons.
You also learn how to create a new Reason.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)

the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the

status of the incident.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This chapter gives an overview of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

2.1

About AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper is a part of AVEVA’s Information Management solution and a key

component of AVEVA’s implementation of the Digital Information Hub concept. It

provides the essential function of loading information into the Digital Information Hub

and ensuring that this information complies with industry and operating standards.

For engineering contractors, this saves the time and cost overhead involved in identifying

and correcting non-compliant information and creates a platform for informed decision

making. It is a key enabler for streamlined handover of high-quality asset information to

an Owner Operator. For asset operators, it ensures that information created or updated

throughout the asset’s working life remains trustworthy to support safe and efficient

operation. In combination with AVEVA Change Manager, it is also the foundation for a

legacy data clean-up program. AVEVA NET Gatekeeper integrates directly with AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard™ and is capable of automatically importing thousands of data

objects in a single upload.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper validates the integrity of incoming information against

configurable, defined criteria, whether applicable industry standards such as ISO 15926 or

enterprise-specific ones. Levels of validation compliance can be defined according to

appropriate criteria. Non-compliant data can be rejected automatically or accepted for

import but flagged for review and resolution. This enables bulk information uploads to be

performed efficiently and any errors or inconsistencies managed by exception, providing

robust control of data quality without unnecessary hindrance to workflows. AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper also enables phased information handover from contractor to operator by

enabling efficient business processes during both project execution and asset operations,

by monitoring automated data uploads.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper provides not only information validation, but valuable additional

capabilities, including:

· A formal process for information acceptance by an information manager.
· Streamlined data loading through the identification and loading of only

information which is new or changed since the previous import.

· Full reporting of compliant and non-compliant information.
· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

Release 5.0.11

Page 8

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.2

About AVEVA Change Manager

AVEVA Change Manager is part of AVEVA’s Information Engineering category of products

within the AVEVA Enterprise portfolio. It provides the essential means for robust and

visible management of the continual changes to information in the digital asset which

accompany the many changes – large and small – made to the physical asset during its

lifecycle.

Throughout an asset’s working life, both in normal operations and during projects such as

brownfield modifications or data clean-up exercises, many different users need to access

and change technical information that originates from a variety of sources. Not only must

this information remain both accurate and available, it must be maintained so by the

rigorous control of any changes made to it. Achieving this requires standardised tools,

business processes and procedures for controlling changes to information and

synchronising them across multiple business systems.

AVEVA Change Manager enables Owner Operators to optimise, control and monitor their

change management process for all technical information. It can be customised and

tailored to your needs. Combined with AVEVA NET, the solution highlights information

changes to its users, enabling better-informed decision making. Implemented as a

combined solution with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, users are able to validate new and legacy

data against configurable, predefined information standards before making controlled

updates using AVEVA Change Manager. By assuring information integrity, AVEVA Change

Manager delivers increased operational efficiency and regulatory compliance on all types

of plants.

Release 5.0.11

Page 9

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

The key features of AVEVA Change Manager:

· Full management of changes to technical information created by Modification

Projects and work packages.

· Reservation of new Tags and Documents resulting from changes entered directly

in AVEVA Change Manager or by an upload process.

· Validation of all data against information handover specifications, external

application registers and code tables.

· Support for ISO 15926.
· Class library driven, multi-lingual.
· Efficient information search functions.
· Fast-track capability for expediting urgent changes without the need for a formal

approval process.

· Configurable best-practice workflows to support complex business processes.
· Integration with AVEVA NET for browsing, navigating, contextualising and

visualising the entire information asset.

· Integration with Enterprise Asset Management (EAM), Document Management

(DM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, whether AVEVA’s or third-

party.

· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

· Role-based user access control, enabling internal and external organisations to

manage large quantities of information in an orderly manner.

· Full audit trail of all changes.
· Better-informed decision making, leading to greater efficiency in both routine

asset operations and brownfield projects.

Release 5.0.11

Page 10

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3

Getting Started

This chapter is an introduction if you are just starting out as an AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or

AVEVA Change Manager Administrator. This chapter is useful if you have upgraded to the

latest released version, or you are learning to administer an existing installed version of

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager.

This chapter includes information about the following topics:

·

·

·

·

·

Log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager.

Access the Home Page.

Summary of the basic page components.

Access the Administration page.

Functions and menu items available on the Administration page.

3.1

Assumptions and Prerequisites

Assumptions

This document assumes that AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager have

been installed and deployed by following the instructions provided in the AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide. To upgrade to the latest

version of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager refer to AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide.

Prerequisites

In preparation for using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager you have to

fulfill the following prerequisites:

· Class Library is uploaded into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager

and it contains the definitions for the required Assets.

· Reference Data are available: Assets, Systems, Disciplines, Areas.
· Web Config file located in the following folder is modified:

· C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.11\Gatekeeper\Customer\Website

· Application Profile (Owner Domain) is configured:

· <Application

Folders>\Customer\OwnerDomain\Config\TIM.ApplicationFactory.
xml

Release 5.0.11

Page 11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

· Application Profile (Application Domain) is configured:

· <Application

Folders>\ApplicationDomain\Config\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml

· WDA System Application configuration file is modified:
· TIM.WDA.SystemApplication.Config.xml

3.2

Start and Log on

The following table details the software requirements for running AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

or AVEVA Change Manager.

Client Software Requirements

Component

Operating systems

Requirement
· Microsoft® Windows® 8.1
· Microsoft® Windows® 10

Web browsers

· Microsoft® Internet Explorer® 9, 10, 11
· Microsoft® Edge®
· Google Chrome™ browser

To start and log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Use a web browser to enter the appropriate URL for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

or AVEVA Change Manager.

2. On the log on page enter your User Name and Password. If your user

credentials are already saved on the log on page, click Login.

3.

To change the language and user locale settings, expand Options and then

select a value from the following drop-down lists:
· Language
· Format date/time & currency

4.

To save your user credentials in the web browser so that you do not have to

re-enter them every time you log on, select the Remember me option.

5.

Click Login.

Note: This language and user locale settings changes the language of the pages in AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper and user locale for a single session. If you want to keep the same settings

across sessions, you need to edit the Manage My Profile page after logging on.

For more information about initial log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change

Manager, see the Upload the Class Library step in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager Installation Guide.

Release 5.0.11

Page 12

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.3

Home Page

The AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Home Page is the first page you

see after logging on. If no Class Library upgrades are in progress the first page all users,

including Administrators, are redirected to is the Home page. This page is used to

facilitate navigation to other pages on AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager and is divided into four area, each containing different menus that list the

functions, tasks and links. The Home Page and the functions available on it can be

different for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager, some of the functions

are configurable and depend on a user's role. In this guide only menu items related to

administration are covered, that is the Other Tasks menu.

3.3.1

Basic Page Components

When using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager, you should be familiar

with the following page components:

Basic Page Components

Component

Description

Title Bar

The title bar at the top of each pages indicates the name of the
page or the particular step the user has to perform. It also displays
the AVEVA logo, and the link for Home Page, Logout and Role
assigned to the user.

Information Icons

These display a summary of a menu or functionality when you
move the pointer over them.

Reset button

To discard any unsaved changes and stay on the current page, click
Reset. If you click Reset after clicking Save or Close, you cannot
discard the changes on a page.

Save button

To save the changes and stay on the current page, click Save.

Close button

Cancel button

Back button

To save any changes that may have been made and return to the
Administration page or previous page at the same time, click
Close.
To return to the Administration page if you do not want to make
any changes on a page, for example, specific items are not
displayed or you do not want to perform any particular function,
task, step, you can also click Close.

If you do not want to perform any particular function, task, step,
process on a page and return to the Administration page or
previous page, click Cancel.

To return to the previous page or step, click Back. The Back button
is available on the pages where you need to make a selection or
the pages are part of multi-step wizard.

Release 5.0.11

Page 13

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

Component

Description

Confirm button

To confirm/accept/verify a particular action on the final page/step
of the wizard, click Confirm.

Next button

Search field

To continue to the next page/step of the wizard when your
confirmation is required, click Next.

If you want to search for a particular item or information in the
database, you can enter a query in the single-line text box and click
Search.

Steps bar

If you want to check which step you are on and how many steps
are left for you to complete in the wizard, check the Steps bar.

Message/Notificati
on Bar

The message or notification bar under the Title bar displays helpful
hints, messages or notifications. It displays an error message when
you encounter an error while performing a step or process.

3.4

Access the Administration page

The Administration page is the entry point to the system administration functionality

available for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager.

To access the Administration page:

1. On the Home Page, on the Other Tasks menu, under Administration, click

System Settings.

2. On the Administration page, under Available Tasks the functions that are

available for an Administrator are grouped under the following menus:
· User Profiles
· The Class Library
· Reasons
· Reference Data

Release 5.0.11

Page 14

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Role Management

4

Role Management

To manage individual users' access to functions, features and tasks in the application, the

user privileges are organised by user Roles. A Role is a set of permissions that are granted

to a user, thus controlling access to application modules and workflow processes. Each

Role also determines the authorisation granted to a user to perform particular functions

and operations. You can log on with read-only access into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and

AVEVA Change Manager even when no TIM-related Roles are assigned to you.

All users that have a specific Role assigned inherit the privileges of that Role. Since any

user may have more than one Role, the user inherits the set of the privileges of those

Roles.

The Role Management gives you access to:

· View available Roles
· Add/Remove users for existing Roles

Release 5.0.11

Page 15

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Role Management

4.1

Role Access Rights and Permissions

The following table provides a summary of Roles in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager:

Role Name

Administrator

Super User

Responsibility

Members of this Role are the only users who have access to
Administration page. Members of this Role can manage users,
Role, Reasons (for Void and Cancel Verification) as well as
update the Class Library version.

Members of this Role have access to Manage Job pages to
review the status and the progress for all the Bulk Upload Jobs,
and they are authorised to update Tags and Documents by
means of Fast Track functionality.

Information Manager Members of this Role are the only users that are allowed to do
the verification process. This Role have access to the following
process: Complete Verification and Cancel Verification.
Members of this Role can approve and reject approval of
Modification Projects in AVEVA Change Manager.

Contractor

Members of this Role can access all Working Sets (in AVEVA
NET Gatekeeper) and manage all Working Sets and
Modification Projects (in AVEVA Change Manager). A
reference to a Modification Project is mandatory when a new
Working Set is being created. Members of this Role can upload
Tags and Documents using Bulk Upload functionality.

Allocator

Members of this Role are the only users that are permitted to

allocate and check in Tag or Document Numbers in AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper. The role is not available in AVEVA Change

Manager.

Note: Members who have not been assigned a Role are given the Role of a Read-only

user. This Role enables such members to log on and only view the data and documents

within the Working Sets.

Page 17

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Role Management

4.2

View Role Details

To view the details of the pre-defined Roles:

1. Go to the Administration page. On the User Profiles menu, click Roles.

2. On the User Roles page, under Available User Roles, click a Role.

3. On the User Role Details page, check the following information:

· Detailed Information. Provides the Role name and a brief Description about

a specific Role.

· Members. Provides the list of Members under a specific Role.

4.3

Add Role Member

To add a member to a Role or assign a Role to an existing active user:

1. Open the User Roles page, under Available User Roles, click a Role.

2. On the User Role Details page, under Members, click Add Members.

3. Under Users that are not Members of this Role, select one or multiple Display

Name of users that should be added as Members of a specific Role.

4.

Click Confirm.

The user is added to the list of users who are Members of this Role, the user has access to

the functionality which is available for the users with that role assigned.

4.4

Remove Role Member

To remove a Member from a specific Role:

1. Open the User Roles page, under Available User Roles, click a Role.

2. On the User Role Details page, under Members, click Remove for a one or

multiple users.

3.

Click Save.

Release 5.0.11

Page 18

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 5 - User Management

5

User Management

User Management is used to add users that exist in the configured Active Directory (AD)

groups into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager. Access to the functions,

features and tasks in the application are controlled by the Roles the user is a Member of.

The User Profile therefore also contains User Role Membership. In addition to a user

existing in the Active Directory, the same user must also exist in AVEVA NET Workhub as

well. This is another pre-requisite for user account creation in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and

AVEVA Change Manager. This requirement does not prevent you from creating the user

but the newly added user are not be able to log on into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA

Change Manager unless the user is authorised in AVEVA NET Workhub as well.

When integration with AVEVA WorkMate™ is enabled, the role assignment in AVEVA

WorkMate overrides the role assignment in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager. Consequently, even if no TIM-related Roles are assigned to the user in AVEVA

WorkMate, the members can still view the records in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager in the read-only mode. When integration is not enabled with AVEVA

WorkMate, and no Roles are assigned in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager, the members can still view the record in the read-only mode.

The User Management gives you access to:

· Create new users
· Activate/Deactivate User Profiles
· Manage the information in existing User Profiles

Release 5.0.11

Page 19

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 5 - User Management

5.1

Add User

Active Directory Groups

You must ensure that before users are added to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA

Change Manager, they should be added to the relevant Active Directory (AD) group.

Follow your Company or Plant IT Policy for requesting membership to Active Directory

security groups.

Add a New User

To add a new user for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Got to the Administration page and then on the User Profiles menu, click

Users.

2. On the Users list page, under Available Users, click New User.

3. On the New User page, under Account Information, enter User Name. This is

a required field.

Note: By default, the Is User from Active Directory option is selected.

4.

Click Next.

5. Under Details Information, enter the following details:

· Display Name
· E-mail

6.

Click Confirm to add the user.

7. Under Details Information, enter or change the following details:

· Phone Number
· User Interface Language
· Format Date/Time & Currency
· Time Zone
· Select one or multiple Role Name under Roles.

8.

Click Close to save the changes and return to the User Details page.

Note: If you want to activate any previously inactivated user or old User Profile, you have

to follow the similar steps for adding a new user.

Release 5.0.11

Page 20

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 5 - User Management

The User Name entered on the Account Information page is authenticated against both

the Active Directory and existing users in the application database. If the entered User

Name does not exist in the Active Directory or matches any existing user record, an error

message is displayed under the navigation bar. You have to select the appropriate

language and time zone settings that matches the specific cultures or region of a specific

user. To understand the functionality assigned to each Role, see Access Rights for User

Roles.

5.2

View User Profile

To view the User Profile of existing user:

1. On the Administration page, on the User Profiles menu, click Users.

2. On the Users list page, under Available Users, click a Display Name to open

the User Details page.

3. Under Manage User, check the Details Information and Roles assigned to a

User Profile.

5.3

Update User Profile

To update specific information in the User Profile of an existing user:

1. Go to the Users list page, and then click a Display Name to open the User

Details page.

2. On the User Details page, under Manage User, check the Details Information

and Roles assigned to a User Profile.

3. Under Details Information, you can update/edit the following details:

· Display Name. This is a required field.
· E-mail. This is a required field.
· Phone Number
· User Interface Language
· Format Date/Time & Currency
· Time Zone

4. Under Roles, you can select single or multiple Role Name to assign new

permissions and access levels.

5.

Click Save.

Release 5.0.11

Page 21

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 5 - User Management

5.4

Remove User

Removal of a specific user from AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager

means inactivating the User Profile. The user continues to exist in Active Directory but is

not able to log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager. The User Profile

is not deleted but it is not accessible from AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change

Manager. This gives an opportunity to reactivate the user using add New User

functionality.

To remove a user from AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Go to the Administration page. On the User Profiles menu, click Users.

2. On the Users list page, under Available Users, click Remove for a one or

multiple users.

3.

Click Save, to inactivate the User Profile of a specific user.

Release 5.0.11

Page 22

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Class Library Administration

6

Class Library Administration

This chapter explains the steps you have to perform to populate AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

or AVEVA Change Manager with the newly generated Class Library.

Note: The Class Library file for upload should be provided by Class Library Managers in

your company/plant. It is recommended to not introduce any manual changes to the

provided file since it should be done in a controlled way.

6.1

Install Updates

This function gives you access to the tasks for uploading and installing updates to the

Class Library.

You can view the following details of the installed Class Library:

· Name
· Description
· Version
· Version Date
· # Attributes
· # Attribute Groups
· # Functional Classes
· # UoM Groups
· # Assets
· # Languages

Release 5.0.11

Page 23

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Class Library Administration

Upload a Class Library

To upload a new or update an existing Class Library:

1. On the Administration page, on the The Class Library menu, click Install

Updates.

2. Under Updates, click Upload updates.

3. On the Upload Updates dialog box, click Browse to select the file containing

the Class Library definition. The upper limit for the size of the uploaded file can

be configured, by default the maximum file size limit is 100MB.

4.

Click OK. If you want to cancel the upload of a Class Library definition, click

Abort. You see an error message if you try to upload the Class Library

Definition of an earlier version or if the file is not in a supported format.

5.

Check the details about the Class Library Definition and then click Update the

Class Library to initiate the upload.

6.

You see the progress of the upload in the Updates the Class Library dialog

box.

7.

After the upload is complete, the Updates the Class Library dialog box closes

and the successful update message is displayed.

You can refer to the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation

Guide to understand the process of installing a new or upgraded version of the Class

Library.

To access the user guide:

1. Go to the Administration page, on the The Class Library menu, click User

Guide.

2. On the Installing Class Library user guide page, click the links under Table of

Contents to understand the process.

Release 5.0.11

Page 24

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Manage Reasons

7

Manage Reasons

This chapter explains how an Administrator can manage the lists of reasons that the

users are prompted to provide when doing one of the following: voiding a Working Set,

voiding a Modification Project or cancelling the verification of the Working Set. It also

describes how to create a new Reason of the corresponding type.

7.1

Create Reason

To create a new Reason:

1. Go to the Administration page, on the Reasons menu, click Manage Reason.

2. On the Reason List page, under Available Reasons, click New Reason.

3. On the New Reason page, under Details Information do the following:

a.

b.

c.

Enter the Reason code. This is a required field.

Enter the Description about the Reason.

Select one of the following from the new Reason Type drop down list

(this is a required field):
· Cancel Verification
· Voiding
· Voiding Mod. Project

d.

Select Active. By default the Active status is selected.

4.

Click Confirm, to add a new Reason.

You can view the newly created Reason on the Reason List page. The newly created

Reason can be selected from the list on the confirmation page when voiding the Working

Set and Modification Project or when cancelling the verification of the Working set.

7.2

View Reason Details

To view the details of a Reason:

1. Go to the Administration page, on the Reasons menu, click Manage Reason.

2. On the Reason List page, under Available Reasons, click a Reason.

3. On the Reason Details page, under Details Information check the following:

· Reason code
· Description
· Type

Release 5.0.11

Page 25

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Manage Reasons

· Active status
· Created By
· Created On
· Updated By
· Updated On

7.3

Update Reason Details

To update the details of a Reason:

1. Open the Reason List page, under Available Reasons, click a Reason code.

2. On the Reason Details page, under Details Information you can update the

following:
· Reason code. This is a required field.
· Description
· Type
· Select or clear Active status.

3.

Click Save.

7.4

Activate or Deactivate Reason

To activate or deactivate a Reason:

1. Open the Reason List page.

2. Under Available Reasons, for a single or multiple Reason codes and

select/clear Active to activate/deactivate a Reason.

3.

Click Save.

Users can select only Active Reasons from the list on the confirmation page for Voiding

Working Set and Modification Project or Cancel Verification of Working Set. You can also

activate/deactivate a Reason on the Reason Details page.

7.5

Delete Reason

To delete a Reason:

1. Open the Reason List page.

2. Under Available Reason, click Delete for a single or multiple Reason code.

3.

Click Save.
