AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 1 - About this Document

1

About this Document

This bulletin describes the new and enhanced functionality available in AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper™ and AVEVA Change Manager™ 5.0.11. It gives an overview of the major

changes, which are described in full in the User Manuals that accompany this release.

Release 5.0.11

Page 5

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 2 - About this Software Release

2

2.1

About this Software Release

Introducing AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change
Manager

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager 5.0.11 is the new release of AVEVA’s

Information Management software and a fundamental component of AVEVA’s Digital

Information Hub solutions for Progressive Handover and Asset Life Cycle Information

Management.

This release delivers several enhancements and numerous other quality improvements.

The details of these changes are outlined in the sections that follow.

2.2

How to Install AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change
Manager

For instructions on how to install AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager

5.0.11 and associated components please refer to the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager Installation Guide 5.0.11, which is available from the AVEVA product

support site. Before installing the software, ensure that your system meets the minimum

System Requirements. For information, see General Product Information.

2.3

Components Delivered by this Release

The following software and user documentation are delivered with this release.

2.3.1

Software

The  AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager 5.0.11 release comprises the

following software components:

· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper 5.0.11
· AVEVA Change Manager 5.0.11

Release 5.0.11

Page 6

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 2 - About this Software Release

2.3.2

User Documentation

New versions of the following documentation are available at this release:

· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin 5.0.11
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide 5.0.11
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide 5.0.11
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide

5.0.11

· AVEVA Change Manager User Guide 5.0.11
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide 5.0.11

2.4

Compatibility

The release is compatible with the following AVEVA products:

· AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.8 with the latest fix release
· AVEVA Information Standards Manager 2.2
· AVEVA WorkMate™ 10.1.2.1

Release 5.0.11

Page 7

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 3 - Introduction

3

Introduction

3.1

About AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper ensures that information is accepted in a controlled way and that

it complies with the defined quality standards. This is a necessary first step in ensuring

that critical information assets are standardised, complete and trustworthy.

For engineering contractors, this saves the time and cost overhead involved in identifying

and correcting the non-compliant information and creates a platform for informed

decision making. It is a key enabler for streamlined handover of high-quality asset

information to an Owner Operator. For asset operators, it ensures that the information

created or updated throughout the asset’s working life remains trustworthy to support

safe and efficient operation. In combination with AVEVA Change Manager, it is also the

foundation for a legacy data clean-up program. AVEVA NET Gatekeeper integrates directly

with AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard and is capable of automatically importing

thousands of data objects in a single upload.

AVEVA Change Manager provides the essential means for robust and visible management

of the continual changes to information in the digital asset that accompany many

changes, big and small, made to a physical asset during its lifecycle.

Throughout an asset’s working life, both in normal operations and during projects, such

as brownfield modifications or data clean-up exercises, many different users need to

access and change technical information that originates from a variety of sources. This

information must remain both accurate and available at the point of need through the

rigorous control of any changes required of it. Achieving this requires standardised tools,

business processes and procedures for controlling changes to information and

synchronising them across multiple business systems.

Release 5.0.11

Page 8

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 4 - Enhancements

4

Enhancements

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager 5.0.11 is a major new version of

AVEVA’s Information Management solution for engineering and operations. It delivers

significant new and enhanced features for efficient and effective search, navigation and

visualisation of asset and project information.

The following sub-sections include the improvements.

4.1

Concurrent partial update of same Document in multiple active
Working Sets

In AVEVA Change Manager, you can now work on the same Document added to two or

more active Working Sets at the same time even if it is checked out in one of them. This is

also possible to perform Partial Update to update the Project Archive with information.

4.2

Support of UTF-16 characters in Code fields

All descriptive fields such as the ones listed below now support UTF-16 symbols in the

Object view:

· Tag Code (Tag#)
· Document Code (Document#)
· Parent Tag Code (Parent Tag#)
· Contractor Code
· Area Code
· System Code
· Discipline Code
· Tag\Document Reference Code

4.3

Use of latest Class Library version in N&N allocation & validation
components

When updating the Class Library, the latest N&N template updates must be used by the

N&N allocation and validation components.

Release 5.0.11

Page 9

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 4 - Enhancements

4.4

Appearance of AreaCode attribute while reserving Tag

Selected Area value (that is, the attribute AreaCode) does not disappear while reserving

Tag if the Back button is pressed and another template is selected to create new Tags.

4.5

Visibility of N&N attribute validation errors on UI

When a Tag/Document has two or more N&N templates assigned and is being validated,

with an unknown attribute value, you can view the validation errors, if any, on the UI.

4.6

Improved Greenfield validation process

In AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, we have improved the Greenfield validation process, which

has optimized the performance.

4.7

Prevention of Tag/Document number reservation by Contractors

In AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, Administrators can now prevent Tag/Document number

reservation through Greenfield bulk upload process.

4.8

Optimization of Working Set validation error collection process

The Working Set Validation Error Report generation mechanism has been redesigned to

speed up the Working Set validation error collection process.

From the user perspective, the PDF reports now work faster as they do not contain as

many details as the CSV reports.

4.9

Upload Tags/Documents/References in AVEVA Change Manager

You can now upload Tags/Documents/References to new Working Set using Upload

Tags\Documents\References function in AVEVA Change Manager with no need of

specifying the Working Set Number.

Release 5.0.11

Page 10

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 4 - Enhancements

4.10

Configurable Columns in Export of Excel in AVEVA Change
Manager

While exporting Excel in AVEVA Change Manager, you can now configure the columns

through the Class Library using AVEVA Information Standards Manager™.

4.11

Support for the Excel format during Tags/Documents/References
upload

While uploading Tags/Documents/References, support for .xlsx format is made available

now.

4.12

Dividing the Working Set workflow for Tags and Docs

An Administrator can now enable Tag/Document partial check-in for the Greenfield data

upload. That in turn enables Information Managers to ensure that Tags or Documents,

without critical errors, can be checked in by the system automatically while the rest data

is left checked out or reserved for further manual verification.

4.13

Russian language support to the list of available languages for
users

The support of the Russian language is added to the list of  languages available with login

and user profile.

4.14

Retaining files in the Working Set when check-in attempt fails

In AVEVA Change Manager, the files remain in the Working Set when the check-in

attempt fails against a DMS so that you can try checking in again.

4.15

Changes made to the WDA System Application Service

The WDA System Application service now supports secure SMTP over TLS in order to

encrypt user or system sensitive information being sent to the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

and AVEVA Change Manager Administrators.

Release 5.0.11

Page 11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 4 - Enhancements

4.16

Support of Class Library enumerations

Now AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager supports Class Library

enumerations (lists with more than 255 characters) within Working Set-to-Excel and

Excel-to-Working Set transformation.

Release 5.0.11

Page 12

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 5 - Upgrade Considerations

5

Upgrade Considerations

5.1

Supported Platforms

The hardware and software requirements for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager are updated for the 5.0.11 release. For full details, refer to AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide 5.0.11.

5.2

Configuration

Back up all configuration files of an older version before upgrading to version 5.0.11. To

modify the configuration files using the information available in this document, see

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide. Then, to

redeploy with the version 5.0.11, refer to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager Installation Guide 5.0.11. The database upgrade scripts included with the

version 5.0.11 help you preserve the existing data and initialise or map the new fields.

5.3

Customisation

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager support wide-ranging configuration

options but customisation of page layouts, page text and images, site colours and icons is

not supported. Any customisations, particularly those that rely on edits to the supplied UI

pages, may not be supported on upgrade.

Release 5.0.11

Page 13

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 6 - Known Issues

6

Known Issues

The following sub-sections include details of the known faults and outstanding issues in

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager 5.0.11.

6.1

Restrictions for Allocator role

Currently, if users are granted the role of Allocator, as with Contractor, they must not be

granted any other role from within AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

6.2

Error message during validation of Working Set

When validating a Working Set where the Modification Project value is available but is

not set, the following message may be received:

“The value'' provided for the Attribute '3834 - Mod. Project Code' does not match

the code of the Mod. Project ''which the current Working Set is associated with”.

This message is cleared when tag is re-validated.

6.3

Document Not Moved to Target Working Set

A Document with file(s) reserved in the Working Set, which already has checked out Tags

or Documents, can be moved only once to a new Working Set; but after that, it cannot be

moved anymore due to the internal restrictions in AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard.

6.4

Failure in Updates to the Tag Description on Document-Tag
Reference

When a document is reserved and has a reference to an existing tag that has been

checked out after the reference was created, an update of the tag's description is not

reflected in the reference's description.

This also happens for the reserved tag and the existing document and the reference

between them.

Release 5.0.11

Page 14

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

CHAPTER 7 - Withdrawn Functionality

7

Withdrawn Functionality

No functionality in the previous version of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager has been withdrawn in 5.0.11.

Release 5.0.11

Page 15

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager User Bulletin

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

This section gives basic information about this guide, training and support.

1.1

About this Guide

This guide describes how to work with AVEVA NET Dashboard to:

· Browse, search, retrieve and view the data stored in multiple formats.
· Categorise and group the data for easy access later.
· Export the data and work on it from your local machine.
· Sort and view the pre-configured reports.
· Print the documents and reports.

1.2

Audience

This guide is for all personnel requiring access to trusted information about the

engineering project or operating asset.

1.3

Related Documents

· AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
· AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site

1.4

Product and Training Information

For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of

AVEVA's website (http://www.aveva.com/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA

Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

Release 5.1.8

Page 7

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.5

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact

AVEVA Helpdesk (https://avevahelpdesk.aveva.com), and register a support incident.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)

and the details of the incident. We will endeavour to fix the software issue or consider

the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the

status of the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA

Helpdesk for which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact

the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

Release 5.1.8

Page 8

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This section gives an overview of AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard™.

2.1

About AVEVA NET

AVEVA NET is a powerful web-based solution that supports users in organising, validating

and collaborating on asset data and documents irrespective of source and location,

providing the ability to link project teams situated in various locations via one unique

application.

AVEVA NET allows information of any type and from any source to be stored and viewed

in a secure environment. Related information is automatically linked together to add

context, can be compared, navigated, visualised and reported upon to provide a single

environment to view and understand information from multiple systems.

AVEVA NET is a necessity for any organisation involved in designing, building or operating

complex engineering assets. It enables organisations to maintain a digital replica of their

asset throughout its lifecycle, and enables the information therein to be accessible to

support the many business processes which make up the life cycle of such an asset.

AVEVA NET Dashboard™ is a browser-based application for accessing the engineering

data stored in AVEVA NET Workhub. They are fundamental components of AVEVA’s

Project Data Visualisation and Asset Visualisation Solutions.

Release 5.1.8

Page 9

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.2

AVEVA NET Dashboard at a Glance

The following illustration shows an example configuration of AVEVA NET Dashboard.

Important: The AVEVA NET Dashboard Home Page and features available in the panels

are configurable. If you are unable to access any of the features described in this guide,

contact your System Administrator for configuring it and granting access rights. For more

information about configuring the AVEVA NET Dashboard Home Page and its features,

refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Release 5.1.8

Page 10

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

· Sets
· Query Forms
· Export
Note: Based on the configuration settings, any of these
features can be hidden or disabled.

Displays tabs for the items, tags or documents you open in
Dashboard.
All the tabs in the Tabs Panel can be closed after use,
except the Dashboard tab.

A drop-down arrow, if clicked displays all the open tabs.
Enables navigating to a tab, or closing any or all of the tabs.

The main area of Dashboard that is customisable, based on
your organisational requirements.

Note: Any modifications to AVEVA NET Dashboard must
continue to display the original copyright statement, as
initially displayed in the 'out of the box' home page. It must
appear in the same place and in the original HTML style on
any new home pages hosted in AVEVA NET Dashboard.

Enables finding information using Search keywords (for
example, attributes, class and items), wildcard characters
or pre-configured filters (defined in the class library). See
also Using Search

Enables finding information, by expanding the nodes, and
then clicking the associated documents, items or tags. See
also Using Browse.

Enables an alternate form-based advanced search method
using search keywords, wildcard characters, or pre-
configured filters (defined in the class library). See also
Using Query Forms

Enables creating Export Definition.
Run or save exports using a specific Export Definition, for
each saved search.
See also Exporting Items and Managing Exports

Enables viewing reports pre-configured by your System
Administrator. See also Working with Reports

2.3

Accessing Dashboard Features

You can access AVEVA NET Dashboard in your local language, if configured. AVEVA NET

Dashboard is compatible with the following Web browsers:

Browser

Supported Version

Internet Explorer (IE)

11

Note: Only the latest version of IE 11 is supported with
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard. For more
information, see https://www.microsoft.com/en-
gb/windowsforbusiness/end-of-ie-support.

Google Chrome

 Latest

Your System Administrator configures the user roles and assigns access rights. You must

have appropriate access rights granted by your System Administrator to access the

Dashboard features.

Also, the Security Access Groups (SAGs) can allow or restrict your access rights. SAG is a

mechanism to control access to specific objects, allowing only its members to access

certain protected objects.

Note: For more information about the user roles, rights and groups, refer to the AVEVA

NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Release 5.1.8

Page 12

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Logging on to AVEVA NET Dashboard

3

Logging on to AVEVA NET Dashboard

By default, you can log on to AVEVA NET Dashboard without any prompt to enter

credentials, if:

· Your organisational domain network credentials are configured by your System

Administrator.

· You are accessing Dashboard from your organisation's network domain

computer.

However, if you are accessing Dashboard from outside the domain network of your

organisation, you must enter your domain credentials to log on.

To log on to AVEVA NET Dashboard:

1.

In your web browser, type the URL configured by your System Administrator

and press ENTER.

Note: A typical URL to access the Dashboard can be in the following format:

http://{myserver}:{myportnumber}/

Dashboard can be configured to use Secure Sockets Layer (SSL). If SSL is used,

you must prefix the URL with https in place of http.

When you are logging on from your organisation's network domain PC,  and a

Windows Security window opens prompting you to enter credentials, contact

your System Administrator to enable Automatic Logon in the browser security

settings.

On a VPN, you are prompted for credentials even if automatic logon is

switched ON, as your login credentials can be different from your domain

credentials.

2. When prompted, enter your Windows domain credentials, and then click OK.

A browser window opens displaying the Dashboard.

Release 5.1.8

Page 13

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

4

Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

After logging on to AVEVA NET Dashboard, you can navigate through its features in any

sequence of your choice, starting from the Global Navigation Panel. The following

sections explain the procedure for working with the features common to the panels that

open in Dashboard:

· Resizing the Panels
· Docking/Undocking Panels
· Changing the View Modes
· Maximising and Minimising Viewing Area
· Opening Items from Tab Panel
· Using Actions Menu in Panels

4.1

Resizing the Panels

If you click any of the features from the Global Navigation Panel, the associated panel

opens in the Dashboard:

· Search Panel
· Browse Panel
· Query Form Panel
· Sets Panel
· Reports Panel
· Export Panel

Note: The height of the panels conform to the height of the window displaying

Dashboard. However, you can resize the panel width.

To resize the panels:

1.

Rest the mouse pointer on the panel border.

2. When the pointer becomes a double-headed arrow, drag the pointer left or

right to move the split line.

The panel width is resized, providing you larger area for the Summary View or

Document View.

Note: The resized panel width remains the same through a session in

Dashboard, including when it is reopened. The panels have a default minimum

width that cannot be reduced further.

Release 5.1.8

Page 14

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

4.2

Docking/Undocking Panels

By default, the following panels open in a docked (

unpinned) state. Undocking the

panels by clicking

 enables a wider viewing area for an opened document. This action

auto-hides the original panel through which you navigate to open a document. Docking (

 pinning) enables you to keep the original navigation panel open, and view the

opened document side-by-side:

· Search Panel
· Browse Panel
· Query Form Panel
· Sets Panel
· Reports Panel
· Export Panel

To undock panels:

1.

Click

.

The panel is expanded.

To dock panels:

2.

Click

.

The panel is collapsed.

By docking a navigation panel, the tabs for opened documents shift to the

right in the Tab Panel.

Release 5.1.8

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

Note: You can resize the docked (

 pinned) navigation panel by resting the

mouse pointer on the panel border. When the pointer becomes a double-

headed arrow, drag the pointer left or right to move the split line. See also

Resizing the Panels.

4.3

Changing the View Modes

The

 menu enables you to view the documents and reports in any of the

following modes.

· Summary View
· Document View

Note: The documents can be SVG files (Scalable Vector Graphics/two-dimensional (2D)

Drawings), non-SVG files (for example, MS Office documents, DWGs, PDFs) or three-

dimensional digital simulations (3D Model).

Any item representing a tag, when clicked from Browse, Search, Query Forms, Sets or

Basket Panel automatically opens in the Summary View mode. The Summary View can be

configured to display information in the zones representing relationships, metadata or

characteristics. For example, it can be configured with the following zones:

· Details
· Key Attributes
· Attributes
· Related Items
· Related Documents
· Related Query Forms
· 2D View
· 3D View

Note: By default, a report opens in the Document View.

Release 5.1.8

Page 16

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

4.3.1

Viewing Documents in Summary View

Any item representing a document clicked from a documents folder initially opens in the

Document View. You can change the view mode from Document View to Summary View

using the

 option. The Summary View mode enables you to get an overview of a

document in zones that are configurable. For example, the zones can be:

· Details
· File Viewer
· Attributes
· Revisions
· Related Items

To view documents in the Summary View:

1.

2.

In the top-right of the Document View, click

.

From the View options, select Summary View.

The view mode changes to Summary View.

4.3.2

Viewing Documents in Document View

To view documents in Document View:

1.

2.

In the top-right of the Summary View, click

.

From the View options, select Document View.

The view mode changes to Document View.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

Default Viewers - Colours

The Document Viewers in Dashboard (such as the 3D VizStream viewer and the 2D SVG

viewer) use colours to indicate the highlighted or selected items. Using the Colours

dialog, you can specify default colours used to indicate these states.

To change a default colour:

1.

Click on the existing colour in the dialog.

A colour picker is displayed allowing you to choose a colour.

Note: In some viewers, you can override the default colours temporarily. The default

colours are reinstated after the current session ends.

4.4

Maximising and Minimising Viewing Area

By clicking

 at the top-right of the viewing areas in the Summary View, you can

maximise or minimise the viewing area.

Release 5.1.8

Page 18

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

4.5

Opening Items from Tab Panel

When a searched document is opened, it opens in a new tab in the Tabs Panel. Each tab is

represented with the ID of the opened item and its associated icon. You can open

multiple documents from your search results, and retain it for further viewing until you

close its tab in the Tabs Panel. By clicking each document's tab from the Tabs Panel, you

can:

· View one selected document at a time.
· Return to the previously viewed documents, and work on it again.

You can close a tab and its opened document by clicking

on a tab.

If you open a large number of items, navigation arrows appear at the left and right of the

Tab Panel. Clicking the drop-down arrow at the right end of the Tab Panel enables you to

select a document to view, from a list of all the open tabs. You can close a document

from the list by clicking

 or you can close all open documents by clicking Close all tabs.

4.6.2

Exporting Items

The Exporting Items option allows you to export one or more items (Documents and

associated files with metadata, Tags) from Dashboard to your local machine in a CSV file

format (.csv). If the items exported are more than 1000, a warning message appears.

By clicking the Export Items option from the

 menu, you can export items

selected from the following panels:

· Search Panel
· Browse Panel
· Sets Panel
· Related Items area in the Summary View
· Related Documents area in the Summary View
· Related Query Form area in the Summary View
· Query Form search results
· Related Items area in a Document Content Card
· Items displayed under the Contents tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Document

Content Card

· Items displayed under the Related Items tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Tag

Content Card

· Documents displayed under the Related Documents tab in a 3D Model/2D

Drawing Tag Content Card

· Basket Panel
· Reports displayed in the Document View

To export items:

1.

2.

In the panel from which you are exporting, select the item(s).

 Click the

 menu, and then click Export Items.

The Export window opens.

Page 23

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

3.

4.

5.

6.

7.

8.

9.

To export item metadata, select the Export Data check box.

In the Export Definition box, click the drop-down arrow, and then select an

Export Definition.

To include files in the export package, select the Export Files check box.

To expand the Select files section, click

 at the top-right of the Select files

section.

To filter the files shown in the Select files section, use the following filters:
· File ID
· File Type
· Document ID
· Document Type

Note: The Select Files section displays files associated with the first 100

documents, by default. A document can have more than one associated file.

Hence, a message appears at the top of the Select files section, displaying the

number limit for documents shown.

Select the check boxes for the files you want to export.

If you have expanded the Select files section by clicking

, click OK to return

to the Export window.

10.

In the Enter the reason for this export box, type the details.

The pre-configured export package output path appears at the bottom of the

Export window. For more information about configuring the output file

location, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

11. From the following two options, select the appropriate export method:

· Export to Server Folder: Exports the files to the export location as provided

in the Settings.

· Download to Client: Downloads the export files to the client.

Note:

· If you have the Run Export right, then you can export data through the

Actions panel.

· If you have the Run Export and Export To Server rights, then the Export to

the Server Folder option is visible to you.

· If you have the Run Export and Download to Client rights, then the

Download to Client export option is visible to you.

Release 5.1.8

Page 24

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Navigating in AVEVA NET Dashboard

12. Click Export.

You will see one of the following results corresponding to the action taken in

Step 11:
· If you have selected the Export to the Server Folder option, the message

The export will be copied to ‘output location’ appears.

· If you have selected the Download to Client option, the message The

Download process has been started appears.

In the bottom panel, which displays the message Do you want to open or

save the .csv/.xlsx file, choose one of the following options:

o Open: Click this to view the export.

o Save: Click this to save your export in your local machine.

o Cancel: Click this if you do NOT want to save your export.

· If you have selected the Export Data check box and the Export Files check

box is cleared, data is exported and the message The Download process has

been started appears.

In the bottom panel, which displays the message Do you want to open or

save the .csv/.xlsx file, choose one of the following options:

o Open: Click this to view the export.

o Save: Click this to save your export in your local machine.

o Cancel: Click this if you do NOT want to save your export.

Note: Files that are exported, either to the client or to the server, are stored

in a zip file. Inside that zip file, you can see a Files folder, under which there

are specific folders for each document whose file(s) has been exported. So,

all files for a document are placed inside the same folder. The folder name

becomes the document full name separated by a hyphen. For example,  if a

document is called SRP|Context 2|A doc{rev 2}, then its folder is called

SRP-Context 2-A doc-rev 2.

4.6.3

Getting Set to Print

The Printing functionality relies on the Accusoft PrizmDoc viewer to render printable

version of different document types stored in AVEVA NET Dashboard.

Note: For more information about the Accusoft PrizmDoc viewer, refer to the AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

The print option will not be available in AVEVA NET Dashboard while:

· A document is open in its native viewer, for example, Microsoft Office.
· A report is displayed in the Document View (the Report Viewer has its own print

button).

· Accusoft PrizmDoc client is not deployed.

Clicking the Print option from the

 menu enables you to print one or more

items selected from the following panels:

· Browse Panel
· Search Panel
· Sets Panel
· Related Items area in the Summary View
· Related Documents area in the Summary View
· Related Query Form area in the Summary View
· Related Items area in a Document Content Card
· Items displayed under the Contents tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Document

Content Card

· Items displayed under the Related Items tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Tag

Content Card

· Documents displayed under the Related Documents tab in a 3D Model/2D

Drawing Tag Content Card

· Basket Panel

Important:

· You must configure the browser pop-up blockers, to allow pop-ups from the

Dashboard Website before printing.

For more information, see:

Configuring Pop-up Blocker Exceptions in IE

Configuring Pop-up Blocker Exceptions in Chrome

· If you are using the IE browser, you must adjust the Page Settings in IE for the

documents to print correctly.

For more information, see:

Adjusting Page Settings in IE

Configuring Pop-up Blocker Exceptions in IE

To configure pop-up blocker exceptions in IE:

1. Open the IE browser.

2.

3.

In the top-right corner, click

, and then click Internet Options.

In the Internet Options window, click the Privacy tab.

4. Under Pop-up Blocker, click Pop-up Blocker Settings.

The Pop-up Blocker Settings window opens.

5.

In the Address of website to allow box, type the Website address, and then

click Add.

6.

Close the Pop-up Blocker Settings window, and in the Internet Options

window, click OK.

 Note: For more information, see http://windows.microsoft.com/en-

gb/internet-explorer/ie-security-privacy-settings#ie=ie-11

Configuring Pop-up Blocker Exceptions in Chrome

To configure pop-up blocker exceptions in Chrome:

1. Open the Chrome browser.

2.

In the top-right corner, click

, and then click Settings.

3. Go to the bottom, and then click Show advanced settings.

4. Under Privacy, click the Content settings button.

5.

Scroll down and under Pop-ups, click Manage Exceptions.

The Pop-up exceptions window opens.

6.

In the Hostname pattern box, enter the domain name for which you want to

7.

8.

create an exception.

In the Behavior drop-down box, click the arrow and select Allow.

Click Done.

Note: For more information, see

https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/95472?hl=en-GB

To adjust page settings in IE:

1.

2.

At the top-right of the IE browser, click

.

Click Print, and then click Page setup.

3. Under Paper Options, clear the Enable shrink to fit check box.

4. Under Margins, set the margin values as required.

5. Under Headers and Footers, set to Empty in all the boxes.

https://help.accusoft.com/PrizmDoc/v13.10/HTML/webframe.html#Printing_

4.6.4

Printing Items

To print items:

1.

2.

In the panel from which you want to execute print, select the item(s).

Click the the

 menu, and then click Print.

The Select Files window opens, listing the printable documents.

Note: The 3D Models, tags, and datasets are NOT printable. The Select Files

window will be empty, if the selected documents are not printable.

3.

Select the ID check box for items to print, and then click OK.

A confirmation message displaying "Preparing item for printing" appears at

the bottom of the screen. Once the items complete processing, and are ready

for printing, the Print window opens.

4.

Select the printer, select the Print Settings, and then click Print.

The documents are sent to the printer for printing.

Notes:
· Your System Administrator can configure a maximum limit for the number

of pages that can be printed. If the limit exceeds, a warning message

appears, and the print process is cancelled. Also, if your banner text is

enabled during configuration, the page setup (header and footer) must be

configured accordingly.

· Your System Administrator can configure background and foreground

colours when printing SVG documents.

For more information, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard

Installation Guide.

4.6.5

Clearing Selection

To clear the selection:

1.

2.

Click the

 menu at the top-right of the panel.

Click the Clear Selection option.

Your item selections are cancelled, clearing the item check boxes.

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

5

Finding Information

Clicking the following functionalities from the Global Navigation Panel enables you to find

information relevant to a document, item or a tag:

· Browse
· Search
· Simple Search
· Advanced Search
· Query Form

To know more about:

How to view and work with

See

Tags

Viewing Tags

Viewing 3D Model Documents

Viewing 2D Drawing Documents

5.1

Using Search

The  Search  functionality  provides  you  with  a  criteria-driven  ability  to  find  information.
Using search keywords, wildcard characters or pre-configured filters (defined in the class
library), you can find information about items, tags or documents.

Clicking

 from the Global Navigation Panel enables you perform the following types

of search:

· Simple Search
· Advanced Search

The search results grid displays items matching the search criterion, you entered in the

Search box.

Release 5.1.8

Page 34

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

5.1.1

Searching with Wildcard Characters

Using the wildcard characters, you can execute search without entering the complete

name or ID of the document, item or tag you are looking for. The wildcard characters

compatible for search in AVEVA NET Dashboard are as follows:

· % (percent) and * (asterisk)

These two wildcard characters behave similarly when used for executing a

search. A wildcard can match any number of characters.

Note: The system can be configured to automatically append a wildcard.

· _ (underscore)

Use _ (underscore) to specify a single character. For instance, when you perform

a search for t_st, the results return all available instances where there is a single

character between t and st.

· [ ] (square brackets)

Use the [ ] (Square brackets) when you are searching for an object that contains

any of the special characters, for example, [_] or [*] or [%].

5.1.2

Executing a CONTAINS-style Search

CONTAINS-style search is used when the search term is enclosed with % on both sides

(for example, “%string%”).

During CONTAINS-style search, the punctuation marks (such as periods, commas, colons,

question marks, ellipses, exclamation marks, single quotation marks, double quotes,

semicolons, parentheses and apostrophes) are ignored. However, hyphens and

underscores, when used to combine words, are not  ignored.

Examples:

· %2”%  ignores the double quote and matches with values that contain a '2', for

example, 2 clouds, 2 nails.

· %area-piping% does not ignore the hyphen and matches values containing 'area-

piping'.

· %-area-piping% ignores the first hyphen as it is not used to combine words. This

example only matches strings that have ‘area-piping’ as a substring.

· %w/c% searches for tags that have a word starting with 'w' and a word starting

with 'c', for example, 'white cloud'.

Release 5.1.8

Page 35

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

· %light bread% finds rows with text of 'light breaded', 'lightly breaded' or 'light

bread'; but it does not return 'lightly toasted bread'.

· %93282-10-% searches for tags with '93282-10’ as a substring. The first ‘-‘ is not

ignored here because it binds two strings ‘93282’ and ‘10’. However, the ‘-‘ in the

end is ignored because it is a trailing one and is of no use to the search.

Note: This is not true with other punctuations. For example, the ‘/’ in %

93282/10% is ignored.

· %-10-% searches for tags with ‘10’ as a substring. The hyphens in the search term

are ignored because they are not being used to combine words.

· %2”- DG% searches for tags with ‘2- DG’ as a substring.

5.1.3

Executing a Simple Search

Entering a part of a keyword, pre-configured filters defined in the class library, or wildcard

character in the Search box, and then clicking

 allows you to do a Simple Search.

To execute a Simple Search:

1.

In the Global Navigation Panel, click

.

The Search Panel opens, docked in the Dashboard.

By default, the item type All is selected in the Type panel.

2.

To change to a different item type, click the arrow to expand the list, and then

click an item type to select it.

The selected item type is displayed on the Type panel.

3.

Enter an Item ID, Name or wildcard character in the Search box or leave it

empty, and then click

.

The search results appear as a list.

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

5.1.4

Executing an Advanced Search

The Advanced Search functionality enables you to filter and refine your search results,

based on the criteria that certain users (with appropriate rights) have created for others

to execute. The criteria can be:

· Constituent class of the Items
· Attribute relationship between Items
· Lifecycle status of the Items
· Attributes of related Items

If you do not know the exact attribute value to filter your search results, you can enter an

alphabet or wildcard character,  and fetch a list of all the available values, starting with

the entered term.

When a Related Item criteria is used, matches are made against the related objects, but

the source objects are displayed in the results.

The Like operator has an implied wildcard on the end of the filter. So, for example, Like=A

is interpreted as Like=A%.

When the Not option is used on a Related Item criteria, the logic applied is NOT

(association AND related type AND filter).

Note: When you are in Advanced Search mode:

· As you expand, all items under the Type panel will have a check box prefixed.
· In the Type panel, all the selected types are displayed, separated by a comma.
When the Type panel cannot accommodate the full list of the selected item

types because of the panel's width, an ellipsis (...) is shown at the end. Hovering

the mouse over this list shows all the item types selected.

· If multiple Item types are selected before a search, the results display the Type
IDs for the OR'ed list of those Item types selected. That means, the results

display the Type IDs for all the Item types selected, not the ones having common

attributes.

· If a child node is selected in the Item type tree, the parent node check box is

shown in green.

5.1.5

Toggling between Simple Search and Advanced Search

If, after an item has already been selected for Simple Search, you want to conduct an

Advanced Search,  a pop-up window appears displaying the message, “You have unsaved

changes that will be lost. Are you sure?”

Click OK to proceed with Advanced Search. Click Cancel to continue with Simple Search.

When you wish to toggle from Advanced Search to Simple Search, the system works in a

similar way.

5.1.6

Loading Search

The Load Search functionality at the bottom of the Search Panel enables you to load the

previously saved search results. By clicking

 Load Search, you can view the saved

searches by their name, and select the one to load into the Search Panel.

Searches loaded into the Search UI are modified to be compatible with the search UI. The

selected Type ID from the saved search is displayed on the Type panel. This means all

ItemIdentifierCriteria are stripped from the search and the obligatory ID or NAME and

CLASSID are added to the beginning of the search.

Note: This only applies to the Search UI and does not apply to the searches configured to

run through the WebAPI.

After loading the Saved search, the corresponding Item types of the items found at the

top level in the search results will be selected in the Type panel. For example, see the

following screenshot in which 3D Models and P&IDs are selected based on the search

results.

5.1.7

Refining Search Criteria

The Refine option:

· Is enabled in the Search Panel, only after a search is executed, and the search

results displayed.

· Clears only the search results for a search criterion, but retains the search

criterion.

Clicking

in the Search Panel displaying the search results, allows you to make

further edits to your original search criterion, and execute a new search.

5.1.8

Resetting Search Criteria

The Reset option:

· Is enabled in the Search Panel, only after a search is executed, and the search

results are displayed.

· Clears both the search criterion and its search results, and resets the selected

item type to All in the Type panel.

· Allows you to enter a new search criterion, and execute a fresh search.

By clicking

  in the Search Panel displaying search results, the search criteria as

well as its search results are cleared. And, you can enter a new search criteria to perform

a new search.

5.1.10

Saving a Search

Saving a search enables you to retrieve the search results, based on a criteria of any

previously executed search. Using the Save Search option, you can save the search results

for a specific search criteria.

Note: You must have the appropriate rights to save a search.

To save a search:

1.

In the bottom-left of the Search Panel displaying the search results, click

.

The Search Details window appears.

2.

Enter a Name for the search results you want to save, and then click OK.

A message appears confirming the saving of the search.

Saving Personal Searches

If you have the CreateAdvancedFind permission, you can create Advanced Searches,

Save Searches and manage the Saved Searches.

You can also save a search as Personal, which means the search can only be viewed by

the author of the Saved Search.

5.2

Using Browse

The Browse functionality enables you to find information by navigating through the root

nodes, and expanding the breakdown nodes. It helps you to instantly retrieve inter-

related or independent documents, Items,  or tags, without the need to specify any

search criteria.

To use Browse:

1.

 In the Global Navigation Panel, click

 Browse.

The Browse Panel opens, docked in the Dashboard.

2.

In the Browse Panel, click to expand the root node, and then click a breakdown

node.

The node expands, displaying tags, items, and documents under the respective

folder.

Note: Your System Administrator can configure the number of

tags/documents to be displayed. If you want to a larger number of browse

results to be displayed, contact your System Administrator.

3.

Click

 to view more objects, or

to go back.

5.3

Using Query Forms

The Query Forms enable you to:

· Find information, using a form-based search mechanism.
· Further your advanced search through the database.
· Define criteria and modify it, while executing searches.
· Generate search results, listing the data related to one or more item in the

database.

Note: Your System Administrator can create and design tailor-made Query Forms, using

the Query Form Designer Panel. For more information, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub

and Dashboard Installation Guide.

The following command buttons appear in the Query Form:

Clicking

Enables

Executing search based on the criteria selected and entered
in the box.

Returning to the search criteria used, after the search result
appears in the search criteria box.

Auto-selecting all the search criteria check boxes.

Deselecting all the search criteria check boxes.

Clearing a selected search criteria check box.

Executing a Query Form Search

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

You can execute a Query Form search by entering the search criteria in the fields

configured by your System Administrator. You can also include wildcard characters in the

search criteria. By default, a Query Form opens under the Form View tab. Clicking the

Table View enables you to view the search results in a table. For more details about the

Table View, see Changing Search Results View to Table View.

When a Related Item criteria is used, matches are made against the related objects, but

the source objects are displayed in the results.

The Like operator has an implied wildcard on the end of the filter. So, for example, Like=A

is interpreted as Like=A%.

When Match All is selected in the Query Form Designer, then, at execution time, all

associated Datasets that have that attribute are searched and all of these attribute values

must match the value given in the search criteria.

When List of Values is selected in the Query Form Designer, then, at execution time, a list

of all the values is provided.

To execute a Query Form search:

1.

 In the Global Navigation Panel, click

.

The Query Forms Panel opens, displaying all the pre-configured Query Forms.

2.

To open a Query Form, click its name.

The Query Form of the selected name opens.

Changing Search Results View to Table View

After executing a Query Form search, clicking the Table View tab enables you to view the

search results in a tabular format (printer-friendly view). Each row in the table represents

a search result.

From the Table View tab, you can do the following tasks with the selected search result

items:

· Open
· Add to
· Sets
· Baskets
· Export Items
· Export All Items
· Print

5.3.2

Query Form CSV Export

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

While performing a CSV Export of the Query Forms, you must configure the following

details as part of the Export Definition:

Primary data separator: This is the character (or characters) that will separate the

columns of data in the CSV file.

Secondary data separator: Columns might contain many values. For example, in the

Object aliases column, if one of the items in the Search results is 'V-2', then the aliases for

this item could be 'SRP|VPD|/V-2' and 'SRP|PDS|Valve-2'. In the CSV file, the secondary

data separator in the Object aliases column would separate these two values.

Include Clash Information: When choosing the primary and secondary data separators,

you must ensure that the actual values that appear in the columns in the CSV file do not

contain either of these separators. In this case, the user should choose a different

separator and export the data again.

Selecting this option includes information at the bottom of the CSV file about any clashes

detected during the creation of the file where values did contain instances of either of the

separators. The line number (in the file) and the column number of any clashes are given.

If no clashes were detected, then no clash information is included in the file.

Include Column Header Information: Select this option if the user would like an initial

record in the CSV file that contains a header for each column. Selecting this option causes

additional fields to be made visible on the page where the user can insert the header text

for each column. If the user leaves a field blank, then a system defined header will be

used which is based on the column type. A text box with the text "UOM" will be displayed

next to the attribute field when this option is selected, and a Property is added in the

User Defined Columns section of the Export Search Results Screen.

Format for Excel: Select this option if the user intends to view the downloaded CSV file

using Microsoft Excel. Using this option prevents Excel doing its own (sometimes

misleading) interpretation of the CSV data.

Release 5.1.8

Page 54

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Finding Information

5.4

Using Asset Visibility

Asset Visibility provides the ability to restrict data to only the assets that the user is

interested in. The Dashboard user can provide the list of assets that are of interest in the

Dashboard URL. A single asset or a list of assets can be specified by adding an assets

parameter, for example:

· Single asset: http://myMachine:16000/?assets=ABC#/

· Multiple assets (separated by a vertical bar |): http://myMachine:16000/?

assets=ABC|XYZ#/

Search

When the user performs a search, then the results are restricted to the specified assets.

Browse

Browse shows the root items that satisfy the specified assets defined in the URL. This is

true if the Root Item Type (see Settings > Role > Browse > Root Items) is, for example, a

PLANT or a 3D Model.

Query Forms

Query Forms only display results that satisfy the specified assets defined in the URL.

Export

Export only exports items that satisfy the specified assets defined in the URL.

Model Composition

Model Composition only lists 3D Models that satisfy the specified assets defined in the

URL.

Release 5.1.8

6

Viewing Information

The information you select to view can constitute a document, item or tag, independent

or inter-related to each other. The following sections explain the procedures for:

· Viewing Tags
· Viewing Content Cards
· Viewing Document Content Card
· Closing Document Content Card
· Viewing Tag Content Card
· Closing Tag Content Card
· Viewing 3D Model Documents

6.1

Viewing Tag

If the information you have selected to view  is of tags, its Summary View opens

displaying the information in the zones configured to include Relationships, Metadata or

Characteristics.

For example, it can be configured with zones for:

· Details
· Key Attributes
· Attributes
· Related Items
· Related Documents
· Related Query Forms
· 2D View
· 3D View

on the tab

in the Tab Panel.

 at the top-right of the viewing areas in the Summary

6.2.5

Expanding Tags and Interpolating Them

Many drawings (2D) contain tag references that use a shorthand notation to reference

more than one object, for example J-9002A/B. AVEVA NET Gateways provides support

to split labels with the  information on all referenced items that are embedded within the

SVG (drawing). For more information, refer to the AVEVA NET Document Indexing

Gateway User Guide.

The following example shows an image of how the screen looks when you click on a split

label in the 2D/3D File Viewer:

Using the returned list of matches, a pop-up list of real objects is presented, from which

you can view the details of the selected tag in the item’s Content Card.

In the following example, clicking on R-9003 from the pop-up dialog in the File Viewer

updates the Content Card accordingly:

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

6.3

Viewing 3D Model Documents

Clicking a document from a 3D Models document folder enables you to view three-

dimensional digital simulations (3D Models) of engineering documents in the Document

View. You can change the view mode from Document View to Summary View, using the

 option. The following actions enable you multi-dimensional viewing of a 3D

Model, adjusting its size:

· Rotating the mouse wheel forward lets you to zoom in (enlarge) and view the 3D

Model.

· By rotating the mouse wheel backward, you can zoom out (shrink) the model.
· To view the 3D Model revolving it in a circular or elliptical motion, click in any

area of the model, and then move

 from a selected point

.

6.3.1

Navigating in the 3D Model

The Navigation menu in the 3D Model Document View provides you with options to

move around the 3D Model, and view it from multiple dimensions.

It enables you to focus on any particular tag constituting a 3D Model item.

Use this Mode

To

Fly

Look

Move the model at a faster pace in any direction.

Rotate the viewing dimensions of a model, so that you can view it
from different angles.

 Orbit Point

Revolve around the mouse-down cursor position.

Orbit
Selected

Pan

Slide

Walk

Revolve around the selected item(s).

Move the model anywhere inside the Document Viewing area.

Slowly move the model as if through a perpendicular line.

Note: This mode moves the model in the opposite direction of the

mouse pointer movement.

Slowly move the model forward and backward.

Note: Clicking the mouse and moving vertically upward magnifies the

model, and moving downward reduces the model size.

Zoom

Magnify the model size, by clicking the mouse and moving upward.
Reduce the model size, by clicking the mouse and moving downward.

· Bottom View

2.

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Zooming a Selection

The Zoom to Selection feature enables you to zoom in (magnify) and view a selected tag.

To zoom a selection:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Zoom to Selection.

The selected tag zooms in.

Highlighting a Selection

The Highlight feature enables a selected tag to appear brighter and stand apart from

other tags in a 3D Model.

To highlight a selection:

1.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Highlight.

2. Go to the 3D Model, and click a tag.

The tag is highlighted.

Isolating a Selected Tag

The Isolate feature enables you to separate out a tag, and view it as a stand-alone object.

To isolate a selected tag:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Isolate.

The selected Tag appears isolated, with rest of the 3D Model hidden.

Release 5.1.8

Page 67

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Isolating the Parent Tag

The Isolate Parent feature enables you to separate out the parent tag of an isolated item.

To isolate the parent tag of an isolated item:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Isolate.

3.

Click Isolate Parent.

The parent tag of the isolated item appears isolated, with rest of the 3D Model

hidden.

Note: The selected tag must be isolated before isolating its parent tag.

Making a Selection Translucent

The Make Translucent feature enables you to view a selected tag as a transparent object.

To make a selection transparent:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Make Translucent.

The selected tag appears as transparent.

Adding a Selected Tag to a Set/Basket

Using the Add to feature, you can add a selected tag in a 3D Model to the Basket.

To add a selected tag to a Set/Basket:

1.

2.

3.

4.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Add to.

The Add to window opens.

To add to Basket, click Basket.

To add to a Set, click the arrow in the drop-down box to select a set type, and

then select a Set.

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Viewing a Selected Tag's Parent

The Select Parent feature enables you to:

·  View the parent of a selected tag in a 3D Model, if that tag has a parent.
· If the selected tag does not have any parent, no other tags will be highlighted.

To view a selected tag's parent:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Select Parent.

The selected tag appears as highlighted.

3.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Select Parent.

The parent of the selected tag is highlighted.

Note: If the selected tag has no parent tags associated with it, nothing will be

highlighted.

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Hiding a Tag

Using the Hide feature, you can hide a selected tag from the 3D Model.

To hide a tag:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Hide.

The selected tag is hidden from view.

Unhiding a Tag

The Show feature enables you to restore a tag in a 3D Model that is hidden using the

Hide feature.

To unhide a tag:

1.

2.

In the 3D Model, click to select a hidden tag.

In the bottom-left of the Document View area, click Selection, and then click

Show.

The hidden tag re-appears.

6.3.5

Using 3D Model Tools

The

 menu in the 3D Model viewing area provides you with the following

capabilities:

· Model Composition
· AVEVA NET Player
· Visual Reporting
· 3D Materials

Model Composition

A 3D Model is composed of a base model, and its constituent components represented by

tags. You can use the Model Composition tool to:

· Dissect and view, any or all of the 3D Model components.
· Display only those model components that you select to view, hiding others.

You can view the model components in the following view modes:

View

Description

Flat view

Displays a complete list of the constituent components of 3D Model.

Folder view

Displays 3D Model components folders in a tree hierarchy.

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

AVEVA NET Player

The AVEVA NET Player enables you to have a simulated 3D-view of the project

construction progress:

· Sequenced phase-by-phase in playback frames with customisable:

o Actual and planned project start and end dates.

o Activity

o Tag

o Progress Status

· Using animations with playback controls.

Note:

· Your System Administrator must install a separate license to enable AVEVA NET

Player. The player can be disabled, while configuring. If this feature is unavailable in

your Dashboard, contact your System Administrator to enable it.

· AVEVA NET Player is not recommended for use with WebGL.

Release 5.1.8

Page 77

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

Applying Filters

To apply filters:

1.

In the Planning Filters area, enter the criteria:

In the box for Do this

Activity

Enter Activity ID.

Tag

Enter a tag ID or a part of the ID.

Status

Click the arrow, and select a construction progress state.

Note: The statuses available to select are configurable.

Filter By

Click the arrow, and select the criterion for construction

progress dates.

Note: The criteria are configurable. For example,
Planned/Actual start and end date.

· From Click

, and then select a start date based on the Filter By

criterion chosen.

· To

, and then select an end date based on the Filter By

Click
criterion chosen.
Note: You can also enter the dates manually. However, if
you enter the date in a wrong format, an alert message will
be displayed while applying filters.

2.

To retrieve the search results for the criteria you entered, click the Apply

Filters button.

The number of search results appears to the left of the Apply Filters button.

Note: If the search criteria used in a saved search finds more results than the

limits configured under Settings Panel > Site > General > Player (Max list

items for AVEVA NET Player) (the default limit is set as 500), a warning

message appears. If this happens, consider changing this setting; but be

careful to not exceed the combined performance of the system, the hardware

and the network latency.

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Using Playback Controls

After retrieving the search results using the Apply Filters feature, you can view the

animation of the step-by-step construction progress using playback controls.

The following table represents the options that can be selected for playback:

If you
select

Standard
mode

Status
calculation
considers

Only Planned
dates (static)
Note: By default,
the player runs
in the Standard
mode.

Dynamic
mode

Both Actual and
Planned dates

Simulation Only Planned

Show
colours

dates

Colours
configured for
each status

Description

In this mode, AVEVA NET Player determines the status of
items using the value of an attribute on a dataset. This
attribute is static, and its value is determined at some
prior point, set as such in the EIWM that comprises the
Dashboard data, and remains at this value until it is
changed in the source application, a new export is
performed, and the data is re-imported.

In this mode, AVEVA NET Player dynamically calculates
and assigns the status colour to an element based on the
associated activity’s planned/actual dates and the
simulation date determined by the current frame within
the playback sequence.

The Simulation check box will be enabled only if the
Dynamic mode is selected.

Note: Your System Administrator can configure the display name and colour for each

construction progress state. For more information, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard Installation Guide.

Release 5.1.8

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

Visual Reporting

Visual Reporting enables you to implement visual changes to your search results,

comprising of 3D Model components.

It allows you to:

· Categorise 3D Model components, altering its colours and transparency levels.
· Save the changes after adding description, enabling quick identification of those

components later.

Applying Visual Changes to Search Results

By default, you are able to apply visual changes to the search results, but the Save button

is enabled only if you have appropriate rights granted by your System Administrator.

To apply visual changes to search results:

1.

In the 3D Model View area, click the

 menu, and then select Visual

Reporting.

The Visual Reporting pane opens.

2.

In the drop-down box, click the arrow to select the Visual Report.

The Searches saved under the selected Visual Report appears as a list.

3.

In the Colour box, click the arrow to select the colour, and then click Set

Colour.

4.

5.

In the Transparency box, click the arrow to select the transparency rate.

Click Apply.

The visual changes are applied to the 3D Model components in that saved

search.

Note: If the search criteria used in a saved search finds more results than the

limits configured under: Settings Panel > Site > General > Player (Max list

items for Visual Reports) (the default limit is set as 500), a warning message

appears. If this happens, consider changing this setting; but be careful to not

exceed the combined performance of the system, the hardware and the

network latency.

6.

To isolate and view the changes for a particular saved search, select Isolate

Results.

Release 5.1.8

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

3D Materials

The 3D Materials tool enables you to make changes to the visual properties of each

element in a 3D Model.

Note: While importing the 3D Models, the names of the materials can be configured. For

more information, see the 3D Materials Name Translation section in the AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

The following table explains the features available in the 3D Materials tool:

Feature

Material

Colour

Show

Description

Represents the colour and translucency that can be applied to multiple
elements in a 3D Model.

Indicates the colour of each 3D Material displayed.
· When you click the arrow in the box, a palette appears, using which

you can change the colour of the 3D Material.

· Clearing the Show check box makes the material 100% invisible.
· Selecting the Show check box restores the colour and translucency of

the 3D Material.

Translucency

Indicates the point on the scale, from transparent to opaque, of a 3D
Material.
· By clicking the arrow in the box, you can change the translucency of

the 3D Material.

· By default, the material is opaque (0% translucency).

6.4

Viewing 2D Drawing Documents

Clicking a two-dimensional vector-based graphic from a 2D Drawing document folder

enables you to view these drawings rendered in the SVG file format (.svg). By default, the

selected 2D Drawing opens in the Document View. Using  the

 menu at the top-

right of the Document View area, you can change the view to Summary View.

Important: For a system/server running on a non-English operating system,  the system

locale settings should be set to English before processing the files during deployment.

Your System Administrator must set it to ensure proper rendering of SVG documents.

6.4.3

Using 2D Drawing Tools

The

 menu in the SVG viewer provides a Visual Reporting option.

Visual Reporting

Visual Reporting enables you to implement visual changes to your search results,

comprising of 2D Drawing components.

Release 5.1.8

Page 88

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

It enables you to:

· Categorise 2D Drawing components and alter their colours.
· Save the Visual Report to re-use later.

By default, you will be able to apply visual changes to the search results; but the Save

button will be enabled only if you have appropriate rights granted by your System

Administrator.

To apply visual changes to the search results:

1.

In the SVG viewer, click the

 menu, and then select Visual

Reporting.

The Visual Reporting pane opens.

2.

In the drop-down box, click the arrow to select New Visual Report.

The searches saved under the selected Visual Report appear as a list.

3.

In the Colour box, click the arrow to select the colour, and then click Set

Colour.

4.

Click Apply.

Release 5.1.8

Page 89

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

The visual changes are applied to the 2D Drawing components in that saved search.

Note: If the search criteria used in a saved search finds more results than the limits

configured under: Settings Panel > Site > General > Player (Max list items for Visual

Reports) (the default limit is set as 500), a warning message appears. If this happens,

consider changing this setting; but be careful to not exceed the combined performance of

the system, the hardware and the network latency.

The following functionality of Visual Reporting for 2D Drawings works the same as for

Visual Reporting for 3D models (see Visual Reporting):

· Visual Reporting drop-down to create a new visual report (select New Visual

Report) or to load an existing visual report

· Add New Search link to add new search
· Remove All link to remove all searches
· New Search link to open prompt
· Colour picker to select colour of search result
· Run button to apply highlight with selected colour on search results
· Save button to save a new visual report with ID and description

6.4.4

Limitation of Viewing Thin Lines When Zoomed in

Due to the algorithms used by internet browsers for scaling vector graphics like SVG

drawings, some content of the drawing, like thin lines or underlines, in the text may

flicker and disappear during zooming when their thickness becomes less than one pixel.

Visibility of small objects may depend on the resolution of the video mode and may be

better in high resolution.

Representing scaled content is browser specific:

· For Internet Explorer 11 and Edge, lines may disappear when the content is

zoomed in.

· For Chrome, thin lines may change their brightness and be less visible.

Release 5.1.8

6.5

Highlighting Tag IDs from Related Items

You can highlight tag IDs via the Related Items panel in a Content Card for a selected

item.

1. While viewing a P&ID, open a document by selecting it from Selected Tag >

Content Card > Related Items tab.

A new tab opens in the Accusoft Viewer with the selected tag ID Content Card

open and the tag ID highlighted in the document. All instances of the tag ID

are highlighted throughout the document. Clicking on the highlighted tag ID

pops up the selected tag Content Card if the tags to document associations

have been processed.
