CHAPTER 6 - Viewing Information

6.6

Configuring View Selection

You can configure the view selection in Dashboard from Settings > Role > General.

6.6.1

Default Views

In Dashboard, when an item is selected (for example, in the Browse tree view), a tab is

opened to display information about that item.

The Default Views dialog enables you to specify a View that is used by default when the

tab opens. You can specify the initial View based on the Type of the item being selected.

In the screenshot below, the second setting specifies that if an item of type  3D MODEL is

selected, then the tab opens in Document View. If the item type is not present in the

Settings, but its parent type is present, then the setting for the parent type is used.

Using this example, imagine that the 3D MODEL row has been removed. If an item of type

3D MODEL is subsequently selected, then the Summary View is displayed, because

DOCUMENT CONTENT is the parent type of 3D MODEL.

Note: Sometimes it is not possible to use the configured View for viewing information

about a selected item. For example, the Document View is designed to display a File of an

item. If the selected item has no Files, then the Document View is not considered for

viewing information for the item, even though the configuration settings say Document

View should be used. In such cases, Summary View is displayed.

Release 5.1.8

Page 93

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7

Using Markup to Collaborate

The Markup functionality enables you to collaborate through annotating the 2D and 3D

Model documents, displayed in:

· AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard File Viewer
· Accusoft Viewer

7.1

Understanding User Rights for Markup

As a default user, you can:

· Mark up 2D and 3D Model documents, using the Annotation tools.
· Select and load the Markups created and saved by the other users.

However, you must have the appropriate rights  granted by a user with administrative

privileges, to execute the following actions:

To do this action

Create a Markup

Save a Markup

Edit your own Markup

Delete your own Markup

Edit other users' Markup

Export a Markup

You  must have this right

CreateMarkup

EditMarkup

ExportMarkup

Note: For more information about configuring the rights, refer to the AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Release 5.1.8

Page 94

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.2

Viewing the Markups

As a default user, you can view the Markup created and saved by the other users.

· The number of saved Markups pertaining to an opened document appears as a

superscript of the Markup icon in a red circle.

· Clicking the Markup icon opens the Markup panel, listing the name and
description of all the saved Markups, with the saved date and time.

· Clicking a Markup's name displays that Markup on the opened document.

Important: This action will clear any currently visible annotations in the file being

viewed.

Release 5.1.8

Page 95

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.3

Creating, Editing and Deleting Markups

You must have the CreateMarkup right to create, edit or delete your Markups in the

documents. If not, these command buttons will be unavailable.

To create, edit and delete Markups:

1.

2.

3.

4.

Add a Markup in the document.

Click the Markup icon.

The Markup panel opens.

To create a Markup, click the Create icon.

In the New Markup panel, enter the Name and Description, and then click

Save.

After the details are saved, the message "Markup has been created"

appears at the bottom-right corner. The new Markup Name, Description, the

Saved Date and Time, appears in the Markup Panel. Also, the number in the

red circle appearing as a superscript of the Markup icon, increases with every

new Markup creation.

5.

To edit a Markup, select the check box next to the Markup you want to edit.

The Edit button is enabled.

6.

Click the Edit icon.

When a Markup is selected for editing, its annotations are loaded into the

viewer, replacing any existing annotations.

In the Edit Markup Panel, make the updates, and then click Save.

If you do not want to save the edits, click Cancel.

To delete a Markup, select the check box next to the Markup you want to

7.

8.

9.

delete.

10. Click the Delete icon.

A confirmation message displaying, the number of Markups you are deleting

and if you would wish to continue, appears.

11. To confirm the deletion, click OK. If not, click Cancel.

Upon your confirmation to delete,  a message "Markup has been deleted"

appears at the bottom-right corner.

Release 5.1.8

Page 96

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.4

Viewing the Markup Descriptions

The Markup description can be expanded or collapsed as follows:

· If the Markup description is more than 100 characters, clicking the ...show more

link expands the remaining text visibility.

· Clicking the show less... link collapses the expanded description.

7.5

Resizing the Markup Panel

To resize the Markup Panel:

1.

Rest the mouse pointer on the panel border.

2. When the pointer becomes a double-headed arrow, drag the pointer left or

right to move the split line.

The panel height or width is resized, providing a larger area for document

viewing.

7.6

Preserving the Markup State

The state of the Markup Panel and the annotations (saved or otherwise) in a document

model are preserved, while navigating around AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard.

Therefore, you can add some annotations to a document, navigate away from the page to

view a different document, and then navigate back to the File Viewer to finalise your

Markup, without losing your work. The state of the Markup in an open document is

persisted, until the document's tab is closed.

7.7

Relocating and Resizing Markups

To relocate and resize Markups:

1.

To move a Markup to another area in the drawing, click the Marked up shape,

and then drag it.

2.

To increase or decrease the Markup size, click the Markup, and then rest the

mouse pointer on the border.

3. When the pointer becomes a double-headed arrow, drag the pointer inward

or outward.

Release 5.1.8

Page 97

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.8

Loading Markups

You can load the Markups saved by you or the other users, into an opened document.

To load Markups:

1.

Click the Markup icon.

The Markup panel opens, displaying all the saved Markups relevant to the

opened document.

2.

3.

Select the check boxes next to the Markups you want to load.

At the bottom of the Markup panel, click the Load icon.

All the selected Markups appear in the opened document.

Important: This action will clear all the other Markups under viewing, and will

show only the Markups you selected to load.

7.9

Creating Default Views

Default Views can only be set for 3D Models. A Default View is based on a single Markup.

After you set a Default View, it will be displayed instead of the original view of the file.

To create a Default View:

1. Open the Markup panel on the 3D View.

2.

Choose an existing Markup or create a new Markup, and then select the check

box for the desired Markup.

3.

Click the Set as Default link located on the Markup panel footer.

To remove a Default View:

1. Open the Markup panel on the 3D View.

2.

Click the Remove Default View icon against the default Markup.

Release 5.1.8

Page 98

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.10

Merging Markups

Important: You cannot merge a combination of a 2D and 3D Markups. The combination

you select to merge should consist either 2D Markups only or 3D Markups only.

To merge Markups:

1.

Click the Markup icon.

The Markup panel opens, displaying all the saved Markups relevant to the

opened document.

2.

3.

Select the check boxes next to the Markups you want to load.

At the bottom of the Markup panel, click the Load icon.

All the selected Markups appear in the opened document.

Important: This action will clear all the other Markups under viewing, and will

show only the Markups you selected to load.

4.

Create a new Markup from the merged result.

The New Markup panel opens, displaying the merged result of all the loaded

Markups.

5.

To save the merged Markup, click Save. If not, click Cancel.

Release 5.1.8

Page 99

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Using Markup to Collaborate

7.11

Distributing Markup URLs

Each Markup is identified by a unique URL in your browser's address bar. You can share

this URL with the other users, who can directly navigate to that document's Markup.

Sharing the Markup URLs enables instantaneous collaboration.

Note: If you are a default user without the rights assigned to create, edit, save or export

Markups, you can add the Markups and send the URL to the other users.

For example, the following URL enables you to directly navigate to the Markup with the

ID, VNET System|Markup|MARKUP_00009, added for the item with the ID, IPE|

C905677.

7.12

Exporting Markups

To export Markups:

1.

Click the Markup icon.

The Markup panel opens, displaying all the saved Markups relevant to the

opened document.

2.

3.

Select the check boxes next to the Markups you want to Export.

At the bottom of the Markup panel, click the Load icon.

All the selected Markups appear in the opened document.

Important: This action will clear all the other Markups under viewing, and will

show only the Markups you selected to load.

4.

Click the Export icon.

The Cover Sheet Panel opens.

5.

In the Cover sheet definition box, click the arrow to select a Cover Sheet.

Note: The Cover Sheets available in the drop-down list are determined by your

System Administrator. A generic Cover Sheet is available by default. However,

your System Administrator can create additional custom Cover Sheets. For

more information about Cover Sheet configuration, refer to the AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

6.

If you do not want a Cover Sheet, select No cover sheet from the drop-down

list.

Click OK.

If you do not want to export, click Cancel.

7.

8.

CHAPTER 8 - Categorising and Grouping Items

8

Categorising and Grouping Items

You can categorise and group a collection of items based on any specific criteria, and view

it later using the following features:

· Sets
· Basket

8.1

Using Sets

The Sets functionality allows you to categorise and group a selection of items, and store it

with unique identifiers for future use. Using the Add to option under the

menu, you can include items to Sets from the following areas in Dashboard:

· Search Panel
· Browse Panel
· Sets Panel
· Related Items area in the Summary View
· Related Documents area in the Summary View
· Related Query Form area in the Summary View
· Related Items area in a Document Content Card
· Items displayed under the Contents tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Document

Content Card

· Items displayed under the Related Items tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Tag

Content Card

· Documents displayed under the Related Documents tab in a 3D Model/2D

Drawing Tag Content Card

· Basket Panel
· Reports displayed in the Document View

Note: The areas in the Summary View, Document Content Card, and Tag Content

Card are configurable to include Relationships, Metadata or Characteristics.

Hence, the naming of the areas can differ based on the configuration.

Release 5.1.8

Page 111

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Categorising and Grouping Items

You can execute the following actions with Sets:

· Create a new Set
· Add items from one Set to the other
· Edit Sets
· Delete Sets
· Exports items in Sets
· Print items in Sets

  to expand the Sets Panel.

8.1.2

Configuring Autonaming of Set ID Prefixes

Note: To configure the autonaming of Set ID prefixes, you must have appropriate right

(ManageSettings) granted by your System Administrator. For more information, refer to

the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

The Set ID is a unique identifier of a Set. While creating a new Set, you can either choose

to auto-generate the Set ID, or specify a Set ID suiting your requirement. The

autogenerated-Set ID assumes a numeric value incremental by one with every new Set

created. The Settings feature in AVEVA NET Dashboard enables you to configure the

autonaming of Set ID prefixes, and number padding for Set IDs.

Release 5.1.8

Page 113

Using the Basket

The Basket functionality enables a personalised grouping and storing of frequently used

items, for using it later.

To use Basket:

1.

2.

Select the item(s), and then click the

 menu.

Select the Add to option, and then click Basket.

The selected items can be added to Basket from the following areas in AVEVA

NET Dashboard:
· Browse Panel
· Search Panel
· Sets Panel
· Related Items area in the Summary View
· Related Documents area in Summary View
· Related Query Form area in Summary View
· Related Items area in an Document Content Card
· Items displayed under the Contents tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing

Document Content Card

Content Card

· Documents displayed under the Related Documents tab in a 3D Model/2D

Drawing Tag Content Card

· Reports displayed in Document View

Clicking

 from the top-right of the following areas also allows

you to add items to the Basket

· Summary View of documents, tags, reports
· Document View of documents, tags, reports
· Query Form

Note: The areas in the Summary View, Document Content Card, and Tag Content

Card are configurable to include Relationships, Metadata or Characteristics.

Hence, the naming of the areas can differ based on the configuration.

Managing Exports

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Managing Exports

Important: The maximum limit for the number of items that can be exported at a time is

set during configuration (for example, 10000 items). If you are exporting more than the

limit set, a warning message appears.

The Export functionality enables you to export one or more items, tags or documents

selected from the following panels:

· Browse Panel
· Search Panel
· Sets Panel
· Related Items area in the Summary View
· Related Documents area in the Summary View
· Related Query Form area in the  Summary View
· Related Items area in a Document Content Card
· Items displayed under the Contents tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Document

Content Card

· Items displayed under the Related Items tab in a 3D Model/2D Drawing Tag

Content Card

· Documents displayed under the Related Documents tab in a 3D Model/2D

Drawing Tag Content Card

· Basket Panel
· Reports displayed in Document View

Note: The areas in the Summary View, Document Content Card and Tag Content Card are
configurable to include Relationships, Metadata, or Characteristics. Hence, the naming of
the areas can differ based on the configuration.

9.1

Creating Export Definitions

The Export Definition selected while executing an export determines the column headings

and data elements, to be included in the Export. The exported items displayed or saved in

the .csv file format include only the data elements, defined in the Export Definition.

AVEVA NET Dashboard provides you with a default Export Definition. However, creating

new Export Definitions enables you to have a more customised export.

Note: You must have appropriate rights (CreateExports rights) to create Export

Definitions. If not, the Create option will not be available in the Export Panel.

Release 5.1.8

Page 123

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Managing Exports

You can add or remove any of the following columns available by default, while creating

an Export Definition:

· Object ID
· Object context
· Object name
· Object aliases
· Class IDs
· Class names
· Incidental class IDs
· Incidental class names
· Class Scheme
· Life-Cycle Status

Additionally, you can create new columns as follows:

· Object attribute for a source class
· Associated attribute
· Number of associated items

To create Export Definitions:

1.

In the Global Navigation Panel, click

.

The Export Panel opens, docked in the Dashboard.

2.

In the drop-down box, click the arrow to select an Export Definition.

3. Go to the left-bottom corner, and click

.

The Export Definition window opens.

4.

Click

 for Export Columns, and then click to select the new Export

Columns.

5.

6.

The newly added Export Columns appear as a list.

To edit the name of the Export Columns, select Modify Label.

To display IDs as hyperlinks that can open AVEVA NET Dashboard in the

context of the ID, select the Hyperlink check box.

Note: This only applies to the IDs and the Related IDs. Hyperlinks are not

displayed if there is more than one ID in the Excel spreadsheet cell (for

example, if there is more than one related item).

7.

To remove the newly added Export Columns, click

.

Release 5.1.8

8.

9.

If you do not want to save the new Export Definition, click Cancel.

.

.

.

.

at a later time. You must have the appropriate right (CreateExport rights) to save your

exports.

.

The Exports Panel opens, docked in the Dashboard.

.

In the bottom panel, which displays the message Do you want to open or

1. Open: Click this to view the export.

2.

3.

Save: Click this to save the export in your local machine.

Cancel: Click this if you do NOT want to save your export.

Note: When the files are exported, either to the client or the server, they

reside inside a zip file. Inside that zip file, you can see a Files folder, under

which there are specific folders for each document whose file(s) has been

exported. So, all files for a document are placed inside the same folder. The

folder name becomes the document full name separated by a hyphen. For

example,  if a document is called SRP|Context 2|A doc{rev 2}, then its

folder will be SRP-Context 2-A doc-rev 2.

CHAPTER 10 - Working with Reports

10

Working with Reports

The Reports functionality enables you to view reports pre-configured by your System

Administrator.

Using the Reports functionality, you can:

· Select a type of report to view
· View the reports of a selected type

And, depending upon the type of report (for SSRS), you may:

· Export the selected reports
· Print one or more reports in a group

10.1

Selecting a Type of Report to View

To select a type of report to view:

1.

 In the Global Navigation Panel, click

.

The Reports Panel opens, docked in the Dashboard.

2.

In the drop-down box, click the arrow to select the type of report.

The reports of the selected type appears in a list.

3.

From the list, click to select the Report you want to view.

The report opens in the Document View.

Release 5.1.8

Page 135

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Working with Reports

10.2

Browsing SSRS Reports

The SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) Reports are a type of Report generated from

the Microsoft SQL Server databases. Your System Administrator configures these reports,

enabling you to access it through the Reports functionality in AVEVA NET Dashboard. If

the reports you are viewing are of the SSRS type, you can browse through multiple

reports of this type using the following procedure.

To browse the SSRS Reports:

1.

To view the successive reports of the SSRS type, click the forward arrow.

The next Report opens.

2.

To view the previously viewed reports again, click the backward arrows.

The previously viewed report re-appears.

Release 5.1.8

CHAPTER 11 - Appendix A: Managing Browser Security Settings

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

11

Appendix A: Managing Browser Security Settings

Note: Your System Administrator must configure the correct browser settings for you to

access AVEVA NET Dashboard. If you encounter any problems as described in the

following table, contact your System Administrator. For more information about the

browser security settings, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation

Guide.

Browser

Internet Explorer (IE)  versions 9,
10, 11

Note: Only the latest version of
IE 11 is supported with AVEVA
NET Workhub and Dashboard.
For more information, see
https://www.microsoft.com/en-
gb/windowsforbusiness/end-of-
ie-support.

Google Chrome
Important: The ActiveX controls
required for AVEVA NET
Dashboard displays are NOT
supported in the Google Chrome
browser.

If your Settings are Wrong
· Blank screen displayed after logging in.
· Security error message while downloading an

export file.

· Blank screen for a 3D Model Document View, with

or without an add-on error message.

· Blank screen displayed after logging in.
· Error messages on executing a command.
· Icons/command buttons not displayed.

Release 5.1.8

Page 139

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 12 - Appendix B: Optimising Screen Resolution

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

12

Appendix B: Optimising Screen Resolution

The minimum screen resolution for viewing AVEVA NET Dashboard is 1024 x 768. If your

system screen resolution is set to a value that is less than minimum, contact your System

Administrator to set it to 1024 x 768 or greater, for optimal viewability.

Release 5.1.8

Page 140

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 13 - Appendix C: Logging Out of AVEVA NET Dashboard

AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide

13

Appendix C: Logging Out of AVEVA NET Dashboard

If the Log Out command is enabled during configuration, it appears under the

  icon at the top-right corner of the Dashboard. Clicking the Log Out

command allows you to exit from your current session in Dashboard.

Note: Logging out of AVEVA NET Dashboard does not log you out of your chosen Identity

Provider or release network credentials for a domain. This means, when you log out of

AVEVA NET Dashboard and try logging in again, you are not prompted for credentials. To

release credentials, you must close your browser session.

Release 5.1.8

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

This chapter gives basic information about this guide, training and support.

1.1

About this Guide

This guide provides quick references to all regular expressions used in the AVEVA NET

Gateways documentation. It includes the following key information:

· Quick reference to all syntax used in the Gateway documentation.
· References to regular expression design and additional material.

1.2

Summary of Chapters

Chapter

Discusses About

Quick Reference

Regular Expression Syntax

References

Regular Expression Design and some additional material

1.3

Audience

This guide is intended for the following users  who will be using AVEVA NET Regular

Expressions while working with AVEVA NET Gateway products.

This guide assumes that you have the knowledge of the following:

· SQL expression construction
· An understanding of XML data sources
· OLE connection string construction

1.4

Related Documents

This section lists the guides which provides users a good starting point to understand the

AVEVA NET Gateway products suite. For more information about AVEVA NET Gateway

products, see the following guides:

· AVEVA NET Gateway Configuration Tool User Guide
· AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide
· AVEVA NET Data Processor Configuration Guide
· AVEVA NET Document Indexing Gateway User Guide

Release 5.0.9

Page 5

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.5

Product and Training Information

For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of

AVEVA's website (http://www.aveva.com/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA

Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

1.6

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact

the AVEVA Helpdesk (https://avevahelpdesk.aveva.com), and register a support incident.

After you register a support incident, we know your identity (from your customer ID) and

the details of the incident. We endeavour to fix the software issue or consider the

software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the status of

the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use the AVEVA

Helpdesk, which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact

the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).

Release 5.0.9

Page 6

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Quick Reference

2

Quick Reference

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide lists out the regular expressions
(Regex), including symbols, ranges, grouping, assertions and some sample patterns to get
you started.

2.1

Regular Expression Syntax

This can be daunting for beginners. A link to Tutorial and Reference is given at the end of

this document (see Additional Material). This document is intended for a quick reference

only.

2.2

Simple Searches

Regular Expression Description

[A-Z]

[A-F]

[a-z]

Matches any capital letter

Matches any capital letter between A and F

Matches any non-capital letter

[A-Z, a-z]

Matches any letter, capital or not

[abc]

[aeiou]

[^abc]

[0-9]

[^0-9]

\d

.

\w

Matches a, b or c

Matches any vowel.

Matches any character except a, b or c

Matches any digit

Matches anything other than a digit

Matches any number

Matches any character (number, letter or other character) except a
new line character

Matches any alpha-numeric character. Or type in the exact string to
be matched, for example: Pump Matches "Pump"

Release 5.0.9

Page 7

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Quick Reference

2.3

Looking for 0, 1 or more things

The following can apply to any expression using [A-Z]:

Regular Expresssion Description

[A-Z]

[A-Z]?

1 capital letter

0 or 1 capital letters, that is, indicates an optional expression

[A-Z]{1,4}

Between 1 and 4 capital 1 letters

[A-Z]{1,} or [A-Z]
+

1 or more capital letters

[A-Z]*

Gr[ae]y

0 or more capital letters

Matches both gray and grey but do not match Graay or Greey or
greay

This is just a simple example. Surround more complex repeating expressions with

brackets, for example: ([A-Z]\d{3}-){1,3}.

2.4

Look Ahead and Look Behind

Use the following to check before and after an expression, but do not include the before

and after bits of the expression in the result:

Regular Expression Description

(?<=[A-Z])

Look behind (before) for a capital letter

(?<![A-Z])

Look behind (before) for anything other than a capital letter

(?=[A-Z])

(?![A-Z])

Look ahead (after) for a capital letter

Look ahead (after) for anything other than a capital letter

2.5

Using the Not Character to Search

Use the following to find everything from the last \ to the end of string:

Regular Expression Description

\\[^\\]+$

Get last \ and everything after it

^[^\\]+\\

Get everything up to 1st '\\'

Release 5.0.9

Page 8

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Quick Reference

2.6

Special Characters

Regular Expression Description

^

$

\b

\t

\r

\n

\d

\

\w

\s

\b

Start of a line

End of a line

Beginning or end of a word

Tab

Carriage return

New line

Any digit. (Short for [0-9])

To search for a \ or a + character use \\ or \+ in your pattern (\ =
escape here)

Any alphanumeric character (digit or letter) (Short for [A-Za-z0-9])

White space (Short for [\t\r\n])

Word boundary

2.7

Negated Operators

Regular Expression Description

D

\S

\W

Any character that is not a number

Any character that is not a whitespace

Any non-alpha-numeric character

Regex tends to use the capital form of expressions to be the opposite of their lower case

alternatives.

2.8

Restricting Regex Search

Regex is by default greedy. For example, if searching for <Hello><World> using

"<.*>", <Hello><World> is returned. However, if only <Hello> is required, "<.*?>"

can be used. The ? makes the search stop at the first match as opposed to the last match.

2.9

ORs

The '|' character is used to search with ORs. [A-Z]{3}|\d{3} would therefore find

three capital letters or three numbers.

Release 5.0.9

Page 9

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Quick Reference

2.10

Shortcuts

It is time consuming to type in every character in a match. The following can therefore be

entered:

For three alphanumerics type: \w\w\w

If any number of alphanumerics are required, a range for the number of matches can be

specified:

Regular Expression Description

\w{1,3}

\w{,7}

\w{1,}

For one to three alphanumerics

For up to seven alphanumerics

For one or more alphanumerics

Symbols

Description

*

+

?

For zero or more (same as {0,})

For one or more (same as {1,})

Means optional (same as {0,1})

2.11

Regex Switches

(?i)[A-Z]*: The (?i) results in a case insensitive search on what comes after. This

example finds upper and lower case letters.

Note: Regex supports a number of switches that can change the search behaviour.

2.12

Use Round Brackets to Group a Match

Groups are captured and are available for use in replacements. They are used to help

specify exactly where operators lie, for example:

Set(Point)?: matches both Set and SetPoint.

The capture (capture number 1) contains nothing for Set and Point for SetPoint. There are

non-capturing groups if required: Set(?:Point)?

Release 5.0.9

Page 10

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Quick Reference

2.13

String Replacement

Used to replace any matched string with a literal or combination of literals and captured

text, for example:

Regular Expression Description

Expression:

Set(Point)?

Replacement:

Setting $1

Input:

Output:

Input:

Output:

SetPoint

Setting Point

Set

Setting

A $0 replacement matches the whole string.

2.14

The Pipe Character is used as an “or” Operator or Alternative

Plant|Marine matches either plant or marine. It is best to place them in a group:

· AVEVA operates in (plant|marine|oil and gas) fields.
· This would capture what area AVEVA operates in.

Note: Be careful when doing a match on object IDs. Assume a match for all pumps in

PLANT 1 is required. From the tagging standard, it is known that all pumps are tagged

with P-nnn, therefore: PLANT1|P-\d{3}: matches either "PLANT1" or anything

starting with "P-", including PLANT2|P-101 Escape the alternative operator "PLANT1\|

P-\d{3}".

Release 5.0.9

Page 11

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Regular Expressions Quick Reference Guide

CHAPTER 3 - References

3

References

The following are the references to Regular Expression design and some related

additional material.

3.1

Regular Expression Design

There is a simple utility in AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway. The following utility is

useful in testing your regular expressions. Use it with test data to debug when things go

wrong:

http://rad-software-regular-expression-designer.software.informer.com/1.4/

Note: Ensure that "Ignore Case" is selected if designing for the AVEVA NET Data Processor

Gateway.

3.2

Additional Material

A complete Regular Expression Tutorial and Reference can be found at:

http://www.regular-expressions.info.

Release 5.0.9

Page 12

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Part I - Introduction
13 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Preface
1
This chapter provides basic information about this guide, training and support.
About this Guide
1.1
This guide describes how to install, configure and deploy AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard.
Audience
1.2
The guide is intended for Systems Administrators responsible for installing AVEVA NET
Workhub and Dashboard.
It is assumed that you are familiar with the maintenance of a Windows network domain
through Active Directory and the following Microsoft Server software configurations:
· Internet Information Services (IIS)
· IIS 10 installation on Windows Server 2019
· IIS 10 installation on Windows Server 2016 
· Microsoft SQL Server
· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2014 -
https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/bb500469(v=sql.120).aspx 
· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2016 -
https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/bb500469(v=sql.130).aspx 
· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2017 -
https://www.microsoft.com/en-in/sql-server/sql-server-editions-express
Related Documents
1.3
· AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide 
· AVEVA NET Dashboard User Guide - Mobile Site
Product and Training Information
1.4
For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of
AVEVA's website (http://www.aveva.com/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA
Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support).
14 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Technical Support
1.5
If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact
AVEVA Helpdesk (https://avevahelpdesk.aveva.com), and register a support incident.
After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)
and the details of the incident. We will endeavour to fix the software issue or consider
the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the
status of the incident. 
If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA
Helpdesk for which you should have valid login credentials. For any other queries, contact
the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre (http://support.aveva.com/support). 
Product Overview
2
This chapter provides an overview of AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard.
About AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard
2.1
AVEVA NET Dashboard is a browser-based application for accessing the engineering data
stored in AVEVA NET Workhub. They are fundamental components of AVEVA's Project
Data Visualisation and Asset Visualisation Solutions.
AVEVA NET is a powerful web-based solution that supports users in organising, validating
and collaborating on asset data and documents irrespective of source and location,
providing the ability to link project teams situated in various locations via one unique
application. 
AVEVA NET allows information of any type and from any source to be stored and viewed
in a secure environment. Related information is automatically linked together to add
context, can be compared, navigated, visualised and reported upon to provide a single
environment to view and understand information from multiple systems.
AVEVA NET is a necessity for any organisation involved in designing, building or operating
complex engineering assets. It enables organisations to maintain a digital replica of their
asset throughout its lifecycle, and enables the information therein to be accessible to
support the many business processes which make up the life cycle of such an asset.
Getting Started
3
This chapter introduces the components and tools that make up AVEVA NET Workhub
and Dashboard, and gives an overview of the various methods of installation.
15 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Overview of Components
3.1
To get up and running you need to:
· Create and populate an AVEVA NET Workhub database.
· Set up an AVEVA NET Dashboard hosting environment.
· Configure an AVEVA NET Dashboard web application, accessed using a browser.
· Set up a File Store, used to securely store imported documents.
· Set up the Import Server, used to import data, documents and models.
· Set up a Job Server that enables the File Export job queuing and processing. 
· Set up AVEVA VizStream server and viewer, for viewing 3D models.
· Set up Accusoft PrizmDoc, for viewing 2D documents.
The interactions between these components and AVEVA Licensing are summarised in the
following diagram: 
16 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Tools used to install, configure and/or deploy
· Deployment Manager - used to install, configure and deploy AVEVA NET
Workhub and Dashboard. This tool creates an XML file that contains all the
information required by the other tools. Deployment Manager then uses this
information to run these tools in the required sequence to complete the
installation.
· AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Package - used to install the
tools.
· Database Manager - used to initialise the AVEVA NET Workhub database.
· Import Controller - used to configure the Import Server, and to load data and
documents into the AVEVA NET Workhub database.
· DMS Checker - used to check compatibility with Document Management Systems
(DMS).
· Replicate Tool - used to replicate the AVEVA NET Workhub database.
· AVEVA NET Enterprise Integration Adapter (EIA) - used for connecting third-party
enterprise systems to AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard via a Service-
Oriented Architecture (SOA).
· Admin Tool - used to administer an AVEVA NET Workhub database, inspect and
edit class libraries, objects and associations, create and edit user settings,
security, and access rights.
Sample Data
· Sample Data - IPE and SRP
· Reporting Sample
17 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Installation at a Glance
3.2
The easiest way to install, configure and deploy is to run the Deployment Manager using
the Basic Configuration, see Installing the Basic Configuration.
Depending on your requirements, you can also:
· Use Deployment Manager to load saved configuration files for your specific
needs, see Installing your own Configurations.
· Install, configure and deploy using individual tools and scripts, see Appendix A.
18 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Quick-Start Installation
3.3
Required Software
· Windows Server 2019, Windows Server 2016, Windows 8.1
· Microsoft .NET Framework 4.6.1
· Microsoft Visual C++ 2012 Redistributable Package (x64)
· Microsoft Visual C++ 2013 Redistributable Package (x64) 
· Microsoft Web Deploy 3.5 – complete install, including Web Deployment Agent
· SQL Server 2017, SQL Server 2016, SQL Server 2014
· Mixed-mode authentication enabled
· Express edition is supported (Express with Advanced Service is needed for
SSRS reports)
· Microsoft® Windows PowerShell Extensions for Microsoft® 
· AVEVA Licensing 4.1 (Client to set server list, server if you have your own license)
· AVEVA VizStream Server 6.1.7
· Requires Microsoft .NET Framework 4.6.1
· Accusoft PrizmDoc Server 13.10 and PrizmDoc Client 13.10
Note: For more information, see System Requirements.
Configure IIS and Install Web Deploy
1.
If IIS is not installed, use Add Features to install it, including:
· Security - Windows Authentication
· Application Development - ASP.NET 4.5
2.
Open IIS Manager, select Server, and then double-click Feature Delegation.
3.
Set Anonymous Authentication and Windows Authentication to Read/Write.
4.
You must install Web Deploy using either the Web Deploy installer or the Web
Platform Installer.
Note: For more information, see Configure IIS and Web Deploy.
19 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Install VizStream
· Proceed with the Defaults.
· Require Microsoft .NET Framework 4.6.1.
If VizStream is already installed, stop VizStream service, start Web Deployment Agent
service, start VizStream again.
Note: For more information, see Install VizStream Server.
Install Accusoft PrizmDoc Server and Client
See Install Accusoft PrizmDoc Server and Client.
Create Database
1.
Open SQL Server Management Studio.
2.
Create new database (for example, Workhub).
3.
Create new user (for example, Workhub).
4.
Select SQL server authentication.
5.
Clear the Enforce password policy check box (or use a strong password).
6.
For Default database, select Workhub.
7.
For User mapping, select Workhub, and then set the role db_owner.
Note: For more information, see Creating a SQL Server Database.
Set Licensing Server
1.
Run ALS Licensing Client Configuration Tool. (If UAC is on, right-click and
select Run as Administrator.)
2.
Enter the port number (default is 4545) followed by the name of your license
server. 
3.
Restart IIS.
Note: For more information, see Set Licensing Server.
20 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Create Deployment Configuration
1.
Run Deployment Manager.
2.
Click Next, and then click Basic Configuration.
3.
Enter SQL server details.
4.
If VizStream is installed, the values auto-populate. If not, enter data directory
and hostname.
5.
Select to load SRP sample data. 
Note: IPE takes longer time to load. Both will be configured in Import Server.
6.
Click OK, and save the configuration file.
7.
Click Deploy.
Note: For more information, see Installing the Demo (Basic Configuration).
Open Dashboard
Dashboard should be deployed to: http://localhost:16000/
21 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Installing Fix Releases
3.4
Periodically AVEVA releases a fix that we recommend is installed after the main release.
Visit the AVEVA Support Site at
http://support.aveva.com/support/United_Kingdom/download_search.asp?/services/up
grades/anwad/anwad_releases.asp to find and download the latest fix.
To install a fix release:
1.
Install the full release as per the instructions in this guide.
2.
Read the release letter for information about the fix release and its contents.
3.
Create backup copies of:
a.
Any customised .config files, located in the application root directory
(for example, C:\inetpub\Dashboard).
b.
Any customised Bootstrap files, see Changing the Out-of-the-box
Installation.
4.
Install the Update using either Deployment Manager (see Step 2: Global
Parameters), or via the command line: 
a.
Locate AVEVA.NET.Workhub.and.Dashboard.[ProductVersion].msp
on the release media.
b.
Click Start, Run, and then type cmd to open a command prompt window.
c.
At the prompt, run the following command:
%windir%\system32\msiexec /update [path to
AVEVA.NET.Workhub.and.Dashboard.[ProductVersion].msp]
reinstall=all reinstallmode=vamus /l*v c: \patchinstall.log
d.
Follow the on-screen instructions to install the release. You may be
prompted for the path to the original msi file for the full release.
e.
Upgrade any affected components, such as the database or the website
deployment, refer to the release letter mentioned in step 2.
f.
To redeploy the Dashboard site using Web Deploy:
i.
On the AVEVA NET Workhub Web Server, open IIS.
ii.
Expand the server instance node.
iii.
Expand the Sites node.
iv.
Right-click the AVEVA NET Dashboard site.
v.
Click Deploy, and then click Import Application.
vi.
Go to <Install Directory>/Dashboard, select the
AVEVA.NET.Dashboard.zip file, and then click Next.
22 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
vii.
Select the contents of the package, and then click Next.
viii. Enter the Application Package information, and then click Next.
ix.
When the package finishes installing, click Finish. 
5.
Refer to the files that you backed up in step 3:
a.
Re-apply any customisations to any newly installed .config files
b.
Replace any new default Bootstrap files with any (backed up)
customised versions.
Note:
· A fix release can be uninstalled separately from the full release.
· Only the latest fix release needs to be installed as it includes all fixes since the
previous full release.
· It is not necessary to uninstall an earlier fix release before installing the latest
one.
23 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Upgrading AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard
4
This chapter describes the procedures for upgrading from AVEVA NET Portal to AVEVA
NET Workhub and Dashboard and also from existing versions of AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard to newer versions.
Important:
· Before upgrading to AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.8 , ensure that both
Microsoft Visual C++ 2012 Redistributable Package (x64) and Microsoft Visual C+
+ 2013 Redistributable Package (x64) are installed on the AVEVA NET Workhub
and Dashboard web server.
· If you already have AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard installed, and you want
to redeploy, then you must specify the installation directory that was used
originally. To change the installation directory, you must first uninstall the
application. 
To create a new database or upgrade an existing database, the SQL Server user requires
db_owner privilege. This can be removed after the creation or upgrade activity is
completed. For security reasons, it is recommended to configure the SQL Server user to
run with a lesser level of privilege during normal operations.
Operation
Required Role
New database creation
db_owner
Upgrade existing database
db_owner
Normal operations
db_datareader, db_datawriter
Upgrading from 5.1.5, or latest patch, to 5.1.8 version of AVEVA NET is supported.
Note: Database migration from other versions is possible. For details, refer to the latest
Release Notes, which provide up-to-date information on additional upgrade paths that
are supported. 
24 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Upgrading from AVEVA NET Portal 4.7.7.x
4.1
To upgrade from AVEVA NET Portal 4.7.7.x:
1.
Create backup copies of the existing system, including:
a.
Any customised VNET.config files, located in the application root
directory (for example, C:\inetpub\Dashboard).
b.
The existing portal folder.
c.
Any existing databases using SQL Server Management Studio.
d.
The VizStream Data folder.
2.
Install AVEVA Licensing Server:
Note: In AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.0, AVEVA Licensing has
changed from Flex to AVEVA Licensing Server (ALS).
·
Install and configure the ALS client tool as necessary. See System
Requirements.
3.
Upgrade AVEVA VizStream:
a.
Uninstall any older versions of AVEVA VizStream Server.
b.
Install AVEVA VizStream Server 6.1.7.
c.
Verify that the Data folder contains the data that was backed up in Step
1.c; if not, manually copy the data.
4.
Uninstall AVEVA NET Portal:
a.
Uninstall AVEVA NET Portal.
b.
Remove the AVEVA NET Portal sites from IIS if you want to use the
same site name.
5.
Install prerequisites:
·
Check and install prerequisites. See System Requirements.
6.
Install AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.8:
·
Install the new release. See AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard MSI
Installer.
7.
For all existing databases, set the Recovery Model to Simple. This ensures that
the upgrade process does not fill the transaction log:
a.
Open SQL Server Management Studio.
b.
In Object Explorer, expand an instance of the SQL Server Database
Engine.
c.
Expand Databases, right-click the database, and then select Properties.
d.
Select the Options page. 
25 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
e.
For Recovery Model, select Simple.
f.
Click OK.
8.
Upgrade the database:
a.
Open Database Manager.
b.
Expand Server Instances, and then double-click the database you want
to update – if the server or database have not already been added, see
Managing Databases.
c.
Confirm the User Account (username) and type the Account Password
(password).
d.
Click Connect.
e.
In the Database not up to date window, click Yes. Progress is shown in
the Upgrade Status box.
f.
Wait for the database to update.
g.
If there are no File Server details, click Register, see Registering File
Store.
9.
For all databases, set the Recovery Model back to Full. Follow the instructions
in Step 7, but select Full rather than Simple for Recovery Model.
10. Migrate filesystem to File Store:
a.
Click Update Files - if this is disabled, register the File Store first, see
Registering File Store. 
b.
Select the Import Database Name, relevant Import Package details are
automatically filled in.
c.
For each Import Package:
i.
Select the package.
ii.
Ensure that the Update SVGs option is selected.
iii.
Click Update.
d.
If an Import Package contains Notes with attachments or markups,
select Manually Enter Package Details.
e.
For each Import Package containing Notes with attachments or
Markups:
i.
Select the package.
ii. In the Document Drop Folder box, enter the drop folder of packages
from Import Controller as used by the previous installation. For
example, C:\Program Files\AVEVA\NET\PortalData.
26 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
iii.  In the Document URL box, enter the URL of packages from Import
Controller as used by the previous installation. For example,
http://inhydvms8r2054:14000/VNETData/.
Note: The last forward slash is required for the update to work
correctly.
iv.  Click Update.
11. Update Bootstrap Files:
a.
Open Import Controller to view the existing packages. If any folders or
files are showing as deleted, then copy the backed-up data from the
staging area.
Note: 
· Do NOT update any existing, customised versions of
BootstrapClasses.xml or BootstrapSampleInstances.xml. 
· If you do not need the ANET Player Classes contained in the
BootstrapClasses.xml, you can remove them by deleting the classes
from the comment <!--AVEVA NET Player Classes--> till <!--END
AVEVA NET Player Classes-->.
b.
Update the default BootstrapSampleClasses.xml,
BootstrapSampleSearchCategories.xml,
BootstrapSampleViews.xml and
BootstrapSampleDataGroups.xml so they are compatible with any
existing, customised Class Library. See Searching Categories,
Configurable Views and Data Groups respectively.
c.
Optionally, if you want to use Search Filters, update
IPEBootstrapSampleDataGroups.xml and
SearchConfigurationExamples_null.xml so they are compatible
with any existing, customised Class Library. See Search Filters.
d.
Bootstrap the database, see Bootstrap the AVEVA NET Workhub
Database.
12. Deploy Site:
a.
Open Internet Information Services (IIS).
b.
If you have not deleted the AVEVA NET Portal site, create a new site
with a different port.
Note: The application pool used by the site must be set to use Microsoft
.NET Framework 4.6.1. 
c.
Deploy the AVEVA NET Dashboard site, see Web Deploy.
27 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
d.
If required, deploy the Accusoft PrizmDoc Viewer into the AVEVA NET
Dashboard site.
13. Apply customisations from the backed-up VNET.config. The information in
VNET.config (backed up in Step 1) can now be found in one of the following
two places:
· The web.config file, located in the application root directory (for
example, C:\inetpub\Dashboard)
· The Settings panel, see Updating AVEVA NET Dashboard Settings.
14. Configure the Notes and Sets panels.
Note: By default, AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard does not display Notes
or Sets in the Browse panel. Sets has its own dedicated panel that makes
interactions easier. Therefore, you should remove it as follows:
a.
Open the AVEVA NET Admin Tool.
b.
In the Class Library pane, search for BREAKDOWNNODE.
c.
In the Object of Class pane, select DefaultInformationBreakdown. 
d.
In the Manager pane, expand Attributes.
e.
Click Edit Object Attributes.
f.
In the Value field of the EXPANSIONRULE attribute, remove any SET
or NOTES classes.
15. To upgrade the SSIS package:
a.
Log in to the server, where the existing SSIS package is deployed.
b.
Open SQL Server Management Studio.
c.
In the Server type drop-down box, click the arrow, and then select
Integration Services.
The server name and credentials are auto-populated.
28 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
d.
Right-click the deployed package, and then click Upgrade Packages.
The SSIS Package Upgrade Wizard opens.
e.
Click Next.
f.
In the Select Source Location window, click the arrow in the Package
source drop-down box, and then select File System.
g.
In the Folder box, enter the path to the updated SSIS package location,
and then click Next.
h.
In all the succeeding windows populated with the default values or
options, click Next.
16. Verify the configurations selected in the summary, and then click Finish.
17. Go back to the Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio.
18. Right-click the deployed package, and then click Run Package.

Release 5.1.8

Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Upgrading from AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.x.x
4.2
You have two options for upgrading an existing installation of the AVEVA NET Workhub
and Dashboard to version 5.1.8:
· Using Deployment Manager - Use this if you have a deployment configuration
file that you used to deploy your previous 5.x version of AVEVA NET Workhub
and Dashboard, and this configuration still matches your current configuration or
is edited to match. You can also create a new configuration file to match your
current configuration using Deployment Manager.
· Using individual tools - Use this to perform the upgrade using individual tools,
such as the Installer (AVEVA.NET.Workhub.and.Dashboard.5.1.8.msi), Database
Manager and Web Deploy.
Important: 
· While upgrading from a version 5 installation, any new files you have added
are deleted, and all files delivered with the application are overwritten.
These files include:
· The web.config settings, such as additional Portal IDs
· The AVEVA.NET.Import.Server.exe.Config settings, such as
database related details 
Note: From 5.1.5 onwards, all the Import Controller related configurations
are available in the file AVEVA.NET.Import.Server.exe.Config under the
<aveva.net.import.server /> section. The default location for this file is
C:\Program Files\AVEVA\AVEVA NET\Import\Server.
· When upgrading to 5.1 or later using an earlier Deployment Configuration
file, if Windows authentication was used, then you must select the Use
windows authentication option. 
· Any custom extensions that have been deployed to the AVEVA NET
Dashboard website as a web deployment package must be redeployed after
upgrade. If Web Deploy was not used, then all custom files must be backed
up and reapplied after the upgrade.
· DO NOT copy previous versions of configuration files back into place, as
these files contain both user configuration and system configuration entries
which may have changed between versions.
30 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
To upgrade using Deployment Manager:
1.
If you have made any customisations to Windows Service configuration files
(that are not configurable within Deployment Manager) or made any changes
to the AVEVA NET configuration files (for example, web.config), create
backups so that you can re-apply these customisations after the upgrade.  
2.
Load your deployment configuration file into Deployment Manager:
a.
From your installation media, double-click the
AVEVA.NET.Deployment.Manager file.
b.
Click Next.
c.
On the Load Configuration page, select Load from file … and browse to
your deployment configuration file.
d.
Click Open.
Deployment Manager upgrades the configuration to the 5.1.8 format. 
e.
Check each page of the configuration to make sure it is correct, and then
click Next > to move to the next page. If necessary, add and configure
new features introduced in AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.8.  
f.
On the Save Configuration page, save your new configuration – do not
overwrite the old file as you might need it again. Click Next >.
g.
Run the upgrade: On the Deploy page, click Deploy.
3.
Re-apply the specific changes you made to the Windows Service configuration
files. 
4.
Re-apply any customisations, such as web.config settings. This includes any
additional Portal IDs, which have been overwritten as part of the upgrade. 
To upgrade using individual tools:
1.
If you have made any customisations to Windows Service configuration files
(that are not configurable within Deployment Manager) or made any changes
to the AVEVA NET configuration files (for example, web.config), create
backups so that you can re-apply these customisations after the upgrade.  
2.
Run the installer:
a.
From your installation media, double-click
AVEVA.NET.Workhub.and.Dashboard.5.1.8.msi. 
b.
On the Welcome, screen, click Next.
Your previous Destination Folder and set of features are maintained.
31 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part I - Introduction
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
c.
Check each page of the wizard to make sure it is correct, and then click
Next > to move to the next page. If necessary, add and configure new
features introduced in AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.8.
d.
Run the upgrade: On the Ready to install ... page, click Install.
3.
Re-apply the specific changes you made to the Windows Service configuration
files. 
4.
Run Database Manager and connect to your AVEVA NET Workhub database or
databases. Accept the prompt to upgrade the database to 5.1.8.
5.
Using the Dashboard.zip Web Deploy package (in the installation directory),
re-deploy the AVEVA NET Dashboard web application.
6.
Re-apply any customisations, such as web.config settings. This includes any
additional Portal IDs, which have been overwritten as part of the upgrade. 
7.
If you are using the Accusoft PrizmDoc Viewer, re-deploy the viewer package.
Note: Ensure that you have upgraded to the correct version of the Accusoft
PrizmDoc; if not, you must re-deploy the site. For information about how to
upgrade Accusoft PrizmDoc, see Appendix D: Upgrading Accusoft PrizmDoc.
8.
Configure any new features introduced in AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard 5.1.8.

Part II - Installation and Deployment
33 
Release 5.1.8

Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
System Requirements
5
This chapter gives details about the hardware and software requirements.
Installation on a Network
5.1
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard is designed to be distributed over several computers
on a network, the reasons for this include:
· Performance
· Scalability
· Security
Note: If you want to install just for demonstration or training purposes it might be easier
to do so on a single computer; although this is possible, it is not recommended.
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard works with any virtualisation software that is
supported by Microsoft, refer to http://support.microsoft.com/kb/957006.
So users can log on to AVEVA NET Dashboard, computers on the network must be part of
a domain managed by a Windows Domain Controller; this is usually a separate computer
set up specifically for this task.
34 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Example Server Topologies
5.2
There are several different combinations of servers that can be used, depending on a
number of factors, including, the installation overhead, the load on the Web Server, the
load on the Database Server and the access control model being used.
The following examples may help in determining your own topology:
Example 1 - Multiple Web Servers and One Database Server
· Multiple Web Servers give a better performance of web requests.
Example 2 - Pairs of One Web Server to One Database Server
· Increased performance of web requests and database requests.
· Increased installation complexity.
Example 3 - One Web Server and Multiple Database Servers
· Multiple Database Servers (for example, to split the data into different Assets or
Disciplines) give a better database performance.
Note: Example 3 is known as using "multiple instances", see Configuring Multiple
Instances.

Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Software Incompatibilities
5.4
The following software must not be installed on the same computer as the AVEVA NET
Workhub and Dashboard web server:
· CITRIX Metaframe (any version)
· Microsoft Exchange (any version)
· Microsoft Site Server (any version)
· Microsoft Office Server Extensions
· VizStream Collaboration 4.1.4
The following software applications must not be installed on the SQL Server Database
Server or on any machine that remotely administers the SQL Server database: 
· Lavasoft Ad-Aware Web Companion
· Lavasoft Ad-Aware Free Antivirus+
· Lavasoft Ad-Aware Personal Security
· Lavasoft Ad-Aware Pro Security
· Lavasoft Ad-Aware Total Security
· Any other Lavasoft Ad-Aware component
· Lavasoft AdBlocker
Installing and Setting-up the Requirements
5.5
This chapter provides further details about installing and setting up some specific
mandatory system requirements. 
User Privileges
5.5.1
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard authenticates with your Microsoft Windows logon
details.
You must have administrator privileges and must be logged on as an administrator, to
carry out the installation, deployment and configuration.
44 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Configure IIS and Web Deploy
5.5.2
For details on installing IIS, refer to:
· IIS 10 installation on Windows Server 2019
· IIS 10 installation on Windows Server 2016
When installing IIS, ensure that the following options are included:
· Security - Windows Authentication
· Application Development - ASP.NET 4.5
You must install Web Deploy using either the Web Deploy installer or the Web Platform
Installer. After installing Web Deploy, if VizStream is already installed, stop the VizStream
service, start Web Deployment Agent Service and then, start VizStream again.
User Authentication in IIS
AVEVA NET Dashboard uses Windows Authentication to verify the identity of users. This
means that, in IIS, Anonymous Authentication must be disabled and Windows
Authentication must be enabled. AVEVA NET Dashboard tries to change these settings
automatically at the server level using a section in the web.config file (found in the
application root directory).
However, by default, web applications are not permitted to alter their authentication
mode, and so, accessing AVEVA NET Dashboard in a browser may result in the following
error:
This configuration section cannot be used at this path. This happens when
the site administrator has locked access to this section using <location
allowOverride="false"> from an inherited configuration file.
45 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
To prevent this error, do either of the following two actions:
· Change the settings at the server level and allow AVEVA NET Dashboard to alter
its authentication mode:
1.
Open IIS Manager.
2.
Select the server, and then in the Management section, double-click
Feature Delegation.
3.
Set Anonymous Authentication and Windows Authentication to
Read/Write.
· Change the settings at the site level:
1.
Open IIS Manager.
2.
Select the AVEVA NET Dashboard application, then in the IIS section,
double-click Authentication.
3.
Set Anonymous Authentication to Disabled.
4.
Set Windows Authentication to Enabled.
5.
Open the web.config file and remove the following section:
<authentication>
<anonymousAuthentication enabled="false" />
<windowsAuthentication enabled="true" />
</authentication>
Install VizStream Server
5.5.3
To install the VizStream server:
1.
Using an account that has administrative privileges, log on to the computer
where you want to install the VizStream server.
2.
Run the VizStream Server Installation program and follow the instructions –
choose carefully where you install the server, because this will contain all of
the 3D data that the server will deal with.
After the server has been installed, there should be a service available on the computer
named “VizStream Server". If you are using Task Manager, you should see five processes
named “VSServer.exe”. Four of these processes are used for data encoding, running the
VizStream extensions, managing files and handling client connections. The fifth process is
used for monitoring the other four processes and restarting them if necessary.
To support the visualisation of 3D models in AVEVA NET Dashboard, the VizStream Server
requires access to AVEVA Licensing. This can be achieved by installing and configuring the
AVEVA Licensing Client Configuration Tool on the computer where the VizStream Server is
installed.
46 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
VizStream Server Recommendations
VizStream publishing and client connections make heavy use of the disk access. Thus,
anything which restricts disk performance, such as anti-virus and continuous backup
systems, can affect both publishing performance and connection performance.
To optimise performance, AVEVA recommends the following hardware and configuration:
· Deploy on a physical multi-core machine, not a Virtual Machine (VM). Although
use of a VM is not recommended if one is used then it should be configured for
high-performance disk access.
· Machine should be used for VizStream server purposes only and not used for
anything else such as IIS.
· X64 with plenty of RAM (~16GB).
· Use separate physical disks for OS, data/cache folder and TEMP.
· Exclude data/cache folder and TEMP path from anti-virus on-access scanning.
· Ensure that there is plenty of free space on TEMP (~50-100GB in the case of large
models processing in parallel) folder.
· Avoid network proxy server where possible, as this can interfere with direct TCP
connection and/or force HTTP instead.
47 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
Troubleshooting VizStream stability issues
If you experience stability issues when viewing 3D model files in the VizStream viewer,
you must verify the following:
1.
Client computers should have quality graphics cards with up to date graphics
drivers. Having an out-of-date graphics driver is by far the most common
cause of OpenGL crashes.
2.
User accounts on a client machine using the ActiveX viewer require access to
the “Microsoft Performance Counters” system. This is the default for admin
users; other users may require a change to the group policy. If VizStream is
unable to access these performance counters, it is likely to encounter out-of-
memory issues, which can cause the browser to crash. The solution is to add
all users to the “Performance Monitor Users” group. For more information,
see http://blogs.msdn.com/b/bclteam/archive/2006/09/08/746900.aspx.
AVEVA Vizstream ActiveX Viewer (Cab file installation)
The VizStream ActiveX Control now has two executable files - a 32-bit and a 64-bit. The
64-bit VizStream Viewer Control is deployed along with 32-bit cab files. The first time
when you open a 3D model, a pop-up appears at the bottom of the browser and after you
click Install, it asks for confirmation and then installs the cab files in the client machine on
the respective locations, such as:
· For X86 - C:\Program Files (x86)\AVEVA Solutions
Limited\Private\VizStream version
· For X64 - C:\Program Files\AVEVA Solutions Limited\Private\VizStream
version
Whenever the 32-bit or 64-bit cab files are deleted from the client machine, the browser
asks for reinstallation of the cab files. Alternatively, the cab files can be installed by their
respective executable files, which are available at the following default location:
C:\Program Files\AVEVA\AVEVA NET\Utilities\VizstreamViewer.
The 32-bit and 64-bit executable files create an external process and use a 32-bit or 64-bit
VizStream Viewer based on the executable file installed. The 32-bit viewer uses up to 1
GB and the 64-bit viewer uses up to 2 GB of the memory by default. For now, it is
mandatory to have both 32-bit and 64-bit executable files installed on the client. The 64-
bit control improves the performance with large 3D models in ActiveX. The models get
fully loaded and do not stop streaming at between 90-99%. Also, the models open
quicker and can be manipulated faster.

Install Accusoft PrizmDoc Server and Client
5.5.4
The PrizmDoc Server is a collection of services that helps in conversion of documents
from source file to SVG or raster, depending on the source document content, for display
in the Viewing Client. The conversion of documents into a common format is the key to
viewing many different file types in a single viewer. Content Conversion Services RESTful
API enables conversion of content from all supported formats into PDF or TIFF output.
Content Conversion Services also enable you to combine documents (or pages within
documents) from disparate formats into a new document.
PrizmDoc Application Service (PrizmDoc Client) provides to the viewer application-level
logic, such as, enabling document viewing through the PrizmDoc Server, saving and
loading of markup, handling opening of documents and creating viewing sessions. This
service works as a gateway between the Viewing Client and the PrizmDoc Server.
Note: 
· It is recommended that Microsoft Office Professional Plus 2019 is installed on the
Accusoft PrizmDoc Server along with an MSO licence. This results in a more native
rendering of Office documents by PrizmDoc and avoids certain formatting issues. To
apply the MSO licence, run as administrator the command
AVEVA.NET.Accusoft.Licence.exe MSO at a command prompt. The file
AVEVA.NET.Accusoft.Licence.exe can be found in the zip file for the release in the
folder: .\AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.7\Accusoft PrizmDoc 13.10 
· If the MSO license has been applied but Office is not going to be installed, then Office
documents will not display, so please revert the license back to using the LibreOffice
renderer. To apply the LibreOffice licence, run as administrator the command
AVEVA.NET.Accusoft.Licence.exe at a command prompt. The file
AVEVA.NET.Accusoft.Licence.exe can be found in the zip file for the release in the
folder: .\AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard 5.1.7\Accusoft PrizmDoc 13.10
· The Prizm and PrizmDoc Application Watchdog Windows Services are registered
during the installation. Both of them are installed with a default configuration and
require further steps to ensure that the server is secured.
To install Accusoft PrizmDoc Server and Client:
1.
Run the Server installer, provide a logon for the service.
2.
Close the licensing tool when it opens. 
3.
Run the AVEVA Licensing tool, and then start the Prizm Service.
4.
Run the Client installer, and provide a logon for the service.
Note: 
49 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
· Re-register is not required. 
· For more information about PrizmDoc Server and PrizmDoc Client, see
https://help.accusoft.com/PrizmDoc/v13.10/HTML/webframe.html#prizmdoc-
overview.html.

Configuring Windows Authentication
IIS must be configured as a reverse proxy to enforce Windows authentication for the
communication channel between the AVEVA NET Dashboard web server and the Accusoft
PrizmDoc Server and Client. Configuring Windows authentication for Accusoft PrizmDoc is
not required if one of the following is true:
· Accusoft PrizmDoc is installed on a separate server, and a firewall has been
configured to prevent external access to server and ports.
· Accusoft PrizmDoc is installed on the same server as AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard, and external access to any other ports has been blocked.
Contact your IT administrator regarding the necessary steps required to configure a
firewall to secure the PrizmDoc Server and Client. For more information about security,
see
https://help.accusoft.com/PrizmDoc/v13.10/HTML/webframe.html#Security_Guidance.h
tml.
Note: Usually, the PrizmDoc Services should not be exposed publicly, and the web tier
(Dashboard) alone should communicate directly with PrizmDoc. 
Important: If the Accusoft client viewer has not been deployed, the parameter
PrizmApplicationServicesPort can be configured during deployment of AVEVA NET
Dashboard. For details, see Installing your own Configurations.
To enable Windows authentication:
1.
From the Start menu, click Administrative Tools, and then click Internet
Information Services (IIS) Manager.
2.
In the Connections pane, expand the server node, right-click Sites, and then
select AddWebsite.
3.
In the Site name field, type PrizmDoc Proxy.
4.
For Physical path, type C:\inetpub\wwwroot\PrizmDocProxy.
5.
For Port, type 23000, and then click OK.
6.
Select the newly created website (that is, PrizmDoc Proxy) and double-click
the URL Rewrite icon under the IIS section.
7.
From the Actions panel, select Add Rule(s)....
8.
Under Inbound and Outbound Rules, double-click Reverse Proxy.
Note: If prompted to enable proxy functionality in ARR, click OK.
9.
For Inbound Rules, type localhost:3000 [this is the server entry point (SEP)],
and then click OK.
51 
Release 5.1.8
© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.
 
Part II - Installation and Deployment
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide
10. In the right pane, under the IIS section, double-click Authentication.
11. In the Authentication pane:
a.
Right-click Anonymous Authentication, and then click Disable.
b.
Right-click Windows Authentication, and then click Enable.
12. Navigate to the AVEVA NET Dashboard website directory.
For example: C:\inetpub\Dashboard
13. In the pcc.config file, look for the XML element,
<PrizmApplicationServicesPort>. 
14. Replace the port number 3000 with the new port number 23000.
15. To reset IIS, click Start, and then run IISRESET.
Note: If you see the following screen after opening a document in AVEVA NET
Workhub and Dashboard, that indicates the above settings are not supported. You
must disable Windows Authentication and enable Anonymous Authentication to
make it work with PrizmDocProxy site.
The Browser Console may display errors like the following:
