AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.2.9

Storage

This setting enables/disables the validation of a Document storage, for example, a paper,

CD/DVD or other media reference.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Set it to true when integrating data with AVEVA WorkMate EAM:
<ReferenceDataEnabledFor>
    <Storage value="true"/>
</ReferenceDataEnabledFor>

false

Set to false to specify a non-existing Storage value during, for example, Brownfield
upload data file.

6.2.10

System

This setting enables/disables Reference Data Management for Systems in AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager. The value for this setting can only be effective

when Reference Data Management is enabled for Assets.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

When the following configuration is set to true, the Systems tab enables when
managing any Asset through Reference Data management in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper
and AVEVA Change Manager:
<ReferenceDataEnabledFor>
    <System value="true"/>
</ReferenceDataEnabledFor>

false

The Systems tab become unavailable when managing any Asset through Reference
Data management in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

6.2.11

Tag

This setting prevents changing the value of Tags in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager.

Value

true

false

Expected Behaviour

The values are imported from AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard and can be
changed or updated during the bulk upload if required.

The values are shown in read-only format. Attempting to change the values
displays a message similar to the following: "tag(0): The Tag Number '%
TAG_NAME% is already in use."

true

false

The Tag Reference tab is unavailable on the Working Set Document Details

Value

true

false

Expected Behaviour

The Document Reference tab is enabled on the Working Set Tag Details page.

The Document Reference tab is unavailable on the Working Set Tag Details
page.

Tek Number

This setting configures the Tek Number, which is a part of equipment, and the reference

of which relates to a Tag data.

Value

true

false

<ReferenceDataEnabledFor>

</ReferenceDataEnabledFor>

Set to false to specify a non-existing TEK Number during, for example, Brownfield
upload data file.

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.3

Send Tag and Document to WHDB on Verification

The SendTagAndDocumentToWHDBOnVerification setting enables/disables sending

of Tags or Documents data to the backend system, like AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard, on verification. If you are using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager version 5.0.5, this setting appears differently for you. To apply this setting, you

will have to set the value for the SendTagToWHDBOnVerification and

SendDocumentToWHDBOnVerification parameter to true. Also, the WorkflowStatus

aspect is only introduced in version 5.0.6. Therefore, the dependencies this aspect will

not be applicable in version 5.0.5.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Add the setting to the <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration" .../> section.

For example:

<ApplicationSettings>
  ...
  <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration">
    ...
    <SendTagAndDocumentToWHDBOnVerification value="true" />
    ...
  </Group>
  ...
</ApplicationSettings>

false

Setting the value to false will disable sending of Tag to AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard.

Notes:

· A permissible attribute with aspect Attributes.WorkflowStatus should exist for

the Tag or Document class in Class Library.

· All the Tags in the Working Set should have the WorkflowStatus set to a value

with aspect Items.ForVerification.

· All the non-safety critical Documents in the Working Set should have the
WorkflowStatus set to a value with aspect Items.ForVerification.
· All the safety-critical Documents in the Working Set should have the

WorkflowStatus set to a value with aspect Items.AsBuilt.

· This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager versions

5.0.6 and above.

Release 5.0.11

Page 43

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.4

Send Tag and Document to WHDB Whilst Active

The SendTagAndDocumentToWHDBWhilstActive setting enables/disables sending of

Tags or Documents data to the backend system or Document Management System (DMS)

while the Working Set is in the Active status.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Add the setting to the <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration" .../> section.

For example:

<ApplicationSettings>
  ...
  <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration">
    ...
    <SendTagAndDocumentToWHDBWhilstActive value="true" />
    ...
  </Group>
  ...
</ApplicationSettings>

false

Setting the value to false will disable sending of Tags or Document to the DMS while the
Working Set is in the Active status.

Notes:

· A permissible attribute with aspect Attributes.WorkflowStatus should exist for

the Tag or Document class in Class Library.

· At least one Tag or Document in the Working Set should have the

WorkflowStatus set to a value with aspect Items.PartialUpdate.

· This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager versions

5.0.6 and above.

6.5

Send Document to DMS Whilst Active

The SendDocumentToDMSWhilstActive setting enables/disables sending of DMS while

it is in the Active status.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Add the setting to the <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration" .../> section.

For example:

<ApplicationSettings>
  ...
  <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration">

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

Value

Expected Behaviour

    ...
    <SendDocumentToDMSWhilstActive value="true" />
    ...
  </Group>
  ...
</ApplicationSettings>

false

Setting the value to false will disable sending of Document to the DMS.

Notes:

· A permissible attribute with aspect Attributes.WorkflowStatus should exist for

the Document class in Class Library.

· At least one Document in the Working Set should have the WorkflowStatus set

to a value with aspect Items.PartialUpdate.

· This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager versions

6.6

Send Document to DMS on Verification

The SendDocumentToDMSOnVerification setting enables/disables sending of

Document files to the Document Management System (DMS) after verification.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Add the setting to the <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration" .../> section.

For example:

<ApplicationSettings>
  ...
  <Group name="TIM.ApplicationProfile"
xmlns="TIM.Application.Configuration">
    ...
    <SendDocumentToDMSOnVerification value="true" />
    ...
  </Group>
  ...
</ApplicationSettings>

false

Setting the value to false will disable sending of Document to the DMS after verification.

Notes:

· A permissible attribute with aspect Attributes.WorkflowStatus should exist for

the Document class in Class Library.

· All the non-safety critical Documents in the Working Set should have the
WorkflowStatus set to a value with aspect Items.ForVerification.

· All the safety-critical Documents in the Working Set should have the

WorkflowStatus set to a value with aspect Items.AsBuilt.

· This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager versions

5.0.6 and above.

6.7

Processing Physical Files

The ProcessingPhysicalFiles setting enables/disables processing of physical files

associated with Working Sets.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

Set the value of the following parameter to true in the Application Factory XML file:

<ProcessingPhysicalFiles value="true"/>

false

Setting the value to false will disable processing of physical files during the processing of
Working Set.

6.8

Fetching Non-Checked Out Physical Files

The NonCheckedOutPhysicalFileFetching setting enables/disables the non-checked

out Document physical files of Working Sets. This setting affects only those Working Sets

that are in the New status.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value Prerequisites

Expected Behaviour

true

<ProcessingPhysicalFi
les value="true"/>

When the following parameter is set to true, you can click
Get Files to download physical files that are associated with
the non-checked out Documents into the currently active
Working Set:

<NonCheckedOutPhysicalFileFetching
value="true"/>

false <ProcessingPhysicalFi

les value="false"/>

Setting the value to false will not let you download the non-
checked out Documents into the currently active Working
Set.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.9

Enabling Physical Files Deletion

Use the PhysicalFilesDeletionEnabled value to enable/disable the physical file deletion

of Working Sets for AVEVA Change Manager. This parameter is available in

OwnerDomain\TIM.ApplicationFactory.xml.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

true

Expected Behaviour

The delete feature is enabled for Working Sets on the Working Set Manage
Document Details Files page. The State column is available on the page, which
shows the following statuses of each attachment for documents within the
Working Set:
· For the non-existing or reserved documents, or for a newly-added physical

file:
· The value in the State column is set to New.
· The physical file record is completely removed from the database after

pressing Delete.

· For the existing or checked-out documents:

· The value in the State column for all the physical files of the checked-out
documents is Unmodified. When the same document is uploaded, the
value is changed to Modified.

· The value in the State column when an existing file is deleted is Deleted. If

the same file is uploaded the value in the State column is Modified.

· The value in the State column when a file is added is New. If the same file

is deleted, its corresponding record is completely removed from the
database.

false

The delete feature is only available when the value in the State column is set to
New.

Release 5.0.11

Page 47

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.10

Enabling Release Mode

Use the ReleaseModeEnabled value to enable/disable the choice to lock/allow

modification of Tags or Documents in other Working Sets.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

true

Expected Behaviour

If the value set as true, the Release option will be available in the Partial Check
In page. It enables you to choose to Release in addition to Check In the
Tags/Documents:

· If you select the Release check box, the Check In check box is also

selected automatically.

· You can lock the Release check box, if you have released

Tags/Documents by mistake.

Important: Tags/Documents with errors cannot be checked in and

therefore released, until the errors are fixed.

· Once the Tags/Documents are checked in, the Check In option will be

disabled. You cannot revert a Check In action.

false

If this configuration is disabled, as soon as the Tag or Document is checked in, it
gets released and is available for modification.

6.11

Enabling Reactive Validation

Use the ReactiveValidationEnabled value to enable/disable the validation/revalidation

of the attributes within a Working Set when it is sent for validation during the brownfield

bulk upload job. The revalidation may be redundant when no changes are made to the

Tags and Documents.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

true

false

Expected Behaviour

If the value set as true, the application validates/revalidates the underlying
attributes only if their values or their Units of Measure (UoMs) have changed or
the attribute has any errors or warnings associated.

If the value is set to false, the validation/revalidation happens every time the
Working Set is sent for validation.

Release 5.0.11

Page 48

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.12

Checking for Non-Permissible Attributes

Use the checknonpermissibleattributes value to enable/disable the checking of the

non-permissible attributes within Tags or Documents.

Value

true

false

Notes:

Expected Behaviour

This is the default value. If the value is set as true, the application checks for
non-permissible attributes for Tags or Documents and, if found, displays an
error.

If the value is set to false, the application does not check for attributes.

·  This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.
· This feature does not check for attributes that are mandatory for creating Tags or

Documents, such as Area or Purchase Order Number.

6.13

Enabling Update of Data in Fast Track in XML

Use the EnableUpdateDataFastTraclXml value to enable/disable the sending of an

XML file to the DMS while performing Fast Track modification of Documents.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

true

false

Expected Behaviour

If the value is set as true, the application sends an XML into the OutBound
folder of your DMS.

This is the default value. If this configuration is disabled, AVEVA Change
Manager does not send an XML.

6.14

Enabling Document Discipline on Working Set

Use the DocumentDisciplineOnWorkingSet value to enable/disable selecting a

Document Discipline code for a Working Set you wish to process. For information, refer to

Manage Working Sets chapter in AVEVA Change Manager User Guide.

Note: This configuration setting is only applicable for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

required

optional

Expected Behaviour

If the value is set to required, the application will filter according to the type of
Documents that can be managed within a Working Set.

This is the default value. If the value is set to optional, the users have the
option of either selecting the Document Discipline or skipping the step.

not applicable

If the value is set to not applicable, the Working Set creation wizard does not
show this step.

Release 5.0.11

Page 49

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.15

Selecting Work Package on Working Set

Use the WorkpackageOnWorkingSet value to enable/disable displaying the Sub-

Project (or Work Package in the context of AVEVA WorkMate) related information on the

Working Set Details page of AVEVA Change Manager.

Note: This configuration setting is applicable for only AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

Status

Expected Behaviour

isEnabled

true

Displays the Sub-Project (or Work Package in the context of AVEVA
WorkMate) related information on the Working Set Details page.

false

Does not display the Sub-Project (or Work Package in the context of
AVEVA WorkMate) related information on the Working Set Details
page.

isMandatory true

false

Users of all Roles have to provide the Sub-Project (or Work Package
in the context of AVEVA WorkMate) related information in the
Working Set. When this value is set to true, the setting overrides the
value set in Modification Projects.

Is not mandatory for the users of all Roles to input the Sub-Project
(or Work Package in the context of AVEVA WorkMate) related
information in the Working Set. But, the Contractor still has to select
the Modification Project.

and partial update of physical files of non-checked out documents in an Active Working

Set against the DMS only (not against AVEVA NET).

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value Prerequisites

Expected Behaviour

true

<ProcessingPhysicalFi
les value="true"/>

Upload and then Partial Update to upload updated
physical files to the non-checked out Documents in an active
Working Set and update them against the DMS only:

<NonCheckedOutActivePhysicalFileUpdate
value="true"/>

false <ProcessingPhysicalFi

les value="false"/>

Setting the value to false prevents you from uploading the

6.17

Enabling Partial Update of Physical Files for Checked Out
Documents

The CheckedOutActivePhysicalFileUpdate setting enables/disables the partial update

of physical files of checked out documents in an Active Working Set against the DMS only

(not against AVEVA NET).

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value Prerequisites

Expected Behaviour

true

<ProcessingPhysicalFi
les value="true"/>

false <ProcessingPhysicalFi

les value="false"/>

If the following parameter is set to true, you can click Partial
Update to do a partial update of the physical files of the
checked out Documents in an active Working Set against the
DMS only:

<CheckedOutActivePhysicalFileUpdate
value="true"/>

Note: The SendDocumentToDMSWhilstActive setting
works only if the value for

active Working Set.

6.18

Disabling Automated Document Check-out Process

The CheckoutDocumentsOnWorkingSetActivation setting enables/disables the

automated check-out of Documents when the Working Set has been activated.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

false

If the following parameter is set to true (which is the default value), Documents are
automatically checked out when the Working Set has been activated:

<CheckoutDocumentsOnWorkingSetActivation value="true"/>

If the value is set to false, Documents are not automatically checked out on the
activation of the Working Set.

Release 5.0.11

Page 51

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.19

Enabling Workflow Status to Tag/Document Status Mapping

The WorkflowStatusToAvevaStatusMappingEnabled setting enables/disables the

mapping of the value of the Workflow Status attribute to that of the Tag/Document

Status attribute.

Value

Expected Behaviour

If the following parameter is set to true, the value of the attribute Tag\Document
Status of a checked out Tag/Document is set to the same value which the attribute
Workflow Status has when it is being updated:

<WorkflowStatusToAvevaStatusMappingEnabled value="true"/>

If the value is set to false (which is the default value), the Tag/Document Status
attribute does not depend on the Workflow Status attribute.

true

false

Note:

· If the configuration setting is set to true, the Class Library should have Tag Status and
Document Status enumeration lists with the aspects Enumerations.TagStatus and

Enumerations.DocumentStatus respectively. The mapping takes place when the

Workflow Status attribute is being updated and the system can find a Tag/Document

Status value having the same identifier as the identifier of the updated value of the

Workflow Status attribute.

· If a Tag has become decommissioned, the Tag Status attribute's value is updated to

Removed and the mapping cannot take place anymore.

· The mapping does not take place when a Tag/Document is being re-activated as the

Tag/Document Status can be set only to Active.

· The mapping does not take place for reserved/installed Tags or reserved Documents.

6.20

Enabling Tag Migration

The TagMigrationEnabled setting enables/disables the ability to move Tags from one

Working Set to another.

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

If the following parameter is set to true, the action menu item Move Tags is
available on the Working Set details page and Tags are allowed to be moved to
another Working Set:

<TagMigrationEnabled value="true"/>

false

If the value is set to false (which is the default value), the Tag migration feature is
unavailable.

Release 5.0.11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.21

Enabling Document Migration

The DocumentMigrationEnabled setting enables/disables the ability to move

Documents from one Working Set to another.

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

If the following parameter is set to true, the action menu item Move Documents is
available on the Working Set details page and Documents are allowed to be moved to
another Working Set:

<DocumentMigrationEnabled value="true"/>

false

If the value is set to false (which is the default value), the Document migration feature
is unavailable.

6.22

Disabling Partially Updated Document Revision Number Update

The PartiallyUpdatedDocumentRevisionNumberUpdateEnabled setting

enables/disables an update of the revision number of a reserved or checked out

Document whose details have been sent to the back-end system or DMS.

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA Change Manager.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

If the following parameter is set to true (which is the default value), the revision
number becomes editable after a reserved or checked out Document has been
submitted to the back-end system or DMS:

<PartiallyUpdatedDocumentRevisionNumberUpdateEnabled
value="true"/>

false

If the value is set to false, the revision number cannot be edited after a reserved or
checked out Document has been submitted to the back-end system or DMS.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Application Factory Settings: Owner Domain

6.23

The GreenfieldDataReservationEnabled setting enables/disables data reservation via

the Greenfield bulk upload process..

Note: This configuration setting is applicable only for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

Value

Expected Behaviour

true

If the following parameter is set to true (which is the default value), unknown Tags or
Documents can be reserved through the Greenfield bulk upload process:

<GreenfieldDataReservationEnabled value="true"/>

Note: The role Allocator is not available in this case and Allocators are not allowed to
access the application.

false

If the value is set to false, Tag or Document Numbers must be created by the Allocator
before submitting Data via the Greenfield bulk upload process.

CHAPTER 7 - WDA System Application Configuration

7

WDA System Application Configuration

These configuration settings are available in and can be accessed through the

TIM.WDA.SystemApplication.Config.xml file, which is available in

{systemApplicationConfigPath}.

7.1

Event Log Monitor Configuration

You must configure the Event Log monitor, which is to monitor events and do appropriate

actions according to the defined rules.

7.1.1

SMTP Listener Configuration

Use these configuration settings to configure an SMTP listener designed for sending e-

mail notifications when a new event long entry is registered.

Parameter
Name

Example Value

Notes

name

SMTP

The name of the listener.

handler WDA.EventLog
Listeners.Smtp
Handler,
{wdaInstallPat
h}
\Assemblies\Pr
ivate\WDA.Eve
ntLogListeners.
SmtpHandler.0
2.dll

serverAd
dress

serverPor
t

NA

25

smtpEnab
leSsl

false

smtpUser
Name

NA

smtpPass
word

NA

The name of the component, which is responsible for sending
e-mail messages and the full path to it.

The address of the SMTP server.

The port of the SMTP server. The parameter is optional. If it is
not specified, the port 25 is used by default.

A flag indicating whether Transport Layer Security (TLS)
protocol is used to make e-mail message sending secure. The
parameter is optional. If it is not specified, the flag's value is set
to false.

A user name used to access the SMTP server when
authentication is required. The parameter is optional. If it is not
specified, Anonymous access is used. The parameter can be
encrypted.

A password used to access the SMTP server when
authentication is required. The parameter is optional. If it is not
specified, Anonymous access is used. The parameter can be
encrypted.

CHAPTER 7 - WDA System Application Configuration

Parameter
Name

Example Value

Notes

smtpDom
ain

NA

A domain name used to access the SMTP server. The parameter
is optional.

The following is a sample of an SMTP Listener configuration:

<WDA.SystemApplication.Configuration
xmlns="WDA.SystemApplication.Configuration">
  ...
  <EventLogMonitor logName="WDA Applications" enabled="true">
    ...
    <Listeners sender="sender@email.com" receiver="reciever@email.com">
      ...
      <Listener name="SMTP" handler="WDA.EventLogListeners.SmtpHandler,
{wdaInstallPath}
\Assemblies\Private\WDA.EventLogListeners.SmtpHandler.02.dll"
serverAddress="mySMTP.server.com" serverPort="25" smtpEnableSsl="true"
smtpUserName="..." smtpPassword="..." smtpDomain="myDomain.com" />
      ...
    </Listeners>
    ...
    <!-- EventLog sources to be monitored  -->
    <EventSources receiver="reciever@email.com" enabled="true" listener="SMTP">
      <EventSource name="TechInfo Manager" />
      <EventSource name="TechInfo Manager WebServices" />
      <EventSource name="WDA System Application" />
      ...
    </EventSources>
    ...
  </EventLogMonitor>
  ...
</WDA.SystemApplication.Configuration>

CHAPTER 8 - Rebuilding Indexes on Databases

8

Rebuilding Indexes on Databases

For improved performance during data exchange, it is recommended that you rebuild

indexes on your AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard and AVEVA Change Manager

databases. The rebuilding of indexes speeds up the check-in or check-out of Working Sets

that contain huge amount of data.

To rebuild the indexes on the AVEVA Change Manager database:

1. Open Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio.

2.

3.

4.

In the Object Explorer, connect to your AVEVA Change Manager database.

In the query window, run the following query:

DECLARE @TableName VARCHAR(255)
DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(500)
DECLARE @fillfactor INT
SET @fillfactor = 80
DECLARE TableCursor CURSOR FOR
SELECT OBJECT_SCHEMA_NAME([object_id])+'.'+name AS
TableName
FROM sys.tables
OPEN TableCursor
FETCH NEXT FROM TableCursor INTO @TableName
WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
BEGIN
SET @sql = 'ALTER INDEX ALL ON ' + @TableName + ' REBUILD WITH
(FILLFACTOR = ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(3),@fillfactor) + ')'
EXEC (@sql)
FETCH NEXT FROM TableCursor INTO @TableName
END
CLOSE TableCursor
DEALLOCATE TableCursor
GO
To update the statistics, execute the sp_updatestats command in the query

window.

This rebuilds the AVEVA Change Manager database indexes.

To rebuild the indexes on AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard database, connect to the

database in Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio, and then repeat steps 2 through 4.

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

Welcome to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide.

This chapter explains how this guide is organised and introduces other sources of

information that can help you.

1.1

About this User Guide

This user guide contains the information you need to work with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper™

effectively. It contains information about the following:

· How to navigate through AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.
· How to manage Bulk Upload jobs, Working Sets, jobs with errors and successful

jobs completion.

· How to manage Bulk Upload jobs and the Working Sets connected to them. The
next action you should take with jobs which have errors identified or Working

Sets that have the jobs completed.

· How to validate the engineering data against defined information standards,

handover that data to Operations and generate various reports.

1.2

Summary of Chapters

Chapter Name

Product Overview

Getting Started

Description

Change Manager™.

Explains the assumptions and prerequisites before using
AVEVA NET Gatekeeper. It also tells how to log on,
access the Home Page, edit User Profile, and User Roles.

Bulk Upload Data Objects

details.

Send the Uploaded Data for
Verification

Verify the Working Set and
Check the Changes

Tells you how to send the Working Set, created as part of
Bulk Upload job processing, for verification to
Information Managers after the data have been
uploaded and the quality level of the data meets your
requirements, to request approval/verification for
further publishing.

Explains how to complete the verification process of the
Working Set sent for verification after the content of the
Working Set have been approved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This chapter gives an overview of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

2.1

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper is a part of AVEVA’s Information Management solution and a key

component of AVEVA’s implementation of the Digital Information Hub concept. It

provides the essential function of loading information into the Digital Information Hub

and ensuring that this information complies with industry and operating standards.

For engineering contractors, this saves the time and cost overhead involved in identifying

and correcting non-compliant information and creates a platform for informed decision

making. It is a key enabler for streamlined handover of high-quality asset information to

an Owner Operator. For asset operators, it ensures that information created or updated

throughout the asset’s working life remains trustworthy to support safe and efficient

operation. In combination with AVEVA Change Manager, it is also the foundation for a

legacy data clean-up program. AVEVA NET Gatekeeper integrates directly with AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard™ and is capable of automatically importing thousands of data

objects in a single upload.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper validates the integrity of incoming information against

configurable, defined criteria, whether applicable industry standards such as ISO 15926 or

enterprise-specific ones. Levels of validation compliance can be defined according to

appropriate criteria. Non-compliant data can be rejected automatically or accepted for

import but flagged for review and resolution. This enables bulk information uploads to be

performed efficiently and any errors or inconsistencies managed by exception, providing

robust control of data quality without unnecessary hindrance to workflows. AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper also enables phased information handover from contractor to operator by

enabling efficient business processes during both project execution and asset operations,

by monitoring automated data uploads.

Release 5.0.11

Page 8

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper provides not only information validation, but valuable additional

capabilities, including:

· A formal process for information acceptance by an information manager.
· Streamlined data loading through the identification and loading of only

information which is new or changed since the previous import.

· Full reporting of compliant and non-compliant information.
· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

Release 5.0.11

Page 9

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3

Getting Started

This chapter introduces you to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper. Specifically, this chapter provides

information on the following topics:

·

·

Log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

Access the Home Page

· Menus and basic page components
·

Edit User Profile

·

User Roles

3.1

Assumptions and Prerequisites

Assumptions

This document assumes that AVEVA NET Gatekeeper have been installed and deployed by

following the instructions provided in the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager Installation Guide. It also assumes that AVEVA NET Gatekeeper has been

configured for you to use in accordance with the requirements, procedures and policies

of your company by the authorised personnel.

Prerequisites

In preparation for using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper the following prerequisites should be

met:

· The same version of the Class Library is uploaded into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper
and AVEVA NET Workhub™ and it contains the definitions for the required

Assets.

· Reference Data are available, for example, Assets, Systems and Disciplines.
· The user account has been created for you by the system Administrator and you

have been granted the required permissions.

Release 5.0.11

Page 10

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.2

Start and Log on

Client Software Requirements

Requirement

Component
Operating Systems · Microsoft® Windows® 8.1
· Microsoft® Windows® 10

Web browsers

· Microsoft® Internet Explorer® 9, 10, 11
· Microsoft® Edge®
· Google Chrome™ browser

To start and log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper:

1. Use a web browser to enter the appropriate URL for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

2. On the log on page enter your User Name and Password. If your user

credentials are already saved on the log on page, click Login.

Note: If you do not have permission to access AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, you can

request permission from the system Administrator by clicking Request Access.

3.

To change the language and user locale settings, expand Options and then

select a value from the following drop-down lists:

· Language
· Format date/time & currency

4.

To save your user credentials in the web browser so that you don't have to re-

enter them every time you log on, select the Remember me option.

5.

Click Login.

Note: This language and user locale settings changes the language of the pages in AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper and user locale for a single session. If you want to keep the same settings

across sessions, you need to edit the Manage My Profile page after logging on.

Release 5.0.11

Page 11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3.3

Home Page

The AVEVA NET Gatekeeper Home Page is the first page you will see after log on. If no

Class Library upgrades are in progress the first page all users, including Administrators,

are redirected to is the Home page. This page is used to facilitate navigation to other

pages on AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and is divided into four area, each containing different

menus that list the functions, tasks and links. The AVEVA NET Gatekeeper Home Page and

the functions available are configurable and depend on a user's Role. If you are unable to

access any of the features described in this guide, contact your system Administrator for

configuring it and granting access rights.

3.3.1

Basic Page Components

When using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, you should be familiar with the following page

components:

Components

Description

Title Bar

The title bar on the top of every pages indicates the name of the
page or the particular step the user has to perform. It also displays
the AVEVA logo, and the links to the Home Page, My Profile, and
Logout.

Information Icons

These display a summary of a menu or functionality when you
move the pointer over them.

Reset button

To discard any unsaved changes and stay on the current page, click
Reset. If you click Reset after clicking Save or Close, you cannot
discard the changes on a page.

Save button

To save the changes and stay on the current page, click Save.

Close button

Cancel button

Back button

If you have applied all the changes on the particular page and want
to return to the Home Page / previous page, and also save all the
changes at the same time, click Close. To return to the previous
page if you do not want to make any changes on a specific page,
click Close.

If you do not want to perform a particular function, task, step,
process on a page and return to the Home Page or previous page,
click Cancel.

To return to the previous page or step, click Back. The Back button
is available on the pages where you need to make a selection or
the pages are part of multi-step wizard.

Confirm button

To confirm/accept/verify a particular action on the final page/step
of the wizard, click Confirm.

Next button

To continue to the next page/step of the wizard when your
confirmation is required, click Next.

button

If you want to search for a particular item or information in the

If you want to check which step you are at and how many steps are

Edit User Profile

Your User Profile contains basic information about you and can be updated after you log

To update your profile information:

1.

2.

In the Manage My Profile page, you can edit the following:

3.5

User Roles

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

To manage individual users access to functions, features and tasks in the application, the

user privileges are organise by user Roles. A Role is a set of permissions that are granted

to a user thus controlling access to application modules and workflow processes. Each

Role also determines the authorisation granted to a user to perform particular functions

and operations, for example, complete the Working Set verification.

All users that have a specific Role assigned inherit the privileges of that Role. Since any

user may have more than one Role, the user inherits the set of the privileges of those

Roles.

The following table provides a summary of Roles in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper:

Role Name

Responsibility

Administrator Members of this Role are the only users who have access to

Administration page. Members of this Role can manage users, Role,
Reasons (for Void and Cancel Verification) as well as update the Class
Library version.

Super User Members of this Role have access to Manage Job pages to review the

status and the progress for all the Bulk Upload Jobs.

Information
Manager

Members of this Role are the only users that are allowed to do the
verification process. This Role have access to the following process:
Complete Verification and Cancel Verification.

Contractor

Allocator

Members of this Role can access all Working Sets. Members of this Role
can upload Tags and Documents using Bulk Upload functionality.

Members of this Role are the only users that are permitted to reserve
and check in Tag or Document numbers. They are permitted to create a
Working Set manually in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and reserve Tags or
Documents.

Note: When a Tag or Document has been reserved, it cannot be modified
anymore.

Release 5.0.11

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

including Contractors

· Send Working Sets for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

4

Bulk Upload Data Objects

The main functional aspect of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper revolves around loading and

validation workflow, allowing for only trusted information to reach the engineering

information store.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper can be customised and configured in such a way to determine

how the Bulk Upload jobs are processed. For more information, see AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide and AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide.

This chapter explains how to manage a Bulk Upload Job and view details.

4.1

Supported File Format

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper is designed to simplify and improve the process of uploading the

information from external applications, staging, transformation and preparation for

handover, supporting a standardised Class Library.

AVEVA Net Gatekeeper supports the following four file formats only for Bulk Upload job:

File Format

Description

XML file

.MDB file

Tab-delimited
files

In accordance with the predefined BU XML Schema.

Microsoft® Access® Databases files of the predefined structure.

It is not a file but several tab-delimited text files with predefined
structure that must be archived (.zip) in order to be uploaded using
Bulk Upload functionality. Extensions should be added in the Class
Library being used, to configure the import and export of the data
using AVEVA Gatekeeper Staging (tab delimited) file format to
BulkUpload xml format Transformer that enables import and export
using tab-delimited text files.

XLS/XLSX file

Microsoft® Excel® files of the predefined structure. For more
information, see Microsoft Excel Support for Bulk Upload and
Download.

Release 5.0.11

Page 17

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

4.2

Upload Tags and Documents

Using a data set, data objects related to new Tags and Documents, can be imported

through Bulk Upload. Modified data for existing Tags and Documents can also be

imported though Bulk Upload to a Working Set which exists in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper.

You need to ensure that all the Tags and Documents that you need to apply modifications

to are added or reserved in the Working Set. You cannot apply changes to the objects

which are not part of the Working Set.

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard, AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change

Manager accept up to 450 characters in the Tag# and Document# name fields, whereas

AVEVA WorkMate accepts only 64 characters (this is by design). When you enable the

integration with AVEVA WorkMate, and have a greater number of characters in the

respective name fields, your name records appear truncated. To avoid data loss, it is

recommended that you limit the number of characters in the naming and numbering

rules.

After the upload process is initiated and confirmed, the Bulk Upload Job will be created

and queued. When the job processing is in progress, the Working Set is locked. After the

successful processing of the Job, all Tags and Documents which are new or which have

been modified since the previous upload, will be added to the Working Set. The Working

Set also gets unlocked and users can further process the Working Set, based on the

requirement. The objects that have not been modified will be skipped during the

uploading process and will not be added to the Working Set. The number of skipped

objects will be reflected in the statistics displayed for the Bulk Upload job.

To upload data, to modify existing Tags/Documents or related to new Tags/Documents:

1. Go to the Home Page, click Bulk Upload Tag & Documents, and then click

Choose File (Browse in the Internet Explorer or Edge) in the file upload box.

2.

In the file explorer, navigate to the data file folder, select the file and then

click Open.

Note: The file should be in one of the supported formats and not already in

the upload process. The size of the file to upload can be configured, by default

the maximum file size is 100MB.

3.

Click Next to proceed with the upload.

· Partial – If Partial, the imported Tags/Docs can be imported again via

owned by” association is added to the AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard, so only the same Project Owner can modify these

Tags/Docs.

· Complete – If Complete, the imported Tags/Docs cannot be imported

again in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper. The ownership is handed over.

8.

You will be redirected to the Home Page and a upload processing message

(similar to the following message) will be displayed under the title bar: "Job

was created and added to a queue for further processing."

Limitation with Unicode characters and AVEVA WorkMate integration

AVEVA WorkMate does not support Unicode Characters, such as the Cyrillic alphabet. So,

while a user can author items with these additional characters, this functionality should

not be used where AVEVA WorkMate integration is implemented.

Limitation with Unicode characters and Excel or Tab-delimited file

If uploading a tab-delimited file with Unicode characters, you must save the file by

selecting Save As and then selecting the file type as Unicode Text (*.txt) before uploading

to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper. This does not affect files that do not use Unicode characters.

4.3

Check the Progress of the Created Job

A user assigned the Role of Super User, can view the status of any on-going Bulk Upload

jobs in order to ensure that upload of the data supplied by the contractor is going

smoothly and that there is no blocking issues with the ongoing jobs that would prevent

the whole queue of jobs from processing. The job status also displays the number of

processed Tags, Documents, Systems, and Disciplines. You can also view the details of

completed and cancelled Bulk Upload jobs.

To check the status and progress of an ongoing Bulk Upload Job:

1. Go to the Home Page, on the Other Tasks menu, click Manage Bulk Upload

Jobs.

2. On the Queue tab, in the Number column, click a Job Number. The Queue tab

displays the jobs that are currently in the queue for processing.

3. Under Selected Job, view the status and progress of the specific job and do

the following:

· Check the current status information of an ongoing job on the Status
bar. After a job is processed, it is moved from the Queue tab to the

Historical tab. To review the details/statistics of a job after it is

processed, click a Job Number on the Historical tab.

· If there are any errors identified during the job processing, the Show

Error Message link is displayed in the Action menu:

· Click Show Error Message to view the information about the

error.

Release 5.0.11

Page 20

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

· You will be shown different suggestions depending on the error.
If some of the data does not validate, you will be required to

inspect the data by navigating to the Working Set Details page.

· Click Ok to close the error message.

· Click the Working Set number link, to review the status and contents of

the uploaded data on the Working Set Details page.

By default the status and progress information will be automatically refreshed every one

minute. You can use the Refresh Now button to refresh the status of all jobs on demand.

To set the automatic refresh time, edit the value in the number box for the Refresh Now

button.

Ongoing jobs status list

Status
Name

New

Description

After a job is created and added to the Jobs list on the Queue tab, the
specific job is marked New.

In Progress Once the job is picked up for processing, the status of the specific job is

changed to In Progress. The Working Set will be automatically created to
process the objects from the uploaded file. A Working Set number will be
assigned to that specific job.

Completed After the job is processed, the status of the specific job is changed to

Complete.

Failed

If any unrecoverable error occurs while processing the job, the status is
marked as Failed.

The jobs list on the Historical tab are marked with the following status:

· Completed
· Cancelled

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper also supports Drop folder for the Greenfield Bulk Upload jobs.

You can use the following settings in the TIM.DataExchange.ImportService import service

to automate the process. For the Greenfield Bulk Upload jobs, the application supports

only the XML file for the Drop folder:

· Partial upload is the default scenario only when projectOwnerName is present

in the Bulk Upload input file. For Complete upload specify

updateMode="Complete".

· The import path for the Greenfield Bulk Upload is configurable. For example: C:
\DocumentManager.TIM\Import. The import paths for the Brownfield Bulk

Upload and Greenfield Bulk Upload should be different. Keeping the same path

may incorrectly trigger the import services simultaneously, which will result in

erroneous data being pushed into the applications. Ensure that the NETWORK

SERVICE account has the required permissions for AVEVA NET Gatekeeper to

access the C:\DocumentManager.TIM\ImportGK\<CustomerName>

folder.

4.4

Check the Metadata of the Objects in the Working Set

In AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, a Working Set is created after the processing of a Bulk Upload

Job starts. To open Working Set details page:

1. Go to the Queue tab and then in the Number column, click a job number.

2. Under Selected Job, click the Working Set number to open the Working Set

Details page.

Based on configuration settings, some of the fields on the Working Set Details page may

be disabled / read only / unavailable.

4.4.1

View Tag Details

Open the Tag Details page:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and then click the Tags tab, to review the

state of the objects.

2. On the Tag# column, click the Tag Number, to open the Tag Details page.

Notes:

· The number in brackets displayed on the Tags tab indicates the number of the

Tags associated to a Working Set.

· The Tag Details page is read-only, and information in the fields cannot be edited.
If you want to update any specific field, you have to re-upload the data using Bulk

Upload.

· The Document References tab will usually be empty as the link between
Documents and Tags will be registered in the Tag References tab under

Documents Details page.

Release 5.0.11

Page 22

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

Tags list page Column description

Column and
Action

Description

Tag#

Displays the Number of the Tag.

Tag
Description

Fill %

State

Provides a brief description about the Tag.

It gives an indication of the number of the populated attributes for the
Tag.

The State column reflects whether or not the Tag has been added to
(into) the engineering information store. The following states are
displayed:

· New: Indicates that this Tag is newly reserved in the context of
this specific Working Set and further verification will have to

happen to publish it to the engineering information store.

· Failed: Not added to the engineering information store. A failed
state usually means that there are errors with the classification

of a Tag, or issues with the classification of one or many of the

attributes for the Tag.

· Checked In: This state means that the Tag has passed validation
without any critical error or failure and has been published

successfully to the engineering information store.

· Checked Out: This state of a Tag indicates that the updates have

been applied to an existing Tag successfully and further

verification will have to happen to publish it to the engineering

information store.

· Not Checked Out: This state for the Tag indicates that no
updates have been applied yet to that existing object.

Release 5.0.11

Page 23

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

Column and
Action

Is Used

Action

Error

Description

This column is disabled in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper for all users but
Allocators. The Allocator must select this option to indicate that the
reserved Tag Number needs to be published into the engineering
information store.

The value in this column will indicate if the uploaded Tag has been
voided or put on hold.

The Error column identifies if a Tag has validation errors or not. If any
validation errors have been reported for the Tag it will be flagged
correspondingly. Significant errors in data, for example related to
classification, will result in a Tag being failed, marked with Failed state.
Tags with errors on the attribute values, such as an attempt to provide
string value wherever a decimal value is expected or the value not listed
in the list of predefined values for the attribute is provided, will result in
the attribute value being ‘stripped off’ from the Tag. This will not
prevent the Tag from loading. All Tags that are not in Failed state will be
updated in the engineering information store. The published updates will
contain only the valid information, the updates flagged as having the
validation errors will not be published into the engineering information
store. Therefore, a Tag can be in the Checked In state but have errors.

Release 5.0.11

4.4.2

View Document Details

Open the Document Details page:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and then click the Documents tab, to

review the state of the objects.

Note: The number in brackets displayed on the tab indicates the number of

the Documents associated to a Working Set.

2. On the Document# column, click the Document Number, to open the

Document Details page.

Note: The page is read-only, and information in the fields cannot be edited. If

you want to update any specific field, you have to re-upload the data using

Bulk Upload.

Documents list page Column description

Column and
Action

Displays the number of the Document.

State

The State column reflects whether or not the Document has been added

displayed:

· New: Indicates that this Document is newly reserved in the

context of this specific Working Set and further verification will

have to happen to publish it to the engineering information

store.

· Failed: Not added to the engineering information store. A failed
state usually means that there are errors with the classification

of a Document, or issues with the classification of one or many

of the attributes for the Document.

· Checked In: This state indicates that the Document has passed
validation without any critical error or failure and has been

published successfully to the engineering information store.

· Checked Out: This state of a Document indicates that the

updates have been applied to an existing Document successfully

and further verification will have to happen to publish it to the

engineering information store.

This column is disabled in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper for all users but

The Error column identifies if a Document has validation errors or not. If
any validation errors have been reported for the Document it will be
flagged correspondingly. Significant errors in data, for example related to
classification, will result in a Document being failed, marked with Failed
state. Documents with errors on the attribute values, such as an attempt
to provide string value wherever a decimal value is expected or the value
not listed in the list of predefined values for the attribute is provided,
will result in the attribute value being stripped off from the Document.
This will not prevent the Document from loading. All Documents that are
not in Failed state will be updated in the engineering information store.
The published updates will contain only the valid information, the
updates flagged as having the validation errors will not be published into
the engineering information store. Therefore, a Document can be in the
Checked In state but have errors.

4.4.3

View History Log

You can view the history log of all the processes a Working Set has completed on the Log

page.

Open the History Log, go to the Working Set Details page and then click the History tab.

4.4.4

Download Working Set

You can download copies of the Working Set details in various file formats, for example

Working Sets that contain Tags with Document references, and save it on your local

machine or an accessible network location.

To download copies of the Working Set details:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page. On the Action menu, click Download

Working Set.

2. On the Download Working Set dialog box, in the File format list, select one of

3.

the available file formats.

Select one of the following:
· Tags and Documents
· Tags only
· Documents only

4.

Select one or all of the following download options:
· Include References – Use this to download Working Sets containing Tags

and Documents with their respective references, if available.

· Include empty Attributes – Use this to generate a list of all attributes

applicable for the class. This would be helpful for dealing with Engineering

Contractors.

· Exclude obsolete Attributes – Use this to exclude all obsolete attributes
from the output file for simplification of the further file editing process. If

you do not select this option, only empty obsolete attributes are excluded

while non-empty ones can be downloaded and updated.

5.

Click OK, to download a copy of the Working Set details in the selected file

format and save the file. If you want to cancel the download of the Working

Set details, click Abort.

Release 5.0.11

Page 27
