AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Bulk Upload Data Objects

4.5

Microsoft Excel Support for Bulk Upload and Download

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper supports Microsoft Excel (Excel) for the Bulk Download and

Upload functionalities. You can use the application-generated Excel file to download and

upload changes to multiple Tags, Documents, Tag References and Document References

that correspond to the same Working Set.

The following are the details:

· Each Class, as defined by the Class Library, has its own worksheet.
· A worksheet contains grouped Tags and Documents. The Document

References are listed within the Tag-To-Document References worksheet, and

Tag References, within the Document-To-Tag References worksheet.

· By design, the application stops the upload process when it encounters an
empty row. Therefore, if your worksheets have an empty row after the

headings, the application reports an error.

· Worksheets that do not match any Class names are ignored by the application

during the upload process.

· Each worksheet has a structure for classifying the information. Each attribute

has a dedicated column where:

· Attributes can contain multiple values.
· A value for each attribute is listed along with its unit of measure (UOM).
· Multiple values and their respective UOMs are separated by a space.

Note: Obsolete attributes are grouped at the end of the worksheet,

marked in yellow and show a tooltip. If the attribute has the extension

<tim:ExcelBulkDownload ../> with the property hidden set to true,

the respective column is not to be included in the output file.
· The Tags, Documents and Reference worksheets are colour coded:

· Purple – Tags worksheets
· Blue – Documents worksheets
· Red – Reference worksheets

Release 5.0.11

Page 28

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

5

Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

Based on user Role and AVEVA NET Gatekeeper configuration, after the successful

completion of the job processes, the Working Set is listed under specific status. If AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper is configured to process the Working Set automatically for the users who

are authorised to verify the information, for example Information Manager, the Working

Set will be listed under the following status:

· Complete status, if the data does not have any validation errors reported or,
· For Verification status, if there are any errors reported for the uploaded data

during validation process.

If there is validation error, a user with Information Managers Role have to review the

reported errors and complete the verification manually (though the objects are already

checked in). The users who have not been assigned the Role of Information Managers,

manual approval of the uploaded data is required. They have to follow the standard

verification process involving Information Managers to approve publishing the data. The

level of automatic processing of the Working Set can also be configures. For example,

through configuration settings, you can enable Silent upload for Information Manager

Role, to process the Working Set to Complete Status, ignoring any reported errors.

Allocator must create a Working Set manually to reserve Tag or Document Numbers as

stated in Creation of Working Set by Allocator.

5.1

Check the Working Set Status

To check the current status of the Working Set connected to a ongoing Bulk Upload job:

1. On the Other Tasks menu, click Manage Bulk Upload Jobs.

2. On the Queue tab, in the Number column, click a Job Number.

3. Under Selected Job, click the Working Set number link, to open the Working

Set Details page.

4. On the Status bar check the Working Set current status.

A Working Set can be in only one status at a given point in time. The Status bar also

displays the next process/task that are needed for further successful publishing of the

data. In some cases this is automated process but sometimes it requires the actions from

the users. If the Job processing is in progress the Working Set is locked. After the job is

completed, the Working set gets unlocked and available for the users to process the

working Set further, as per the requirement.

Release 5.0.11

Page 29

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

The following list of statuses represents the state of a Working Set at a particular point:

· New
· Active
· For Verification
· Verification Complete
· Complete
· Void

5.2

Generate Reports on a Working Set

You can generate reports on a specific Working Set to identify the list of Tags and

Documents, validations errors, changes/modifications, completeness status.

To generate report for a Working Set:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and then click the Reports tab.

2. On the Reports list page, click one of predefined report.

List of Reports

Report Name

Description

Working Set Tag
Report

Generates a report providing list of Tags associated to a
specific Working Set.

Working Set Document
Report

Generates a report providing list of Documents associated to a
specific Working Set.

Working Set Validation
Error Report

Generates a report providing list of validations errors in a
specific Working Set.

Working Set Validation
Error Details

Generates a report providing list of validations errors in a
specific Working Set into a CSV format file.

Working Set Summary Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and

changes done for the objects (Tags and Documents) associated
to a specific Working Set.

Working Set Tag
Details

Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and
changes to a Working Set Tags.

Working Set Document
Details

Generates a report summarizing all additions, deletion and
changes to a Working Set Documents.

Working Set
Completeness

Generates a reports detaining Tag data completeness, of a
specific Working Set, for each system, discipline and class.

Working Set Attribute
Completeness

Generates a report detaining Tag data completeness of a
specific Working Set, per attribute, providing the number and
the percentage of Tags that do not have a value for the given
class/attribute combination.

Release 5.0.11

Page 30

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

5.3

Bulk Upload Jobs with Errors

If there are any errors identified during the Bulk Upload job processing, the Show Error

Message link is displayed in the Action view for the specific Job. The job will have the

status Completed but the Working Set will have Active or For Verification status. If the

Working Set is in Active status that means that it is required to send it to the Information

Manager for verification. If the Working Set is in For Verification status already that

means that the Working Set is already under the review of the Information Manager who

just need to complete the verification after reviewing all the reported errors.

Examine Working Set Details

To indicate that a particular Tag or Document has errors, the corresponding Error box on

the Working Set Tags or Documents list page will be marked as selected. You can move

the mouse over the Error box of a particular Tag or Document to view the related error

message. Under Attributes check if any attribute is marked in different colour, move the

pointer over the attribute to view the related error message. On the Tag and Document

Details page, an error message is also displayed on the top notification bar. All Tags and

Documents that are not in Failed state will be updated in the engineering information

store. The published updates will contain only the valid information, the updates flagged

as having the validation errors will not be published into the engineering information

store.

5.3.1

Resume Job

Sometimes it could be useful to try to resume the job that has stopped progressing

before cancelling it, especially in the cases when the Job was stopped processing due to

infrastructure issues, not related to the data itself.

To resume a Bulk Upload job:

1. Go to the Home Page and on the Other Tasks menu, click Manage Bulk

Upload Jobs.

2. On the Queue tab, in the Number column, click a Job Number that has

stopped progressing.

3. Under Selected Job, check the details about the specific job.

4. On the Action menu, click Resume Job.

Release 5.0.11

Page 31

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

5.3.2

Cancel Job

You can cancel a Bulk Upload job and void the Working Set due to failures or errors. If a

Bulk Upload Job could not be completed for any specific reason, the job status will be In

Progress or Failed.

To cancel a Bulk Upload job:

1. Go to the Home Page and on the Other Tasks menu, click Manage Bulk

Upload Jobs.

2. On the Queue tab, in the Number column, click a Job Number that you want

to cancel.

3. Under Selected Job, check the details about the specific job.

4. On the Action menu, click Cancel Job.

Note: Changes that have been already checked in cannot be reverted.

After you cancel the Bulk Upload Job, the status of the job changes to Cancelled and

connected Working Set changes to Void. The uploaded changes will be reverted. All the

invalid Working Set are viewable in the Voided Working Set list on Manage Working Sets

page.

5.3.3

Transfer the Working Set

You can transfer a Working Set to assign the Working Set back to the originator (for

example, the Contractor who initiated the Bulk Upload job) or the Information Manager

to review and rectify errors in the Tags or Documents.

Note: The Allocator only can transfer a Working Set to another Allocator.

To transfer a Working Set:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page and on the Other menu, click Transfer.

2. On the List of available users, in the Display Name column, click a new owner.

3. On the Transfer the Working Set to a new owner confirmation page, you can

enter a message for the new owner which will be added to the auto-generated

e-mail delivered to the e-mail address associated with the select user.

4.

Click Confirm, to transfer the Working Set.

Release 5.0.11

Page 32

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

5.4

Send Working Set for Verification

A Working Set with an Active status can be sent for verification to the Information

Manager or Allocator. The status of the Working Set changes from Active to For

Verification after it is sent for verification.

Note: The Allocator only can send a Working Set for verification to himself or another

Allocator. Before sending the Working Set for verification, it must be validated using the

Validate Working Set option and must have no errors.

To send a Working Set for verification:

1. Go to the Details page of an Working Set with Active status. On the Status

bar, you can check the Working Set status, which would be marked as Active.

2.

Click For Verification.

3. On the List of available users, in the Display Name column, click a user, to

select the corresponding person as an Information Manger/Allocator to verify

the data of the Working Set.

4. On the Send the Working Set for verification confirmation page, you can

enter a message for the Information Manger/Allocator which will be added to

the auto-generated e-mail delivered to the e-mail address associated with the

Information Manger user profile.

5.

Click Confirm to send the Working Set for verification.

Release 5.0.11

Page 33

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 5 - Send the Uploaded or Reserved Data for Verification

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

5.5

Creation of Working Set by Allocator

If you are an Allocator, to create a Working Set:

1. On the Home Page, on the Manage Working Sets menu, click Create.

2. On the Available Assets list, in the Asset column, select the Asset Code with

which the Working Set being created is associated.

3.

Based on the configuration settings, do the following:
· On the Available Project Owners list, in the Code column, click the required

Project Owner code.

· On the Available Disciplines list, in the Discipline column, click the required

Document Discipline code.

· On the Confirmation page, in the Title box, type the Working Set title. This is

a required field.

· If the Available Project Owners list is not enabled, in the Project Owner

box, type the Project Owner code. This is also a required field.

Note: All other required fields are auto-populated based on the user selection

following the steps of the Working Set creation wizard. You can change the

previously selected values by navigating to the corresponding step of the

wizard by clicking the Back button.

4.

After you have entered or changed the value for all the required fields, click

Confirm, to confirm creation of the Working Set.

You will be redirected to the Working Set Details page in case of successful

creation of a Working Set. The Working Set Number will be auto-generated. A

message similar to the following one will be displayed under the title bar:

A new Working Set was created. You may now continue to work on
this Working Set here.

Release 5.0.11

Page 34

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Verify the Working Set and Check the Changes

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

6

Verify the Working Set and Check the Changes

A user assigned the Role of Information Manager or Allocator can verify the contents of

the Working Set sent for verification. The Information Manager or Allocator finalises the

upload or checks in Tags/Documents reserved by publishing the data into integrated

back-end systems, that is AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard. The status of the Working

Set changes from For Verification to Verification Complete after the verification process

is complete.

6.1

Complete Verification of Working Set

To complete the verification of the Working Set:

1. Go to the Home Page.

2. On the Ongoing Tasks menu, expand the Working Set for Verification list. You

can also click Manage Working Set and then click the Working Set for

Verification tab.

3.

Click the Working Set link you have to verify.

4. On the Status bar, you can check the Working Set status which would be

marked as For Verification.

5. On the Working Set Details page do the following, to check the metadata of

the objects in the Working Set:

· Click the Tags tab and then click on the Tag Number, to open the Tag

Details page.

· Click the Documents tab and then click on the Document Number, to

open the Document Details page.

6.

7.

8.

9.

Click Complete Verification to confirm the verification of the Working Set.

Read the information provided on the confirmation page.

Click Confirm, to confirm completion of the Working Set verification.

You will be redirected to the Home Page and a check in processing message

(similar to the following message) will be displayed under the title bar:

Example: Check in of the current Working Set is now being processed.

The update data are pushed into back-end systems in the background and due to this

process initiation the Working Set gets locked. You have to re-open the Working Set

Details page or Working Set list pages to check the status of the check in. After the check

in is finished, the Working Set is unlocked and the status of the Working Set changes to

Verification Complete.

Release 5.0.11

Page 35

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Verify the Working Set and Check the Changes

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

6.2

Check the Data in AVEVA NET Dashboard

To check the data in AVEVA NET Dashboard:

1. Go to the Home Page and then click Manage Working Set.

2.

Click the Verified Working Sets tab to access the list of Working Sets with the

status Verification Complete. If you cannot find a specific Working Set use the

Search Box.

3. On the Number column, click the Working Set Number,

4. On the Working Set Details page, do one of the following:

· Click the Tags tab and then click the information icon on the Tag
Number, to open a specific Tag on the AVEVA NET Dashboard.
· Click the Documents tab and then click the information icon on the
Document Number, to open a specific Document on the AVEVA NET

Dashboard.

5. On the AVEVA NET Dashboard you can do the following:

· The Tag Summary page is displayed with the information about the Tag.

You can review the updated data.

· Click the Association icon to view the association information

added to the Tag.

· has owner association is added to the all the objects uploaded
through AVEVA NET Gatekeeper when projects delivery mode is

specific as Partial. Reference to the Project Owner specified

during the upload will be displayed.

· The Document details is displayed. You can review the updated data:

· Click on the Properties icon to see the details about the uploaded

Document.

· Click the Association icon to view the association information

added to the Document.

· has owner association is added to the all the objects uploaded
through AVEVA NET Gatekeeper when projects delivery mode is

specific as Partial. Reference to the Project Owner specified

during the upload will be displayed.

· is a document for association will be added to all the documents
that have references to the Tags. It will contain the Tag that you

added the reference to during upload.

Release 5.0.11

Page 36

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

CHAPTER 6 - Verify the Working Set and Check the Changes

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

6.3

Cancel Verification of Working Set

The verification of a Working Set with a For Verification status can be cancelled by the

Information Manager or Allocator. The status of the Working Set changes from For

Verification to Active after the verification is cancelled. The Working Set is sent back to

the user who requested the verification for any changes or review.

To cancel the verification of the Working Set:

1. Go to the Home Page.

2. On the Ongoing Tasks menu, expand the Working Set for Verification list. You

can also click Manage Working Set and then click the Working Set for

Verification tab.

3.

Click the Working Set link you have to verify.

4. On the Status bar, you can check the Working Set status which would be

marked as For Verification.

5. On the Working Set Details page do the following, to check the metadata of

the objects in the Working Set:

· Click the Tags tab and then click on the Tag Number, to open the Tag

Details page.

· Click the Documents tab and then click on the Document Number, to

open the Document Details page.

6.

Click Cancel Verification for cancelling the verification of the Working Set.

7. Under Return the Working Set to the owner, select a reason from the

Cancellation reason drop down list. This is a required field.

8.

You can enter a message for the user, which will be added to the auto-

generated e-mail delivered to the e-mail address associated with the user

profile.

9.

Click confirm to confirm cancellation of the Working Set verification.

After you have cancelled the verification, the status of the Working Set changes to Active.

Release 5.0.11

Page 37

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Data Handover

7

Data Handover

During the course of the design or building phase of the project, AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

supports iterative uploads of the project-owned data. This is final stage of delivering the

latest changes to the project data after which the data from designing and building phase

are handed over to the Owner Operator for starting maintaining the designed Asset or

the Platform. A user assigned the Role of Information Manager, can upload such data

related to the Tags and Documents. The file should be in one of the supported formats.

7.1

Bulk Upload Data File

To upload data for modifying existing Tags/Documents or new Tags/Documents using

Bulk Upload:

1. Go to the Home Page, click Bulk Upload Tag & Documents, and then click

Browse... in the file upload box.

2.

In the file explorer, navigate to the source file folder, select the file and then

click Open.

Note: You should not upload a file which is already in the upload process. The

size of the file to upload can be configured, by default the maximum file size is

3.

4.

100MB.

Click Next to proceed with the upload.

Review the information under Details Information and File Content retrieved

from the contents of the data file that you have uploaded.

5.

In the Project Delivery box, as this data is for final delivery on handover, select

6.

7.

8.

9.

the Complete mode.

Click Confirm to start with the upload.

Read through the information displayed on the Confirm Handover to

Operations message box which is shown when executing the upload for the

final handover.

Click Ok to confirm the upload.

You will be redirected to the Home Page and a upload processing message

(similar to the following message) will be displayed under the title bar:

Example: Job was created and added to a queue for further processing.

Note: After you have confirmed the upload of the data, to check the progress of the job,

on the Queue tab, click the Job Number. Under Selected Job view the status and progress

of the specific job.

Release 5.0.11

Page 38

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Data Handover

7.2

Check the Data in AVEVA NET Dashboard

To check the data in AVEVA NET Dashboard:

1. Go to the Working Set Details page, do one of the following:

· Click the Tags tab and then click the information icon on the Tag
Number, to open a specific Tag on the AVEVA NET Dashboard.
· Click the Documents tab and then click the information icon on the
Document Number, to open a specific Document on the AVEVA NET

Dashboard.

2. On the AVEVA NET Dashboard you can do the following:

· The Tag Summary page is displayed with the information about the Tag.

You can review the updated data:

· Click the Association icon to view the association information

added to the Tag.

· Notice that there is no has owner association for the Tag which

has been handed over.

· The Document details is displayed. You can review the updated data:
· Click the Association icon to view the association information

added to the Document.

· Notice that there is no has owner association for the Document

which has been handed over.

Release 5.0.11

Page 39

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Documentation Centre

8

Documentation Centre

The Documentation Centre menu has to be configured to a company or plant

requirements. It will contain links to predefined locations. The System Administrator can

configure the required locations and available content.

When you move the pointer over a specific link on the Documentation Centre menu,

Information about the document message is displays which provides a brief statistics

about a particular link. The Documentation Centre may contain links to Documents that

include the Company Policy, describes processes or steps to be followed by the user, and

other types of Documents related to the application. It may also contain links to internal

and external systems.

The Administrator can edit the DocumentationCentre.xml file located under the

application installation path, such as "\Customer\Website\XmlData\", to update the

Documentation Centre links.

Release 5.0.11

Page 40

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Report Centre

9

Report Centre

The Report Centre contain the links for the following reports:

· Working Set Overview
· Working Set Documents and Tags
· Working Set Tag list

On the Report Centre menu, you can also view a summary of the report by moving the

pointer over a specific report link.

9.1

Generate Working Set Overview Report

The Working Set Overview report lists all existing Working Sets on a specific Asset with a

specified Status. This report helps in monitoring existing Working Sets. To run this report

you have to specify the criteria for filtering and limiting the report output.

To generate a Working Set Overview report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Overview.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the overview of the

Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Select the Status for the Working Set Status from the Status list, that

you would like to be included in the report.

3.

Click Finish to generate the report in a separate window against the provided

report criteria.

Release 5.0.11

Page 41

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Report Centre

9.2

Generate Working Set Documents and Tags Report

The Working Set Documents and Tags report lists all the Documents and Tags for all

Working Sets within a specific Modification Project (shown as an AFA Number). This

report helps to monitor the information processed in the Working Set for any

Modification Project. To run this report you have to specify the criteria for filtering and

limiting the report output.

To generate Working Set Document and Tags report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Documents

and Tags report.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the information about

the Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Specify the AFA Number from the AFA Number list, to get the

information about the Working Sets within the selected Modification

Project (AFA).

3.

Read the Note text and then click Finish to generate the report in a separate

window against the provided report criteria.

Release 5.0.11

Page 42

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Report Centre

9.3

Generate Working Set Tag List Report

The Working Set Tag List report lists Tags processed in all Working Sets within a specific

Modification Project and System. This report helps in monitoring the list of available Tags

and their criticalities. To run this report you have to specify the criteria for filtering and

limiting the report output.

To generate Working Set Tag List report:

1. On the Home Page, on the Report Centre menu, click Working Set Tag List

report.

2. On the Report Query page, under Report Criteria, do the following:

· Select the Asset code from the Asset list, to get the information about
the Tags processed in the Working Sets available for the specific Asset.

· Specify the AFA Number from the AFA Number list, to get the

information about the Tags processed in the Working Sets within the

selected Modification Project (AFA).

· Specify the System from the System list, to get the information about

the Tags processed in the Working Sets of the specific System.

3.

Click Finish to generate the report in a separate window against the provided

report criteria.

Release 5.0.11

Page 43

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI-enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

22

© 2024 AVEVA Group Limited or its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Комплексная автоматизация на базе технологий AVEVA
AVEVA Plant  – интегрированная линейка программных продуктов, предназначенных для эффективной организации и выполнения проектирования и поддержки инжиниринговых задач.
AVEVA NET    – управление инженерными данными – поддержка реализации проектов капитального строительства.
AVEVA ERM   – комплексная система управления процессом строительства (комплектация, закупки).

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI -enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

www.aveva.com

© 2025 AVEVA Group Limited or its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1

Preface

Welcome to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide. This

chapter explains how this guide is organised and introduces other sources of information

that can help you.

1.1

About this Guide

This document provides instructions on how to install or upgrade AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper™ and AVEVA Change Manager™. This document describes installation

considerations, pre-deployment tasks, using the AVEVA Application deployment tool to

complete the deployment and post-deployment tasks. The document also describes

creating and configuring a SQL Server Database before installing either AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager. Additionally, this document contains the

information on upgrading the database and installing the latest version of AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

Release 5.0.11

Page 5

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

Page 6

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.3

Audience

This guide is intended for anyone responsible for installing or upgrading AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

To use this document, it is assumed that you are familiar with the maintenance of a

Windows network domain (through Active Directory) and the following Microsoft server

software configurations:

· Internet Information Services (IIS):

· IIS 8.5 installation on Windows Server 2012 R2 -

http://www.iis.net/learn/install/installing-iis-85/installing-iis-85-on-windows-

server-2012-r2

· IIS 10.0 installation on Windows Server 2016

· Microsoft SQL Server:

· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2012 - http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-

us/library/bb500469(v=sql.110).aspx

· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2014 - https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-

us/library/bb500469(v=sql.120).aspx

· SQL Server installation for SQL Server 2016 - https://docs.microsoft.com/en-

us/sql/sql-server/what-s-new-in-sql-server-2016

1.4

Related Documents

· AVEVA Change Manager User Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper User Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Administration Guide
· AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Configuration Guide

Release 5.0.11

Page 7

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 1 - Preface

1.5

Product and Training Information

For information about product training courses, see the Product Training section of

AVEVA's website (https://www.aveva.com/en/training), or contact the nearest AVEVA

Regional Support Centre (https://www.aveva.com/en/support).

1.6

Technical Support

If you encounter software issues or have suggestions for software improvements, contact

AVEVA Knowledge & Support Center (https://softwaresupport.aveva.com), and register a

support incident.

After you register a support incident, we will know your identity (from your customer ID)

and the details of the incident. We will endeavour to fix the software issue or consider

the software improvement suggestion as soon as possible, and notify you about the

status of the incident.

If you are an accredited user of the AVEVA products, you are entitled to use AVEVA

Knowledge & Support Center for which you should have valid login credentials. For any

other queries, contact the nearest AVEVA Regional Support Centre

(https://www.aveva.com/en/support).

Release 5.0.11

Page 8

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2

Product Overview

This chapter gives an overview of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

2.1

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper is a part of AVEVA’s Information Management solution and a key

component of AVEVA’s implementation of the Digital Information Hub concept. It

provides the essential function of loading information into the Digital Information Hub

and ensuring that this information complies with industry and operating standards.

For engineering contractors, this saves the time and cost overhead involved in identifying

and correcting non-compliant information and creates a platform for informed decision

making. It is a key enabler for streamlined handover of high-quality asset information to

an Owner Operator. For asset operators, it ensures that information created or updated

throughout the asset’s working life remains trustworthy to support safe and efficient

operation. In combination with AVEVA Change Manager, it is also the foundation for a

legacy data clean-up program. AVEVA NET Gatekeeper integrates directly with AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard and is capable of automatically importing thousands of data

objects in a single upload.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper validates the integrity of incoming information against

configurable, defined criteria, whether applicable industry standards such as ISO 15926 or

enterprise-specific ones.Levels of validation compliance can be defined according to

appropriate criteria. Non-compliant data can be rejected automatically or accepted for

import but flagged for review and resolution. This enables bulk information uploads to be

performed efficiently and any errors or inconsistencies managed by exception, providing

robust control of data quality without unnecessary hindrance to workflows. AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper also enables phased information handover from contractor to operator by

enabling efficient business processes during both project execution and asset operations,

by monitoring automated data uploads.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper provides not only information validation, but valuable additional

capabilities, including:

· A formal process for information acceptance by an information manager.
· Streamlined data loading through the identification and loading of only

information which is new or changed since the previous import.

· Full reporting of compliant and non-compliant information.
· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

Release 5.0.11

Page 9

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

2.2

AVEVA Change Manager

AVEVA Change Manager is part of AVEVA’s Information Engineering category of products

within the AVEVA Enterprise portfolio. It provides the essential means for robust and

visible management of the continual changes to information in the digital asset which

accompany the many changes – large and small – made to the physical asset during its

lifecycle.

Throughout an asset’s working life, both in normal operations and during projects such as

brownfield modifications or data clean-up exercises, many different users need to access

and change technical information that originates from a variety of sources. Not only must

this information remain both accurate and available, it must be maintained so by the

rigorous control of any changes made to it. Achieving this requires standardised tools,

business processes and procedures for controlling changes to information and

synchronising them across multiple business systems.

AVEVA Change Manager enables Owner Operators to optimise, control and monitor their

change management process for all technical information. It can be customised and

tailored to your needs. Combined with AVEVA NET, the solution highlights information

changes to its users, enabling better-informed decision making. Implemented as a

combined solution with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, users are able to validate new and legacy

data against configurable, predefined information standards before making controlled

updates using AVEVA Change Manager. By assuring information integrity, AVEVA Change

Manager delivers increased operational efficiency and regulatory compliance on all types

of plants.

Release 5.0.11

Page 10

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 2 - Product Overview

The key features of AVEVA Change Manager:

· Full management of changes to technical information created by Modification

Projects and work packages.

· Reservation of new Tags and Documents resulting from changes entered directly

in AVEVA Change Manager or by an upload process.

· Validation of all data against information handover specifications, external

application registers and code tables.

· Support for ISO 15926.
· Class library driven, multi-lingual.
· Efficient information search functions.
· Fast-track capability for expediting urgent changes without the need for a formal

approval process.

· Configurable best-practice workflows to support complex business processes.
· Integration with AVEVA NET for browsing, navigating, contextualising and

visualising the entire information asset.

· Integration with Enterprise Asset Management (EAM), Document Management

(DM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, whether AVEVA’s or third-

party.

· Tools to provide configurable reports and key performance indicators on data

quality.

· Role-based user access control, enabling internal and external organisations to

manage large quantities of information in an orderly manner.

· Full audit trail of all changes.
· Better-informed decision making, leading to greater efficiency in both routine

asset operations and brownfield projects.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 3 - Getting Started

3

Getting Started

This chapter provides an overview of all the installation steps and the order in which you

should perform them.

The AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager installation process consists of

the following steps:

1. Review the system requirements

2. Run the AVEVA WDA Framework Installer

3. Create a SQL Server Database and a database user

4. Run the Common Files Installer

5. Use AVEVA Application deployment tool to configure deployment parameters

6. Run deployment of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager

7. Complete post-deployment tasks

8. Start using AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager

The chapter also gives an overview of the steps required to upgrade AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager, which typically include the following steps:

1.

Prepare to Uninstall the Existing Version

2. Uninstall the Existing Version

3.

Install the New (Upgrade) Version

CHAPTER 4 - System Requirements

4.3

Installing PowerShell Extension for SQL Server

If you are intending to deploy AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager from

a machine other than where Microsoft SQL Server is installed, you will need to install the

PowerShell Extension for SQL Server so that the deployment tool can communicate with

the SQL Server.

The PowerShell Extension for SQL Server requires you to download the necessary files

from the Microsoft SQL Server Feature Pack collection page for your respective SQL

version. To install the PowerShell Extension for SQL Server:

·

For the appropriate SQL Server version, download and install the three feature

packages in the order they are listed:

· Microsoft SQL Server 2012 (https://www.microsoft.com/en-

us/download/details.aspx?id=29065):

1. Microsoft® System CLR Types for Microsoft® SQL Server® 2012

(SQLSysClrTypes.msi)

2. Microsoft® SQL Server® 2012 Shared Management Objects

(SharedManagementObjects.msi)

3. Microsoft® Windows PowerShell Extensions for Microsoft® SQL

Server® 2012 (PowerShellTools.msi)

· Microsoft SQL Server 2012 SP1 (https://www.microsoft.com/en-

us/download/details.aspx?id=35580):

1. Microsoft® System CLR Types for Microsoft® SQL Server® 2012

SP1 (SQLSysClrTypes.msi)

2. Microsoft® SQL Server® 2012 SP1 Shared Management Objects

(SharedManagementObjects.msi)

3. Microsoft® Windows PowerShell Extensions for Microsoft® SQL

Server® 2012 SP1 (PowerShellTools.msi)

Release 5.0.11

Page 16

· Microsoft SQL Server 2014 (https://www.microsoft.com/en-

us/download/details.aspx?id=42295):

CHAPTER 4 - System Requirements

4.5

System Architecture

The following diagrams display the flow of data and information in AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager:

Engineering Data Warehouse Pre-Processor

Engineering Data Warehouse (EDW) Pre-Processor™ is not an integral part of the system

architecture on a day-to-day basis. It is responsible for the production of input data files

in a format that is recognised by AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager

based on customer-specific data and is usually used in greenfield scenarios.

System Architecture with EDW Pre-Processor

CHAPTER 4 - System Requirements

Recommended System Architecture

AVEVA Information Standards Manager

AVEVA Information Standards Manager™ (AVEVA ISM) is not an integral part of the

system architecture on a day-to-day basis. It is responsible for the production of Class

Libraries used within AVEVA NET Gatekeeper, AVEVA Change Manager and AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard and is installed on a separate physical or virtual machine. For

more information on AVEVA ISM, refer to the product documentation.

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5

Installation Procedure

This section contains information about installing AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA

Change Manager.

5.1

Pre-Deployment Tasks

This chapter describes the following tasks you must complete before you start deploying

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager:

· Install the AVEVA WDA Framework
· Create a SQL Server Database and login
· If required, create an EDW Pre-Processor SQL Server Database and login
· Configure Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) E-mail
· Install the Common Files
· If required, install EDW Pre-Processor
· Configure Local DTC Settings

5.1.1

Install AVEVA WDA Framework

Review the following guidelines before you start the AVEVA WDA Framework Installer:

· Installation Directory

· The default installation directory is: C:\Program

Files\AVEVA\WDA.Platform.

· Users/Groups. The ComServer Consumers group should exist before you run the

Installer.

· The following users/groups are added to the ComServer Consumers group

by the Installer.
· IIS_IUSRS
· Administrator
· NETWORK SERVICE
· LOCAL SERVICE
· SYSTEM

· Installation will pause at Removing backup files for around two minutes, while it

sets up the COM+ applications.

Release 5.0.11

Page 22

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

To install AVEVA WDA Framework:

1. Double-click the AVEVA WDA Framework.msi to execute it and the

Windows Installer application launches.

2.

Follow the instructions in the Windows Installer window.

After you have installed the AVEVA WDA Framework, you must perform the following

post-installation tasks:

· Verify the installation of four COM+ Applications:

1. On the Start menu, click Control Panel, double-click Administrative

Tools, and then double-click Component Services.

2.

In the console tree, double-click Computers, and then double-click My

Computer.

3.

To display all the applications, double-click the COM+ Applications

folder for the specified computer.

4.

Verify that the following applications are present:

· WDA Net Libraries
· WDA Net Retrieve
· WDA Net Update
· WDA System Application

5.1.2

Create a SQL Server Database and Login

You must create an empty database in Microsoft SQL Server and a login for both AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager and also a separate empty Naming-and-

Numbering database and a login by using SQL Server Management Studio.

Note: You must also select the Mixed mode security authentication mode for the

Database Engine by using Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio. Mixed mode

enables both Windows Authentication and SQL Server Authentication.

To open SQL Server Management Studio:

1. On the Start menu, point to All Programs, point to Microsoft SQL Server and

click SQL Server Management Studio.

2.

In the Connect to Server dialog box, verify the default settings, and then click

Connect. To connect, the Server name box must contain the name of the

computer where SQL Server is installed.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

Create a Database

To create an empty database using Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio:

1.

In Object Explorer, connect to an instance of the SQL Server Database Engine

and then expand that instance.

Right-click Databases, and then click New Database.

In New Database, enter one of the following names in the Database name

box:
· Gatekeeper
· ChangeManager
· NandN

Click OK to create the database.

Repeat steps 2, 3 and 4 to create the other database.

2.

3.

4.

5.

Create a Login

To create a SQL Server login by using Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio:

1.

In Object Explorer, expand the folder of the server instance in which you want

2.

3.

to create the new login.

Right-click the Security folder and then click New Login.

In the Login – New dialog box, on the General page, enter dba_user in the

Login name box.

4.

Select SQL Server authentication, to create a login that is saved on a SQL

Server database.
· In the Password box, enter a password for the new user. Enter that

password again into the Confirm Password box.

· Clear Enforce password policy. Enforce password policy is selected by

default when SQL Server authentication is selected.

5.

From the Default database list, select the database you entered while creating

an empty database.

6.

From the Default language list, select English.

Note: Select the same database you entered while creating an empty

database.

· For Database role membership for <database name> select the db_owner

role for the user in the specified database.

Notes:

· Select the dba_user role to create a business user.
· Select the db_owner role to create a user with the administrator

privileges.

in Database.

3.

5.1.5

Install Common Files

Review the following guidelines before starting the Common Files Installer:

· Installation Directory

· The default installation directory is: C:\Program

Files\AVEVA\WDA.Applications.

To install AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager common files ensure that

you have the administrator privileges:

1.

Right-click the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.msi

and select Run as administrator to run the installer.

2.

Follow the instructions in the Windows Installer window.

Note: Common Files is a mandatory part of the application.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

After you have installed AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Common

Files, you must cross-check if the default installation folder contains the

WDA.Applications folder. For example: C:\Program Files\AVEVA\WDA.Applications.

The WDA.Applications folder contains the Deployer.exe. Along with the Deployer, you

can find the template files, which are typically installed in the C:\Program

Files\AVEVA\WDA.Applications\Deployer\Templates folder location.

5.1.6

Install EDW Pre-Processor

Note: You should follow the procedures within this chapter only if you require data pre-

processing using EDW Pre-Processor.

EDW Pre-Processor is intended for pre-processing customer-specific data, which is

typically stored in .xls(x) or .txt format, and transforming it into XML files that can be

uploaded into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager. It contains different

tools, some of which are:

· EDW Pre-Processor Database: This is the core part of EDW Pre-Processor. You
can only deploy the EDW Pre-Processor Database or use the other EDW Pre-

Processor tools if this core part is installed.

· AVEVA NET File-to-Table Loader: You can use this console to upload your data

from files to the EDW Pre-Processor Database.

· AVEVA Gateway Controller: A Windows-based application that is intended for
customer-specific data pre-processing. It may use AVEVA NET File-to-Table

Loader, if configured.

· EDW Transformer Tool: Transforms your pre-processed data into XML files.

Release 5.0.11

After you have installed EDW Pre-Processor, you must confirm that the default

see Change EDW Pre-Processor Feature Installation.

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5.1.7

Trouble with the MSI Installer

If there is an error with either of the two .msi files during installation, re-run by

completing the following steps to generate a log file:

1. Open Command Prompt and change directory into the installer directory.

2.

Re-run the installer with the command line parameter: msiexec/i "AVEVA

WDA Framework.msi"/l*v installer.log

Note: Change the MSI filename as appropriate.

After the log file is generated, you may refer to it to find the cause of the problem.

Transaction Coordinator.

5.2

Deployment Tasks

You have to use the graphical user interface (GUI) provided by the AVEVA Application

deployment tool to deploy AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager.

The purpose of the AVEVA Application deployment tool is to:

· Copy the base application files to their deployment location.
· Rename directories to match where AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change

Manager is being installed.

· Add the application to the IIS - application pool and web application.
· Modify the XML configuration files in the application so it can start up.

Pre-Deployment Considerations

Review the information in System Requirements and complete the tasks listed in Pre-

Deployment Tasks.

Release 5.0.11

Page 32

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5.2.1

Configure the Deployer

Use the AVEVA Application deployment tool to configure the deployment parameters.

There are three sets of parameters:

· Host Parameters: Common between both AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA
Change Manager (and EDW Pre-Processor, if installed), including installation

paths to the shared components.

· Application Parameters: Specific to the application being installed.
· EDW Pre-Processor Parameters: Specific to EDW Pre-Processor, if installed.

Notes:

· The following configuration files pre-filled with best practice values will be

delivered with the installer. These configuration files should be updated with the

correct server names and other implementation specific details:

· ChangeManager.Parameters.xml

· Gatekeeper.Parameters.xml

· Host.Parameters.xml

· The AVEVA Application deployment tool uses three blank parameter XML files
(one for each set of parameters) for a new configuration - these contain all the

required parameters with blank values.

· The three sets of parameters must be configured before deployment can start.

To configure the parameters:

1. Open the AVEVA Application deployment tool, double-click Deployer.exe,

2.

3.

4.

which can be found in the installation folder.

Complete one of the following steps:
· To create a new configuration, click New.
· To edit an existing configuration, click Edit.

Select a parameter, and then click Edit Value.

Change the Value, see Configure the Host Parameters, Configure the

Application Parameters or Configure the EDW Pre-Processor Parameters.

5.

Click OK.

6. On the File menu, click Save.

7.

If prompted, select a location, enter a File name, and then click Save.

Note: Do not overwrite either of the blank parameter XML files.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

Encrypting Parameters

Parameter values can be encrypted if required.

To encrypt the parameter values:

1.

2.

3.

4.

5.

In Application Deployment Tool, go to the username field.

Click Edit Value and ensure that the Can be encrypted check box is selected

for the Name field.

In the Value field, enter a value that you wish to encrypt.

Select the Encrypt value check box.

Repeat steps 2 to 5 to create and encrypt the value for the password.

The parameter values are encrypted.

Additionally, the Can be encrypted option is disabled for all parameters by default. This is

to prevent accidental encrypting of parameters that would cause the deployment to fail.

Notes

Adjust if installed
elsewhere

To enable the Can be encrypted option for a parameter:

1.

2.

If open, close the Edit Parameter Value window.

Select the parameter, and then click Edit Definition.

The parameter value of
UserLogonStatus confirms whether
the user account, which has the
name or email address provided on
the login form, is locked in the
application's database. It is used to
provide additional security by
preventing multiple incorrect login
attempts. The default number of
failed login attempts allowed is 3,
but may be changed in the
web.config file: <TIM
maxNumberOfFailedLogonAttem
pts="3" ... />
If neither Microsoft Active Directory
nor AVEVA WorkMate authentication
components are used, ensure that an
application user who is allowed to
upload data using the Bulk Upload
functionality has two user accounts
added to AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard: one each for domain and
non-domain access. The domain user
is a AVEVA NET Workhub and
Dashboard server user. It is
recommended that the access
password for it should not be shared
with the non-domain user (or a local
user). The WDA System Application
uses the login credentials for the
domain user to run the job created
by the business user (Super User,
Information Manager or Contractor)
in the non-domain environment.

Indicates whether user accounts and
roles are managed externally in the
back-end system, which is currently
only supported by AVEVA
WorkMate. If set to true, at login, the
AVEVA NET Gatekeeper user account
data is synchronised with the
respective data retrieved from the
back-end system.

Creating Separate Websites on the Same Web Server

In environments that run both AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager, you
can optionally create and host separate websites for the applications on the same web
server. As part of the prerequisites, enable IIS on the server and HTTP Redirect.

To create separate websites on the same web server:

1. Access Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager in Control Panel, and

navigate to Sites.

2. Right-click Sites, and select Add Web Site.

3. Enter the site name, physical path (for example, C:

\inetpub\wwwroot\<Application>\), and port in the respective fields.

4. To create a separate website for each of the applications, repeat Step 2.

5. To add an application to the App virtual directory, right-click on the site and

select Add Virtual Directory. In the Physical path field, browse for the

application's physical path. For example, C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.11\ChangeManager\Customer\Website. In the Alias field,

provide an intuitive name for the application. Repeat this step for all added

sites. Right-click App, and select Convert to Application.

Now, you can browse the website. The URL may look like

http://<hostname>:<port>/<Virtual directory alias>.

Release 5.0.11

Page 43

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

To automatically append the virtual directory name at the end of the website URL, refer

to the following information. This enables you to access the website using only

http://<hostname>:<port>:

1. Create a configuration file named web.config under the website physical path.

2. Add the following configuration setting to the file under <system.webserver>:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>

<system.webServer>

<httpRedirect enabled="true" destination="">

<add wildcard="/" destination="/App/Tim.aspx" />

</httpRedirect>
</system.webServer>

</configuration>

Creating EDW Pre-Processor Data Sources

The view of the EDW Pre-Processor Parameter Window differs from the view of the other

Windows as it provides extra functionality to support creation of EDW Pre-Processor Data

Sources (the tables intended for storing customer specific data in an applicable for further

import to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager format). Only the Window

is available if the feature EDW Pre-Processor Database is installed because the EDW Pre-

Processor Database creation SQL script is used as the source of the Window.

Release 5.0.11

Page 45

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

To configure EDW Pre-Processor Data Sources:

1. Double-click Deployer.exe from the installation folder.

2. Do one of the following:

·

·

To create a configuration, click New, then click Add.

To modify an existing configuration, click Edit, then in the data source

click Edit Value.

Provide a name for the data source.

Select the concept, either FUNCTIONAL or DOCUMENT.

Specify the attributes of the data source. Some mandatory attributes, such as

Facility, TagNumber or DocNumber may already be provided by the

application.

Click OK.

To include the Data Sources specified into the EDW Pre-Processor Database

3.

4.

5.

6.

7.

creation script, click Update Script.

8. Optionally, click Open Script to open the updated script.

9.

Click File, then click Save.

To remove all the Data Sources from the script, remove them from the parameter file and

update the script.

Release 5.0.11

Page 46

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5.2.2

Run Deployment

Create the FileStore folder in the following location before you run the deployment:

· C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo Manager\

To run the deployer:

1.

In the AVEVA Application deployment tool, in the Deployment steps area, click

Select All.

2.

Click Run deployment.

Notes:

· The deployment progress is displayed and any errors will be highlighted in red.
· If all the options in the Deployment steps area gets unchecked and if the

deployment progression is show as complete, then the deployment of AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager is complete.

5.2.3

Amend Configuration

Configuration options can be amended after the deployment.

To change a configuration after deployment:

1.

 In the AVEVA Application deployment tool, edit and save the parameters. For

information, see Configure the Deployer.

2. Under Deployment steps, click Select None, and then select

UpdateConfigFiles only.

3.

Click Run Deployment. The configuration files will be updated.

5.3

Required Post-Deployment Tasks

This chapter describes the following post-deployment tasks that you should perform after

completing the deployment of AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager using

the AVEVA Application deployment tool:

· Configure the Anonymous Authentication Identity
· Upload the Class Library to the database
· Configure SSL security

Release 5.0.11

Page 47

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5.3.1

Configure the Anonymous Authentication Identity

You have to configure anonymous authentication for both AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and

AVEVA Change Manager in Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.

To configure anonymous authentication:

1. Open IIS Manager.

2.

In the Connections pane, expand the Sites node, click to select the Default

Web Site and then select one of the following:
· Gatekeeper
· ChangeManager

3.

In Features View, double-click Authentication.

4. On the Authentication page, select Anonymous Authentication.

5.

In the Actions pane, click Enable to use Anonymous authentication with the

default settings.

5.3.2

Upload the Class Library

To upload the Class Library to the database you have to complete the following tasks:

·

·

Access the AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager

Administration page.

Upload the Class Library.

To log on to AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Go to the log on page by entering the appropriate URL in a web browser.

Note: To check the compatible web browsers, see System Requirements.

2. On the log on page enter your User Name and Password.

3.

Click Login.

A single user as part of the initial AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager

configuration, will be automatically added as an Administrator. You need to use the

username and password linked to this pre-defined User Profile to log on to AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager. The default Administrator user for AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager is tima. This user should exist in AVEVA NET

Portal or AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard. If user management is using Active

Directory, tima should also exist in the relevant Domain group.

Release 5.0.11

Page 48

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

You will be automatically redirected to the Administration page when you first log on to

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager. Until the Class Library is uploaded

into AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager (and while the Class Library is

being updated) only the system administrator will be allowed to log on. All other users

will get the message displayed on the top of the log on page explaining the situation

when attempting to log on. This is to prevent users from creating data that might be

incompatible with the Class Library. As soon as the Class Library is uploaded and all

applicable products are running against the same version of the Class Library, the users

will be able to work with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager properly.

Trouble with log on

If you face any user authentication issues, refer to the AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard Installation Guide.

Upload the Class Library

To upload the Class Library in AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager:

1. Go to the Administration page, in the Other Tasks section, click System

Settings.

2.

To select a file containing the Class Library to upload, in System Settings, click

Install Updates.

3. On the Upload Updates dialog box, click Browse to select the file containing

the Class Library definition. The upper limit for the size of the uploaded file can

be configured, by default the maximum file size limit is 100MB.

4.

Click OK. If you want to cancel the upload of a Class Library definition, click

Abort.
· You will see an error message if you try to upload the Class Library

Definition of an earlier version or if the file is not in a supported format.

5.

Check the details about the Class Library Definition and then click Update the

Class Library to initiate the upload.

6.

You will see the progress of the upload in the Updates the Class Library dialog

box.

7.

After the upload is complete, the Updates the Class Library dialog box will

close and the successful update message will be displayed.

Release 5.0.11

Page 49

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

Notes:

· If you want to cancel the upload of a Class Library definition, click Abort.
· The size of the file to be uploaded can be configured, by default the maximum

file size limit is 100MB.

· You will see an error message if you try to upload the Class Library of an earlier

version or if the provided Class Library file is not in a supported format.

5.3.3

Configure SSL Security

View and verify the site bindings for AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard site for SSL

security. All the underlying sites inherit the security properties from the parent website.

You may use SSL and run AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager on HTTPS

on defined individual ports. To verify whether an SSL certificate is configured for AVEVA

NET Workhub and Dashboard, click View and check if the site bindings for the required

port are configured. The default ports for HTTP and SSL are 80 and 443, respectively.

However, you may configure a port that is available to you.

To configure:

1. Navigate to Internet Information Service (IIS) Manager.

2.

Select Default Web Site from the website list in IIS under which the AVEVA

NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager sites are configured.

3.

4.

5.

Click Bindings from the Actions panel list.

Click Add and provide the required values.

Click OK.

For more information, see the SSL security-related section of the AVEVA NET Workhub

and Dashboard Installation Guide. The following is the default HTTPS configuration:

Release 5.0.11

Page 50

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

To configure SSL secure ports:

· Open the Custom.xml file from the following default application paths for

each of the application:

C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.11\Gatekeeper\Customer\WebSite\Dictionary\Custom\

Custom.xml

C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.11\ChangeManager\Customer\WebSite\Dictionary\Cus

tom\Custom.xml

For example, if the application site runs on HTTP using port 80, then configure

the settings as follows:

<Item name="HttpRequest.Default">
 <Culture>
   <ports>
    <unsecure>80</unsecure>
   </ports>
   <files>
    <file name="*" secure="false" />
  </files>
 </Culture>
</Item>
Alternatively, if the application site runs on HTTPS using port 443, then
configure the information in the Securing ASP .NET Session Cookies and
HTTP to HTTPS Redirection sections as follows after you configure the
settings:
<Item name="HttpRequest.Default">
 <Culture>
  <ports>
   <secure>443</secure>
  </ports>
  <files>
    <file name="*" secure="true" />
  </files>
 </Culture>
</Item>

· Open the Web.config file of AVEVA Change Manager and update the

<binding

name="TIM.WebSite.AjaxServices.AutoCompleteAspNetAjaxBinding"
.../> setting in the auto-complete service configuration section as follows:

<binding
name="TIM.WebSite.AjaxServices.AutoCompleteAspNetAjaxBinding"
maxReceivedMessageSize="10485760" maxBufferPoolSize="10485760">
  <security mode="Transport" />
</binding>

Release 5.0.11

Page 51

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

Securing ASP .NET Session Cookies

To restrict AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager from sending user-related

information in text, secure the ASP .NET session cookie by adding <httpCookies

requireSSL="true" /> into the <system.web …/> section in the web.config file. For

example:

<system.web>
 ...
 <httpCookies requireSSL="true" />
 …
</system.web>

HTTP to HTTPS Redirection

To enforce the policy enabling AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change Manager to

serve secure traffic over HTTPS, HTTP to HTTPS redirection is recommended. To raise a

security level, configure redirection as part of application configuration. This will make

sure that all incoming requests get redirected from HTTP to HTTPS appropriately. Ensure

that the IIS URL Rewrite extension is installed, and then, update the web.config file by

specification of the following rule within the <system.webServer ../> section.

<system.webServer>
...
<rewrite>
 <rules>
  <rule name="HTTP to HTTPS redirect" stopProcessing="true">
   <match url="(.*)" />
   <conditions>
    <add input="{HTTPS}" pattern="off" ignoreCase="true" />
   </conditions>
   <action type="Redirect" url="https://{HTTP_HOST}/{REQUEST_URI}"
redirectType="Permanent" />
  </rule>
 </rules>
</rewrite>
...
</system.webServer>

Release 5.0.11

Page 52

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

5.3.4

Change the favicon

To change the default favicon:

1. Navigate to the appropriate application images folder. The default path is:

· C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.9\Gatekeeper\Customer\Website\Images

· C:\WDA.Applications\TechInfo

Manager\5.0.9\ChangeManager\Customer\Website\Images

2.

Replace the AVEVA file favicon.ico with the custom one.

5.3.5

Defining Illegal Characters

For AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager, you may define a list of illegal

characters. The applications refer to this list when reserving [also reserving via Excel

(.xlsx) or tab-delimited text (.txt) file upload] Tags and assigning the Naming and

Numbering codes.

To add a custom list of illegal characters, access the

\TIM.WebSite\Dictionary\TIM.Dictionary.Data.xml path and refer to the following

section:

...
<Item name="Local.TagCode.ForbiddenSymbols">
  <Culture name="no">
    <rows>
      <row id="0" value="|"/>
      <row id="1" value=" " />
      ...
    </rows>
  </Culture>
  <Culture>
    <rows>
      <row id="0" value="|"/>
      <row id="1" value=" " />
      ...
    </rows>
  </Culture>
</Item>
...
Note: The characters that are prohibited in AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard, such as,
{, } and |, must be excluded from this list.

5.3.6

Defining Illegal Child Tag Statuses

AVEVA Change Manager must guarantee Tag hierarchy consistency, which means, you

cannot, for example, void the current Tag if its child Tag has not been voided yet.

Release 5.0.11

Page 53

© Copyright 2021. AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Gatekeeper and AVEVA Change Manager Installation Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Installation Procedure

To specify the list of statuses that a child Tag may have when the current Tag's status is

being changed, you must access the

\TIM.WebSite\Dictionary\TIM.Dictionary.Pages.xml path and refer to the following

section:

...
<!--Specify a semicolon separated list of child Tag statuses that are allowed
when the current Tag's status is changed-->
<!--If the child Tag's status is not in the list associated with the value the current
Tag's status is being set to, the current Tag's status modification will be rejected
with an appropriate error message raised-->
<!--<allowedChildTagStatusesForCurrentTagStatusToBeChangedTo>

<Void>Void;Removed;...</Void>
<Removed>Removed;Void;...</Removed>

 ...
</allowedChildTagStatusesForCurrentTagStatusToBeChangedTo>-->
<!--Be aware that the status value must be from the list of supported statuses-->
<!-- Active    -->
<!-- On Hold   --><!-- Be aware that the space in the middle must be omitted in
the XML tag but not in its value, e.g. <OnHold>Active;On Hold</OnHold> -->
<!-- Removed   -->
<!-- Void      -->
<!-- Reserved  -->
<!-- Future    -->
<!-- Installed -->
<allowedChildTagStatusesForCurrentTagStatusToBeChangedTo>

<Void>Void</Void>

</allowedChildTagStatusesForCurrentTagStatusToBeChangedTo>
...

Note:
· You must switch on the child Tag status validation feature, which is turned off by

default.

· This feature is available only in AVEVA Change Manager (not in AVEVA NET

Gatekeeper).

· If the section <allowedChildTagStatusesForCurrentTagStatusToBeChangedTo/>
is not empty (which means, the child Tag status validation feature is turned on), the
Parent Tag status validation feature is turned on automatically (which means, it is not
possible to re-activate the current Tag if its parent Tag is not active).

5.4

Working with AVEVA NET Gatekeeper or AVEVA Change
Manager

This chapter provides information about few functions you need to start working,

including information about log on, navigation and using the menus.

Complete the installation steps listed in Pre-Deployment Tasks, Deployment Tasks and

Post-Deployment Tasks.

Release 5.0.11

Page 54
