that any data source column name referred to in a From mapping exists in the data source

schema. It does not consider any column names used in more complex mappings (that is,

ApplyBehaviour).

Release 5.0.11

Page 24

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

4.2.3

Encrypt Connection Strings

For those data sources that have connection strings, you can store the connection string

encrypted in the configuration file, as the connection string may have sensitive

information such as a user name and password.

To encrypt the connection strings:

1.

 Determine the connection string, for example,

"Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended Properties="Excel
8.0;IMEX=1;"
Select the Data Extractor menu option Tools > Encrypt … and enter the

2.

connection string.

3.

Select the Encrypt button to get the encrypted version of the connection

string:

4.

Take a copy of the encrypted string and paste it in place of the connection

string in the configuration file, for example:

<Datasource

File="C:\Data Extractor Tests \Lines\Source\ Process Line

List.xls"

ConnectionString="+kYx1qBEeW9EW1qyzoqx0n8mVfoXEi4Z…"
ConnectionStringEncrypted ="true"
Table="Process Line List">

</Datasource>
Specify the attribute ConnectionStringEncrypted as "true". The encryption

5.

uses Advanced Encryption Standard. In the Gateway from 5.0.7 version

onwards, Advanced Encryption Standard SHA256 is implemented. Backward

compatibility for SHA1 standard is maintained for previous versions of

Gateway. Configurations created with older versions of Gateway used SHA1

Encryption Standard.

Release 5.0.11

Page 25

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

4.2.4

Analyse Datasource

The datasource section of the configuration file specifies where the data is to be accessed

from. There must be one and only one data source specified per configuration file. The

data source can be a file, a directory, or a database.

If the Primavera Enterprise version (P6 EPPM) is available, then Primavera Activities can

be used as a data source. The supported Primavera installation requirements are a

Windows 2008 R2 (or later) server running:

· Oracle database 11g Release 2 (11.2.0.1.0) or later.
· Primavera version 8.3 - Enterprise only.
· WebLogic web server.

Please refer to the Oracle documentation for details of the installation and the

management of the above.

Read Large Data Sources

On reading a very large data source (more than 1 million rows and many columns), an out

of memory error may be reported. It is possible to get around this by having the Data

Extractor read the data source using a different technology. To do this, specify the data

source as 'Large':

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;DataSource=D:

\Tags.mdb;"

Large="true"
Table="Tags"/>

The downside of this is that the 'Directory' setting for the output file, [see Output

File/Directory (Mandatory)], cannot be used to break the output up:

<OutputFile

<File Value="c:\temp\ExampleOut_null.xml"/>
<Directory>Classification</Directory>

</OutputFile>

Release 5.0.11

Page 26

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Datasource Examples

The datasource section is different depending on the data source type being used and

there can be multiple valid connection strings for some data sources. The following

sections give samples for common data sources. Help on connection strings can be found

on the Internet, for example at: http://www.connectionstrings.com/.

Oracle

If using a provider from Microsoft:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=MSDAORA;Data

Source=<server_name>;User ID=sample; Password=sample"

Table="MyTable">

</Datasource>

If using a provider from Oracle:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=OraOLEDB.Oracle;Data

Source=<server_name>;User ID=sample; password=sample"

Table="MyTable">

</Datasource>

The datasource value is the Oracle net service name. This can be determined from the file

tnsnames.ora on the machine where the Oracle client is installed, typically found in a

directory such as:

C:\Oracle\product\10.1.0\Client_1\network\ADMIN

The actual directory depends on how the Oracle client has been installed.

SQLServer

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=SQLOLEDB;DataSource=<server_name>;User
ID=sample;Password=sample"

Table="MyTable">

</Datasource>

Microsoft Access Files

For Access 2003:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;DataSource=C:

\MyDb.mdb;"

Table="Cables">

</Datasource>

Release 5.0.11

Page 27

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

For Access 2007 (Standard Security):

<Datasource

ConnectionString= "Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Data

Source=C:\MyDb.accdb;Persist Security Info=False;"

Table="Cables">

</Datasource>

where Table specifies the table name within the Access file to be used.

Delimited Text Files

There are two mechanisms for extracting data from delimited text files. The first is by

using OLEDB (the same mechanism as used for other data sources):

<Datasource

File="C:\Tag.txt"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended

Properties=&quot;text;HDR=Yes;FMT=Delimited&quot;">
</Datasource>

The second is using a proprietary text file processing capability:

<Datasource

File="C:\DataExtractorTest\Tag.txt"
ConnectionString="DelimitedFile"
Delimiter = "Tab">
<Details

HeadingsRow="1"
FirstDataRow ="2"
LastDataRow ="3"/>

</Datasource>

Use of the OLEDB method may sometimes result in odd behaviour, for example a value

including a double quote, for example, '2"' is read as '2'. The proprietary text file method

avoids such issues and is the recommended option for handling delimited files.

Proprietary Text File Processing

The datasource definition for this method resembles:

<Datasource

File="C:\DataExtractorTest\Tag.txt"
ConnectionString="DelimitedFile"
Delimiter = "Tab"
RegExRemovePattern="^'|'$">
<Details

HeadingsRow="1"
FirstDataRow ="2"
LastDataRow ="3"/>

</Datasource>

The value of the setting Delimiter can be "Tab" or "Comma" or any other character, for

example, "|" or ";". If the Delimiter setting is missing it is assumed that the delimiter is a

comma.

Release 5.0.11

Page 28

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

The setting RegExRemovePattern is optional and allows characters to be removed from

the values as they are read from the text file. The example above removes leading and

trailing apostrophes from all values as they are read in. This can make the mapping easier

as these characters do not have to be accounted for anywhere in the mapping file.

The applicable Details settings are shown above. All are optional, however if either

'HeadingsRow' or 'FirstDataRow' is specified then both must be specified:

Settings

Description

HeadingsRow

FirstDataRow

The row that contains the column headings. If omitted it is assumed
that there is no heading row.

The row that contains the first of the data rows. If omitted it is
assumed that the first data row is immediately after the
'HeadingsRow' or if that's also omitted the first row in the file.

LastDataRow

The last row that contains a valid data rows. If omitted all rows to the
end of the file are considered data rows.

OLEDB

Any number of delimiters can be used for delimited files. Common ones are commas and

tabs, but potentially any character can be used.

<Datasource

File="C:\Tag.txt"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;text;HDR=Yes;FMT=Delimited&quot;">

</Datasource>

Note: 'HDR=Yes' specifies that the first row in the file is a headings row.

For comma delimited files, the above type of datasource setting is all that is needed. For

other delimiting characters (for example, tab delimited) it is necessary to create a file

called schema.ini in the same directory as the data source file(s), the content of which

resembles:

[Tag.txt]
Format=TabDelimited
[Myfile.txt]
Format=Delimited(*)

This states that Tag.txt is a tab delimited file and that Myfile.txt is delimited by the

character *. A schema.ini file can have a section for any number of delimited files in the

same directory as itself.

More information on OLEDB handling of delimited files can be found at:

http://msdn2.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms709353.aspx. The OLEDB handling of

delimited files can be sensitive to spaces adjacent to the delimiting characters. This can

lead to incorrect results.

Release 5.0.11

Page 29

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

SharePoint Lists or Document Libraries

It is possible to read data from SharePoint lists or document libraries.

For SharePoint 2007 (SharePoint 3) or SharePoint 2010:

Note: The "2007 Office System Driver: Data Connectivity Components" may need to be

installed if Office 2007 is not installed.

To retrieve all the columns from a list:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;WSS;IMEX=1;Retr
ieveIds=Yes;DATABASE=http://MachineName/SiteName/
;List={CA3318AA-AB66-4C21-9CC0-1AEA49252122}"

</Datasource>

To retrieve selected columns from a list:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;WSS;IMEX=1;Ret
rieveIds=Yes;DATABASE=http://MachineName/SiteName/
;List={CA3318AA-AB66-4C21-9CC0-1AEA49252122}"
SQLSelect="Select Title from List">

</Datasource>

Note: The database setting is always a site name and should not include a document

library or list name as this covered by the 'List' setting.

Or selected rows:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;WSS;IMEX=1;Ret
rieveIds=Yes;DATABASE=http://MachineName/SiteName/
;List={CA3318AA-AB66-4C21-9CC0-1AEA49252122}"
SQLSelect="Select * from List where Modified &gt;CDate('14/07/2010
11:05:18')">

</Datasource>

In the SQLSelect examples the term 'List' is just a placeholder and can be any text, for

example, 'xyz'.

The actual list name is not needed in the select statement as it is in the connection string.

In order to make a valid select statement there must be such a placeholder. Also 'CDate'

is a function that converts a string to a date.

In the above connection strings, the DATABASE setting is the address of the site containing

the list and the List value is SharePoint's id for the list. It must be in the format shown

above.

Release 5.0.11

Page 30

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

To determine the list id, proceed as follows:

1. Navigate to the SharePoint list using Internet Explorer.

2.

3.

Select the Settings > List Settings or Document Library Settings menu option.

Copy the URL from the Internet Explorer's address bar.

4. Delete everything before and including "List=" resulting in: %7BAAAE1F2D%

2D9CF6%2D4431%2D9E3C%2D08C67C7AD660%7D.

5.

6.

7.

Change "%7B" to "{".

Change all "%2D" to "-".

Change "%7D" to "}".

The final result is the list's id, for example, {AAAE1F2D-9CF6-4431-9E3C-

08C67C7AD660}.

XML Files

There are three operations that may be carried out on XML files by the Data Extractor:

· Transform an input XML file using an XSLT file (in this case no mappings would be

specified).

· Transform an input XML file using an XSLT file and then applying mapping(s) to

the XML result of the transformation.
· Apply mappings to an input XML file.

In each case, the result should be AVEVA NET format XML file(s).

For an XML file data source, the type setting must be set to XML. If any mappings are to be

defined then the path to the nodes representing the objects of interest must be specified

using ObjectNodePath setting.

XSLTs

An XSLT can be used to change the format of an XSLT file or to replace certain values in an

XML file. What it does is up to the author of the XSLT file (help on XSLT syntax can be

found on the Internet).

Release 5.0.11

Page 31

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

To specify an XSLT to apply to an XML file, include the ApplyXSLT setting in the data

source definition:

<Datasource

File="C:\myxmlfile.xml"
Type="Xml"
ApplyXSLT="C:\Example.xslt"
ObjectNodePath="Instrumentation/Instrument"> <!-- only ifmappings to
be defined -->

</Datasource>

Whether or not the ObjectNodePath/Mappings are specified, the output file is

determined from the Output File section of the configuration file (as with any Data

Extractor operation).

XPath Queries

The identification of relevant nodes is achieved by XPath queries. For simple single

queries the Gateway adds relevant XPath syntax such as '/', '[' and ']' to complete the

query expressions. But if the query is complex, for example, containing multiple

conditions separated by an arithmetic operator, then you need to explicitly provide the

full XPath syntax.

Note: XPath 1.0 is supported for XSLT 1.0 files.

For example, a query such as

default:ClassID='TestClass' and
default:Characteristic[default:Name='TestChar' and default:Value='TestValue']

is a combination of multiple conditions so you need to enclose it within outer square

brackets '[ ...]', as shown below:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="[default:ClassID='TestClass' and
default:Characteristic[default:Name='TestChar' and
default:Value='TestValue']]">

XML File Mappings

When specifying mappings, the paths used within the configuration file to access specific

data (for example, a tag) are relative to the ObjectNodePath setting. For example, if the

XML source file had the following structure:

<Instrumentation>

<Instrument>

<TagName>FT-12345</TagName>
…..

</Instrument>
<Instrument>

<TagName>FT-12346</TagName>
…..

</Instrument>

</Instrumentation>

Release 5.0.11

Page 32

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Then the data source section resembles:

<Datasource

File="C:\myxmlfile.xml"
Type="Xml"
ObjectNodePath="Instrumentation/Instrument">

</Datasource>

...and subsequent mappings:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From= "TagName"/>
<Context Value="Test"/>
…

</Mapping>

To map properties in child nodes of the main node (Instrument in the above example), use

'/'s to represent the transition to the child node, for example:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From= "TagName"/>
<ClassID From="Properties/ClassName"/>
<Context Value="Test"/>
…

If an XML source file contains namespaces, for example:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<vl:VNETList xmlns:vl="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/List"
xmlns="http://www.aveva.com/VNET/eiwm">
<Template>

<ID>C:\\ABCD1-E12345_null.xml</ID>
<Object>

<ID> ABCD1-E12345</ID>

Then each namespace must be specified for the data source in the configuration file, for

example:

<Datasource

File="C:\MyXmlFile_null.xml"
Type="Xml"
ObjectNodePath="vl:VNETList/default:Template/default:Object">
<Namespace
Prefix="default">http://www.aveva.com/VNET/eiwm</Namespace>
<Namespace
Prefix="vl">http://www.aveva.com/VNET/List</Namespace>

</Datasource>

All namespaces must have their prefix specified, as shown for the namespace prefix vl

above. For the default namespace (that is, the namespace with no prefix in the source

file), a prefix must be specified in the data source mapping. This prefix need not be

default.

Release 5.0.11

Page 33

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

In addition, all paths referred to in the configuration file (including the ObjectNodePath

as shown above) must have the namespace prefix included, for example:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="default:ID"/>
<Context From="default:Context"/>
<Name ApplyBehaviour="concat(default:ID, '-',default:Description)"/>

</Mapping>

To get an attribute value for use as a value, precede the attribute name with a '@'. For

example:

<Name From="@Name"/>

Or if the attribute is lower down the tree than the ObjectNodePath:

<ClassID From="Properties/ClassName/@Class"/>

Or if the attribute has a namespace:

<ClassID From="Properties/ClassName/@xsi:Class"/>

You may map to the ClassID or ID any attribute value that is in a node in the XML file, for

example:

<ClassID From="NodeName/@attributeName"/>

A conditional filter can also be used to control when that node is used for the ClassID, for

example:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="NodeName[@attributeName = value of an
attribute in the xml]">

If the relevant part of the input XML file is:

<InformationNode xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
 xsi:type="SystemData">
<dateCreation>2014-01-01T01:00:00+01:00</dateCreation>
<id>6868-fghj-2323-thws-8384</id>
</InformationNode>

then your configuration file can map the object's ClassID as:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="InformationNode[@xsi:type =
SystemData]">
<ClassID From="InformationNode/@xsi:type"/>

and the resultant object in the EIWM will have a ClassID = SystemData.

To map characteristics or properties where not every characteristic or property is to be

included as an explicit mapping, use a wildcard. For example, if the source XML file is:

<Attributes>

<Version No="2">

<description>fire water pump</description>
<design_temperature_max uom="C">100
</design_temperature_max>
<design_temperature_min uom="C">-5
</design_temperature_min>

</Version>
<Version No="0">

Release 5.0.11

Page 34

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

<description>fire water pump</description>
<design_temperature_max uom="C">100
</design_temperature_max>
<design_temperature_min uom="C">-5
</design_temperature_min>

</Version>

</Attributes>

Then a mapping such as might be appropriate:

<Property Condition="string-length(Attributes/Version[@No=2]/*/@uom)
>0">

<ID From="Attributes/Version[@No=2]/*" RegExRemovePattern="_"/>

<Value From="Attributes/Version[@No=2]/*"/>
<Units From="Attributes/Version[@No=2]/*/@uom"/>

</Property>

Notes: The above example uses the following:

· a wildcard '*' to get all properties below the 'Version' node.
· a 'condition' to only consider attributes that have a 'uom' set.
· an XPath test 'Version[@No=2]' to indicate interest only in the node where the
attribute 'No' equals '2'. XPath tests such as 'last' or 'position' to get to the node

of interest could also have been used.

Primavera

<Datasource

ConnectionString="http://10.0.101.243:8206/p6ws/services/; 0User
Id=P6User;Password=p6gateway;"
Type="Primavera"
DbInstanceId="1"
ProjectShortName="EC00515"
WbsList="all"
TaskTagsOnly="true"
TagsUDF="Tag List" />

Attributes ConnectionString and Type are existing attributes. The others are new and are

specific to the Primavera data source.

Attribute ConnectionString specifies the base URL for the Primavera Web Services.

Setting Type=”Primavera” switches processing to use Primavera web services to fetch

Activities for the given Project and its WBS Elements.

The attributes User, Password and DbInstanceId are for the Primavera connection. The

Database instance number is almost always “1”. The Primavera Administrator can advise if

not. If you create Primavera user P6User with password p6gateway as in the above

example, then these attributes can be omitted because Primavera defaults to this user.

Attribute ProjectShortName is the name of the Primavera project to be processed.

Release 5.0.11

Page 35

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Attribute WbsList is normally set to “all”. It can also be set to a comma-separated list of

WBS Element names in which case only Activities which are children of the listed WBS

Elements are output.

Attribute TaskTagsOnly is set to “true” to fetch and output only those Activities which

have associated Tags in Primavera. If set to “false”, all Activities are output.

Attribute TagsUDF is the name of the Primavera User Defined Field which contains a Tags

List.

Refer to Appendix A - Primavera Data Fields for a list of the standard Primavera data fields

available.

User-defined fields are also available. One of these, Tag List for example, must be created

to hold Tags associated with a Primavera Activity. Appendix B - Create Tags in Primavera

gives an example. Use the Tools ' Datasource Schema option to list all available fields,

including User defined fields.

The Create Starter Configuration File option can also be used to view and select all fields.

Note that you must do the following to prevent errors when you use a generated

Primavera starter file:

· Add a proper path to the <LogFile> command. Ditto the <OutputFile>

command path.

· Replace &quot; with "
· Remove the duplicate " before the Table= attribute.

Primavera Licensing

The Primavera add on data source option requires a separate license. You can contact

your AVEVA representative for details.

Primavera Gateway

A separate Primavera Gateway is available. This allows the full hierarchy of project, WBS

Elements, Activities, Resources and Primavera Documents to be loaded into AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard.

Release 5.0.11

Page 36

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

The first method for Excel is to use OLEDB (the same mechanism as used for other data

sources).

For Excel 2003:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 8.0;IMEX=1;&quot;"
Table="LineList">

</Datasource>

For Excel 2007 and 2010 - XSLX (Macros disabled):

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 12.0;IMEX=1;HDR=YES;&quot;"
Table="LineList">

</Datasource>

For Excel 2007 and 2010 - XSLM (Macros enabled):

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xlsm"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Extended
Properties=&quot; Excel 12.0 Macro;IMEX=1;HDR=YES;&quot;"
Table="LineList">

</Datasource>

The setting IMEX=1; can avoid some odd data type problems with numeric Excel fields,

for example the heading can be read incorrectly and reported as 'F7' (can be any number),

instead of the correct name.

A further problem with the OLEDB method referred to above for Excel is that they can

omit data in the case where a column has a mixture of numeric and text values. The driver

can mistakenly determine the column is numeric and therefore discard text values.

Release 5.0.11

Page 37

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Opening the file in Excel and changing the format of the cells in the column to text can

resolve this (by making all the cells text), as follows:

· Select Column.
· Right-click -> Format Cells.
· Select Text.

The second method is to use Excel's API:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Excel"
Table=" LineList">
<Details

HeadingsRow="1"
FirstDataRow="2"/>

</Datasource>

Note: Table specifies the worksheet name within the Excel file to be used.

Use of the OLEDB method may sometimes result in odd behaviour, for example a value in

Excel '1.3' can be retrieved as '1.299999999999'. The Excel API method avoids such issues.

Detailed Settings - OLEDB

Some additional settings can be used, and may be needed depending on which of the two

above mechanisms are used, and on the Excel file format.

Release 5.0.11

Page 38

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

A datasource setting such as:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 8.0;IMEX=1;&quot;"
Table="LineList">

</Datasource>

works if the first populated row in the Excel worksheet contains headings, and the row

immediately following it is the first row of the data, for example:

However, the data in many Excel files does not follow this pattern, for example:

In such cases, additional settings can be used to inform the Gateway how to correctly get

the data from the worksheet:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 8.0;IMEX=1;&quot;"
Table="LineList">
<Details

FirstPopulatedRow="2"
HeadingsRow="4"
FirstDataRow ="6"
LastDataRow ="100"/>

</Datasource>

Release 5.0.11

Page 39

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

The additional settings in the example above are explained below:

Settings

Description

FirstPopulatedRow

First row in the worksheet to have any content.

HeadingsRow

FirstDataRow

The row that contains the column headings. If omitted it
is assumed that there is no heading row.

The row that contains the first of the data rows. If
omitted it is assumed that the first data row is
immediately after the 'HeadingsRow' or, if that is also
omitted, the first row in the file.

LastDataRow

The last row that contains a valid data rows.

The first three settings must be set. The LastDataRow setting is only necessary if there is

text below the last row that is not to be considered as data. The datasource section would

resemble:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Excel"
Table="LineList">
<Details

HeadingsRow="1"
FirstDataRow ="2"
LastDataRow ="23"/>

</Datasource>

The LastDataRow setting is only necessary if there is text below the last row that is not to

be considered as data.

The 'FirstPopulatedRow' setting that is relevant for the Excel OLEDB connection detailed

earlier is not relevant for this type of connection.

Note: Excel needs to be installed if the Data Extractor is to be used this way.

Release 5.0.11

Page 40

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Use Unstructured Files in Directory as Datasource

You can use the Data Extractor to consider a directory of document files (including sub-

directories) as a data source. For example, if a document register was not available, but

only a set of document files, the Data Extractor could be used to create the document

records and file links in AVEVA NET. The available properties supported by the Data

Extractor are listed below:

Label

Name

Description

File Name (includes extension)

NameNoExtension

File name (no extension)

DirectoryName

Full directory of a file

FullName

Extension

Full name of a file (directory plus file name)

File extension (lower case and no trailing '.'

CreationDate

File creation date and time

LastModifiedDate

File last modified date and time

SizeInBytes

File size in bytes

The configuration file resembles:

<Datasource Directory="C:\MyFiles"

Type="Directory"
FileFilterRegExPattern="[.]docx|[.]doc">
LastModifiedFilter="14/03/2014 13:36:20">
<OutputFile File="C:\DE Tests\Directory\Output_null.xml" />
<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="Name" RegExPattern="[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z]{3}-\d{5}"/>
<Name From="Name" />
<ClassID Value="Document Content" />
<Association Type="is fulfilled by">
….
</Assocaition>

</Mapping>

The FileFilterRegExPattern setting is optional and takes a regular expression pattern such

that only files that match the pattern is considered. The LastModifiedFilter setting is

optional and specifies a date/time that means files are excluded if their last modified date

is before this date. The special variable ‘*LASTRUNTIME*’(see Store the Last Run Start

Time) can be used so that Data Extractor can process only files modified after it last ran

that is, LastModifiedFilter="*LASTRUNTIME*.

Note: The Data Extractor can also be used to manipulate and search the files at the same

time, as described in Handle Document Files.

Release 5.0.11

Page 41

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

If you need to process one document file (unstructured file) for a scan, use Data Source

Type = Unstructured. For example:

<Datasource File="D:\GatewayProjects\PDF_DE\Input\SOM6652070_001_A.pdf"
Type="Unstructured"/>

Note: If the filename special variables are used, they are updated with the value of the

filename that is being processed.

If you need to use the filename special variables when the Data Source Type =

Directory, you must set the filter so that only a single file is selected. For example:

<Datasource Directory="E:\Gateway_Projects\PDF_DE\Input\" Type="Directory"
 Filter="SOM6652070_001_A.pdf"/>

Note: If the directory contains more than one file and the filter identifies more than a

single file, and a filename special variable is used, an error is logged.

At the command line: When the data source type in the configuration is "Directory" or

"Unstructured", the configured file can be overridden by specifying the filename with -

f on the command line. For more information, see Command Line.

Use Multiple Datasources

The SQLSelect statement described in Specify an SQL SELECT Statement can be used to

consider multiple tables (or in the case of Excel, worksheets) from the same database (or

in the case of Excel the same Workbook). If tables from two different sources are to be

considered, or in the case of Excel, if the worksheets have different layouts (for example,

different headings row) then the use of SQLSelect is not appropriate. In these cases the

multiple data source sections can be used, and the Data Extractor merges the tables

before applying the mappings.

It is not an SQL JOIN, but is similar, for example:

<Datasources>

<Datasource

File="c:\tags.xls"
ConnectionString="..."
Table="Sheet1">
<Link>

<Column>Tag</Column>
<RegExRemovePattern>\s+</RegExRemovePattern>

</Link>
</Datasource>
<Datasource

File="c:\tag_attributes.xls" ConnectionString="..."
Table="Sheet2">

Release 5.0.11

Page 42

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

<Link>

<Column>Tag Name</Column>
<Type>Left</Type>
<RegExPattern RemoveFailedMatches="true">^[A-Z]
</RegExPattern>
<RegExRemovePattern>^ [A-Z]{3}-|
\s+</RegExRemovePattern>

</Link>
</Datasource>

</Datasources>

The settings within the Datasource sections are the same as those when using a single

data source. The difference is that when using multiple data sources, a Link section must

be set for each data source.

The data sources are linked to create a single 'combined' data source. The combined data

source is then processed in the normal way.

Exactly how the data sources are combined depends on settings below:

Settings

Column

Type

Description

Mandatory
The name of the column in the data source to be used for linking.

Optional
The default behaviour is to merge the data from the primary and
secondary data sources. Rows not in the primary data source but in a
secondary data source are added to the 'combined' data source.

Settings:
Left: The rows of the primary data source set the scope of the
combined data source. Rows from secondary data  sources that do not
have a match in the primary data source are discarded.
Inner: Only rows that exist in both the primary and the secondary data
sources are in the combined data source.
LeftOnly: The scope of the data retrieved are those rows from the
primary data source that are not in the secondary data source. All
other rows from the primary data source are discarded and no rows
from the secondary data source is in the scope. The data source
schema is that of the primary table.
RightOnly: The scope of the data retrieved are those rows from the
secondary data source that are not in the primary data source. All
other rows from the secondary data source are discarded and no rows
from the primary data source is in the scope. The data source schema
is that of the secondary table.

In all cases of conflicting data, the data found in later data sources
overwrites data found in earlier data sources. It is best to avoid this
situation however.

Release 5.0.11

Page 43

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Settings

Description

RegExPattern Optional

A regular expression pattern that is applied to each value in the linking
column to aide linking. For example if one data source had a tag
expressed as 'P-100' and another the same tag as '23-P-100', a regular
expression can be applied to the '23-P-100' to strip off the '23-' and
allow linking.

RemoveFailed
Matches

RegExRemove
Pattern

Optional
If a RegExPattern is specified and a row does not match the pattern
and this is 'true' then the row is ignored.
Default = false

Optional
If specified, text matching the pattern is removed from the values in
the link column. This can be use full to aide linking as different data-
sources can refer to the same object/tag using different identifiers.

Using a Data Source File from a Web Location

In many data sources (Excel, Access, Delimited files, XML) a file is specified as the data

source. In addition to being a UNC path (for example, c:\temp\tags.xls), this can also be

a web location (including a SharePoint location), for example,

http://servername:1234/data/tags.xls. The file is downloaded to the temporary

directory and processed as normal from there. For this to work, the user running the

Gateway must have access to web location.

Using Data Extractor on a 64-bit Operating System

The Data Extractor is a 32-bit application that runs on 64-bit operating systems. To run the

Data Extractor on a 64-bit operating system and connect to Microsoft Office products (for

example, Excel and Access), the relevant version of the 2010 Office Connectivity Drivers is

needed:

http://www.microsoft.com/downloads/en/details.aspx?FamilyID=C06B8369-60DD-

4B64-A44B-84B371EDE16D&displaylang=en

These drivers can be used even if connecting to older versions of Office products (for

example, Excel 2003, 2007). See Microsoft Access Database Engine for details on which

driver to install, as this is Office version dependent. However, these drivers do not install if

you have 32-bit Office version dependent products installed. If searching/indexing PDF

files for tags (see Searching Document Files for Content/Tags) then the 64-bit Adobe PDF

IndexFilter should be used:

http://www.adobe.com/support/downloads/detail.jsp?ftpID=4025

Release 5.0.11

Page 44

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

4.2.5

Specify Regular Expression Utility

The Regular Expression Utility can be useful to develop regular expressions to use in the

Data Extractor. This utility is available from the menu option Tools > Regular Expression

Utility:

In this dialog, a regular expression search pattern or patterns can be specified in the

Search Pattern(s) section. Multiple patterns can be specified by putting each pattern on a

new line. The source text can be typed into the Source Text section or selected from a text

file using  the Select from File(s) button. If the files to be searched are not text files (if they

were Excel or Word or text PDF files for example), then checking the Index File(s)

checkbox will result in the file content being read as text (using Index filters if installed).

Release 5.0.11

Page 45

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Two search options are as follows:

· Search

With this option the search patterns are joined together with an OR logic. The

combined pattern will then be matched against the source text.

· Detailed Search

With this option each pattern in turn is used to search each line of the source

text. If a pattern finds a match on a line that line will no longer be consider by

subsequent patterns.

The Detailed Search option is useful when analysing a large list of data. For example if the

source text were a list of all the tags on a project, this option could help develop pattern(s)

to match the tags.

Release 5.0.11

Page 46

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

Notes:

· If the Right to Left checkbox is checked, the patterns will start searching from the

end of lines in the source text and not the start.

· The Java RegEx option can be used to employ the Java flavour of RegEx (used in

the vnet config.xml file for VizStream).

· Include Matches: For the 'Detailed Search', check the Include Matches checkbox
to include the lines where a match was found for specified pattern(s) in the

search results.

· Include Misses: For the 'Detailed Search', check the Include Misses checkbox to
include the lines where a match was not found for specified pattern(s) in the

search results.

· When there is 'Source Text' specified, the Generate Patterns button is activated
and will generate Regular Expression patterns in the 'Search Pattern(s)’ section

that match the lines in the 'Source Text' section.

4.2.6

Export a Datasource to Text or CSV File

To export a datasource to Text or a CSV file:

1.

Select the menu option Tools > Export Datasource to Text or CSV File.

This enables the content of the data source defined in a configuration file to be

written to a comma-separated text file that can be read into Excel or similar for

viewing:

2.

Specify the file name that the data is to be exported to.

4.3

Options Menu

The Options menu allow you to validate mapping without creating output files.

4.3.1

Validate without Creating Output Files

To validate the data without creating Output files:

1. Navigate to Options > Do Not Create Output Files.

This allows validation checks to be run without creation of output files (see

Data Validation).

Note: Creation of output files can be slow, if large numbers of output files are

created.

Release 5.0.11

Page 47

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 4 - Working with the Gateway User Interface

4.4

Help Menu

The Help menu provides you information about the user manual and the Gateway version

details along with copyright information.

To work with the Help menu:

· To view the content of the user manual, navigate to Help>User Manual.
· To determine the version of the Gateway and copyright information, navigate to

Help > About.

4.5

Run Button

The Gateway queries the data source and produces the output XML file(s) as specified in

the configuration file. To initiate data extraction, click Run.

Progress of data extraction, and any processing error are reported in the Progress Report

section of the user interface.

Release 5.0.11

Page 48

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

5

Working with Configuration Files

Configuration files are in XML format and are best edited in an XML Editor, but Notepad or

any text editor can also be used. Any number of configuration files may exist, typically one

per data source table or XML file. The high level structure of a  configuration file

resembles:

<ConfigurationSettings>

<LastRunData>...</LastRunData>
<Datasource>...</Datasource>
<Validation>...</Validation>
<OutputFile>...</OutputFile>
<TemplateID>...</TemplateID>
<LogFile>...</LogFile>
<TempDirectory>...</TempDirectory>
<Mapping>...</Mapping>
<Mapping>...</Mapping>
<Mapping>...</Mapping>

</ConfigurationSettings>

Each of these settings is detailed in the following sections.

One potential limitation to be aware of when using the OLEDB retrieval mechanisms

below is that there is often a limit of 255 columns that can be retrieved. For Excel and

delimited files there are alternative mechanisms which are described which avoids this

limitation. Where no alternative is available, it is necessary to reduce the number of

columns retrieved (for example, by breaking up the table or performing a SQLSelect to

reduce the number of columns retrieved).

5.1

Set Column Names in the Configuration File

Sometimes a data source does not have a heading row. This makes it difficult to use these

columns in mappings as there is no name to use to reference the columns. It is possible to

map such columns but the obscure names provided by the system must be used. These

can be found using the Tools > Datasource Schema menu option, as described in

Reference Column Names Containing Special Characters in Mapping.

An alternative is available for specific connection types:

ConnectionString="DelimitedFile"
Or:
ConnectionString="excel"

When using these connection types, it is possible to set column names in the data source

definition and then use those column names when specifying the mapping in the

configuration file. To set the column names in this way, use either:

<Datasource

File="C:\Data Extractor Tests\DelimitedFile\MyDelimitedFile.txt"

Release 5.0.11

Page 49

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

ConnectionString="DelimitedFile"
Delimiter = "Tab">
<ColumnNames>
<Name/>
<Description/>
<Size/>
</ColumnNames>

</Datasource>

or:

<Datasource

File="C:\Data Extractor Tests\DelimitedFile\MyDelimitedFile.txt"
ConnectionString="DelimitedFile"
Delimiter = "Tab">
<ColumnNames>
<Name Column="1"/>
<Description Column="2"/>
<Size Column="4"/>
</ColumnNames>

</Datasource>

In the first case, the first column in the file is identified by 'Name', the second by

'Description' and the third by 'Size'. In the second case the first column in the file will be

identified by 'Name', the second by 'Description' and the fourth by 'Size'. The second

case enables users to name only those columns that are to be used in the mapping.

Columns not assigned names in this way can still be referenced using the system

generated names accessible from the Tools > Datasource  Schema menu option.

5.2

Use Mapping for a Directory of Source Files

Sometimes it is necessary to apply the same mapping to a set of source files. For this to be

possible, each source file must have a similar structure. This can be achieved by specifying

a directory for the data source in which case every file in that directory, and any

subdirectories, are processed based on the configuration file mappings.

<Datasource

Directory="C:\Source"
Filter="*.*"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 8.0;IMEX=1;&quot;"
Table="LineList">

</Datasource>
<OutputFile Directory = "C:\linelist\output" XSLT="XYZ"/>

If a directory is specified for the data source, then the output must also be specified as a

directory, as there is an output file for each input file. The input file names are used as the

basis for the output file names.

Release 5.0.11

Page 50

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

For example, if the input file name was myfile.xls then the corresponding output file

would be myfile.xls_null.xml. If it is required that AVEVA NET use an XSLT file on

loading then (optionally) specify an XSLT as shown above. In this case the output file

names would be of the form myfile.xls_xyz.xml. This only affects the file name, not the

content.

The optional setting Filter is used to specify which files in the directory are to be treated

as data source files. By default, all files in the directory are assumed to be data source files

but if for example only files with an extension such as CSV are required, then the filter

setting *.csv would achieve this.

The filter is not case sensitive so files with an extension CSV or Csv would be included also.

The filtering is not solely on the file extension, so source* could be added so that all files

beginning with text source are included.

5.3

Transpose a Table

By default, the Gateway expects tabular data (so this is not relevant for XML input) to be

of a format where each row represents a document/tagged item, and each column a

characteristic, for example:

However, sometimes a column represents a tagged item, for example,

ID

T

P

P-100

10

2

P-101

11

3

P-102

20

2

In such a case, it is necessary to specify that the table be transposed before processing by

using the Transpose setting:

<Datasource

File="C:\Line List.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Extended
Properties=&quot;Excel 8.0;IMEX=1;&quot;"
Table="LineList">
Transpose="True">

</Datasource>

It is possible that two rows in the data source have the same label, for example,

Temperature. Transposing this therefore produces two columns of the same name which

makes it impossible to map.

Release 5.0.11

Page 51

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

To get around this problem, if a duplicate is found, a corresponding column name is

generated by the system, for example, Column2. It is this name that must be used when

mapping. To find out what the duplicate column is called, use the  Tools > Datasource

Schema menu option, see Reference Column Names Containing Special Characters in

Mapping.

Note: For Excel input any settings (for example, FirstPopulatedRow) relating to the

location of the headings and data within a worksheet is applied before transposing the

table.

5.4

Specify an SQL SELECT Statement

For all the examples considered so far, the data source has been a complete worksheet,

table or delimited text file. Consider the following scenarios where this is not the case:

· The data required is spread across multiple worksheets/tables in the data source.
· The data required is a subset of the data in the specified worksheet/table. For
example, a document register could have multiple entries for each document

representing different versions, but only the latest revision is needed.

These two scenarios and others can be handled by using a SQLSelect statement instead

of the Table specification (or in the case of a delimited text file, an addition to the existing

specifications).

The examples below correspond to potential approaches for these two scenarios:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=MSDAORA;Data
Source=<server_name>;User ID=sample; Password=sample"
SQLSelect="Select * FROM TABLE1, TABLE2 WHERE
TABLE1.ID=TABLE2.ID"

</Datasource>

Or:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=MSDAORA;Data
Source=<server_name>;User ID=sample; Password=sample"
SQLSelect="Select DocReg.* From DocReg, (SELECT Id, Max(Rev) AS
MaxRev FROM DocReg GROUP BY Id) MaxRevs WHERE Id = MaxRevs.ID
AND Rev = MaxRevs.MaxRev"

</Datasource>

The value of SQLSelect can be any valid SQL SELECT statement that returns a set of

rows gathered from the data source. Help on SQL statements can be found on many

Internet sites and is not considered further here.

In the mapping sections in the configuration file, the column names mapped must

correspond to column names returned by the SQLSelect statement.

Release 5.0.11

Page 52

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

See Datasheet Mappings for how the last run time can be used in a SQLSelect statement

to incrementally retrieve data.

Notes:

· SQLSelect can only be used with an OLEDB data source, that is, it cannot be
used with XML data sources or when the data source is specified as 'Excel' or

'DelimitedFile'.

· If the data source is Excel, the worksheet name in a SELECT statement must be

prefixed by '[' and followed by '$], for example,

"SELECT * FROM [MyWorksheet$] WHERE Diameter > '4’”.
· If the data source is Excel and the worksheet has a format other than a simple
table (see Process All Excel Worksheets in a Workbook) then it is likely not

possible to use the SQLSelect option.

· If a column name contains a space, it needs to be enclosed in square brackets, for

example:

 select [Revision Date] from…

5.5

Process All Excel Worksheets in a Workbook

If you want to process all the worksheets in an Excel file/Workbook then

*ALL_WORKSHEETS* can be used as the table name in the datasource:

<Datasource

File="C:\DE Tests\All Excel Sheets\DIV 1_PART 2_INDEX III - CIVIL.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Extended

Properties=&quot;Excel 12.0;HDR=YES;IMEX=1&quot;"

Table="*ALL_WORKSHEETS*" ExcludePattern="^In|^Test">
<Details

HeadingsRow="8"
FirstPopulatedRow="1"
FirstDataRow ="10"/>

</Datasource>

Notes:

· All the worksheets must have the same structure, that is, headings in same row,

same column names and so on.

· The setting 'ExcludePattern' is optional and if used can exclude worksheets

(based on their name) that are not wanted for processing. The value is a regular

expression pattern.

Release 5.0.11

Page 53

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

The worksheet name can then be used in the mapping by using WorksheetName as

shown:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="Client_x0020_ID" />
<Context From="WorksheetName" />

</Mapping>

5.6

Execute a Post-Process Stored Procedure

For data base data sources such as SQL Server it is possible to get the Data Extractor to run

a stored procedure in the source database after data has been processed. This can be

useful as it enables the storing of a record in the data source database indicating that data

has been successfully processed. The syntax or specifying this:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=SQLOLEDB.1;Data Source=….."
Table="TestTable"
StoredProcedurePostProcess="usp_TestProcedure"/>

Note: The connecting user must have permission to run the specified stored procedure.

5.7

Use Parameterised Queries

The Gateway uses OLEDB to connect to and query databases, but OLEDB does not support

Named Parameters.

To avoid query injection attacks it is a good practice to control the types of SQL queries

your project will accept. The Gateway supports this by allowing parameterised queries to

be defined in the project configuration file by separating the body of the query from the

data values used in that query. Data type enforcement can then be applied to those

submitted values.

To create a parameterised query, in the configuration file specify data values in the query

text either with the placeholder " ? " or by named parameters and their type specified in

the parameter section, as shown in the below example. By default, parameter names shall

be ' ? '  if a name is not specified. The parameter type must one of the OLEDB types (see

https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.data.oledb.oledbtype%28v=vs.110%

29.aspx?f=255&MSPPError=-2147217396).  The default OLEDB type is "Empty".  The

order of parameters should be maintained in the same order you want to use them in the

query.

Release 5.0.11

Page 54

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

Example:

SQLSelect="Select * FROM [Table] where [TableColumn1] = ? AND
[TableColumn2] = ?">
  <Parameters>

  <Parameter Name='Param1' Type='VarChar'>
       <ParamValue ApplyBehaviour="concat('*SpecialAttribute1*',
'*SpecialAttribute2*')"/>
   </Parameter>
   <Parameter Name='Param2' Type='VarChar'>
       <ParamValue Value= 'TestClass' />
   </Parameter>

  </Parameters>

You can derive parameter values by using hard coded values (or) by using special variable

which can be passed from the command line (or) by using ApplyBehaviour.

5.8

Output File/Directory (Mandatory)

The Gateway creates XML output that can be loaded by AVEVA NET.

The output for a data source may be a single file or a set of files, as it is sometimes

desirable to break up the output into multiple files. AVEVA NET handles smaller files more

efficiently and can fail to load XML files when they get large (approximately >60MB). To

create a single output file from a data source, the configuration file settings resemble:

<OutputFile>

<File Value="c:\temp\ExampleOut_null.xml"/>

</OutputFile>

To create an output file for each object in a data source, the configuration file settings

would resemble:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

In this case, an XML file is created in the directory 'c:\temp' with the objects' id/tag

prepended to the file name to generate a unique file name, for example, 'P-

100_null.xml'. This should be avoided if it generates a very large number of files as

Windows take time to process if moving/copying tens of thousands of files, especially if

they are in the same directory.

Release 5.0.11

Page 55

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

To avoid some of the issues mentioned it is possible to break the output into different

directories. This can be done in a number of ways, for example:

<OutputFile Directory="C:\Temp">

<File Value="ExampleOut_null.xml"/>
<Directory>Item Class</Directory>

</OutputFile>

The above results in a subdirectory of 'c:\temp' being created for each classification.

Each directory contains a file 'ExampleOut_null.xml', that contains the information for

all tags of that classification. It would also be possible to create a single file per tag within

the subdirectories using either of the following two mappings:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>
<Directory>Item Class</Directory>

</OutputFile>

Or:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Item Class,'\', Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

Note '\' or '/' characters in the 'File' setting can be used to specify a folder in the output.

You can break the output of data source into different directories by grouping tags into

directories based on part of a value. For example, to group tags into sub-directories where

their tags have a common section:

Example 1:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp">

<File Value="ExampleOut_null.xml"/>
<Directory SearchExpression="substring (Tag, 1, 4)"/>

</OutputFile>

The Example 1 script results in all tags with the same first four characters being included

in a file 'ExampleOut_null.xml' in a sub-directory 'c:\temp', where the sub-directory

name is the first four characters of the tags. This results in many sub-directories (names

are four characters long), each containing a single file with every tag within that file

having the same first four characters.

Note: The column references used in the <Directory> setting and its related Search

Expression setting must be the exact column name in the data source. You need not

accommodate special characters such as those needed when mapping columns, as

described in Reference Column Names Containing Special Characters in Mapping.  In

addition, if the column name contains any SQL special characters (for example, spaces), it

should be enclosed in square brackets [..] in the same way as in an SQL statement.

Release 5.0.11

Page 56

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

For example, if you have a tag C-9001 then the Search Expression creates C-9001 folders in

the output location and each tagged folder contains the EIWM file with corresponding tag

information.

Example 2:

<OutputFile Directory="Output">

<File Value="ExampleOut_null.xml"/>
<Directory SearchExpression="Tag + '\' + [Description]"/>

</OutputFile>

The Example 2 script results in Tag name directories with the description as a sub-

directory and under those directories, EIWM files will be created. This results in many sub-

directories, each description sub-directories contains a single file with

ExampleOut_null.xml name.

For example, if you have a tag C-9001 and its description is OVERHEAD CONDENSER then

the preceding Search Expression will create C-9001\OVERHEAD CONDENSER folders in the

output location and each sub-folder will contain the EIWM file with corresponding tag

information.

Note: The logic/pattern of Search Expression follows the DataColumn expression rules.

Refer to the following link for more information about how this Search Expression works:

http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.data.datacolumn.expression.aspx

5.8.1

Replace Invalid Characters in the File Name

When the Data Extractor creates output files based on data source data, it is possible that

the file name will contain characters that are not permitted in Windows file names (for

example, '*' or '\'), in which case an error will be reported or directories will be created

when not required. It is possible to instruct Data Extractor to replace invalid characters

from file names and therefore avoid such errors. For example:

<OutputFile Directory="D:\VNET Data\Staging Area\Doc List"
DeleteExisting="true">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')" ReplaceInvalidChrs="_"/>

</OutputFile>

The ReplaceInvalidChrs setting can take any string and can also be an empty string "",

in which case invalid characters will be removed. To replace with a space(s), include the

setting 'xml:space', as shown here:

<File xml:space="preserve" ApplyBehaviour="concat(doc_num,'_null.xml')"
ReplaceInvalidChrs=" "/>

Release 5.0.11

Page 57

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

5.8.2

Delete Existing Output Files

Sometimes it is desirable to have the output directory cleared of files to avoid confusion

between different processing results. This can be achieved using the setting

DeleteExisitng setting, as shown below:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp" DeleteExisitng="true">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

This will result in ALL files and subdirectories in the output directory being deleted the

deleting will be carried out only if some data found in the datasource to avoid

unnecessary deletions if there is a problem with the datasource.

If only one output file is expected, then the case may arise that there is no data found and

the output file from a previous run is required to be deleted (but not all files in the output

directory). This can be achieved using the DeleteIfNoData setting:

<OutputFile DeleteIfNoData="true">

<File Value="c:\temp\ExampleOut_null.xml"/>

</OutputFile>

5.8.3

Check for Trigger Files

If running Data Extractor and Import Controller automatically (using trigger.start files

and 'Watch' folders) problems can arise (particularly if the DeleteExisiting setting is set

to true) if the Import Controller is still importing the results of a Data Extractor run when a

second run starts. To avoid this, use the CheckForTrigger setting:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp" CheckForTrigger="true">
<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

With this setting set to true, the Data Extractor will look in the output directory and will

abort the run if it finds either a trigger.process or trigger.queue file.

5.8.4

Create a Trigger File

Using the TriggerFileName setting, the Data Extractor can be instructed to create an

empty file in the output directory:

<OutputFile Directory="c:\temp" TriggerFileName="trigger.start">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

The file name specified can be any file name - the standard file names expected by the

AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Import Controller is trigger.start or

trigger_force.start. The trigger file will be created after all processing has completed, if

no errors have occurred.

Release 5.0.11

Page 58

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

5.8.5

Produce Revisions in Output File Names

If creating an output file per object/document, the Data Extractor will by default put all

revisions of a document into a single file. For example, the settings below would produce

an output file for all revisions of a document:

<OutputFile Directory="Output" DeleteExisting="true">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(DocumentNo,'-',Revision,'_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

If it is required to have a single output file for each revision, then the Mapping section

must have the RevisionInID setting set to true:

<Mapping Type="Object" RevisionInID="true">

<ID From="DocumentNo"/>

5.8.6

Create Duplicates and Output File Names

If Data Extractor identifies multiple entries in the source that it considers to be the same

object, then by default it will consider them as 'duplicates' and treat them as one merged

object. Once entries have been identified as 'duplicates', they cannot be written to

separate output files. For the purposes of identifying what are 'duplicates' the following

mapping settings are considered (if they are set): ID, Context and Revision. When it is

necessary to create an output XML file per object then it may be required to stop Data

Extractor considering objects as duplicates, this can be stopped by setting the

MergeDuplicates setting to false:

<Mapping Type="Object" MergeDuplicates="false">

A practical usage of this is where a document register is being processed and there are

multiple files per revision and an output file is required per file. In such a case a mapping

such as that below could be used:

<OutputFile Directory="..\..\..\Staging\Documents">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Document_x0020_Type, '\',FILE_ID,
'_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>
<Mapping Type="Object" MergeDuplicates="false">
<ID From="Document_x0020_Number"/>

Without this setting, all the files would be considered within the same document object

(as the ID setting is the document number) and all would be written to one output file for

the document.

Release 5.0.11

Page 59

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

5.8.7

Apply XSLTs to Output Files

The result of a Data Extractor operation is one or more XML files for import into AVEVA

NET. You can get the Data Extractor to apply an XSLT transformation to output file(s) to

modify the content/format of the file.

<OutputFile

Directory="c:\temp"
ApplyXSLT="C:\Example.xslt"/>
<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

Note: XPath 1.0 is supported for XSLT 1.0 files.

This is another option to modify the values reported in the XML file, but it will generally be

simpler to use the ApplyBehaviour or regular expression options in the mappings with

XSLTs only being used when additional capability was needed.

5.9

Template ID (Optional)

Each XML output file (except when a class definition output file is to be produced) must

have a template id that must be unique to that file for the project. The template Id is

typically based on the output file name. The template id can be specified in the

configuration file in a number of ways:

· No template id specified. In this case the full file name (including the path) of the

output file will be used for the template id.

· Fixed template id specified, for example:

<TemplateID>C:\test\output\
MyOutputFile_null.xml</TemplateID>

In this case all file(s) produced based on the configuration file will have the

template id specified. This should not be used if multiple files are being

produced.

· Root template id specified, for example:

<TemplateID Root="True">C:\test</TemplateID>

In this case the template id will be the full output file name with the string

specified above removed from its start, for example,

"output\MyOutputFile_null.xml".

· If writing out a file per object then a From or ApplyBehaviour mapping can be

used:

<TemplateID ApplyBehaviour="concat(Code, ' :Template')"/>

Release 5.0.11

Page 60

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

5.10

Log File (Optional)

Progress information, including errors encountered, will be reported to the user in the

Progress Report section of the user interface. This information can additionally be

reported to a log file if one is specified in the configuration file:

<LogFile Append="false">c:\temp\mylog.txt</LogFile>

The ‘Append’ setting is optional and defaults to ‘false’. If specified and set to ‘true’ the

log file will get appended to with each Data Extractor run, otherwise the log will be

overwritten.

The log file is primarily of use when running the Gateway from a command line (see

Command Line).

Note: 'Special Variables' (see Special Variables in Object Mapping) can be used in this

setting, for example:

<LogFile>./*CONFIG_FILE_NAME_SHORT_NO_EXT*.log</LogFile>

Notes:

· If the datasource is a single input file (for example, Excel, CSV and TXT), you can

use special variables to specify the Log File.

· For the datasource containing multiple input files inside a directory, and for

servers, such as Oracle, SQL, Access, SharePoint, you cannot use special variables

to specify the Log File.

When using certain special variables, problems can arise in that they can introduce

characters considered invalid for file names/paths. For example '*DATE*' will result in a

value such as '' which has characters that are invalid for a file name. To accommodate this

<LogFile ReplaceInvalidChrs="_">..\Logs\*DATE*.txt</LogFile>

Note: The 'ReplaceInvalidChrs' setting can be used.

Any invalid characters in the file name will be replaced by the string specified ('_' in the

example above). So a file name that would have been invalid '..\\Logs\\4/18/2013

2:02:56 PM.txt', would become the valid '..\\Logs_4_18_2013 2_02_56 PM.txt'.

5.11

Report File (Optional)

A report file can be specified that will create a CSV report that matches the data written to

the output XML file. This allows you to view the data in an easier to understand format. To

specify a report file add a line to the configuration file that resembles:

<ReportFile>..\Reports\Report.csv</ReportFile>

Release 5.0.11

Page 61

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

The report file will be of the format:

The report will have a row per attribute for an item and if relevant a row per association

and if relevant, a row per attribute on an associated object.

5.12

Set the Temporary Directory (Optional)

The Data Extractor will create temporary XML files while processing. By default the files

will be created in the Windows application data directory, typically:

C:\Documents and Settings\<UserName>\Application Data

If running the Data Extractor programmatically there may be security problems writing to

this directory, so you can specify a different temporary directory in the configuration file

directory as follows:

<TempDirectory>d:\temp</TempDirectory>

5.13

Use Relative File/Directory Paths

Use of relative paths for the input, output, temp, log and validation files/directories will

make moving the Data Extractor configurations around easier. It is possible to specify

paths in a number of ways:

<Datasource File="LineList.xls" ConnectionString=...../>

<Validation>
<Report>

<FileName>Report\ Validation Report.html</FileName>
....
</Report>
</Validation>

<OutputFile>

<File Value="..\Output\Lines_null.xml"/>

</OutputFile>

Or:

<OutputFile Directory="..\Output">

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Line_Num, '_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>
<TempDirectory>.\Temp</TempDirectory>

Release 5.0.11

Page 62

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 5 - Working with Configuration Files

A relative path cannot be used for the output directory if specifying the output directory

as shown below:

<OutputFile>

<File ApplyBehaviour="concat('..\Output\', Line_Num,'_null.xml')"/>

</OutputFile>

All relative paths are relative to the configuration file.

5.14

Always Generate EIWM

When the input source contains a number of objects, it is common to output all the

objects into a single EIWM template. If the input source changes and is re-processed with

the same template ID, any missing objects are deleted from AVEVA NET by the Import

Controller.

By default, the Gateway does not generate an empty EIWM template if the input source is

empty. You can force the Gateway to generate an empty EIWM template to delete the

missing objects from AVEVA NET either of the following two methods:

· Through the configuration file:

<ConfigurationSettings>

.....
.....
<AlwaysGenerateEiwm>true</AlwaysGenerateEiwm>
.....
.....

</ConfigurationSettings>

· By selecting the Always Generate Eiwm check box from the Create Starter

Configuration File window:

When you select the Always Generate Eiwm check box, it forces the creation of

an empty EIWM template for the empty input source. This in turn helps in

removing all the objects from AVEVA NET.

If this check box is clear (by default), no EIWM template is created. Hence, the

objects will not be deleted from AVEVA NET.

Release 5.0.11

Page 63

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

6

Handle Document Files

This section describes how to handle Document Files, and how to run a command line

from a Document File.

6.1

Download Document Files

When processing a document register, a link is often created to the file(s) relating to the

document (using the InfoLocator characteristic). You can specify that the file is

downloaded so that it can be made available for viewing in AVEVA NET or for further

processing (by the Document Indexing Gateway for example). This can be set up by

specifying the characteristic as follows:

<Characteristic ID="InfoLocator">
<Value From="FileLocation">

<DocumentFile MaxAttempts="2" DOSFTP="true"
Condition="not(contains(DocNum, 'PID'))">
<SourceLocation From="FileLocation"/>
<TargetLocation Value="c:\MyFiles"/>
<TargetFile ApplyBehaviour="concat(DocNum, '_',Rev, '.xls')"/>
<Overwrite Value="False"/>
<MoveFile Value="true"/>
<Credentials>

<UserName Value="domain\username"/>
<Password Value="xyz123"/>

</Credentials>

</DocumentFile>
<DocumentFile Condition="contains(DocNum, 'PID')>

<SourceLocation From="FileLocation"/>
<TargetLocation Value="c:\MyFilesPIDs"/>
<TargetFile ApplyBehaviour="concat(DocNum, '_',Rev, '.xls')"/>
<Overwrite Value="False"/>

</DocumentFile>

</Value>
</Characteristic>

Note that multiple DocumentFile sections can be specified.

Property

Description

MaxAttempts

Condition

Optional.
If downloading large number of files in one run, problems can arise
with the website saying a file is not there when in fact it is. Setting
this to >1 will instruct the Data Extractor to try up to the maximum
number of tries (with a pause of 2 seconds between each attempt). If
the download has not succeeded after the maximum number of tries,
an error will be reported and processing stopped.

Optional.
A test that can be applied to determine whether the section will be
carried out for a specific document record.

Release 5.0.11

Page 64

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

Property

Description

SourceLocation Mandatory. The full path to the file to consider or download 'http://

..../tags.xls' or 'c:\mydatashwwt.pdf' or 'ftp://.../ tags.xls'.

TargetLocation Optional. Local directory to download or copy the file to. If the file is
already 'local' (not a http or ftp link) then you can deal with the file in
its original location.

TargetFile

Optional.
Name (no path) of the downloaded file.
Note: The file can be renamed if required.
For SharePoint, the file name is stored in its Name property but has
some text appended to the file name that is unwanted. Use a
RegExPattern statement to extract just the file name, that is,:
From="Name" RegExPattern=".*(?=#)"

Credentials/Use
rName
Credentials/Pas
sword

Optional.
The whole credentials section is optional.
If the location that the file is to be downloaded from requires a user
name and password for access, then these should be specified here.

ProxyServer

Overwrite

MoveFile

DOSFTP

Optional.
A proxy server to use to access a remote file.

Optional. Default = "true".
If 'True', the file will be downloaded/copied to overwrite the target
file if it already exists. If 'False', and a target file exists, then the
source file will not be downloaded/copied. This can be useful to
avoid downloading files downloaded in the past.

Optional. Default = "false".
If 'true', the file will be moved from the source location to the target
location instead of being copied. This only applies when the source is
on a local drive or shared, that is, it will not apply if the source is an
HTTP or FTP path.

Optional. Default = "false".
If the target file is on an FTP site then it will be downloaded. The
default FTP download mechanism uses a DOTNET class but if
problems are experienced with that setting the DOSFTP property to
'true' will change the mechanism used to a DOS based FTP
mechanism.

Release 5.0.11

Page 65

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

6.2

Search Document Files for Content/Tags

When dealing with a document register it is possible (depending on the file type) to have

files searched for tags (or other content of interest). This can be used either in conjunction

with the file download option described above or if the files are on your local network the

searching can be done without any download.

For a file type to be searchable an index filter must be installed - for Office documents

index filters are included with the Windows OS, for other file types, for example, PDF (not

scanned PDFs), Zip, DWG index filters can be found on the Internet and are usually free

but not always (DWG index filter is not free).

Limitations:

When processing Microsoft Office documents, the iFilter for Office ignores the newline

characters. For example, for a .docx file having the following content (with newline

characters at the end of each line):

ABC-444 ABC-445
ABC-446

this is interpreted by the iFilter for Office as:

ABC-444 ABC-445ABC-446

If the regex pattern was

 "^[A-Z]{3}-[0-9]{3}$"

then only the first tag would be extracted where three tags might be expected. A different

regex pattern would be needed to identify the three tags, for example:

 "[A-Z]{3}-[0-9]{3}"

To specify that files are to be searched in this way a DocumentFile settings can be

specified:

<Mapping Type="Object">
<ID From="Doc_No"/>
<Context Value="Test|Doc" />
<Revision From="Revision" Default=""/>
<DocumentFile>

<SourceLocation ApplyBehaviour="concat('c:\Files\',File_Path)"/>

<SearchCriteria

FileFilterRegExPattern="[.]dwg|[.]zip|[.]pdf|[.]xlsx|[.]xls"
DiscardDocumentIfNoTagsFound="true"
TimeOutMilliSecs="100000"
NewFilesOnly="true"
OnErrorContinue="true">

<ProcessedFile MoveFile="false">

<PathOnSuccess ApplyBehaviour="concat('C:\Success\',
Name)"/>
<PathOnFail ApplyBehaviour="concat('C:\Fail\',Name)"/>

</ProcessedFile>

Release 5.0.11

Page 66

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

<Search IncludeEndPatterns="true">

<Pattern Value="[A-Z]{2,3}-\d{5,7}(-[A-Z]/[AZ]|)"/>
<Context Value="Test"/>
<ClassID Value="Equipment"/>

</Search>
<Search>

<Pattern Value="[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z]{3}-\d{4}"/>
<Context Value="Test|Doc"/>
<ClassID Value="Document Content"/>

</Search>
<IdManipulation>
<Expand>

<Pattern>[A-Z]/[A-Z]</Pattern>
<Char>/</Char>

</Expand>
</IdManipulation>
</SearchCriteria>

</DocumentFile>

Or if downloading the files, the same can be specified in the DocumentFile section

specifying a download (as discussed above):

<DocumentFile>

<SourceLocation From="FileLocation"/>
<TargetLocation Value="c:\MyFiles"/>
<TargetFile ApplyBehaviour="concat(DocNum, '_', Rev,'.xls')"/>
