<SearchCriteria

FileFilterRegExPattern="[.]pdf|[.]xls"
DiscardDocumentIfNoTagsFound="true"
TimeOutMilliSecs="100000"
BaseFilePath="C:\Documents\DE Tests\Source\"
FirstMatchOnly="false"
OnErrorContinue="true"
Characteristic="Tag"
CharacteristicMaxLength="255"
CreateAssociations="false">

<Search>

<Pattern Value="[A-Z]{2}-\d{2}-\d{3}"/>
<Context Value="Test"/>
<ClassID Value="Equipment"/>
<AssociationType Value="has document" />

</Search>
</SearchCriteria>

</ DocumentFile>

Release 5.0.11

Page 67

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

When you modify the Object ID using "IdManipulation" section, an alias may be

required to detect and hot-spot the Object ID in the documents referenced in AVEVA NET

Workhub and Dashboard. You can create this alias by adding an IncludeAlias=”true”

option in the "IdManipulation" section, as shown below:

<DocumentFile>

<SourceLocation ApplyBehaviour="concat('C:\Files\',Name)"/>
<SearchCriteria TimeOutMilliSecs="100000" OnErrorContinue="true" >

<Search>

<Pattern Value="ABC-1234"/>
<Context Value="Test"/>
<ClassID Value="Equipment"/>

</Search>

      <IdManipulation IncludeAlias="true">

<Replace>

<Original>ABC-1234</Original>
<ReplacementString>ABC*1234</ReplacementString>

</Replace>

</IdManipulation>

</SearchCriteria>

</DocumentFile>

There can be one or more Search sections. Each will be applied to the file in turn.

Search Patterns

Description

FileFilterRegExPattern

DiscardDocument
IfNoTagsFound

TimeOutMilliSecs

OnErrorContinue

Optional. A regular expression that will be applied to file
names to search only certain files based on their extension
or other criteria.
If this is omitted then all downloaded files will be searched.

Optional. Default = "false"
If set to "true" then a document for which no tags are
found will not be included in the output XML file.

Optional. Default is that there's no time out.
When searching some files the index filters can hang, so it
is wise to specify a timeout otherwise the Data Extractor
will never finish.

Optional. Default="false".
Errors can occur for example if a file is not where it is
expected to be. By default, the Data Extractor will stop in
such a case. If it should continue, set this to "true".

Release 5.0.11

Page 68

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

Search Patterns

Description

IncludeEndPatterns

Pattern

Context

ClassID

AssociationType

NewFilesOnly

Optional. Default-“false”.
When indexing documents a common issue is finding text
that is part of a bigger word that is incorrectly determined
to be a tag or document number. To avoid this start and
end word patterns (‘anchors’ in RegEx terminology) can be
included in the pattern. RegEx has some defaults that can
be used, for example, ‘\b’ however the default  RegEx
options consider a '-' to end a word but this is very
common within the tag and document numbers that are
being searched for; this means that these default options
are generally not useable. It is therefore often necessary to
devise a pattern to really identify the start and end of a tag
or document number. By setting ‘IncludeEndPatterns’ to
‘true’ the Data Extractor will include its own default start
and end options to the pattern specified in the
configuration file (note these will be added at runtime and
you will not see them in the configuration file). The pattern
in effect has a start and end patterns added as shown
which says that a tag or document number will be
considered to be finished when a character is found that is
not a ‘word’ character (that is, a letter or a number) and
not a dash ‘-‘.:
<Pattern Value="(?&lt;!(-|\w))<Your Tag or
Document Pattern>(?!(-|\w))">

Mandatory. Regular expression pattern specifying the thing
to be searched for (for example, the tag).

Optional - though almost certainly required. Context to be
used for entities found by the pattern.

Optional - class of the things (for example, tags) found.

Optional. Default = "refers to".
This specifies the association to be created between the
document and the entity found by the search.

Optional. Default="false".
If set to true, files will only be searched if they are
considered new, that is, if the file has been downloaded or
created by an application run using the CommandLine
option: see Run a Command Line for a Document File.

Search Patterns

BaseFilePath

FirstMatchOnly

Characteristic

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

Description

Optional.
As a file is searched, its content is stored temporarily in a
text file. By default the text files are deleted once searching
is completed. It may be desirable to keep the temporary
text files for debugging or for using as the input to Data
Extractor during testing (it will be much quicker to have
Data Extractor search the text files as opposed to having
them be recreated from the originals). To keep the text
files a directory must be specified to store the files. The
text files will be stored under the temporary directory
specified using the TempDirectory setting (see Set the
Temporary Directory (Optional)):
<TempDirectory>c:\Temp</TempDirectory>
The files will be stored in a directory structure under the
TempDirectory path. The sub-directory will be the input file
path with the BaseFilePath setting path removed from the
start.
So if an input file path was:
C:\Documents\DE
Tests\Test\Source\F1\myfile.doc
And the temp directory:
<TempDirectory>c:\Temp</TempDirectory>
And the BaseFilePath (must be a parent folder of the input
folder):
BaseFilePath="C:\Documents\DE
Tests\Test\Source\"
Then the temp file created would be:
C:\Temp\F1\myfile.doc.txt

Optional. Default="false".
If set to true then the searching of files will stop after the
first tag is found and subsequent tags (if any) will not be
considered. The purpose of this is that often when
searching a file for tags many tags can be found that are
not the main subject of the file. For example, on a
datasheet it is likely that the first tag found is the tag the
datasheet is defining and any subsequent tags are just
references. In such cases it may be desirable to only
associate the datasheet to the first tag and not subsequent
tags. This setting allows that.

Optional.
If specified, the tags found in the file will be written to a
characteristic of the document object. The characteristic
name is that specified and the value will be a comma
separated list of tags.

Search Patterns

Description

CharacteristicMax
Length

CreateAssociations

Optional.
If a 'Characteristic' is specified and the length of the value
of that characteristic (list of tags) exceeds this value then a
warning will be reported. The characteristic and full value
will still be written out.

Optional. Default="true".
If using the 'Characteristic' setting and associations are not
to be written out then set this to 'false'.

ProcessedFile/MoveFile Optional. Default = "true".

ProcessedFile/PathOnSu
ccess

ProcessedFile/PathOnFa
il

If PathOnSuccess or PathOnFail are specified then the file
processed will be either moved or copied to the specified
location. If this is set to "false" then the file will be copied
otherwise moved.

Optional.
If specified, any file processed that does not error will be
copied or moved to this path (full file path).

Optional.
If specified, any file processed that does error will be
copied or moved to this path (full file path).
A file may typically error if there is no indexfilter installed
for the file type or timeout is exceeded.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

The entities/tags found in a file will be associated to the top object in the Mapping section

which should generally be a document object. The mapping structure should generally

follow this pattern:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="DocumentNumber"/>    Top object
..
<Association Type=" is fulfilled by ">

<TargetID ApplyBehaviour="concat('File: ',DocumentNumber)"/>
<TargetClassID Value="File"/>
<TargetContext Value="Test "/>
<Characteristic ID="InfoType">

<Value Value="text/aspx"/>

</Characteristic>
<Characteristic ID="InfoLocator">

<Value From="FileLocation">

<DocumentFile>
...

which would result in XML output such as:

<Object>

<ID>Document Number 1234</ID>
Context>
..
<Association type="is fulfilled by">

<Object>

<ID>File: Document Number 1234</ID>
<Context>

<ID>Test</ID>

</Context>
<ClassID>File</ClassID>
<Characteristic>

<Name>InfoType</Name>
<Value>text/aspx</Value>
</Characteristic>

<Characteristic>

<Name>InfoLocator</Name>
<Value>http://....pdf</Value>

</Characteristic>

</Object>

</Association>
<Association type="refers to">

<Object>

<ID>P-100</ID>
<Context>

<ID>Test</ID>

</Context>

</Object>

</Association>

Release 5.0.11

Page 72

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

<Association type="refers to">

<Object>

<ID>ABC*1234</ID>
<Context>

<ID>Test</ID>

</Context>
<ClassID>Equipment</ClassID>
<Association type="is identified by">

<Object>

<ID>ABC-1234</ID>
<Context>

<ID>Test</ID>

</Context>

</Object>

</Association>

</Object>

</Association>
..

Validation of Content/Tags Found

When searching document files for content (typically tags) often a good percentage of the

tags found may not be real tags, but just text with a similar format to a tag. The same

validation rules that can be applied to any mapping from a data source (see Data

Validation) can be applied to the tags found by the document indexing:

<Search>

<Pattern Value="[A-Z]{2,3}-\d{5,7}(-[A-Z]|)">

<Validate Name="TagCheck">
<Datasource Id="Ds1"/>
<Action>DiscardElement</Action>

</Validate>

</Pattern>
<Context Value="Tag"/>

</Search>

In the example above, any tags found would be compared with the tags from the

validation data source (lookup list), and if not present the tag will not be reported in the

Data Extractor output. The default action is DiscardElement so it need not be specified,

but is included above for completeness.

Release 5.0.11

Page 73

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

6.3

Run a Command Line for a Document File

When dealing with a document register it is possible to have the document files processed

by an external application (for example if an AutoCAD P&ID were found and/or

downloaded it may be desirable to run the AutoCAD Gateway on it).

To specify that an external application should be run, a command line should be specified

in the DocumentFile settings as shown below:

<DocumentFile>

<CommandLine TimeOutMilliSecs="100000" NewFileOnly="true">

<PathToExecutable Value = "&quot;C:\...\4.5
SP1\GatewayConfig.exe&quot;"/>

<Arguments ApplyBehaviour = "concat('&quot;c:\My Files\',
File_Path,'\', File_Name,'&quot; &quot;C:\Acad GW
Projects\Output&quot;','&quot;C:\Acad GW
Projs\Proj1.xml&quot; -gAC2D-oSVG-noProgress')"/>

</CommandLine>

</DocumentFile>

Settings

Description

PathToExecutable Mandatory.

Full path to the executable to be run. If there are spaces in the
path it will need to be surrounded by double quotes which in
XML syntax is: &quot;

Arguments

Optional.
Arguments to be passed to the executable.

TimeOutMilliSecs

NewFileOnly

Optional. Default = infinite.
By default, the Data Extractor will wait for the executable called
to finish. Any problem in the executable called could mean that
the Data Extractor cannot complete, therefore it is advisable to
specify a time out. When the time out is reached, the Data
Extractor will stop the executable called and then continue.

Optional. Default = "False".
If set to true, the command line will only be run if the TargetFile
is considered to be new, that is, if the target file does not exist
before the document record is processed then it is new. Note
that if no target file is specified then the SourceFile existence is
considered for the purposes of determining newness, as it is
assumed that the source file is being processed.
If source file is not specified, then this setting should not be
used.
This option can allow the command line to be run for new files
which can act as a sort of incremental processing

Release 5.0.11

Page 74

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

6.4

Post-Process SVG Files

Often tags (especially instrument tags) and document numbers (in off sheet connectors)

on P&IDs are numbered with a partial tag or document number to save space. This

incomplete numbering will generally lead to hot-spotting issues for the output of the

AVEVA Gateway Configuration Tool. For example instrument tags may not, show the 'full'

tag number - a tag '50-TI-1234' may appear on the P&ID as 'TI-1234', and post-processing

is necessary to add the missing information. The Data Extractor supports post-processing

of AVEVA Gateway Configuration Tool SVGs (and optionally their associated XML files).

The post-processing capability allows manipulation of tags and/or document numbers to:

· Add a prefix to a tag and/or document number
· Replace/remove part of a tag and/or document number
· Insert text into a tag and/or document number, for example, a dash

An example Data Extractor configuration file for post-processing SVGs is shown below:

<ConfigurationSettings>

<Datasource Directory="Source" Type="Directory"Filter="*.svg"/>
<OutputFile Directory="Output"/>
<Mapping Type="Object">
<ID From="Name" />
<Context Value="TEST"/>
<SVGProcess UpdateAvngateFile="true">

<SourceLocation From="FullName"/>
<TargetLocation Value="C:\SVGs\Output"/>
<Prefix

Separator="-"
FileNamePattern="(?&lt;=-[A-Z]{2})\d{3}"
ExcludePattern="^\d{3}-|PID-|&quot;"/>

<Prefix

IncludePattern="^\d{3}-[A-Z]"
ExcludePattern="^\d{3}-|PID-|&quot;"

            Separator="-"
            FileNamePattern="(?&lt;=-[A-Z]{2})\d{3}-"/>
      <IdManipulation>

<Replace>

<Original>-PIT-</Original>
<ReplacementString>-PT-</ReplacementString>

</Replace>
</IdManipulation>

</SVGProcess>

</Mapping>

</ConfigurationSettings>

Release 5.0.11

Page 75

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 6 - Handle Document Files

Settings

SourceLocation

TargetLocation

IncludePattern

ExcludePattern

Separator

FileNamePattern

UpdateAvngateFile

IdManipulation

Description

Mandatory. The full path to the SVG file.

Optional. Directory to write the modified SVG (and Avngate
XML file) to. If not specified the originals will be
overwritten.

Optional. If specified only tags that match this regular
expression pattern will get prefixed. If not specified then all
tags will be prefixed if they pass the 'ExcludePattern'
check.

Optional. If specified tags that match this regular expression
will not be prefix even if the tag matches the
'IncludePattern' pattern. So for example if a tag has a
double quote then it is likely a line and may not need
prefixing.

Optional. A sting, for example, "-" that will go between the
prefix and the tag.

A regular expression pattern that is applied to the SVG file
name to extract the prefix. The assumption is that the prefix
is in the SVG file name. This is necessary as without this
there will be nothing to prefix the tags with.

Optional. Default = "true".
By default the Data Extractor will look for the associated
_avngate.xml file and update the tags in that file. To
prevent this set this to false.

Optional. The syntax for this is the same as that explained in
the section Modifying Tags Found except that the 'Expand'
and 'Separate' options are not supported when
IDManipulation is used for post-processing SVG files.

Release 5.0.11

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

7

Mapping (Mandatory)

The mapping section is where rows/nodes from the data source are mapped to AVEVA

NET objects/classes/attributes. Each configuration file will have one or more mapping

sections. Each mapping section will be applied to each entity (row or node) in the data

source in turn. A typical usage of multiple mapping sections would be where the first

mapping section creates a tagged object in the XML output file(s) and the second mapping

section creates a dataset relating to the same tagged object and creates an association

between the two.

Each mapping section format will resemble:

<Mapping Type="Object">

….

</Mapping>

The type setting can be either 'Object', 'Class' or 'CharacteristicClass'. 'Class' and

'CharacteristicClass' mappings can exist in the same configuration file but they cannot

be mixed with 'Object' mappings.

7.1

Object Mapping

A typical object mapping is as follows:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="ID"/>
<ClassID From="TYPE"/>
<Context Value="TEST"/>

</Mapping>

A typical input Object mapping file

<Mapping Type="Object">
    <!--Any From or ApplyBehaviour mapping can have 0 or more of the following
XML attributes to manipulate the value: RegExPattern=".."
RegExPatternRightToLeft=".." RegExSearch=".." RegExReplacePattern=".."
RegExReplaceString=".." RegExRemovePattern=".." RegExReconstruct=".."
SetCase="Upper/Lower/Proper" Default=".."-->
    <ID From="TAG_x0020_ID" />
    <Name From="Tag_x0020_Class" />
    <ClassID From="Tag_x0020_Class" />
    <Context Value="IPE" />
    <LifecycleState>
      <Context Value="IPE" />
      <ID
From="Tag_x0020_Life_x0020_Cycle_x0020__x0020_Status_x0020_" />
      <LifecycleGroup>
        <Context Value="IPE" />
        <ID From="Life_x0020_Cycle_x0020_Group" />
      </LifecycleGroup>
      <Characteristic>

Release 5.0.11

Page 77

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

        <ID Value="Author " />
        <Value From="Author_x0020_" Default="" />
      </Characteristic>
      <Characteristic>
        <ID Value="Author Role" />
        <Value From="Author_x0020_Role" Default="" />
      </Characteristic>
    </LifecycleState>
  </Mapping>
</ConfigurationSettings>

Note:  The value of the special variable *MAIN_ID*is replaced by the value calculated

from the first ID in the mapping section, that is,

<ID From="ID"/>

The calculation of the main id can often be quite complex and involve searching and

replacing etc. If such a case, it is easier to re-use the result of that calculation rather than

respecify it. This variable can be used in any mapping line and is not restricted to id or

TargetID mappings.

The output EIWM XML for the above mapping is as follows:

 <Object>
   <ID>E-9001</ID>
   <Context>
    <ID>IPE</ID>
   </Context>
   <Name>Pump</Name>
   <ClassID>Pump</ClassID>
   <LifecycleStates>
    <LifecycleState>
     <ID>IN PROGRESS</ID>
     <Context>
      <ID>IPE</ID>
     </Context>
     <LifecycleGroup>
      <ID>WORK STATUS</ID>
      <Context>
       <ID>IPE</ID>
      </Context>
     </LifecycleGroup>
     <Characteristic>
      <Name>Author</Name>
      <Value>Nick</Value>
     </Characteristic>
     <Characteristic>
      <Name>Author Role</Name>
      <Value>Reviewer</Value>
     </Characteristic>
    </LifecycleState>
   </LifecycleStates>
  </Object>

Release 5.0.11

Page 78

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Notes:

· For the ClassID mapping, any multiple spaces found are replaced by a single
space automatically as AVEVA NET does not not allow a class with multiple

spaces.

· Lifecycle State: Represents the state of an object at different stages in its

lifecycle. There are a set of attributes which can be assigned to a lifecycle state

associated to an Object instance. The attribute data exists for the lifecycle state

within the context of the object instance. For example, the lifecycle state can be

Not Released, General Release, Release to Design, Release to Safety, Release to

Manufacturing and so on.

· Lifecycle Group: It is the grouping of all the lifecycle states for a specific process.
Each lifecycle state within a lifecycle group has an optional sequence number. If

the lifecycle group is subject to sequential control, then a subject can only be

associated to lifecycle states in the order determined by their respective

sequence numbers. A lifecycle state can be rolled back to an earlier state in the

group sequence. Within each lifecycle group, the user can record lifecycle status

in two modes:

· Exclusively: Allows only a single state type within the group to be set on the

object at one time.

· Non-exclusively: Allows multiple state types within the group to be set on
the object at one time. For example, “approved for design” and “approved

for construction”.

· Lifecycle Characteristic: Each lifecycle group has specific properties or attributes
known as lifecycle characteristics. These lifecycle states and lifecycle groups are

created in Workhub & Dashboard if not present.

Basic Mappings

Object mapping will create object instances for AVEVA NET and the format of the mapping

for the high level object data (identity, class…) will resemble:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID ApplyBehaviour="concat(Prefix, '-', Tag, Suffix)"/>
<Name From="Description" Default="N/A" RegExPattern="[AZ]{3}"

RegExRemovePattern="\s"/>

<ClassID Value="Pump"/>
<Context Value="Training"/>
….

</Mapping>

Release 5.0.11

Page 79

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

The example shows four mappings. The left side, for example, id is the AVEVA NET

property which is followed by either: From, Value or ApplyBehaviour which define how

the value for the property should be determined and one, and only one of these three

should be specified:

Property

Description

From

Value

Datasource column (or node) name from which value should be read

Hard coded string that will be used for the value.
Note: There are some special variables than can be used - see Special
Variables in Object Mapping.

ApplyBehaviour Perform some processing on datasource value(s) to determine the

value. The setting for the ApplyBehaviour can be any XPath 1.0
functions (XPath 2.0 functions are not supported as it is not supported
by .Net Framework).
Note: There are some special variables than can be used - see Special
Variables in Object Mapping.

For any mapping (including characteristics) that uses From or ApplyBehaviour it is

possible to optionally specify some additional settings:

Property

Default

SetCase

Description

A default value that will be used if no value is found in the datasource.
If no value is found in the datasource for a mapped
property/characteristic then that will not be included in the output file
unless you should define a default for it - if you want it written with no
value specify default="".

Allows the case of a value to be altered as it is written out. The
allowable values of this attribute are Upper; Lower or Proper. The
option 'Proper' will make the text more user friendly by removing
multiple spaces and convert text such as 'HELLO world' to 'Hello
World'.

RegExPattern A regular expression pattern that will be applied to the value to search
the value for a sub-string and that sub-string will then be the reported
value. If no match is found then the value returned will be an empty
string.

RegExPattern
RightToLeft

When extracting part of a value using the RegExPattern setting the
pattern may be found more than once, for example, if value was VX-
100/LT-100 and the RegExPattern were [A-Z]{2}-\d{3}. By
default the first string from the left to match the pattern is the
reported value (for example, VX-100) but by setting this option to
'true' then the first string from the right will be the reported value (for
example, LT-100).

Release 5.0.11

Page 80

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Property

Description

RegExSearch

A regular expression pattern defining a part of the value to be
replaced. What the replacing string will be is determined by either a
RegExReplaceString or a RegExReplacePattern setting. At least
1 of the replace setting must be specified, otherwise setting the search
will be meaningless and an error will be reported.
It is possible to specify multiple search patterns and multiple replace
patterns. To specify multiple patterns, separate them with ||, for
example:

RegExSearch="[A-Z]-\d{3}||[A-Z]\d||[AZ]-[A-Z]"

Similarly for the replace settings RegExReplacePattern and
RegExReplaceString:

RegExReplaceString="X-||X||X-X"

The number or search and replaces specified must match or an error
will be reported.

RegExReplace
String

A string that will replace the value found by the RegExSearch
setting.

RegExReplace
Pattern

A regular expression pattern that is applied to the value found that will
determine the string to replace the value found by the RegExSearch
setting. The RegExReplacePattern setting will take precedence
over RegExReplaceString if both are specified.

RegExRemove
Pattern

A regular expression pattern to identify part of the value to be
removed. This could be used to remove spaces or dashes from a value.

RegExReconst
ruct

A list of regular expressions that allow a value to be reconstructed
from its constituent parts:
RegExReconstruct=
"[A-Z]{2}||'-'||\d{4}||'-'||\d$"

The list should be separated by ||. Each regular expression will be
applied to the value in turn with the results of the regular expressions
being joined together to form the final value. So the above should
result in a value of format: AB-1234-1. Note that strings (for
example, '-') can be included within the regular expression list to add
separators for example.

Release 5.0.11

Page 81

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Multiple regular expression options can be specified that modify a found value. They are

applied in the following order, with the result of the preceding operation being the value

passed to the next:

· RegExPattern
· RegExReplacePattern
· RegExReplaceString
· RegExRemovePattern
· RegExReconstruct

If the data source is XML, then it is acceptable to put a path for From and

ApplyBehaviour statements to reference from the base path (see ObjectNodePath in

XML Files) to the desired node, for example:

<Name From="Details/Name"/>

Mapping Characteristics, Properties and Associations

Characteristic mappings have the format:

<Characteristic>
   <ID Value="Status">
   <Value From="State" Default=""/>
</Characteristic>

Or the characteristic id can be read from data source values:

<Characteristic>
   <ID From="Names">
   <Value From="Values" Default=""/>/>
</Characteristic>

Properties are very similar but they can also have a unit of measure specified:

<Property>
   <ID Value="Press">
   <Value From="PRESSURE" Default=""/>
   <Units Value="barg"/>
</Property>

The ID, Value and Units settings are specified in the same way as the basic mappings, see

Basic Mappings in Object Mapping.

Association mappings have the format:

<Association Type="is referenced in">
   <TargetID From="DocID"/>
   <TargetRevision From="Rev"/>
   <TargetClassID From="Type"/>
   <TargetContext Value="TEST"/>
   <TargetName From="Description"/>
   <Characteristic>
      <ID Value="State">
      <Value From="Status"/>

Release 5.0.11

Page 82

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

   </Characteristic>
</Association>

The association type, for example, is referenced in, should be a valid AVEVA NET

association type.

An association can contain an association (associations can be cascaded to any number of

levels):

<Association Type="is referenced in">
   <TargetID From="DocID"/>
   <TargetRevision From="Rev"/>
   <TargetClassID From="Type"/>
   <TargetContext Value="TEST"/>
   <TargetName From="Description"/>
   <Characteristic>
      <ID Value="State">
      <Value From="Status"/>
   </Characteristic>
<Association Type="is in security access group">
   <TargetID ....
</Association>
</Association>

Associations can have an additional (optional) attribute called 'Intent' specified:

<Association Type="is classified as" Intent="Delete">

Setting Intent to 'Delete' will force the association to be deleted, no matter which

template/file created the association.

Wildcards in Characteristic and Property Mapping

For a tabular (that is, non-XML) data sources there may be many columns, each

representing an attribute. These could be mapped by adding a Characteristic or Property

mapping for each column, but that could mean a lot of mapping sections. An alternative is

to use a 'wildcard' that will map every column as an attribute with just one mapping

section:

<Characteristic>
   <ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
   <Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
</Characteristic>

In this case, every column will be mapped as a Characteristic. In general the mapping will

need to be a little more complex, as there are likely to be some columns that are not to be

treated as characteristics:

<Characteristic ConditionColumnName="not(startswith('*COLUMN_NAME*',)
'Tag')">
   <ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
   <Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
</Characteristic>

Release 5.0.11

Page 83

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

In this case all columns except those whose header begins with 'Tag' will be mapped as

characteristics. Note the use of the setting 'ConditionColumnName' that is column

names.

This is very similar to the 'Condition' setting frequently used in Data Extractor

configuration files except that the setting 'ConditionColumnName' is used to filter column

names and not the values within the columns.

A further complication is that for properties, the unit of measure value is often stored in a

separate column to the actual values. In this case, both columns need to be considered

together. As an example, if there was a column 'Temp' and a column 'Temp_UOM', then

one property from these two columns would be required:

<Property ConditionColumnName="not(contains('*COLUMN_NAME*','_UOM'))
and count(*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM)>0">
<ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
<Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
<Units From="*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM"/>

</Property>

The condition excludes columns with '_UOM' in the header as these are not wanted as

properties. They are just units of measure. The second part of the condition (the 'count')

only considers columns where there is an additional column with the same name with

'_UOM' appended, that is, if there is no such second column, then there is no UOM and

therefore it is likely a characteristic and not a property.

So typically a characteristic and a property section would be required and each column

will fall into:

<Characteristic
ConditionColumnName="stringlength(*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM)=0 and starts-
with(*COLUMN_NAME*, 'Att_')">
   <ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
   <Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
</Characteristic>
<Property ConditionColumnName="stringlength(*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM)>0
and starts-with(*COLUMN_NAME*, 'Att_')">
   <ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
   <Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
   <Units From="*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM"/>
</Property>

The 'Validate', 'ValueMap' and 'ClassCharacteristicDatasource' operations that are

often used to manipulate attributes can be used, for example:

<Property ConditionColumnName="stringlength(*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM)
>0">
   <ID From="*COLUMN_NAME*" SetCase="Upper">
      <ValueMap>
         <Datasource Id = "Attribute Map"/>
         <FailAction>EmptyValue</FailAction>
      </ValueMap>

Release 5.0.11

Page 84

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

   </ID>
<Value From="*COLUMN_NAME*"/>
<Units From="*COLUMN_NAME*_UOM">
   <Validate Name="UOM Lookup">
   <Datasource Id="UOM Lookup"/>
   <FailAction>DiscardElement</FailAction>
</Validate>
</Units>
</Property>

Having a wild carded column name introduces a complication for look up values in that

the lookup list name can't be specified as it will vary from column to column. A special

validation datasource can be defined that only is necessary when using wild carded

column names:

<ValidationDatasource
   Id="All Characteristic Lookup Values"
   File="C:\ Tests\Class Lib.xlsx"
   ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;… Table="
Lookups">
   <Details FilterColumn = "ATTRIBUTE_NAME"
   LookupList="ATTRIBUTE_VALUE" />
</ValidationDatasource>

The additional setting 'FilterColumn' allows the lookups relevant to a particular attribute

to be determined by searching a list that holds values for many attributes, for example:

In such a case the actual characteristic or property ID value will be applied to the

'FilterColumn' so that only those values relevant become considered for the lookup list for

each characteristic or property.

Release 5.0.11

Page 85

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Conditions

Often not all the items in a data source will need to be considered, or items with some

common property differently may be needed to be treated different. This can be achieved

by applying a condition to a mapping, for example:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="Class='Heat exchanger'' and
not(Manufacturer='ACME')">

In this case, the mapping will only be applied (and therefore output data generated for) to

those items where the 'Class' column does and the 'Manufacturer' column does not

match the values listed. Any number of and/or statements can be combined to provide

the desired logic (use lower case for 'and' and for 'or').

A condition can in the same way be applied to an association mapping and a characteristic

mapping:

<Association Type="is referenced in" Condition="Area='Area1'">
<TargetID From="Document"/>
<TargetClassID Value="Document"/>
..
</Association>

Or:

<Characteristic Condition="string-length(Temp)>0">
   <ID Value=" Temperature "/>
   <Value From="Temp">
</Characteristic >

In such cases, the association or characteristic will only be generated in the output for an

object if the condition is passed.

Common examples of uses of a condition

Testing a field exists in the input:

<Characteristic Condition="Temp">

Testing a field has a value:

<Characteristic Condition="string-length(Temp)>0">

Testing a field does not have a value:

<Characteristic Condition="string-length(Temp)=0">

Note: 'Special Variables' (see Special Variables in Object Mapping) can be used in a

condition.

<Characteristic Condition="substring-after(*INPUT_FILE_NAME_SHORT*,
'-')='test'">

Release 5.0.11

Page 86

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

In some scenarios it may be possible to achieve the same result using the data source

SQLSelect setting described in  Specify an SQL SELECT Statement instead of a Table (not for

XML file data sources), but these two mechanisms do differ in a number of ways:

· With a condition it is not possible to filter rows based on the maximum/minimum
value of a property - only those entries that represent the maximum revision of a

document are considered.

· If the SQLSelect statement used filters out certain rows, then these rows will not
be available to any mapping section whereas different mapping sections can use

different conditions to treat the data source rows differently based on a given

criteria.

· SQLSelect cannot be used on XML data sources as the SQL language is only

relevant to databases.

Modifying Tags Found

Tags may come from different sources that may not be consistent in their naming. Such

inconsistencies may be allowed for by manipulating a tag once found, and using the

manipulated tag in the output.

For example, one application may report a tag as 'P-100A' whereas the same item may

be referred to in another by 'P100A' and in another application as 'P-100A/B' or 'P-

100A-C'. For AVEVA NET, it is essential to consolidate these.

Within a mapping, it is possible to manipulate the value found via the id mapping by using

an IdManipulation section:

<Mapping Type="Object">
<ID From="Tag"/>
<Name From="Description"/>
<ClassID Value="Pump"/>
<Context Value="Training"/>
<IdManipulation>
   <Expand>
      <Pattern>[A-Z]&amp;[A-Z]</Pattern>
      <Char>&amp;</Char>
   </Expand>
   <Expand Interpolate = "false">
      <Pattern>[A-Z]-[A-Z]\b</Pattern>
      <Char>-</Char>
   </Expand>
<Exclude>\bPID</Exclude>
<Replace>
   <Original>[A-Z]-\d{3}</Original>
   <Replacement>[A-Z]\d{3}</Replacement>
</Replace>
<Replace>
   <Original>-A$</Original>

Release 5.0.11

Page 87

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

   <ReplacementString>A</ReplacementString>
</Replace>
   <Insert Position="before">
      <Pattern>\d{3}[A-Z]</Pattern>
      <String>-</String>
   </Insert>
   <Separate>
   <Pattern>;</Pattern>
   </Separate>
</IdManipulation>

There are various types of manipulation supported, all of which use Regular Expressions

for searching within a tag. These are detailed in following sections.

Sometimes it is necessary to be aware of the order in which these are applied as the result

of one manipulation will be the input to the next manipulation. The order is as follows:

the separation of tags is done, then each tag is expanded and each resulting tag separately

has all the search/replace applied, and then finally all inserts are applied.

'Expanding' a Tag

One or more '<Expand>' sections specify expansion characters '<Char>' that when

found in a tag may result in that tag's expansion. The example below states that both

'&' (represented in XML as &amp;) and '-' will result in expansion if found in a sequence

represented by the corresponding pattern.

For example, if a tag 'P-100A&B&C' is found in a document then the search pattern '[A-

Z](&amp; [A-Z]){1,}' would be applied to it resulting in 'A', 'B' and 'C', which would

then be split using the character '&' resulting in 'A', 'B' and 'C';." The gateway would then

report 'P-100A', 'P-100B' and 'P-100C' as being associated with the document.

Note that 'P-100A&B&C' also contains a '-', which could be an expansion character, but

as it is not within a string matched by the associated, that is, '[A-Z]-[A-Z]' then it will not

be used for expansion for this tag. So the expansion character and the pattern (which

must contain the expansion character), allow the necessary flexibility for handling

common tag formats.

Finally, it is possible for an '<Expand>' to set the attribute 'Interpolate' to "true" (default

is false), which will result in the gateway creating items implied within a tag such as 'P-

100A-D' that is, 'P-100A', 'P-100B' etc. The interpolate feature is restricted to cases

where the expansion character separates two other characters.

<Expand>
   <Pattern>[A-Z](&amp;[A-Z]{1,}</Pattern>
   <Char>&amp;</Char>
</Expand>
<Expand Interpolate = "true">
   <Pattern>[A-Z]-[A-Z]</Pattern>
   <Char>-</Char>
</Expand>

Release 5.0.11

Page 88

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Only the final occurrence of each pattern will be considered for expansion. So the tag 'P-

A5-100A-B' would not be expanded at both 'P-A' and 'A-B'. If such a scenario were possible

it would be better to make the pattern only apply at the end of a tag, that is,

<Pattern>[A-Z]-[AZ]$</Pattern>', to avoid unintended expansions.

Replacing Part of a Tag

'<Original>' takes a Regular Expression pattern that specifies the part of the tag that will

be replaced. There are two options to define what the replacement text would be:

Option

Replacement Text

Replacement

A Regular Expression pattern that will be applied to the text
found by the 'Original' pattern to determine what the
replacement text is. This option only allows replacement of the
'Original' with a sub-part of the 'Original'. So for example: 'P-'
could be replaced by 'P' with the following:
<Replace>
     <Original>[A-Z]-</Original>
     <Replacement>[A-Z]</Replacement>
</Replace>
Note: If an 'Original' pattern is found more than once then each
occurrence will be replaced.

ReplacementString A simple piece of text that will replace the text found by the

'Original' pattern. So for example: 'XV-' at the start of a tag could
be replaced by 'HV' with the following:
<Replace>
     <Original>^XV-</Original>
     <Replacement>HV-</Replacement>
</Replace>
Note: If an 'Original' pattern is found more than once then each
occurrence will be replaced.

Inserting a String into a Tag

One or more 'Insert' sections enables a '<String>' to be inserted into a tag at the

position determined by the result of a '<Pattern>' search.

The example below would enable a tag found in a document as 'P100' to be reported as

'P- 100'. This could not be achieved with the 'Replace' section discussed above. The

'<Position>' attribute specifies whether the '<String>' is added 'before' or 'after' the

'<Pattern>'. It defaults to 'after'.

<Insert Position="after">
   <Pattern>\b[A-Z]</Pattern>
   <String>-</String>
</Insert>

Release 5.0.11

Page 89

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Separating a Value into Multiple Tags

A 'Separate' section (only one per IdManipulation section allowed) takes a value

representing a list of tags and breaks it into its constituent tags.

For example, if a value were 'Doc1;Doc2;Doc3', then the following section could be

used to report the three tags as separate entities:

<Separate>
   <Pattern>;</Pattern>
</Separate>

The 'Pattern' can be any regular expression pattern. If the pattern is not found in a value,

then the value is treated as representing one entity.

Modifying an Association's TargetID

It is also possible to manipulate the TargetID of an association in a similar way to that of

the primary tag:

<Association Type="is a part of">
<TargetID From="Tag2"/>
<TargetContext Value="Training"/>
<TargetIdManipulation>
      <Expand>
         <Pattern>[A-Z]/[A-Z]</Pattern>
         <Char>/</Char>
      </Expand>
</TargetIdManipulation>
</Association>

The Expand, Exclude, Replace and Insert sections can all be used within the

TargetIdManipulation section.

Special Variables

There are a number of special values that can be used in a 'Value' or 'ApplyBehaviour'

mapping or in a 'Condition'. The special variables are indicated by a start and end '*'

character, for example, *DATE_TIME* and can be used as shown below:

<Characteristic>
   <ID Value="DateTime"/>
   <Value Value="*DATE_TIME*"/>
</Characteristic>

In this case the characteristic value in the output file will be the date-time of the Data

Extractor run.

Release 5.0.11

Page 90

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Special Variable

Description

*CURRENT_USER*

Windows account of user the Data Extractor is run as

*DATE_TIME*

Time and date of the Data Extractor run

*DATE*

Date of the Data Extractor run

*INPUT_FILE_NAME*

Full path of datasource input file name

*INPUT_FILE_NAME_S
HORT*

Name of datasource input file with no path

*INPUT_FILE_NAME_S
HORT_NO_EXT*

Name of datasource input file with no path and no
extension

*CONFIG_FILE_NAME* Full path of configuration file name

*CONFIG_FILE_NAME_
SHORT*

*CONFIG_FILE_NAME_
SHORT_NO_EXT*

Name of configuration file with no path

Name of configuration file with no path and no extension

Note: These variables can also be used when specifying a log file, see Log File (Optional).

Reading Excel Serialised Dates

Dates read from Excel can sometimes be read as a 'serialised' date, that is, an integer that

Excel uses to internally store a date. So for example, '12th of March 2013' can be returned

as '41345'. This can be corrected using the 'ConvertSerialDate' setting:

<Characteristic>

<ID Value="Date" />
<Value From="Date" ConvertSerialDate="true"/>

</Characteristic>

This will convert the serialised date to a meaningful date '12/3/2013' - the date format is

determined by local settings of the machine that Data Extractor is running on. If a value is

read that is not a date (for example, if there is some non-date text in a cell), then a

warning will be issued and the original value will be written to the output file.

Release 5.0.11

Page 91

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

For example:

An empty value will not result in a warning.

Adding Days/Month/Year to Excel Date

Command

Functionalities

AddDays (Syntax example: AddDays="61") Adds the specified number of days to the
given date column in the Data source, for
example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

AddYears (Syntax example: AddYears="1") Adds the specified number of years to the
given date column in the Data source, for
example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

AddMonths (Syntax example:
AddMonths="16")

Adds the specified number of months to
the given date column in the Data source,
for example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

Syntax:

<Association Type="has date">

<TargetID From="Date" AddDays="2"/>
<TargetContext Value="FW_TEST"/>

</Association>

Removing Days/Month/Year from Excel Date

Command

Functionalities

RemoveDays (Syntax example:
RemoveDays="61")

RemoveYears (Syntax example:
RemoveYears="1")

RemoveMonths (Syntax example:
RemoveMonths="16")

Removes the specified number of days to
the given date column in the Data source,
for example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

Removes the specified number of years to
the given date column in the Data source,
for example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

Removes the specified number of months
to the given date column in the Data
source, for example, Excel, Oracle, SQL

Release 5.0.11

Page 92

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Syntax:

<Association Type="has date">

<TargetID From="Date" RemoveDays="2"/>
<TargetContext Value="FW_TEST"/>

</Association>

Notes:

· The date column gets modified into a new Date column after you apply the Date
manipulation function to the dates and you can get the desired output. If the

Date is not in the correct format, for example, 1stdecember1990, the date

manipulation function is not applicable to this date value. Date manipulation

function proceeds  with the other Date values present in the data source.

· If you enter the Date in serial format, on AddDays function, it converts the serial
Date format to general Date format and Days are added to the converted Date

format.

Capturing Object Deletion

The most common method of using Data Extractor is to delete and recreate the relevant

staging area XML file(s) as part of each run. This will capture the deletion of any object

from the data-source.

When the Data Extractor is setup to write out an XML file per object (see Output

File/Directory (Mandatory) the processing times can take a few hours, which can be a

problem.

An alternative is to only write out XML files for objects that have been modified (the

datasource needs to indicate what's modified - see Store the Last Run Start Time). In

addition, it may be required to remove the existing staging area XML files for objects that

have been 'deleted'. If the datasource indicates what has been 'deleted' then the 'Intent'

setting can be used:

<OutputFile Directory="C: \Output" TriggerFileName="trigger.start">
   <File ApplyBehaviour="concat(Tag_x0020_Status, ' ', Tag_x0020_Number,
'_null.xml')" ReplaceInvalidChrs="_"/>
</OutputFile>
<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="not(Tag_x0020_Status='VOID')">
      <ID From="Tag_x0020_Number"/>
      <ClassID From="Class" />
      <Context Value="Test" />
  …
</Mapping>
<Mapping Type="Object" Intent="Delete"
Condition="Tag_x0020_Status='VOID'">
   <ID From="Tag_x0020_Number"/>
</Mapping>

Release 5.0.11

Page 93

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

In the example above, the 'deletion' is indicated by a column in the data-source 'Tag

Status' having the value 'VOID'. The first 'Mapping' section will create an XML file for

each current object and the second 'Mapping' section will delete the XML for all 'VOID'

objects.

The mappings are carried out in order and order could be important if the 'Condition' are

not mutually exclusive, for example, the first mapping could create an XML and the

second could then delete it or vice versa. For a deletion mapping the ID mapping only

needs to be specified as shown above - other mappings will be ignored.

7.2

Class Mapping

A typical class mapping resembles:

<Mapping Type="Class">

<ClassID From="Type"/>
<ClassName From="Type"/>
<PluralName From="ClassName"/>
<ParentClassID Value="DOCUMENT CONTENT"/>
<Definition Value="U" />
<ReplaceAttributes Value="true"/>
<Visible Value="true"/>
<Hidden Value="true"/>
<ClassIcon From="Icon"/>
<ClassProperty>

<SchemeName Value="RDS"/>
<ID From="RDS"/>

</ClassProperty>
<ClassProperty>

<SchemeName Value="ISO"/>
<ID From="ISO"/>

</ClassProperty>

</Mapping>

The principle is generally very similar to that for the object mapping, though the

specifications required are different, for example, ClassID instead of ID, and Context is not

relevant.

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<Class>

<ClassID>PID</ClassID>
<ParentClassID>DOCUMENT CONTENT</ParentClassID>
<ClassName>PID</ClassName >
<PluralName>PID</PluralName>
<ReplaceAttributes>true</ReplaceAttributes>
<Definition>U</Definition>
<Visible>true</Visible>
<Hidden>true</Hidden>
<ClassProperties>

Release 5.0.11

Page 94

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

<ClassProperty SchemeName="RDS" ID="RDS12345" />
<ClassProperty SchemeName="ISO" ID="ISO12345" />

</ClassProperties>
</Class>

Notes:

· The 'ReplaceAttributes' setting is only relevant for AVEVA NET Workhub and

Dashboard 5.0.2 and later versions.

· The 'Definition' setting is only relevant for import to the 'Data & Documents'

product. It is also possible to associate characteristic and property classes with a

class. This is most commonly needed when creating a dataset class. This can also

be specified in the class mapping:

<Mapping Type="Class" Condition="Discipline='Process'">

<ClassID Value="Process Dataset"/>
<ParentClassID Value="Dataset"/>
<Visible Value="true"/>
<Property>

<ID From="Property"/>

</Property>
<Characteristic>

<ID Value="Weight"/>

</Characteristic>

</Mapping>

The property or characteristic ids can be specified in the mapping (as with Weight above),

or can be read from the 'Property' column of the data source.

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<Class>
<ClassID>Process Dataset</ClassID>
<ParentClassID>Dataset</ParentClassID>
<Visible>true</Visible>
<MetaData>

<PropertyClassID>Press</PropertyClassID>
<PropertyClassID>Temp</PropertyClassID>
<CharacteristicClassID>Weight</CharacteristicClassID>

</MetaData>
</Class>

7.3

Attribute Class Grouping

Data and Documents XML supports the grouping and positioning of attributes which in

turn sets grouping and position of attributes in the Data and Documents user interface.

The Data Extractor can produce this XML using a mapping such as:

<Mapping Type="Class">

<ClassID ApplyBehaviour="concat(ClassID, ' - Dataset')" />
<ParentClassID Value="Dataset" />
<Characteristic>

<ID From="AttributeClassID" />
<AttributeGroupID From="AttributeGroup" />

Release 5.0.11

Page 95

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

<AttributeGroupPosition From="AttributeGroupPosition"
/>
<Position From="Position" />

</Characteristic>

</Mapping>

7.4

Characteristic Class Mapping

A Characteristic Class mapping defines a characteristic class and will typically resemble:

<Mapping Type="CharacteristicClass">

<ClassID From="Class"/>
<ParentClassID Value="QualitativeCharacteristic"/>
<ClassName From="Class"/>
<DataType From="Type"/>
<ClassProperty>

<SchemeName Value="RDS"/>
<ID From="RDS"/>

</ClassProperty>
<AllowInvalidDataType Value="true" />

</Mapping>

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<CharacteristicClass>

<ClassID>Model Part</ClassID>
<ParentClassID>QualitativeCharacteristic</ParentClassID>
<ClassName>Model Part</ClassName>
<DataType>string</DataType>
<AllowInvalidDataType>true</AllowInvalidDataType>
<ClassProperties>

<ClassProperty SchemeName="RDS" ID="RDS10000" />

</ClassProperties>
</CharacteristicClass>

Release 5.0.11

Page 96

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

7.5

Permissible Association Mapping

Permissible association mapping defines the objects of a source class that can be

associated with a defined type (with an object of a target class) to create a Class Definition

File. The creation of this Class Definition File also requires mapping of the source class,

association type and target class to column names in a data source (usually a

spreadsheet).

You can use the selected columns of the user interface to generate a project configuration

file that defines those columns to be read from the Excel input file, for example:

<Mapping Type="Class">
      <ClassID From="SourceClassID"/>
      <Association>
         <AssociationType From="SourceToTarget"/>
         <TargetClassID From="TargetClassID"/>
      </Association>
</Mapping>

where the From= values are column names in the data source.

Manual editing of the config file in the old syntax is also supported, for example:

<Mapping Type="Class">
      <ClassID From="SourceClassID"/>
      <Association Type="Enter Association Type">
        <TargetClassID From="TargetClassID"/>
      </Association>
</Mapping>

You can run the Gateway with that project configuration to generate a Class Definition File

that specifies permissible associations, for example:

   <Class>
    <ClassID>A source class from SourceClassID column</ClassID>
    <Association type="type from permissible association type SourceToTarget
column">
      <Class>
        <ClassID>A target class from TargetClassID column</ClassID>
      </Class>
    </Association>
  </Class>

Release 5.0.11

Page 97

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Note: A warning message is logged if both <AssociationType> (new syntax) and

<Association Type=""> (old syntax) are used in the same <Association> element.

In this case, the <AssociationType> is used.  For example, the following syntax is

incorrect and defaults to taking the look-up value using the new <AssociationType>

syntax:

   <Mapping Type="Class">
      <ClassID From="SourceClassID"/>
      <Association Type="Enter Association Type">
        <AssociationType From="SourceToTarget"/>
        <TargetClassID From="TargetClassID"/>
      </Association>
   </Mapping>

7.6

Property Related Class Definitions

The following are the property related class definitions:

PropertyClass Mapping

A PropertyClass mapping defines a property class and would typically resemble:

<Mapping Type="PropertyClass">
<ClassID From="Property"/>
<ClassName From="Property"/>
<ParentClassID Value="Physical Property"/>
<Definition From="Definition"/>
<MeasureClassID From="MeasureClass"/>
<AllowInvalidDataType Value="true" />

<ClassProperty>

<SchemeName Value="RDS"/>
<ID From="RDS"/>

</ClassProperty>

</Mapping>

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<PropertyClass>

<ClassID>Diameter</ClassID>
<ParentClassID>Physical Property</ParentClassID>
<ClassName>Diameter</ClassName>
<Definition>Diameter</Definition>
<MeasureClassID>Length</MeasureClassID>
<AllowInvalidDataType>true</AllowInvalidDataType>
<ClassProperties>

<ClassProperty SchemeName="RDS" ID="RDS20000" />

</ClassProperties>

</PropertyClass>

Note: Each PropertyClass has a MeasureClassID which will need to be defined

separately (see MeasureClass Mappings).

Release 5.0.11

Page 98

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

MeasureClass Mappings

A MeasureClass is a grouping of related UnitOfMeasure classes (for example, all

temperature units) and can be created using a mapping. For example:

<Mapping Type="MeasureClass">
<ClassID From="Class"/>
<ClassName From="ClassName"/>
<Definition From="Definition"/>
<UOMClassID From="UOM"/>
<Scale From="Scale"/>
<Constant From="Constant"/>

</Mapping>

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<MeasureClass>

<ClassID>Temperature</ClassID>
<ClassName>Temperature</ClassName>
<Definition>Temperature</Definition>
<UnitOfMeasure>

<UOMClassID>Celsius</UOMClassID>

</UnitOfMeasure>
<UnitOfMeasure>

<UOMClassID>Kelvin</UOMClassID>
<Constant>-273</Constant>

</UnitOfMeasure>

</MeasureClass>
<MeasureClass>

<ClassID>Length</ClassID>
<ClassName>Length</ClassName>
<Definition>This is for length properties</Definition>
<UnitOfMeasure>

<UOMClassID>Metre</UOMClassID>

</UnitOfMeasure>
<UnitOfMeasure>

<UOMClassID>KiloMetre</UOMClassID>
<Scale>1000</Scale>

</UnitOfMeasure>

</MeasureClass>

In these cases, Celsius and Metre will be the base unit for their respective MeasureClass

as they have no conversion settings (Scale or Constant).

Note: Each UOMClassID will need to be separately associated to a SystemOfUOMClass

class (see SystemOfUOMClass Mappings).

Release 5.0.11

Page 99

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

SystemOfUOMClass Mappings

A SystemOfUOMClass is a grouping of related UOMClass classes (for example, all SI or all

Imperial units), and can be created using a mapping. For example:

<Mapping Type="SystemOfUOMClass">
<ClassID From="Class"/>
<ClassName From="ClassName"/>
<Definition From="Definition"/>
<UOMClass Condition="ClassID = 'SI'">
<UOMID From="UOM"/>
<UOMName From="Name"/>
<UOMDefinition From="Definition"/>
<UOMAbbrev From="Abbreviation"/>

</UOMClass>

</Mapping>

The example above would create class definition(s) such as:

<SystemOfUOMClass>

<ClassID>SI</ClassID>
<Definition>SI Units</Definition>
<UOMClass>

<ClassID>Metre</ClassID>
<ClassName>Metre</ClassName>
<Definition>Metre</Definition>
<Abbrev>m</Abbrev>

</UOMClass>
<UOMClass>

<ClassID>KiloMetre</ClassID>
<ClassName>KiloMetre</ClassName>
<Definition>KiloMetre</Definition>
<Abbrev>km</Abbrev>

</UOMClass>
<UOMClass>

<ClassID>Celsius</ClassID>
<ClassName>Celsius</ClassName>
<Definition>Celsius</Definition>
<Abbrev>C</Abbrev>

</UOMClass>
<UOMClass>

<ClassID>Kelvin</ClassID>
<ClassName>Kelvin</ClassName>
<Definition>Kelvin</Definition>
<Abbrev>K</Abbrev>

</UOMClass>

</SystemOfUOMClass>

Release 5.0.11

Page 100

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

7.7

Set Revision Sequences

The Data Extractor will write out document revisions in the order that it reads them from

the source. The AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard Import Controller will attempt to

load the first as the oldest revision and the last as the newest, and create an "is

succeeded by" association from the 'older' to 'newer' revisions. The Data Extractor can

explicitly put in the associations in the XML file if the SetSuccession setting is specified as

follows:

<Mapping Type="Object" SetSuccession="true">

<ID From="DocNo" />
<Name From="Title" />
<ClassID Value="Datasheet" />
<Context Value="MyPlant" />
<Revision From="Rev" />

</Mapping>

This will result in output such as:

<Object>

<ID>Doc1</ID>
<Context>

<ID>MyPlant</ID>

</Context>
<Revision>A</Revision>
<Name>Doc 1 Rev A Title</Name>
<ClassID>Datasheet</ClassID>
<Association type="is succeeded by">

<Object>
<ID>Doc1</ID>
<Context>

<ID>MyPlant</ID>

</Context>
<Revision>B</Revision>

</Object>
</Association>
</Object>

<Object>

The Data Extractor will set the first it finds as being succeeded by the second, and the

second by the third and so on.

7.8

Datasheet Mappings

The primary use of the Data Extractor is to take input from common lists, for example, Tag

Registers, Document Registers, Line Lists and so on. You can also map data from

datasheets. In order to map a datasheet, the data source definition must resemble:

<Datasource

Directory="Input"
ConnectionString="Provider=....;Extended Properties=&quot;Excel

12.0;IMEX=1;HDR=yes;&quot;"

Release 5.0.11

Page 101

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

Table="[Process Data] [Mech Data]"
Type="Datasheet"/>

The key differences from the OLEDB data sources described in Datasource Examples are:

· Type = "Datasheet"
· Table = "[Process Data] [Mech]"

The table can be a single worksheet name, or if multiple worksheets are to be mapped,

then a list of worksheets each surrounded by square brackets, that is, […].

The mappings will then be very similar to mapping lists except that if multiple

worksheets/ tables are specified in the data source section, then the source references

must be prefixed with the worksheet name and an underscore '_'.

If one table/worksheet is specified:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="string-length(B1)>0">

<ID From="B1"/>
<Context From="C7"/>
....

If more than one tables/worksheets are specified:

<Mapping Type="Object" Condition="string-length(Process_x0020_Data_B1)
>0">

<ID From="Process_x0020_Data_B1"/>
<Context From="Mech_E7"/>
....

The prefixes are shown in bold. Note that any special characters in the worksheet name

have to be encoded. Use the Tools> Datasource Schema menu option to determine the

worksheet names in the correct format.

Any empty rows at the top of the worksheets or empty columns to the left of the

worksheet will not be considered when specifying the cell references.

This means that if the cell C2 in the example above is to be mapped, in order to get the

text 'P-100', the reference would in fact have to be to B1.

Release 5.0.11

Page 102

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

7.9

Store the Last Run Start Time

It is possible to have the Data Extractor store the last time a configuration file was

processed (the start time of the processing, not the finish) within the configuration file.

This then can be used within an SQLSelect statement to retrieve only data modified since

the last time the configuration file was considered.

To get this stored, create a section in the configuration file:

<ConfigurationSettings>
<LastRunData>
<LastRunStarted>14/07/2010 11:05:18</LastRunStarted>
</LastRunData>
...

The Data Extractor will read this value and update it with the processing start time if the
processing completes successfully. If there is an error in the processing, then this will not
be updated.

The main use of this, is that it can be used within a SQLSelect statement (see Specify an

SQL SELECT Statement (not for XML file data sources)). The example below shows a

SharePoint data source where the intent is to retrieve only information from a list or

document library that has been modified since the last time this configuration file was

processed:

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;WSS;IM

EX=1;RetrieveIds=Yes;DATABASE=http://..;List={...}"

SQLSelect="select * from list where
Modified&gt;CDate('*LASTRUNTIME*')" />

The special variable *LASTRUNTIME* will be replaced by the date-time stored in the

configuration file's LastRunStarted setting described above.

Release 5.0.11

Page 103

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

7.10

Data Value Mapping

It is often necessary to change a value that has been read from a data source. There are a

number of ways of doing this with Data Extractor, for example by using regular

expressions as described above.

An additional way of changing the value reported is to use an external mapping table. For

example, if a value for a tag classification was read from the data source as 'P', then a

mapping table could be used to report the class to AVEVA NET as 'Pump'. Such a mapping

table could be stored in any data source format support by Data Extractor (Excel, Access,

Oracle and so on), and would likely resemble:

To Connect with OLEDB Data Sources:

To make use of an external mapping table, the first step is to identify the mapping table in

the Data Extractor configuration file:

<Datasource

File="InstList.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;......
Table="Sheet1"/>
<ValueMapping>
<ValueMappingDatasource

Id="Class Map"
File="Class_Mapping_List.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;..;"
Table="MappingSheet">
<Details SourceColumn="SourceClass"
TargetColumn="TargetClass"/>

</ValueMappingDatasource>
</ValueMapping>

<OutputFile
File="C:\DETests\Excel\MappingDatasource\Output\Mapping
Output_null.xml"/>

To Connect with ODBC Data Sources:

The ODBC data source support is provided to connect the PostGre SQL database systems

for lookups in a value mapping configuration.

The ODBC supporting library 'NpgSql' is to connect PostGre Sql Database and the user

needs to be configured like below:

Release 5.0.11

Page 104

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

<ValueMappingDatasource Id="NameMapping" OdbcProvider="NpgSql"
ConnectionString="Server=<IP>;Port=5432;Username=<username>;Password
=<password>;Database=<database>;"
SQLSelect="SELECT CONCAT_WS('\', filepathname, filename) AS filename,
documentnumber from pgtest where filename = abc.dwg
<Details SourceColumn="filename" TargetColumn="documentnumber" />
</ValueMappingDatasource>

If user wants to query with parameters, ODBC will support only named parameters and it

should be configured like below:

 <ValueMapping>
    <ValueMappingDatasource Id="NameMapping" OdbcProvider="NpgSql"
   ConnectionString="Server=<IP>;Port=5432;Username=<username>;Passwor
d=<password>;Database=<database>;"
        SQLSelect="SELECT CONCAT_WS('\', filepathname, filename) AS filename,
documentnumber from pgtest where filename = @filename1 or filename =
@filename2">
   <Parameters>
      <Parameter Name='FileName1' Type='string'>
          <ParamValue Value= abc.dwg' />
      </Parameter>
      <Parameter Name='FileName2' Type='string'>
          <ParamValue Value= abc.dwg' />
      </Parameter>
   </Parameters>

And the type of parameter (Type= string), the below datatypes are supporting for the

ODBC

Type =
"ansistring","string","ansistringfixedlength","int32","uint32","double","date","
currency","int64","datetime","boolean"
"single","object","decimal","guid","binary","byte","time","bigint","xml","varnu
meric","uint64","stringFixedLength";

Any number of ValueMappingDatasource sections can be specified. Next, the mapping

can be assigned to a mapping. For example:

<ConfigurationSettings>

<Datasource File="D:\InputFile\InputFile.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Extended
      Properties=&quot;Excel 12.0;HDR=YES;IMEX=1&quot;;"
Table="Sheet1">
      <Details FirstPopulatedRow="1" HeadingsRow="1" FirstDataRow="2" />
</Datasource>
<ValueMapping>

<ValueMappingDatasource Id="Class Map"
File="Class_Mapping_List.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;Extended
   Properties=&quot;Excel
12.0;HDR=YES;IMEX=1&quot;;"Table="MappingSheet">

         <Details SourceColumn="SourceClass"TargetColumn="TargetClass"/>
      </ValueMappingDatasource>
 </ValueMapping>

Release 5.0.11

Page 105

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

 <OutputFile File="D:\Output\Data Extractor\InputFile.xml" />
 <TemplateID>D:\Output\Data Extractor\InputFile.xml</TemplateID>
 <LogFile>Logs\LogFile.txt</LogFile>
 <Mapping Type="Object">
      <ID From="Pump" />
      <ClassID From="Class">

      <ValueMap>
            <Datasource Id = "Class Map"/>
            <FailAction>EmptyValue</FailAction>
       </ValueMap>

      </ClassID>
      <Context Value="Enter Context" />
   </Mapping>

</ConfigurationSettings>

The id for the data source 'Class Map' in the example above must match a

ValueMappingDatasource defined earlier in the configuration file.

It is likely that a value read from a data source is not present in the mapping file. In such

cases, the default behaviour will be to use the source value in the output. It is possible

however to omit the value by setting the FailAction to EmptyValue as shown above. An

additional option is to specify a fixed value for a failed mapping by setting the FailAction

to FixedValue and then specifying the fixed value using the setting

'ValueMapFailDefault' settings. For example:

<ClassID From="Class" ValueMapFailDefault="UNKNOWN">

<ValueMap>

<Datasource Id = "Class Map"/>
<FailAction>FixedValue</FailAction>

</ValueMap>

</ClassID>

Note: Any value manipulation that has been specified (for example, RegExSearch,

RegExRemovePattern) will be carried out before the value is passed to the mapping

table.

The Gateway can source data using an ODBC connection in a

<ValueMappingDatasource> clause.

Release 5.0.11

Page 106

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 7 - Mapping (Mandatory)

The new configuration will be as follows:

<Parameters>

<Parameter Name='FileName1' Type='string' >

<Value ApplyBehaviour="concat('ORIGINAL_FILENAME1', '::Template')"/>

</Parameter>
<Parameter Name='FileName2' Type='string' >

<Value ApplyBehaviour="concat('ORIGINAL_FILENAME2', '::Template')"/>

</Parameter>
</Parameters>

7.11

Include Mappings from External Files

To simplify a configuration file or to reuse part of a configuration, you can define

configuration settings in a separate file(s) and then 'include' it in the main configuration.

This uses the 'Include' setting:

...
<ClassID From="Class"/>
<Context Value="TEST"/>
<Include>Include File For Assoc Cfg.xml</Include>

The value is a file name and can be a full path or relative path (relative to the main

configuration file).The 'included' file would resemble:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ConfigurationSettings>

<Association Type="is a part of">
<TargetID From="Unit" />
<TargetContext Value="Test"/>

</Association>

</ConfigurationSettings>

The included file must have the first, second and final lines as shown above. Any valid

Data Extractor mapping can be put between the 'ConfigurationSettings'. The easiest

way to consider this is that the whole 'Include' line is replaced by the lines between the

'ConfigurationSettings' in the included file.

The setting from the included file would replace the 'Include' so the result would be

equivalent to:

…
<ClassID From="Class"/>
<Context Value="TEST"/>

<Association Type="is a part of">
<TargetID From="Unit"/>
<TargetContext Value="Test"/>

</Association>

An included file can include other files and there's no limit to the depth of the chain of

included files.

Release 5.0.11

Page 107

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Use Class-Characteristics Definitions

8

Use Class-Characteristics Definitions

It is often required to limit the characteristics for an object to a predefined set based on

the item's class. The sections below specify how this can be achieved with the Data

Extractor.

8.1

Define the Class-Characteristic Specification

The characteristics relevant to classes can be specified in a table in Excel, SQLServer or any

other source supported by the Data Extractor. A very simple example is shown:

Class

Pump

Pump

Vessel

Vessel

Attribute

Discharge Pressure

Suction Pressure

Normal Operating Pressure

Diameter

Such a definition can be made use of in Data Extractor and the first step is to specify in the

configuration file the details of the specification:

<Datasource

File="TestClassCharacteristics.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;…."
Table="TagRegister"/>
<ClassCharacteristicMapping>

<ClassCharacteristicDatasource

id="Class Char Check"
File="ClassCharacteristicsSpecification.xlsx"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;…"
Table="ClassCharacteristics">
<Details ClassColumn="Class" CharacteristicColumn="Attribute"/>

</ClassCharacteristicDatasource>

</ClassCharacteristicMapping>

Any number of ClassCharacteristicDatasource sections can be specified within a single

ClassCharacteristicMapping section. Each ClassCharacteristicDatasource section

instructs Data Extractor how to read the information from the class-characteristic

definition source plus the expected behaviour where the data deviates from this

definition.

Release 5.0.11

Page 108

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Use Class-Characteristics Definitions

The Details section (can also have FirstDataRow settings) contains two settings that

must be specified:

Class

Description

ClassColumn

The name of the column that holds the class names.

CharacteristicColumn

The name of the column that holds the characteristic
names.

8.2

Define Behaviour due to Data Deviation

The default behaviour is to omit any characteristics from the Data Extractor output that

are found in a datasource but that are not deemed relevant to the class. If a characteristic

is omitted this will be reported in the Data Extractor window (and the log file if one

specified):

However it is possible to specify different behaviour:

<ClassCharacteristicMapping>

<ClassCharacteristicDatasource

Id="Class Char Check"
File="ClassCharacteristicsSpecification.xlsx"

ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0;..."
Table="ClassCharacteristics">

<Details ClassColumn="Class" CharacteristicColumn="Attribute"/>
<AdditionalCharacteristics>

<Include Value="Extra">true</Include>

</AdditionalCharacteristics>
<MissingCharacteristics>

<Include Value="Missing">true</Include>

</MissingCharacteristics>
</ClassCharacteristicDatasource>

</ClassCharacteristicMapping>

There are two deviations from the definition: AdditionalCharacteristics and

MissingCharacteristics.

Release 5.0.11

Page 109

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Use Class-Characteristics Definitions

8.3

AdditionalCharacteristics

If a characteristic is found in the datasource for an item that is not deemed relevant for

that item's class then this characteristic is an 'additional' characteristic. By default

additional characteristics will be omitted. It is possible to have such 'additional'

characteristics included in the Data Extractor output either with their original value or a

replacement value:

<AdditionalCharacteristics>

<Include Value="Extra">true</Include>

</AdditionalCharacteristics>

The 'Value' setting is optional but if included its value, for example, "Extra" will be used in

place of any value for the characteristic read from the datasource. An empty string can

also be specified Value = "". So the output XML for such a characteristic could be:

…

<Characteristic>

<Name>Temperature</Name>
<Value>Extra</Value>

</Characteristic>

</Object>

</Association>

Note: If an item has a class that is not found in the class-characteristic specification then

all its attributes will be considered 'additional'.

8.4

MissingCharacteristics

If a characteristic is specified for a class (in the class-characteristic definition) but not

found in the datasource for an item then this characteristic is a 'missing' characteristic.

By default 'missing' characteristics will not be included in the Data  Extractor output. It is

possible to have such 'missing' characteristics included in the Data Extractor output with

a value specified as shown:

<AdditionalCharacteristics>

<Include Value="Missing">true</Include>

</AdditionalCharacteristics>

The 'Value' setting is optional (if omitted an empty string will be used) but if included its

value, for example, "Missing" will be used for the characteristic value. So the output XML

for such a characteristic could be:

…

<Characteristic>

<Name>Temperature</Name>
<Value>Missing</Value>

</Characteristic>

</Object>

</Association>

Release 5.0.11

Page 110

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 8 - Use Class-Characteristics Definitions

8.5

Apply a Class-Characteristic Specification
