The above sections have defined how to define a class-characteristic specification and

how to set the behaviour once something deviates from the specification. The last part is

to associate the class-characteristic specification to an 'Association' or 'Mapping' section

as these two 'hold' characteristics:

<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="Tag_x0020_Number"/>
<Context Value="Test" />
<Association Type="has dataset"
ClassCharacteristicDatasource="Class Char Check">

<TargetID ApplyBehaviour = "concat(Tag_x0020_Number,

'Dataset')" />

<TargetContext Value="Test" />
<TargetClassID Value="Test Dataset" />
<ClassCharacteristicClassIdLookUp From=" Type" />

<Characteristic>

<ID From="AttName"/>
<Value From="AttValue"/>

</Characteristic>

</Association>

The ClassCharacteristicDatasource setting specifies that the characteristics (and

properties) in the association will be checked against the

ClassCharacteristicDatasource with the id specified, that is, "Class Char Check". The

ClassCharacteristicClassIdLookUp specifies the class that will be used for the lookup

to determine the relevant characteristics, for example, the tag class. If no

ClassCharacteristicClassIdLookUp is specified then the class of the associated item

TargetClassID (or main item if defining the lookup against the top Mapping section) will

be used. If neither is provided then the check will not be possible.

Release 5.0.11

Page 111

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

9

Data Validation

The Data Extractor supports some aspects of data validation, namely:

· Validate that a value conforms to a pattern.
· Validate that a part or parts of a value conform to a pattern.
· Validate the a value (or a part of a value) is contained in a look up list.
· Validate that a value (or a part of a value) falls within a numeric range.

Any validation failure will be reported to the user in the Data Extractor user interface,

included in a log file (if specified), and included in an optional validation report.

The validation logic is specified within the configuration file and is specified by defining

validation section(s) for any mapping described in Mapping (Mandatory).

A validation section would resemble:

<ID From="Tag" ValidateOriginal="false">

<Validate Name="TagCheck" StopFurtherValidationOnFail="false">

<Pattern>[A-Z]-\d{3}</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>

</Validate>

</ID>

The available options for specifying a validation check are listed below:

Options

Name

Pattern

MaxValue

MinValue

Specifies Validation Checks

Mandatory.
Name used when reporting validation failures. It need not be
unique but could avoid confusion if it is.

Name Optional.
Pattern applied to a value to determine if it is valid. If a pattern
is specified (and matched), then further validation
checks within the same 'Validate' section (that is, Datasource,
MaxValue, MinValue) will be carried out on the result of the
pattern match. In this way, a pattern can be used to extract a
part of a value and then validate that part.

Optional.
Specify a maximum value. If a value exceeds this then the value
will fail the validation.

Optional.
Specify a minimum value. If a value is less than this then the
value will fail the validation.

Release 5.0.11

Page 112

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

Options

Datasource

Specifies Validation Checks

Optional.
Specify an external data source that contains a list of allowable
values, that is, a look up list. If a value is found in such a data
source then it is assumed to be valid, and 'MaxValue' and
'MinValue' checks within the same 'Validate' section will
not be carried out, that is, as there is no point in specifying a
data source plus a 'MaxValue' or 'MinValue' setting.

DateCheck

Optional.
Checks if values are valid dates.
<Validate Name="DateCheck">

<DateCheck
<FailAction>DiscardElement</FailAction>

</Validate>
The check that determines if a value is a valid date uses the .NET
DateTime.Parse method.

ValidateEmptyValue Optional.

Defaults to true.
Determines whether or not an empty value should have the
validation check applied. If set to false then an empty value will
never fail the validation check.
<Name From="Name">

<Validate Name="Name Format Check">

<Pattern>^[A-Z]+</Pattern>
<ValidateEmptyValue
>false</ValidateEmptyValue>

</Validate>

</Name>

Release 5.0.11

Page 113

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Options

FailAction

StopFurtherValidati
onOnFail

FailOnMatch

ReportFail

FileCheckExists

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

Specifies Validation Checks

Optional.
Specify what should happen if a value fails any validation rules.
If omitted any object/characteristic…. that fails
validation will be written to the output XML.
The allowable values are:
· DiscardElement - The value that failed validation is omitted

from the Data Extractor output. If this were a mandatory value
for an object (for example, 'ID'), then the object would not be
in the output (same as DiscardObject option).

· DiscardObject - The object to which the failed value belongs is
omitted from the Data Extractor output. The whole Mapping
section will not be applied for the 'failed' object.

· EmptyValue - The value that failed validation will be replaced

in the output by an empty string value.

· FixedValue - If a value fails validation then a fixed value will
be written to the output. The value written out is specified
using the setting 'ValidationFailDefault' For example:
<ID From="Area" ValidationFailDefault="No Area">

<Validate Name="Area Check">
<Pattern>\d{2}</Pattern>
<FailAction>FixedValue</FailAction>

</Validate>

</ID>

Optional.
Defaults to false.
If true this means that subsequent validation checks on the
value will not be carried out if the value fails this validation rule.
Only relevant if multiple Validate sections are specified for a
value mapping.

Optional.
Defaults to false. If true, this will mean that validation is failed if
the condition is matched. For example, if it is required that
validation is to fail if a tag were found in a lookup list, then this
could be done by setting this option to true.
This option is only relevant for Pattern and Datasource
validation checks.

Optional. Defaults to true. If false, then a failure will not be
reported in any way. The 'FailAction' will still be carried out.

Optional. Defaults to false.
If a value is expected to be a path to a file it is possible to
validate that the file exists. See Checking a File Exists for more
details.

Release 5.0.11

Page 114

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

Options

Specifies Validation Checks

ValidateOriginal

Optional. Defaults to false.
By default, validation of a value will be carried out after a value
has been modified by RegExPattern (and the other RegEx
options) and ValueMap (see Data Value Mapping). To use the
unmodified value for validation set this setting to 'true'.

ValidationFailClass Optional.

DuplicateCheck

Specifies a class to set for the main object if the item fails
validation. This will override any class value from class 'ClassID'
mapping for the object. The setting takes a text value.
<Name From="Tag" ValidationFailClass="Invalid Equipment
Tag">

<Validate Name="Name Format Check">

<Pattern>^[A-Z]{1,2}-\d{4}</Pattern>

</Validate>

</Name>
Any validation fail action specified will be applied in addition to
the classification change.

Note: Potentially, two or more mappings could have this setting,
each specifying a different fail class. In such a case only the last
one considered would be used. This will not impact any class
settings specified using associations.

Optional.
This setting can only be applied to an 'ID' mapping and will cause
a validation failure to be reported if two items are found in the
source with the same ID:
<ID From="Tag">

<Validate Name="Tag Format Check">

<Pattern>^[A-Z]{1,2}-\d{4}</Pattern>

</Validate>
<Validate Name="Duplicate Tag Check">

<DuplicateCheck>true</DuplicateCheck>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>

</Validate>

</ID>
For this purpose IDs are only considered duplicates if the ID,
Context and Revision values are identical.

9.1

Specify a Pattern

The simplest use of the pattern section is to specify that if a value is matched by the

regular expression pattern, then the value is valid.

<ID From="Tag">

<Validate Name="UnitCheck">

<Pattern>^\d{3}</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>

</Validate>

</ID>

Release 5.0.11

Page 115

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

In the above example, if a tag value begins ('^' indicates beginning) with three numbers

then the value is valid. If the whole value is to be validated, a pattern such as ^\d{3}-

[A-Z]-\d{3}$ could be specified, where '$' indicates the end of a value.

A pattern can also be used to check values against a basic lookup. The example below

checks that values are integers between 1 and 3:

<Characteristic>

<ID Value="Review Class"/>
<Value From="Approval_x0020_Class">

<Validate Name="Review Class Lookup Check">

<Pattern>[1-3]</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardElement</FailAction>

</Validate>

</Value>

9.2

Specify a Maximum and/or Minimum Value

It is possible to specify a maximum and/or minimum value for any mapping, as shown

below for a characteristic mapping:

<Characteristic ID="Temperature">

<Value From="Temp">

<Validate Name = "TempRangeCheck">

<FailAction>DiscardElement</FailAction>
<MaxValue>60</MaxValue>
<MinValue>0</MinValue>

</Validate>

</Value>
</Characteristic>

In the above example, any temperature found outside of the range specified will fail

validation and be omitted from the Data Extractor output (based on the 'FailAction'

specified).

9.3

Set FileCheckExists

When setting a value to point to a file (for example, when defining the InfoLocator

characteristic for a document file) it may be required to validate that the file exists to

avoid 'broken links' in AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard. A validation configuration, as

shown below, can be specified:

<Characteristic>

<ID Value="InfoLocator" />

<Value ApplyBehaviour="concat('http:/ServerName/Documents/',
Path)" >

<Validate Name="File Check">

<FileCheckExists
RegExSearch="http:/ServerName/Documents/
"RegExReplaceString="\

Release 5.0.11

Page 116

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

\ServerName\ShareName\Documents\">true</FileCheckExis
ts>
<Action>DiscardObject</Action>
<ReportFail>false</ReportFail>

</Validate>

</Value>

</Characteristic>

FileCheckExists will validate that the file represented by the 'Value' setting exists. The

check will only validate a simple file path (for example, C:\folder\..\filename.xls) or a

UNC path (for example, \\ServerName\ShareName\..\ filename.xls).

As the 'Value' for the InfoLocator will probably be a 'Http://…' style path, a search and

replace will be needed to convert the path style (as shown above).

'RegExSearch' setting will take a value of a Regular Expression pattern that will be

applied to the 'Value' and 'RegExReplaceString' will take a simple text string.

9.4

Lookups

9.4.1

Define Lookup List(s)

It is possible to define one of more lookup lists, for example, a supplier list or a classes list,

in external files that can be used by the Data Extractor for validation of values. In such

cases, the source of the list must be specified in the configuration file before any mapping

sections (see high level file structure in Configuration Files). Specifying a list source is

almost identical to specifying a data source (see Output File/Directory (Mandatory)).

There can be any number of such list sources specified in a single configuration file. For

example:

<Validation>

<ValidationDatasource

Id="Ds1"
File="C:\Data Extractor Tests\TestValidation\ClassList.xls"
ConnectionString="EXCEL"
Table="Sheet1">
<Details

HeadingsRow="1"
FirstDataRow="2"
LookupList="Class"/>

</ValidationDatasource>
<ValidationDatasource

Id="Ds2"
File="C:\Data Extractor Tests\TestValidation\TagPattern.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB....;Excel
12.0;HDR=YES&quot;"
Table="Sheet1">
<Details

LookupList="Prefix"
LookupListRegExPattern="^[A-Z]-[A-Z]{3}.*"
LookupListRegExRemovePattern="^[A-Z]-|\s+">

Release 5.0.11

Page 117

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

</ValidationDatasource>
<ValidationDatasource

Id="Ds3"
File="C:\Data Extractor Tests\TestValidation\TagPattern.xls"
ConnectionString="Provider=Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB....;Excel
12.0;HDR=YES&quot;"
Table="Sheet1">
<Details

HeadingsRow="5"
FirstDataRow="7"
FirstPopulatedRow="1"
LookupList="Unit"/>
</ValidationDatasource>

</Validation>

The above example shows three list sources (all Excel, but they need not be). As there are

potentially multiple list sources, each one must have an id and a single column name

'LookupList' (this follows same rules for special characters as other column mappings)

from the source, specifying where the list values can be found. Each of the

ValidationDatasource sections represents a list of values from an external file or database.

If two (or more) ValidationDatasource sections have the same 'Id' value then the values in

these will be combined to create a single lookup list. This is useful if the values for a

lookup list need to come from two different data sources.

Note that in the above example, the second and third sources are from different columns

in the same file.

The optional setting LookupListRegExPattern applies the regular expression pattern

specified to each value found in the look up list source and the resulting value is

considered to be the lookup value (not the original). If a value is found that isn't matched

by the pattern then the value is not included in the lookup list.

The optional setting LookupListRegExRemovePattern removes any text that matches

the pattern from each value found in the look up list source and the resulting value is

considered to be the lookup value (not the original).

Note: The setting LookupListRegExPattern is applied before the setting

LookupListRegExRemovePattern.

Release 5.0.11

Page 118

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

9.4.2

Use Lookup List(s)

Once a lookup list has been defined it is possible to reference it within the validation

sections of any mapping within the same configuration file, for example:

<ClassID From="Class">

<Validate Name="ClassCheck">
<Datasource Id="Ds1"/>

</Validate>

</ClassID>

In the above example, each value read from the 'Class' column will be validated against

the lookup list defined by the ID 'Ds1'.

Note that in the above example no FailAction is specified, therefore any invalid values

will be reported but will still be included in the Data Extractor output.

9.5

Specify a Pattern to Validate Parts of a Value

It is possible to validate parts of a value. For example in the case of a tag with a value of

51-XV-1000, the three parts of this tag may each have their own significance and could be

validated individually:

<ID From="Tag">

<Validate Name="UnitCheck" ReportDetails="false">

<Pattern>^\d{3}</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>
<Datasource Id="Ds1"/>

</Validate>
<Validate Name="PrefixCheck" ReportDetails="false">

<Pattern>[A-Z]{2}</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>
<Datasource Id="Ds2"/>

</Validate>
<Validate Name="SeqNumCheck">

<Pattern>\d(4}$</Pattern>
<FailAction>DiscardObject</FailAction>
<MinValue>1000</MinValue>
<MaxValue>5000</MaxValue>

</Validate>

</ID>

The three validation sections above will:

· Take first three numbers and compare them against a list of unit numbers in an

external lookup.

· Take the two letters and compare them against a list of function codes in an

external lookup.

· Take the last four numbers and check that the value is between 1000 and 5000.

Release 5.0.11

Page 119

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

Notes:

· If the pattern fails to find a match then the value will fail the validation.
· All validation checks will be carried out, even if an earlier one fails.
· If a value is to pass validation if a pattern is matched or the value is empty then a

pattern such as this achieves that:

<Pattern>^\(d{2}-[A-Z]{3}$)|^$</Pattern>

· By default, if a 'Pattern' is failed then the pattern will be reported to the user and

reports generated, for example:

Such patterns are not going to be understood by general users, so these can be

omitted by setting the flag ReportDetails="false" as shown above. In this case

the result would be:

9.6

Create a Validation Dataset

The Data Extractor may record validation failures using a dataset that is linked to the

object that has failed validation. To do this, include in the validation section of the

configuration file a Dataset section:

<Validation>
<Report>
…
</Report>

<Dataset>

<ID>Tag Validation Dataset</ID>
<Revision>*DATE*</Revision>
<Class>Validation Dataset</Class>
<AssociationType>Has Validation Dataset
</AssociationType>

</Dataset>
…

Release 5.0.11

Page 120

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

With such a setting, any object that fails validation will get an associated dataset. Any

object that passes all validation rules will not get such a dataset created. The settings for

the dataset are explained in the table below:

Settings

ID

Revision

Class

Description

Optional.
By default any dataset object created will be given an ID that is the
tag/object ID followed by the text 'Validation Dataset', for example,
'200-P-101 Validation Dataset'. If the ID setting is specified then the
text 'Validation Dataset' will be replaced by whatever test is
specified.

Optional.
By default the dataset revision setting will not be included in the
XML output. If the 'Revision' setting is included then a revision will
be written out for the dataset. The 'Revision' can be set to a fixed
value, for example, 'A' or can use Special Variables.

Mandatory.
This specifies the class for the dataset to be created. If no class is
specified for the dataset then the dataset will not be created.

AssociationType Optional.

By default, the association type created between the object being
validated and the validation dataset is 'has dataset'. A different
association type can be specified using this setting.

The resulting XML for an object that fails validation will resemble:

…
<Association type="has dataset">

<Object>

<ID>P-100 Tag Validation Dataset</ID>
<Context>
<ID>TEST</ID>
</Context>
<Revision>4/19/2013</Revision>
<ClassID>Validation Dataset</ClassID>
<Characteristic>
<Name>Valid</Name>
<Value>False</Value>
</Characteristic>
<Characteristic>
<Name>Description</Name>
<Value>Tag Format Check: Pattern '^[A-Z]{1,2}-\d{4}' failed
for value: 'P-100'. </Value>
</Characteristic>

</Object>

</Association>

Release 5.0.11

Page 121

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

So the dataset will have two characteristics:

· Valid: indicates that the object has failed 1 or more validation rules. If the dataset
exists this will always be false. Note: make sure you add a characteristic class to

AVEVA NET so this dataset will load.

· Description: string listing the rule(s) that failed.

The characteristic names shown above are the default ones (that is, 'Valid' and

'Description'). These can be modified if required as follows. The first characteristic 'Valid'

can be modified by setting the 'ValidFlagAttributeName' property in the 'Dataset'

section, for example:

<Dataset>

<Class>Validation Dataset</Class>
<ValidFlagAttributeName>Is Valid Flag
</ValidFlagAttributeName>

</Dataset>

The second characteristic 'Description' can be changed and in fact >1 characteristic

specified (one per validation rule on a value being read) as follows:

 ID="Unit Number">

<Value From="Unit">
<Validate Name="Invalid Unit"

ValidationDatasetCharacteristic = "Invalid NonNumeric Unit Format">
<Pattern>\d{4}|^$</Pattern>

</Validate>
<Validate Name="Invalid Temp"
ValidationDatasetCharacteristic = "Invalid Non AlphaNumeric Unit Format">

<Pattern>[A-Z]\d{4}|^$</Pattern>

</Validate>

</Value>

In the above, case two characteristics, 'Invalid NonNumeric Unit Format' and 'Invalid

Non-AlphaNumeric Unit Format' could be written out to the dataset XML. If an

attribute is not specified for a 'Validate' section, the default attribute 'Description' will be

used. If the same attribute is specified for more than one 'Validate' sections (or the

default used), then the validation messages will be concatenated in the attribute value.

Release 5.0.11

Page 122

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

9.7

Create a Validation Report File

Validation results are reported to the user in the Data Extractor and also written to a log

file if specified. You can use the Gateway to create a validation report. The report can be

either a text file or an HTML file. A sample Tag validation report is as follows:

To do this, specify a 'Report' section in the 'Validation' section as follows:

<Validation IncludeAllItems="false" >

<Report>

<FileName>ValidationReport.txt</FileName>
<Heading>Tag Validation Report</Heading>
<Class>Document Content</Class>
<Context>Test</Context>
<SaveToExcel>true</SaveToExcel>
<TagEveryLine>true</TagEveryLine>
<Colours>
<Background>White</Background>
<HeadingBackground>Lightgray</HeadingBackground>
<Text>Blue</Text>
</Colours>

<AdditionalColumns>
<Column>Service</Column>
<Column>Manufact.</Column>

</AdditionalColumns>

</Report>
<ValidationDatasource>

...

</ValidationDatasource>

</Validation>

Release 5.0.11

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

where:

Settings

FileName

Heading

Class

Context

SaveToExcel

TagEveryLine

Colours

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

Description

Mandatory. Name of the report file. If no path is included then
the report will be written to the same directory as the Data
Extractor config file.
If the file extension is html or htm then the report will be a html
report otherwise it will be a text report.

Optional. A title to display at top of the report.

Optional. The class of the report document. This should be a valid
AVEVA NET document class.

Optional. The context for the report document.

Optional. Sets whether or not the 'Save to Excel' button is
displayed. The default is not to display this button. Set the value
to 'true' to make the button appear. Excel must be present on the
client machine for this button to work.
Depending on the user's Internet Explorer settings and the
AVEVA NET Workhub and Dashboard site's trusted status the
user may be prompted 'An ActiveX control might be unsafe to
interact with other parts of the page. Do you want to allow this
interaction?' or the page may error. To resolve these issues the
user's Internet Explorer ActiveX settings (Tools->Internet
Options->Security->Custom Level) need to be changed: set
'Initialize and script ActiveX controls not marked as safe for
scripting' to 'Prompt' or 'Enable'.

Optional. If 'true' then the tag will be reported in the 1st column
for  every row in the report. Otherwise if a tag fails multiple
validation rules (therefore has multiple rows in the report) then
the tag will be listed on the first row only giving the appearance
of grouping the validation messages by tag.
Default = false.

Optional. Some of the colours of the HTML report produced can
be modified.
Background - sets the background colour of the HTML page
HeadingBackground - set the background colour of the table
header Text - sets the colour of all the text on the report. The
setting shown in the example above results in a report such as:

AdditionalColumns Optional. By default the report will have only two columns: 'ID'
and 'Validation Error' it is possible to add additional columns to
the report.

Settings

Description

To do this you must first add the column names to the report
definition:
<AdditionalColumns>

<Column>Service</Column>
<Column>Manufact.</Column>

</AdditionalColumns>
In this example two new columns will be added as shown:

In order to assign values to the report you must assign values to
columns as you map from the datasource:
<Characteristic >

<ID Value="Manufacturer"/>
<Value From="Manufacturer"
ValidationReportColumn="Manufact."/>

</Characteristic>
The setting above specifies that the 'Manufacturer' value will be
added to the cell in the corresponding to the 'Manufact.' column
in the report. The 'ValidationReportColumn' setting can be
used on any mapping and not just for characteristics.

Optional. If 'true' then all items will be reported even if they have
passed all validation rules. Items that pass valid will get a row
with a light green background:

IncludeAllItems

Default = false.

The Data Extractor will create a '<FileName>.vnet' file along with the report file that

will include the class and context if specified.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

The report file will contain information on all the tags found in the datasource that failed

validation. Some tags may not be reported in the output XML as they have failed

validation rules. For those tags that are included in the output XML an association to the

validation report document will be automatically included in the output XML and those

tags will be hotspotted in the HTML validation report (not relevant if text report selected).

The hot-spotting will aid navigation when the report is loaded into AVEVA NET.

DateTime Format Patterns

You can reformat the date and time mentioned in the data source file to any of the date

and time formats supported by Microsoft .NET.

When the Gateway reads any column representing a date, the dates are extracted to a

serial number format, see Reading Excel Serialised Dates. By default this is converted to

the local machine configured date and time format when written to the EIWM file. You

can convert to any other date and time format by setting the attribute

ConvertSerialDate="true" and using the attribute

DateTimeFormat="MM/dd/yyyy".

For example:

<Characteristic >

<ID Value="Date"/>
<Value From="Date" ConvertSerialDate="true" DateTimeFormat="MM/dd/yyyy"
ValidationReportColumn="DateBlock"/>

</Characteristic>

Release 5.0.11

Page 126

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

yyyy MMMM

18:30

· When you enter an invalid date and time format, a warning message will be
logged specifying that the date format mentioned is incorrect and the default

format will be used, that is, the local machine configured date format.

· If you use a Windows Server 2012 machine where the date format is changed,

then you need to restart that machine for the changes to take effect.

CHAPTER 9 - Data Validation

9.8

Add Security Access Groups to the Validation

Security access groups (SAGs) can be applied to validation datasets (see Create a

Validation Dataset) and validation reports (see Create a Validation Report File) in order to

restrict access to them. One of more SAGs can be specified, as shown below:

<Validation>
<Report>

<FileName>..\Output\ValidationReport.html</FileName>
<Heading>Tag Validation Report</Heading>
<Class>Validation Report</Class>

</Report>
<Dataset>

<ID>Tag Validation Dataset</ID>
<Revision>*DATE*</Revision>
<Class>Validation Dataset</Class>

</Dataset>
<SecurityAccessGroups>

<SecurityAccessGroup>
<ID>SAG1</ID>
</SecurityAccessGroup>
<SecurityAccessGroup>
<ID>SAG2</ID>
</SecurityAccessGroup>

</SecurityAccessGroups>

</Validation>

Release 5.0.11

Page 128

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Data Analysis

10

Data Analysis

The Data Extractor enables the user to perform an analysis of the data in a data source to:

· See the column names in the data source.
· Select column(s) to determine the unique values that exist in the column(s) and

the value’s frequency.

· Select column(s) to determine the pattern/format of values that exist in the

column(s) and the pattern's frequency.

To perform the analysis, a configuration file must be created. The primary purpose of the

configuration file is to identify the data source to be analysed, but other minimum

information must also be there. For example:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ConfigurationSettings>

<Datasource

ConnectionString="Provider=SQLOLEDB.1;........"

Table="Docs_Summary">

</Datasource>
<OutputFile File="Test.xml"/>
<Mapping Type="Object">

<ID From="Doc_No"/>

</Mapping>

</ConfigurationSettings>

After selecting a configuration file from the Data Extractor, the Tools > Analyse

Datasource menu option will become available:

Release 5.0.11

Page 129

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Data Analysis

A summary of the data source will then be presented:

To analyse the data in data source columns, select one or more column and then choose a

button from the right.

10.1

Analyse Values Report

If the Values Report button is selected, a list of all the unique values in the selected

column(s), along with a count of the number of times a value is displayed, with the

percentage that equates to the total rows in the data source:

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 10 - Data Analysis

It is also possible to hide empty values or to show only empty values by right-clicking on

the screen and selecting the required option:

10.2

Analyse Pattern Report

If the Pattern Report button is selected, a list of all the unique value patterns in the

selected column(s), along with a count of the number of times a pattern appears, with the

percentage that equates to the total rows in the data source:

It is also possible to see the value corresponding to any pattern by selecting the pattern,

as it is a link to it:

From the values screen you can additionally choose to see a value broken into its

component parts, to do this right-click and 'Show Parts':

The parts will be displayed:

The parts are determined by splitting the value at a space or dashes.

CHAPTER 11 - Status Messages from Gateway Processing

11

Status Messages from Gateway Processing

Status Messages from Gateway processing

The log file contains all information, warnings and errors relevant to a project’s processing

of source data, However, this is often not a convenient format for you to assess whether

the processing has proceeded as expected. A better way to monitor the status of a

Gateway's processing is to use an additional option for generating a JavaScript Object

Notation (JSON) report file. This option is controlled by the project setting in the selected

section of the Gateway (this option is set to false by default).

<GenerateCategorizedLog>true</GenerateCategorizedLog>

When set to true, a JSON report file is written in the same location as the log file. The

level of detail in the JSON report file depends on the project settings.

The JSON report contains headers with general information as listed below:

Field

"Product"

Value

Description

"AVEVA Components
Reporting Utility"

Reporter program name.

"ReportingVersion"

"1.1.0.0"

Reporter program version.

"ReportedProduct"

"AVEVA NET Data
Extractor Gateway"

Gateway name.

"ReportedProductVersion
"

"5.0.10.0"

Gateway version number.

"StartDateTime"

"06-04-2019 19 06 43"

Processing start time.

"FinishDateTime"

"06-04-2019 19 06 44"

Processing end time.

"ElapsedTime"

"00 00 00 (0.97
seconds)"

Time of the processing.

"SourceName"

"905677small.csv"

Name of source data.

er"

"ProcessingResult"

"Success"

"OutputData"

...

processed objects.

following note).

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 11 - Status Messages from Gateway Processing

Note: The ProcessingResult field is set according to the following rules:

· “Success”, if no warnings or errors are detected.
· “Warnings”, if at least one warning is detected but no errors.
· “Errors”, if at least one minor error is detected but none that have halted the
processing, for example, one or more objects might have been skipped but the

entire source was read and processed.

· “Failure”, if a major error has caused the processing to be terminated early.

The JSON report then contains one or more status messages:

"ID"

"DateTime"

"Data Extractor"

"Severity"

"Warning"

component of the generated
message.

g/Error/ErrorProcessingFail
ed/ErrorCritical.

"

(see the following table.).

Message

Access

Break

Message Description

An Issue connected with accessing provided location, file or database.

Processing interrupted by the expiration of a timeout or cancellation by a
user.

missing installation of required software

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 11 - Status Messages from Gateway Processing

Message
Category

Extract

Message Description

Problem with extracting the source data, such as unexpected API
behaviour.

Information

Status message not connected with a bug nor low level processing. Only
for severity Information.

Initialize

Internal

Invalid

Issues connected with initialisation of the processing.

An uncategorised issue.

Formatting issue with provided data: input file, configuration and
connection string.

License

Issue with validating license.

MissingData

Expected data required for processing is missing in expected location.

Overload

Issue with the amount of data or crossing an allowed range.

Redundancy

Configuration contains redundant expressions for mapping, lookup table
contains multiple items which are expected to be unique or processed
data contains repeated entity or reference, causing overwriting during
processing.

Reformat

The format of the data has been adjusted to comply with the expected
output format.

Respond

No response or an unexpected response to a query sent by the Gateway.

Support

Limited or no support of the type of source data or configuration item.

Transform

An issue related to the transform mapping being used.

Unknown

An unhandled exception received from code supplied by a 3rd party.

JSON Report Limitations:

The report is generated during processing each file on the Gateway level. Therefore, it

does not contain messages connected with issues, which may appear during Gateway

initialisation, for example, a missing license or use of incorrectly formatted configuration

files. In such cases, if the Gateway is run from the GUI, then a message is delivered as a

pop-up. Otherwise, it can be found in the Summary.txt file written in the log folder.

Release 5.0.11

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 12 - Running the Gateway from the Command Line

12

Running the Gateway from the Command Line

The Gateway can be run from the command line instead of the user interface:

"C:\...\AVEVA.NET.DataExtractor.exe" -c "c:\..\myconfig.xml"

Setting

Description

-c "c:\..\myconfig.xml"

The configuration file to be used as the basis of the run.

-d "c:\..\cfg_dir"

A directory containing configuration files, all of which will
be processed.

-f "c:\...\MyFile.csv"

The source input file. Any input file specified in the
configuration file will be ignored.

-o "c:\..\Myfile_null.xml" The output file. Any output file settings specified in the

-v

configuration file will be ignored.

Runs the equivalent of a Tools >Validate Mapping menu
option command, writing results to log file if specified in
the configuration file.

-u "myusername"

-p "mypassword"

-s "proxyservername"

If the cfg file (specified with the -c setting) is stored on a
protected web location then the username and password
and, optionally, the proxy server name can be specified
using these settings.

*VariableName*
"Variable Value "

-e "c:\...\ExportFile.txt"

-n

The use of variables allows the passing in of a value to the
configuration file from the command line. For example, if
you do not want to hard code the context in the config file
you can use a variable in the config file, for example,
Value= "*MyContext* "
and set the value of that from the command line:
…exe -c "…. " *MyContext* "IPE Test"
Any number of variables can be used from the command
line and the variable name can be anything you like.
However, they must start and end with the '*' character. If
there are spaces in the variable name or value then they
must be surrounded by double quotes. Variables can be
used for 'ApplyBehaviour', 'From', 'SQLSelect' and other
mappings as well as 'Value' mappings.

If specified the data source contents will be written as a
comma separated data to the file specified.
Note if using the LastRunData setting then the date will
be updated when running an export from the command
line.

Run equivalent of a 'Tools -> Datasource Schema' menu
command writing results to log file if specified in the config
file.

Release 5.0.11

Page 135

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 12 - Running the Gateway from the Command Line

Note: The –e and –o options should not both be used at the same time. If they are used,

the –e option which is applied, and the –o option is ignored.

Exit Codes

The following command line exit codes are defined for AVEVA.NET.DataExtractor.exe.

Exit Code

0

1001

1002

1003

1004

Definition

Success

License checkout failed due to expiry of license

License checkout failed due to not enough seats

License for the product is missing or not requested

Invalid license server details/unavailable

Note: The exit code returned from the gateway can be captured in the %errorlevel%

variable when executed in batch mode.

CHAPTER 13 - Use Plugins to Add New Datasource Types

13

Use Plugins to Add New Datasource Types

Data Extractor by default supports a number of datasource types; for example, CSV, XML

and those that support connection strings (Databases, SharePoint, Excel). Alternative

datasource types (for example, for a document or maintenance management system) can

be added by writing code to create a plugin DLL that Data Extractor will know how to

communicate with it. An example C# plugin is provided under the Data Extractor install

directory 'Examples\Plugin DataProvider Example.zip', this can be used as a

template to follow so that code specific to the datasource to be added can be written. The

structure of the class within the DLL that Data Extractor will interact with, is shown below:

Once the DLL has been created it will need to be placed on the same machine as the Data

Extractor application and then configuration files written that are similar to any other Data

Extractor configuration file except that the ‘Datasource’ will be specified differently. A

simple example configuration is shown  below:

<ConfigurationSettings>

<Datasource Type="Plugin" Name="DataProviderExample.dll"
Parameters="-x abc -c xyz" ParametersEncrypted="false"/>
<OutputFile File="Output\Tags_null.xml" />
<Mapping Type="Object">
<ID From="Tag" />
<Name From="Description" />

…..

</Mapping>

</ConfigurationSettings>

Release 5.0.11

Page 137

© Copyright 2003 to current year. AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

CHAPTER 13 - Use Plugins to Add New Datasource Types

The settings are explained in the table below:

Settings

Type

Name

Parameters

ParametersEncrypted

Description

Mandatory
Must be set to "Plugin".

Mandatory
Name/location of the new DLL that has been created.
This can be specified in a number of ways:
- If the DLL is in the ‘Plugins’ sub-folder of the Data
Extractor install directory then the file name can be
specified as in the example above.
- If the DLL is in another directory then the full path to
the DLL can be specified:
Name=c:\Somefolder\DataProviderExample.dll
- The DLL can be pointed to using a relative path
(relative to the Data Extractor configuaration file):
Name=Somefolder\DataProviderExample.dll
- In all cases the user that Data Extractor runs as will
need access to the DLL.

Optional
If the DLL requires parameters/arguments then these
should all be listed in this setting in one string. This
string will be passed to the DLL and the DLL will need to
handle the string and do whatever is necessary.

Optional. Default = “false”.
The parameters may include a user name and password
therefore encryption may be required. The whole
Parameter value can be specified as an encrypted
string if the ‘ParametersEncrypted’ setting is ‘true’.
To get the encrypted value use the encryption dialog
from the Tools>Encrypt menu. There you can enter the
unencrypted string and get the corresponding
encrypted string:

This can be copied to the ‘Parameters’ setting in the
configuration file:
<Datasource

Type="Plugin"
Name="DataProviderExample.dll"
Parameters="-E0TkqcnMuVtJtjOe+5Dh1w=="
ParametersEncrypted="true"

/>

· PlannedEnd
· ActualStart
· ActualEnd
· LateStart
· LateEnd
· ActualLaborQuantity
· ActualEquipmentQuantity
· RemainingLaborQuantity
· RemainingEquipmentQuantity
· TargetLaborQuantity
· RemainingDuration
· TargetDuration
· ActualLaborCost
· ActualEquipmentCost
· ActualMaterialCost
· ScheduledLaborQuantity
· PerformedLaborQuantity

Note: For more information, see the AVEVA Primavera documentation.

CHAPTER 16 - Appendix C - Enable Win32 Long Path Support on Windows Server 2016 and .NET
4.6.2

AVEVA NET Data Extractor Gateway User Guide

16

Appendix C - Enable Win32 Long Path Support on
Windows Server 2016 and .NET 4.6.2

Long paths are not enabled by default on Windows Server 2016. You need to perform the

following to enable it:

1.

Enable a policy named Enable Win32 long paths to enable the long paths on

Windows Server 2016.

You can open the group policy window by running gpedit.msc from the Run

command Windows-R.

In the Local Group Policy Editor navigate to Local Computer Policy-

>Computer Configuration->Administrative Templates->All Settings.

In this location, you can find Enable Win32 long paths".

Release 5.0.11

CHAPTER 17 - Appendix D - Compatibility with 8.3 Filenames

17

Appendix D - Compatibility with 8.3 Filenames

Files generated by an 8.3 filename system (for example, very old files that end in ~1, ~2

and so on) may be read ambiguously as the default behaviour for Windows is to ignore

everything after the ~ character. Hence, Windows cannot distinguish between "APPX-

C~1.xml" and "APPX-C~1_null.xml".

To resolve the compatibility issue with 8.3 Filenames, you can disable the 8.3 filename

generation at the OS level:

1.

 Run the command prompt in Admin mode and type the following commands

after navigating to C:\ drive:

· To disable the 8.3 Filename: fsutil.exe behavior set disable8dot3 1

· To re-enable 8.3 Filename: fsutil.exe behavior set disable8dot3 0
